name: Sesame Butter Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup soft butter
1 1/2 cup sugar
3 cup flour
1 cup sesame seed (toasted)
2 cup shredded coconut
1/2 cup finely chopped almonds
instruction_blocks:
1. In large mixing bowl cream butter. Gradually add sugar and beat until light and fluffy.
2. Add flour and mix, just until combined.
3. Stir in sesame seed, coconut, and almonds, just until well mixed.
4. Divide dough into thirds. Place 1/3 on long sheet of wax paper.
5. Shape into a long roll 2" in dia.
notes: Recipe from the kitchen of Rita Sloan. Serves 4 dozen. Recipe card appears to be cut off - likely includes additional instructions for chilling and baking the cookie rolls.
tags: cookies, butter cookies, sesame, coconut, slice and bake

name: Peanut Butter Balls
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup peanut butter
1 cup confectioners sugar
1 cup chopped walnuts
1 cup chopped dates (don't have to have)
1 tbsp butter or margarine
3/4 oz German sweet chocolate
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix peanut butter, sugar, nuts, dates (if using), and butter well.
2. Break chocolate into top of double broiler and let it melt while shaping dough into marble size balls.
3. With melted spatula or knife spread top of balls with melted chocolate and swirl giving bonbon effect.
4. Refrigerate until set.
notes: Makes about 110 balls. The dates are optional according to the recipe.
tags: candy, no-bake, peanut butter, chocolate, confection

name: Cheese Cake
summary: Classic cheesecake with zwieback crust
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 box zwieback crumbs
cinnamon
sugar (for crust)
butter (very thick along sides of pan)
1 lb cream cheese
1/2 lb pot cheese
1 cup sugar (add to the cheese)
1 tsp vanilla
1/2 tsp almond extract
1/2 tsp fresh lemon
1/2 pint sour cream
3 tbs flour
6  eggs
instruction_blocks:
1. Take pot cheese put in mixer, beat smooth.
2. Add cream cheese and mix, add sugar, beat well.
3. Separate eggs, drop yolks one at a time in mixture, add flavoring and sour cream and flour, mix well.
4. Beat egg whites until stiff, fold in whites.
5. Put in oven at 325 to 350. Bake for 1 hr.
6. Turn off oven and leave in at least 10 min.
notes: Crust is made with zwieback crumbs mixed with cinnamon and sugar, butter applied very thick along sides of pan. Some handwriting unclear - 'pot cheese' is similar to ricotta or farmer's cheese.
tags: dessert, cheesecake, baking, classic

name: Cookie Wreaths
ingredient_blocks:
3 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
1/4 tsp salt
1/4 cup sugar
2 tsp grated orange rind
1 1/3 cup butter or margarine
1/4 cup orange juice
1  egg white
1 tsp water
1/4 cup sugar
1/3 cup ground almonds
1 tsp grated orange rind
1  egg white
1 3/4 cup sifted powdered sugar
green paste food coloring
red cinnamon candies
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine all-purpose flour, salt, sugar and orange rind, cut in the butter with a pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
2. Sprinkle the orange juice evenly over the surface, stir mixture with a fork until dry ingredients are moistened.
3. Shape dough into a ball; cover and chill.
4. Work with half of dough at time and store remainder in refrigerator.
5. Divide first portion into 48 balls.
6. Roll 2 balls into two 5-inch ropes.
7. Place ropes on lightly greased cookie sheet, pinch ends together to form wreath shape.
notes: Some handwriting was difficult to read. The recipe appears to continue beyond what is fully visible. Instructions for finishing the wreaths with icing and decorations may be incomplete.
tags: cookies, Christmas, holiday, wreaths, orange

name: Cookie Recipe
summary: Recipe from the kitchen of - appears to be twisted rope-style cookies with almond topping
ingredient_blocks:
1  egg white (for egg wash)
water (combine with egg white)
1/4 cup sugar (for topping)
almond (for topping)
1 tsp orange rind (for topping)
1  egg white (beat until frothy, stir in powdered sugar)
powdered sugar
food coloring (small amount)
instruction_blocks:
1. Pinch ends together at one end to seal
2. Twist ropes together and shape strip into circle
3. Pinch ends to seal. Repeat, makes 46 cookies
4. Combine 1 egg white and water and brush mixture over cookies
5. Combine 1/4 cup sugar, almond, and 1 tsp orange rind; sprinkle mixture evenly over cookies
6. Bake at 400° for 8 to 10 minutes
7. Beat 1 egg white until frothy, stir in powdered sugar and color with small amount of food coloring
notes: This appears to be a partial recipe - likely the second page of shaping and finishing instructions for twisted rope cookies. The main dough recipe may be missing. Makes approximately 46 cookies. Some handwriting is difficult to read with certainty.
tags: cookies, twisted cookies, almond, orange, holiday baking

name: Chocolate Dipped Orange Logs
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup butter or margarine
1/2 cup sifted powdered sugar
1 tsp grated orange rind
1 tsp orange extract
2 cup all purpose flour
6 oz chocolate flavored candy coating
1/2 cup finely chopped toasted almonds
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream butter; gradually add sugar, beating until light and fluffy.
2. Stir in orange rind and extract.
3. Gradually add flour to butter mixture, mix well.
4. Shape dough into 2 1/2 x 1/2 in logs. Place on ungreased cookie sheets.
5. Flatten three quarters of each cookie lengthwise with a fork to 1/4 thickness.
6. Bake at 350° for 12 to 14 min.
7. Let cool. Dip unflattened tips of cookies in melted coating to coat both sides.
8. Roll chocolate ends into almonds. Cool.
notes: Some handwriting was difficult to read, particularly around measurements and certain words. Recipe appears to be from a vintage recipe card collection.
tags: cookies, chocolate, orange, Christmas, holiday

name: Filled Cookies
summary: Cookie bars with apricot preserve or chocolate chip topping and meringue
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup butter
1/2 cup margarine
1 cup granulated sugar
1 tsp vanilla
3  egg yolks
2 cup all-purpose flour (sifted)
1/2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp baking soda
1 cup apricot preserve (or 2 pkg (8 oz) semi-sweet chocolate bits)
1/3 cup sugar (for meringue)
3  egg whites
1 cup walnuts (finely chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat butter or margarine, sugar, vanilla until light and fluffy.
2. Beat in the egg yolks.
3. Mix in the combined flour, salt and soda which has been sifted.
4. With the palm of the hand press out the dough to cover the surface of a shallow oiled pan (10 x 15 inches).
5. Chill for 30 min or if you wish keep in refrigerator until ready to use.
6. When ready to bake, spread apricot or chocolate chips over dough.
7. Beat the egg whites and 1/3 cup of sugar until stiff.
8. Spread over the top of jam or chocolate bits.
9. Sprinkle chopped walnuts over meringue.
10. Bake in oven until meringue is firm. Bake at 350 degrees for 20 or 30 minutes.
11. Cut in squares when cool.
notes: Recipe card says 'over' at bottom of first page indicating instructions continue on back. Can use either apricot preserves or semi-sweet chocolate chips for filling.
tags: cookies, bars, meringue, dessert, filled cookies

name: Harvey Wallbanger Cake
summary: A boozy cake made with Galliano liqueur and vodka, inspired by the Harvey Wallbanger cocktail
ingredient_blocks:
1  orange or lemon cake mix (1 package)
1  vanilla instant pudding (1 package)
4  eggs
1/3 cup orange juice
1/3 cup oil
1/4 cup Galliano
2 tbsp vodka
1 cup powdered sugar (for glaze)
1 tsp orange juice (for glaze)
1 tsp Galliano (for glaze)
1 tsp vodka (for glaze)
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine cake mix, pudding mix, eggs, orange juice, oil, Galliano, and vodka.
2. Beat on low speed for 1/2 minute.
3. Beat on medium speed for 5 minutes.
4. Bake at 350°F for 45 minutes.
5. For glaze: Mix powdered sugar, orange juice, Galliano, and vodka together.
6. Glaze the cake while warm.
notes: This is a vintage recipe card. The word 'Galiano' appears to be written as 'Galiano' throughout - this refers to Galliano liqueur.
tags: cake, boozy dessert, vintage, 1970s, cocktail-inspired

name: Cheese Cake
summary: Sue Jalsano's cheesecake recipe
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 pint sour cream
7  eggs
4 oz cream cheese (8 oz packages (4 packages))
4 tsp vanilla extract
2 cup sugar
instruction_blocks:
1. Put filling ingredients in large bowl and beat for 15-20 minutes.
2. Butter bottom and sides of spring pan and line with a layer of crushed graham crackers to form crust.
3. Pour in filling mixture.
4. Bake in 375 degree oven until top turns brown (about 55 minutes).
notes: Recipe attributed to Sue Jalsano. The cream cheese measurement appears to be 4 8-oz packages based on context. Extended beating time of 15-20 minutes is specified for the filling.
tags: cheesecake, dessert, baked

name: Lemon Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1 pkg yellow cake mix
3/4 cup Mazola oil
4  eggs
1 pkg lemon Jello (dissolved in 1 cup of boiling water, let cool)
3  lemon rinds (for glaze)
juice of lemons (for glaze)
3/4 cup sugar (for glaze)
instruction_blocks:
1. Dissolve lemon Jello in 1 cup of boiling water and let cool.
2. Sift cake mix in large bowl.
3. Add oil and eggs, then Jello.
4. Grease and flour pan.
5. Bake 1 hour at 350 degrees.
6. For glaze: Mix together 3 lemon rinds, juice of lemons, and 3/4 cup sugar.
7. Pour glaze over warm cake.
notes: This is a classic lemon Jello cake recipe. The glaze is poured over the cake while still warm.
tags: cake, lemon, dessert, vintage

name: Bottom Nut Cake with Mace
summary: Jean Spatabone's nut cake with walnuts on the bottom
ingredient_blocks:
2  sticks butter or margarine
2 cup sugar (take out 2 tablespoons and set aside)
4  eggs
3 cup self-rising flour
1 cup milk (skim or regular)
1 pinch mace
1 cup chopped walnuts
instruction_blocks:
1. Grease a tube/bundt pan. Add chopped nuts and the reserved sugar together and sprinkle around bottom of pan.
2. Mix ingredients in order of recipe: butter and sugar - mix, then add 1 egg at a time.
3. Then add flour and milk - alternate milk and flour etc. until finished.
4. Add pinch of mace - mix and pour over nuts and sugar. Bake.
5. Bake at 350°F for 1 hour + 15 min or 30 min. Check - each oven is different.
notes: Oven temperature 350°F. Baking time is approximately 1 hour and 15-30 minutes, but check as each oven is different.
tags: cake, nut cake, bundt cake, walnuts, vintage recipe

name: Noel Cookie Gems
summary: Thumbprint cookies filled with jam, jellies or preserves
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup shortening
3/4 cup sugar
1  egg
1 tsp vanilla
2 2/3 cup unsifted flour
1 1/4 cup finely chopped nuts
Bama jams, jellies or preserves (for filling)
1/2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp baking powder
1/4 tsp baking soda
1/2 cup Borden Sour Cream
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat oven to 400°.
2. In large mixer bowl, combine first 4 items (shortening, sugar, egg, vanilla). Mix well.
3. Sift together dry ingredients and add to shortening mixture alternately with sour cream; mix well.
4. Shape into 1 1/4 inch balls; roll in nuts.
5. Place 1 inch apart on greased baking sheets.
6. Press thumb in center of each ball; fill with jam.
7. Bake 10 to 12 minutes or until lightly browned.
notes: Tip: Noel Cookie Gems can be made ahead and frozen. Recipe dated 1/79 from Bakes. Some text obscured by blue ink stains on original recipe card.
tags: Christmas, cookies, thumbprint cookies, holiday, nuts, jam-filled

name: Banana Split Cake
summary: A no-bake layered dessert inspired by banana splits
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup graham cracker crumbs
5 tbsp melted margarine
2 cup powdered sugar
1  stick margarine
2  eggs
1 tsp vanilla
3  sliced bananas (lengthwise)
1  20 oz can crushed pineapple
1  9 oz container Cool Whip
crushed walnuts (for topping)
maraschino cherries (for topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix graham cracker crumbs with 5 tbsp melted margarine.
2. Press into 13x9 glass dish and refrigerate.
3. Beat 2 cups powdered sugar, 1 stick margarine, 2 eggs, and 1 tsp vanilla at high speed for 5 minutes.
4. Place on top of crumb mixture.
5. Layer 3 sliced bananas (lengthwise) on top of filling.
6. Spread 1 (20 oz) can crushed pineapple over bananas.
7. Spread 1 (9 oz) container Cool Whip over pineapple.
8. Top with crushed walnuts and maraschino cherries.
9. Refrigerate overnight.
notes: Recipe from the kitchen of Debbie. Delicious! Some handwriting was difficult to read but context helped clarify ingredients.
tags: no-bake, dessert, banana, layered dessert, refrigerator cake

name: Cheese Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup corn flakes crumbs (for crust)
1/2 stick margarine (melted, for crust)
2 tbsp sugar (for crust)
1 tsp cinnamon (for crust)
5  eggs (separated)
1 lb ricotta
1 lb cream cheese
1 pint sour cream
1/2 cup sugar
1/3 cup corn starch
2 tbsp lemon juice
1 tsp vanilla
1/2 stick margarine or butter (optional, can be omitted)
instruction_blocks:
1. 1st step - Crust: Mix corn flakes crumbs, melted margarine, sugar, and cinnamon. Line spring pan (line just bottom of pan).
2. 2nd step: Separate 5 eggs and beat whites until stand in peaks. Set aside.
3. 3rd step: Combine ricotta, cream cheese, sour cream, and egg yolks. Add sugar and corn starch, then blend all ingredients.
4. Then add 2 tablespoons lemon juice, 1 teaspoon vanilla. Add 1/2 stick margarine or butter (this can be omitted).
5. Then beat mixture well. Pour into spring pan.
6. Cook at 325° for 1 hour.
7. Then leave in oven 2 hours with door open.
notes: Note found: Better results to cook 10 min more then brown. Some handwriting was difficult to read but best interpretations were made.
tags: cheesecake, dessert, baked

name: Plop Cake
summary: A simple dump-style cake with cherry pie filling and pineapple
ingredient_blocks:
1 can cherry pie filling
1 can chunky pineapple (with juice)
1 box Duncan Hines white cake mix
1 stick margarine (cut in slices)
1/4 cup chopped nuts
instruction_blocks:
1. Grease a 9x13 pan.
2. Add 1 can cherry pie filling to the pan.
3. Add 1 can chunky pineapple with juice on top.
4. Sprinkle Duncan Hines white cake mix over the fruit.
5. Cut 1 stick of margarine in slices and put on top of cake mix.
6. Sprinkle 1/4 cup chopped nuts over the top.
7. Bake at 350 degrees for 40 to 50 minutes.
notes: This is a dump cake style recipe - no mixing required, just layer ingredients
tags: dump cake, dessert, easy, cherry, pineapple

name: Eclair Cake
summary: Make ahead dessert - prepare 1 day in advance
ingredient_blocks:
2  small instant French Vanilla pudding
1 box graham crackers
1  large 9 oz Cool Whip
3 cup milk (for pudding)
3 sq Baker's unsweetened chocolate
2 tbsp light Karo syrup
1 tsp vanilla
3-5 tbsp milk (for frosting)
1 1/2 cup powdered sugar
instruction_blocks:
1. Butter a 13x9 pan on bottom and sides.
2. Line pan with crackers.
3. Make pudding with 3 cups of milk (2 packages).
4. Mix in Cool Whip.
5. Pour 1/2 mixture over crackers.
6. Add 1 more layer of crackers.
7. Pour in other half of mixture.
8. Add 1 more layer of crackers.
9. Mix and melt 3 squares Baker's unsweetened chocolate, 2 Tbls light Karo syrup, 1 tsp vanilla, and 3-5 tbsp milk.
10. Cool awhile and then mix in 1 1/2 cups powdered sugar.
11. Pour over top layer of crackers.
12. Refrigerate.
notes: Serves 12-14. Make ahead 1 day before serving. Some handwriting was difficult to read, particularly measurements for the chocolate frosting.
tags: dessert, no-bake, make ahead, eclair, chocolate

name: Janet's Persellas Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup brown sugar (cream well with granulated sugar and butter)
1 cup granulated sugar (cream well with brown sugar and butter)
2 sticks butter (cream well with sugars)
4  eggs
1 tsp baking soda
1 tsp cinnamon
4 1/2 cups flour (or 5 cups)
1 cup nuts
1 cup chocolate chips
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream together brown sugar, granulated sugar, and butter well.
2. Add eggs, baking soda, cinnamon, and flour. Mix to combine.
3. Fold in nuts and chocolate chips.
4. Grease cookie sheet.
5. Bake for 10 minutes at 350°F.
notes: Recipe title may read 'Perellas' or 'Persellas' - handwriting is slightly unclear. Flour amount is listed as 4 1/2 or 5 cups.
tags: cookies, chocolate chip, dessert, family recipe

name: Anise Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup flour
1 cup sugar
3  eggs
1 1/2 tsp anisette
1/4 tsp salt
3 1/2 tsp baking powder
1 jigger oil
instruction_blocks:
1. Divide dough into three loaves.
2. Place on greased cookie sheet.
3. Slice while still hot.
4. Bake at 350° for 25 minutes.
5. For icing: Use white of egg and confectioners sugar as much as you need.
6. Ice cookies before they are cut.
notes: The icing is made with egg white and confectioners sugar. Ice the cookies before cutting them. Some handwriting was difficult to read - 'jigger' is a small measure typically 1-1.5 oz.
tags: Italian, cookies, anise, biscotti-style

name: Coffee Pecan Torte
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 lb ground pecans
1 cup sugar (divided)
1 tbsp instant coffee
3 tbsp bread crumbs
7  eggs (separated)
1  whole egg
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oven to 350°.
2. Separate 7 eggs. Add 1 whole egg to yolks and beat with 1/2 cup of the sugar and beat yolks until they are pale yellow and creamy.
3. Add into instant coffee and bread crumbs.
4. Beat egg whites with remaining 1/2 cup of sugar until they are very stiff.
5. Take a big scoop of egg whites and put it into the egg yolk mixture.
6. Put into pans that have been well greased with butter (9" pan).
7. Bake 30 to 40 min. Cakes will be very springy when done.
8. Let cool and remove from pans.
notes: Recipe uses 8 egg yolks total (7 separated eggs plus 1 whole egg) and 7 egg whites. Some handwriting was difficult to read but best interpretation provided.
tags: torte, dessert, coffee, pecan, cake

name: 1 lb Cake
summary: Traditional pound cake recipe
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb butter (could use oleo/margarine)
5  eggs
3 cup flour
1 tsp vanilla
1 tsp lemon juice
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream butter and sugar, add eggs
2. Cream about 10 minutes, add flour and flavoring
3. Put in oven at 350° for about 1 hour
notes: Recipe appears to be missing sugar from ingredients list but mentions it in instructions - traditional pound cakes use 1 lb sugar. Handwriting is somewhat difficult to read in places.
tags: pound cake, classic, dessert, cake

name: Chocolate-Chocolate Chip Cookies
summary: From the kitchen of Lue
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup butter or margarine (softened)
1 cup sugar
1/2 cup brown sugar
2 tbsp half and half
1 tsp vanilla extract
1 3/4 cup all-purpose flour
1/3 cup cocoa
1/4 tsp baking soda
1 cup chopped pecans
1  6 oz. package semisweet chocolate morsels
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream butter, gradually add sugar, beating until light and fluffy.
2. Add half-and-half and vanilla.
3. Combine flour, cocoa and soda; add to creamed mixture beating until blended.
4. Stir in chopped pecans and chocolate morsels.
5. Drop dough by teaspoonfuls 1 1/2 inches apart on ungreased cookie sheets.
6. Bake at 350° for 10 to 12 minutes (do not overbake).
7. Cool slightly on cookie sheet before removing to rack.
notes: Serves 6 dozen cookies. Recipe card copyright 1980 Conimar, Inc.
tags: cookies, chocolate, dessert, drop cookies

name: Rauch's Dessert
summary: A no-bake layered dessert with cream cheese, cool whip, graham crackers, and peach pie filling
ingredient_blocks:
1  8 oz cream cheese (room temperature)
1 cup sugar
1 tsp vanilla
13 oz cool whip
graham crackers (whole crackers for lining pan)
2  cans peach pie filling (Thank You brand)
instruction_blocks:
1. Use spoon to mix, not mixer.
2. Beat cheese, sugar, vanilla & cool whip.
3. Line pan with whole graham crackers.
4. Spread half of mixture on them.
5. Then another layer of graham crackers, then one can of peach pie filling, then rest of mixture (cool whip).
6. Then the 2nd can of pie filling.
7. You can arrange fruit any way you want on top.
8. Put in refrigerator & let stand about 2 hours.
notes: Recipe attributed to 'Brach'. The recipe emphasizes using a spoon to mix rather than an electric mixer.
tags: no-bake, dessert, layered dessert, cream cheese, peach

name: Italian Holiday Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
1  stick butter
1/4 cup sugar
1  egg yolk
1/4 tsp vanilla
1 1/4 cup sifted flour
1/2 tsp salt
1  egg white (slightly beaten)
1 cup flaked coconut
preserves (for filling)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a mixing bowl cream butter, gradually add sugar and beat until light fluffy.
2. Add egg yolk and vanilla.
3. Sift together flour and salt, add gradually to cream mixture.
4. Chill dough for easy handling.
5. Shape into balls about 1 inch in diameter.
6. Dip ball in egg white and roll in coconuts.
7. Bake at 300 degree oven.
8. Fill center with preserves.
notes: Some handwriting was difficult to read. The word 'preserves' appears to be used both as an ingredient and for filling the cookies after baking.
tags: Italian, holiday, cookies, coconut

name: Italian Cookies
summary: Traditional Italian drop cookies with anisette flavoring and icing
ingredient_blocks:
5 cup flour
6  eggs
1 cup oil
1 cup orange juice
1 cup sugar
2 tsp vanilla
4 tsp baking powder
1 tbsp anisette
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Mix all ingredients well.
3. Drop teaspoons of mixture on greased cookie sheet, an inch apart.
4. Bake until light brown.
5. Cool before icing.
6. For icing: Blend confectioners sugar, milk and anisette (add food coloring to tint icing) until desired thickness.
7. Dip cookies in icing and add sprinkles on top.
notes: Some handwriting is faded but legible. The icing does not specify exact amounts - blend to desired thickness.
tags: Italian, cookies, anisette, drop cookies, iced cookies

name: Anise Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
6  eggs (beat)
1 1/2 cup sugar
1 cup Wesson oil
3 tsp baking powder
3 cup flour
1 oz anisette
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat eggs
2. Spoon 2 rolls on cookie sheet
3. Bake at 375 to 400 for 12 minutes
4. Slice on an angle, turn over on side to brown
notes: Makes 5 rolls. Recipe appears to be for Italian anise biscotti-style cookies that are sliced and toasted.
tags: Italian, cookies, biscotti, anise

name: Hidden Cherries
summary: Cherry-filled cookies coated in walnuts and egg whites
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup butter
1/4 cup sugar
1 cup flour
1  egg yolk
1/2 tsp vanilla
2  drops lemon (lemon extract or juice)
24  cherries
walnuts (chopped fine)
egg whites (beaten)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix all ingredients in bowl.
2. Roll cookies flat, then put cherries in.
3. Dip in chopped fine walnuts and beaten egg whites.
4. Place on ungreased cookie sheet.
5. Bake at 350°F for 20-25 minutes.
notes: Recipe makes 24 cookies (one cherry per cookie). The dough is wrapped around each cherry before coating.
tags: cookies, cherries, walnuts, holiday, vintage recipe

name: Oatmeal Carmelitas
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup flour
2 cup quick cooking oats
1 1/2 cup dark brown sugar
3 stick butter or margarine (melted)
1/2 tsp salt
1/2 tsp baking soda
1 package chocolate morsels (large)
1 cup chopped walnuts (generous)
12 oz caramel sauce (one jar)
4 tbsp flour (mixed with caramel sauce)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix ingredients with wooden spoon until well blended.
2. Spread a large half in 9x13 pan.
3. Bake at 350° for 15-20 minutes.
4. Sprinkle one large package of chocolate morsels, a generous cup of chopped walnuts.
5. Drizzle one 12 oz jar of caramel sauce mixed with 4 tablespoons of flour.
6. Crumble the rest of the oatmeal mixture over top.
7. Bake another 15 or 20 minutes.
8. Cool in fridge until they can be cut in squares.
notes: From the kitchen of Mary Jane Thun. Recipe card has decorative strawberry illustration.
tags: dessert, bars, oatmeal, caramel, chocolate, cookies

name: Anise Cookies
summary: Mrs. Ferrante's Italian anise cookies, twice-baked biscotti style
ingredient_blocks:
6 cup flour
6 tsp baking powder
6  eggs
2 cup sugar
2  sticks margarine or butter
2 tsp anisette flavoring
instruction_blocks:
1. First mix dry ingredients together.
2. Add the rest and mix well.
3. Roll dough in long rolls and bake.
4. Cut them in slices about 1/2" thick and put them back in the oven to brown.
5. Bake at 375 degrees.
notes: Recipe from Mrs. Ferrante. This is a biscotti-style cookie that is baked twice - first as logs, then sliced and toasted.
tags: Italian, cookies, anise, biscotti, twice-baked

name: Sour Cream Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 lb soft salted butter
2 cup sugar
4  eggs (beaten lightly)
1 pint sour cream
2 tsp vanilla
4 cup flour (sifted)
2 tsp baking powder
2 tsp baking soda
1/2 tsp salt
1 cup walnuts (chopped)
1/2 cup sugar (for topping, mixed with cinnamon)
1 tsp cinnamon (for topping, mixed with sugar)
instruction_blocks:
1. In bowl cream butter and sugar.
2. Add eggs, sour cream and vanilla, beating until well blended.
3. Sift flour, baking powder, salt, and baking soda together.
4. Add gradually to cream mixture, beating after each addition.
5. Have the walnuts, cinnamon, and sugar mixed together.
6. Grease a tube pan.
7. Turn half of the batter into the pan.
8. Sprinkle over the top, half of the nut and sugar mixture.
9. Add remaining batter.
10. Cover the top with the rest of nut and sugar mixture.
11. Bake in moderate oven (375) for 1 hour and 45 minutes.
notes: The cinnamon-sugar and walnut topping is layered in the middle and on top of the cake, creating a streusel-like layer.
tags: cake, sour cream, coffee cake, tube pan, walnut, cinnamon

name: Frozen Cherry Loaf
summary: A frozen fruit and cream cheese dessert
ingredient_blocks:
1 can dark sweet pitted cherries (16 oz, drained)
1 can crushed pineapple (large can, drained)
1 can mandarin oranges (11 oz, drained)
1 pkg cream cheese (8 oz, softened)
1 cup sour cream
1/4 cup sugar
1/4 tsp salt
1 cup miniature marshmallows
1/2 cup chopped nuts
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream together cream cheese, sour cream, sugar and salt.
2. Add drained fruit, marshmallows and nuts.
3. Freeze in loaf pan, mold pan, or 12 cupcake pan.
notes: All canned fruits should be drained before adding to mixture.
tags: frozen dessert, no-bake, vintage, fruit dessert

name: Popovers
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup sifted flour
1/4 tsp salt
1 cup milk
2  eggs (well beaten)
1 tsp melted butter or other shortening
instruction_blocks:
1. Sift flour and salt together and gradually add milk, mixing well.
2. Add eggs and melted butter and beat hard for 2 minutes. Use rotary egg beater.
3. Pour into greased custard cups or metal popover pans that have been well greased and thoroughly heated.
4. Bake in very hot oven at 450 degrees for about 20 minutes (until popovers puff).
5. Then reduce heat to moderate 350 degrees and brown.
notes: Use rotary egg beater for mixing. Pans should be well greased and thoroughly heated before adding batter.
tags: bread, popovers, quick bread, vintage

name: Oatmeal Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
1 tub soft margarine (8 oz)
2 cup brown sugar
2  eggs
1 tsp vanilla
1 1/2 cup flour
1 tsp baking soda
1 tsp cinnamon
1/2 tsp salt
3 cup old fashioned or quick cooking oats
1 cup raisins or chopped nuts
instruction_blocks:
1. In large mixing bowl, beat on low speed until well blended: margarine, sugar, eggs, vanilla, flour, baking soda, cinnamon, salt, oats and raisins or nuts, adding in order given.
2. Drop by heaping teaspoons 2" apart onto baking sheet.
3. Bake at 350° for 12-15 minutes or until lightly browned.
4. Remove immediately to wire rack. Cool completely.
notes: Makes about 3 1/2 dozen. Recipe from the kitchen of Elaine.
tags: cookies, oatmeal, dessert, baking

name: Nut Cups
summary: Small pastry cups with a walnut filling, dusted with powdered sugar
ingredient_blocks:
1 oz cream cheese (3 oz pkg)
1/4 lb butter (for shells)
1 cup flour (for shells)
3/4 cup chopped walnuts (for filling)
1  egg (for filling)
1 tsp vanilla (for filling)
3/4 cup light brown sugar (do not pack down)
1 tbsp butter (for filling)
powdered sugar (for sprinkling)
instruction_blocks:
1. For shells: Mix together cream cheese, 1/4 lb butter, and flour.
2. Roll mixture into small balls and press into ungreased cupcake tins. Chill.
3. For filling: Mix together chopped walnuts, egg, vanilla, light brown sugar (do not pack down), and 1 tbsp butter.
4. Fill the chilled shells with the filling.
5. Bake at 325°F for 25-30 minutes.
6. When cool, sprinkle with powdered sugar.
notes: The cream cheese package is noted as 3 oz. Recipe card indicates 'over' suggesting content continues on back.
tags: dessert, pastry, nuts, holiday, cookies

name: Sugar Cookies
summary: From the kitchen of Luci
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup butter flavored Crisco
1 cup sugar
1 1/2 tsp vanilla
1  egg
2 cup all-purpose flour (unsifted)
1 tsp baking soda
1/3 tsp cream of tartar
1/2 tsp salt
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream butter flavor Crisco and sugar in large bowl at med speed.
2. Beat in eggs and vanilla.
3. Combine flour with baking powder, cream of tartar and salt. Blend into cream mixture.
4. Cover and chill at least 3 hrs.
5. Preheat oven to 375°.
6. Roll out dough on floured surface to 1/5 in thickness.
7. Cut with cookie cutter.
8. Place on ungreased sheets.
9. Bake at 375° for 6 to 7 min.
10. Cool on baking sheets for about 1 min.
notes: Recipe card has decorative vintage stove illustration. Some handwriting unclear - 'baking powder' may actually be 'baking soda' based on context. Thickness measurement appears to be 1/5 inch but may be 1/8 inch.
tags: cookies, sugar cookies, dessert, baking, rolled cookies

name: Walnut Squares
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup flour (for crust)
1/2 cup margarine (for crust)
1/2 cup brown sugar (for crust)
1 1/2 cup brown sugar (for topping)
2  eggs (beaten)
1/2 cup coconut
1/2 cup walnuts (to 1 cup)
2 tbsp flour (heaping)
1/2 tsp baking powder
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Mix together 1 cup flour, 1/2 cup margarine, and 1/2 cup brown sugar. Place in 8x8 pan for crust.
3. Mix together 1 1/2 cups brown sugar, 2 beaten eggs, 1/2 cup coconut, 1/2 to 1 cup walnuts, 2 heaping tbsp flour, and 1/2 tsp baking powder.
4. Place topping mixture on top of crust.
5. Bake at 350°F till brown.
notes: Recipe does not specify exact baking time - bake until brown.
tags: dessert, bars, walnut, coconut, vintage

name: Ice Cream Dessert
ingredient_blocks:
1 pkg Dream Whip (follow inst. on pkg)
1/2 gallon vanilla ice cream (melt ice cream)
1 pt lime sherbet
2/3 cup creme de menthe
instruction_blocks:
1. Prepare Dream Whip according to package instructions.
2. Melt the vanilla ice cream.
3. Combine all ingredients plus the creme de menthe.
4. Freeze 24 to 48 hours.
notes: Total time is based on minimum freeze time of 24 hours (1440 minutes) to maximum of 48 hours (2880 minutes).
tags: frozen dessert, ice cream, mint, no-bake

name: Lime Jello - Pineapple Salad
summary: A light and creamy molded gelatin salad with cottage cheese and pineapple
ingredient_blocks:
1  3oz box Lime Jello
1 cup boiling water
1 cup cold water
1/2  container Light & Lively cottage cheese (16 oz size container)
1/2  can crushed pineapple (16 oz size, DRAINED)
2 tbsp mayonnaise
instruction_blocks:
1. Add 1 cup boiling water to lime Jello and mix
2. Add 1 cup cold water
3. Put in freezer to thicken slightly
4. Then whip the Jello
5. Add 1/2 container Light & Lively cottage cheese (16 oz size)
6. Add 1/2 can crushed pineapple, DRAINED (16 oz size)
7. Add 2 Tbs. mayonnaise
8. Fold together
9. Refrigerate at least 4 hrs. or overnight
notes: Recipe attributed to 'Peggy' as noted in top right corner. Serves 5 to 6.
tags: salad, gelatin, Jello, side dish, retro, potluck, no-bake

name: Lime Pineapple Cheese Mold
summary: A gelatin-based salad mold with lime Jello, cottage cheese, pineapple, and mayonnaise
ingredient_blocks:
1  pkg (3 oz size) lime flavor Jello
1 cup boiling water
1 cup evaporated milk
1 cup cottage cheese
1/4 cup chopped celery
1/4 cup chopped nuts
1 can crushed pineapple (1 lb size, drained)
1/3 cup mayonnaise
instruction_blocks:
1. Dissolve gelatin in boiling water and cool.
2. Add milk, cheese, and drained pineapple (do not use fresh pineapple).
3. Stir until well blended.
4. Fold in mayonnaise, celery and nuts.
5. After mixing, chill until firm.
6. Turn into quart size mold.
7. Chill for 2 hours.
notes: Recipe attributed to Lucy Montavone. Do not use fresh pineapple - must use canned.
tags: gelatin salad, retro, Jello mold, side dish, vintage

name: Topping
summary: Coffee-flavored whipped cream topping for cake
ingredient_blocks:
1 pt whipping cream
1/2 cup very fine sugar
1 1/2 tbs instant coffee
1 tsp vanilla
1 1/2 tbs cocoa
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat cream, add the rest of ingredients and beat until stiff.
2. Can decorate with coffee candy beans or crushed pecans all around cake.
notes: This appears to be a topping recipe, likely for a cake. The 'IV' or 'II' in the corner may indicate this is page 4 or part of a series of recipe cards.
tags: topping, whipped cream, coffee, frosting, dessert

name: Layered Jello Salad
summary: A layered fruit jello dessert with sour cream middle layer
ingredient_blocks:
3  mixed fruit Jello (packages)
3 cup boiling water
2  frozen strawberries (10 oz packages)
1  crushed pineapple (15 oz can)
1 pint sour cream (or more)
instruction_blocks:
1. Put half this mixture in 9x13 dish.
2. Refrigerate till jelled.
3. Spoon 1 pint or more sour cream on top.
4. Spoon rest of Jello mixture on top.
5. Refrigerate till firm.
notes: Recipe attributed to 'Dolores' (written in top right corner). The Jello mixture would be made by dissolving the Jello packages in boiling water, then mixing in frozen strawberries and crushed pineapple before layering.
tags: jello salad, dessert, potluck, vintage, layered dessert

name: Pet Ritz Deep Dish Pie
summary: A frozen sherbet and pineapple dessert pie with whipped topping
ingredient_blocks:
1  Pet Ritz Deep Dish Pie shell (baked)
2 cup lime sherbet (softened)
1 can crushed pineapple (8 oz, drained)
1 container La Creme whipped topping (8 oz, thawed)
instruction_blocks:
1. In medium bowl, blend sherbet and pineapple.
2. Fold in whipped topping.
3. Spoon into baked pie shell.
4. Cover with plastic wrap.
5. Freeze several hours until firm.
6. Allow pie to defrost 15 to 20 minutes before slicing.
notes: Some handwriting was difficult to read but appears to be a frozen sherbet pie recipe using Pet Ritz brand pie crust and La Creme whipped topping.
tags: dessert, frozen pie, no-bake, summer, easy

name: Cheese Pie
ingredient_blocks:
3  eggs
1 cup sugar
1 tsp vanilla
1 lb cottage cheese (small curd)
1/2 lb cream cheese
1/2 box Zwieback (rolled into crumbs, for crust)
1/4 cup XXXX sugar (powdered sugar, for crust)
2 tbsp butter (soft, for crust)
1 cup sour cream (for topping)
2 tbsp sugar (for topping)
1 tsp vanilla (for topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat eggs, add sugar and vanilla, add cream cheese and cottage cheese.
2. For crust: Roll 1/2 box of Zwieback into crumbs. Mix with 1/4 cup powdered sugar and 2 tbsp soft butter.
3. Rub butter around pie dish, rub in crumbs.
4. Bake 25 minutes at 350° or until center is solid.
5. Take out of oven, let stand 5 minutes.
6. Mix together 1 cup sour cream, 2 tbsp sugar, and 1 tsp vanilla.
7. Cover top of cheese pie with sour cream mixture, bake 5 minutes more.
notes: This is a vintage cheesecake-style pie with a Zwieback crumb crust and sour cream topping. XXXX sugar refers to confectioner's/powdered sugar.
tags: cheesecake, pie, dessert, vintage, baked

name: Ricotta Pie
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup butter
1/4 tsp salt
1/4 cup sugar
2  eggs
1/2 tsp vanilla
2 1/2 cup sifted flour
1 tsp baking powder
instruction_blocks: 1. No instructions provided on this recipe card - this appears to be the crust/dough ingredients only
notes: This recipe card appears to contain only the crust/dough ingredients for a ricotta pie. The ricotta filling ingredients and baking instructions are not included on this card.
tags: pie, Italian, dessert, ricotta

name: 1-2-3 Pastry
summary: Simple oil-based pie crust recipe
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup all-purpose flour (plus 2 T for 2-crust pie)
1/4 tsp salt
1/3 cup oil
2 tbsp water
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oven 450° for 1 crust pie, 425° for 2 crust pie.
2. Mix flour, salt. Blend in oil, thoroughly with fork.
3. Sprinkle all of water over mixture; mix well.
4. Press dough firmly into ball with hands. If too dry, add 1-2 T more oil.
5. Flatten dough slightly, roll into 12" circle between 2 pieces of waxed paper. Wipe table with damp cloth to keep paper from slipping.
6. Peel off top wax paper. Place pastry in pan, paper side-up. Peel off paper.
7. Fit pastry loosely in pan. Flute.
8. Bake 12-15 min.
notes: For 2-crust 8-9" pie: 1 3/4 C flour, 1 tsp salt, 1/2 C oil, 3 T water. Recipe from the kitchen of Elaine.
tags: pastry, pie crust, basics

name: Jello-Ice Cream Pie
ingredient_blocks:
1  graham cracker crust (homemade, baked)
1 pkg Jello (any flavor)
1 pint ice cream
instruction_blocks:
1. Make graham cracker crust - bake and set aside.
2. Make 1 package Jello (any flavor) and put in refrigerator to chill.
3. Set out one pint ice cream to soften.
4. When Jello starts to thicken, beat until frothy.
5. Add ice cream - beat until well blended.
6. Pour into graham cracker crust.
7. Sprinkle some crumbs on top and refrigerate until firm.
notes: Recipe card marked 'JAPAN' - possibly where recipe was collected or where the family was stationed
tags: pie, dessert, no-bake, Jello, ice cream, vintage

name: Eggnog
ingredient_blocks:
2 doz eggs (separate yolk from white)
2 cup sugar
1/2 gallon milk
1 pint cream
1 1/2 cup rum
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat yolks until light, add sugar. Beat until thick and pale.
2. Add milk to yolks over.
3. Chill 3 hours, whip cream until thick and fold in.
notes: The handwriting at the end of page 1 appears to trail off mid-instruction. The phrase 'Add milk to yolks over' may be incomplete. This is a large batch recipe using 2 dozen eggs.
tags: holiday, beverage, Christmas, dessert drink

name: Meringue
summary: Mrs Wallace's meringue recipe
ingredient_blocks:
3  egg whites (beaten stiff with pinch of salt)
3/4 cup sugar (beaten in)
6 oz chocolate mints
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat 3 egg whites with pinch of salt until stiff
2. Beat in 3/4 cup sugar
3. Add 6 oz chocolate mints
4. Heat oven and bake at 275 degrees
5. Leave in oven about 1 hour or longer if needed
notes: Recipe attributed to Mrs Wallace. The chocolate mints appear to be folded into the meringue.
tags: dessert, meringue, chocolate, vintage recipe

name: Amazing Coconut Pie
summary: makes its own crust
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup milk
4  eggs
1 cup coconut
1/4 cup margarine
3/4 cup sugar
1/2 cup bisquick mix
1-1 1/2 tsp vanilla
instruction_blocks:
1. Grease pan before filling.
2. Combine milk, sugar, eggs, margarine, bisquick mix, and vanilla into blender container, cover and blend on slow speed for 3 minutes.
3. Pour into 9 inch pie plate, let stand for 5 minutes.
4. Sprinkle with coconut.
5. Bake at 350 degrees for 40 mins.
tags: pie, coconut, dessert, self-crusting, easy

name: Meringue
ingredient_blocks:
3  egg whites
1/4 tsp cream of tartar
6 tbsp sugar
1/2 tsp vanilla (or use 1 tsp lemon juice)
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat egg whites until firm
2. Add 1/4 tsp cream of tartar and beat until stiff, about 1 minute
3. Then beat in 6 tbsp sugar
4. Add 1/2 tsp vanilla or 1 tsp lemon juice
5. Bake at 325° for 20 minutes
tags: meringue, dessert, topping

name: English Muffin Loaves
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup all-purpose flour (about)
1 pkg dry yeast
1 tbsp sugar
3/4 tsp salt
1 1/4 cup warm water (105 to 115 degrees)
corn meal (for pan)
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine 1 cup flour, yeast, sugar and salt in a large mixing bowl.
2. Add water and beat on low speed of electric mixer 3 min.
3. Stir in enough remaining flour to make a very soft dough.
4. Shape dough into ball. Place in well greased bowl, turning to grease top.
5. Cover and let rise, free from drafts, about 1 hr.
6. Punch down, shape into an 8 x 4 pan.
7. Grease 9 x 5 x 3 in. loaf pan, sprinkle bottom and sides with cornmeal.
8. Cover and let rise in warm place about 30 min.
9. Bake at 400 degrees for 35 minutes.
notes: Recipe from the kitchen of Sue. Some handwriting difficult to read - 'Harmon' appears to be written near baking temperature.
tags: bread, yeast bread, English muffin, loaf

name: Hungarian Dumplings
ingredient_blocks:
3  eggs
2 cup flour
1 tsp salt
1/3 cup water
dash black pepper
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat eggs, add flour, salt, pepper and water. Mix well.
2. Add to boiling salted water.
3. Boil about 8-10 minutes.
4. Strain, rinse with cold water.
5. Heat in buttered skillet.
tags: Hungarian, dumplings, side dish, Eastern European

name: Avocado Dip
ingredient_blocks:
1  ripe avocado
1/2  small onion
1 clove garlic
1  green chile pepper
salt (to taste)
black pepper (to taste)
1/4 tsp Tabasco sauce (to taste)
instruction_blocks: 1. No instructions provided on recipe card. Presumably combine all ingredients and mash or blend to make dip.
notes: This appears to be a basic guacamole-style dip recipe. No preparation instructions were written on the original recipe card.

name: Cheese Ball
ingredient_blocks:
16 oz soft cream cheese
8 1/2 oz crushed pineapple (canned, drained)
2 tbsp minced onion
1/4 cup green pepper (chopped)
2 cup pecans (crushed)
1 tbsp seasoned salt
4  drops tobasco sauce
instruction_blocks:
1. Blend together (with blender) add 1/2 pecans and all other ingredients.
2. Chill.
3. Make ball and add nuts (roll in remaining pecans).
notes: Some handwriting was difficult to read. The recipe calls for rolling the finished cheese ball in the remaining crushed pecans.
tags: appetizer, cheese ball, party food, vintage recipe

name: Artichoke Squares
ingredient_blocks:
2  6-ounce jars marinated artichoke hearts
1  small onion (minced)
4  large eggs
1  8-ounce package shredded mozzarella cheese (2 cups)
1/4 cup dried bread crumbs
2 tbsp chopped parsley
1/4 tsp salt
1/4 tsp pepper
instruction_blocks: 1. Drain liquid from 1 jar of artichokes into 1 qt. saucepan. Drain liquid from second jar and discard. Finely chop all artichokes.
notes: Recipe appears to be incomplete - only the first instruction step is visible on this page
tags: appetizer, artichoke, cheese

name: Artichoke Appetizer
summary: Baked artichoke and mozzarella appetizer squares
instruction_blocks:
1. Artichokes; set aside.
2. Grease 12" x 8" baking dish. In liquid in saucepan, over medium heat, cook minced onion until tender.
3. In medium bowl, with fork, beat eggs until blended. Stir in cooked onion, chopped artichokes, mozzarella cheese, bread crumbs, parsley, salt, and pepper. Spread mixture evenly in baking dish. If not baking right away, cover and refrigerate.
4. Preheat oven to 325°F. Bake mixture 30-35 minutes until set and lightly browned.
5. Cool 15 minutes in dish on wire rack. With knife, cut lengthwise into 4 strips then cut each strip crosswise into 6 pcs. Arrange appetizer on platter. Makes 24 hors d'oeuvres.
notes: Recipe appears to be missing the full ingredients list from page 1. Based on instructions, ingredients include: artichokes, onion, eggs, mozzarella cheese, bread crumbs, parsley, salt, and pepper. The liquid from artichokes is used for cooking the onion.
tags: appetizer, artichoke, cheese, party food, hors d'oeuvres

name: Cream Puff Filling
ingredient_blocks:
1  small can evaporated milk
1 tsp vanilla
1 pkg instant vanilla pudding
1 pt heavy cream
instruction_blocks: 1. Mix everything together in a bowl until thick.

name: Quick Peanut Blossoms
ingredient_blocks:
1  Pillsbury Refrigerated Peanut Butter or Sugar Cookies (20-oz. pkg)
Sugar (for rolling)
36  Milk chocolate candy kisses
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oven to 375°F.
2. Shape dough into 1-inch balls; roll in sugar.
3. Place 2 inches apart on ungreased cookie sheets.
4. Bake at 375°F for 10 to 12 minutes or until golden brown.
5. Immediately top each cookie with a candy kiss, pressing down firmly so cookie cracks around edge.
6. Remove from cookie sheets. Cool completely.
notes: Makes 3 dozen cookies. ©1993 The Pillsbury Company
tags: cookies, peanut butter, chocolate, quick, easy

name: Nut Cups
ingredient_blocks:
1 oz cream cheese (3 oz package)
1 cup flour
1/4 lb butter (for shells)
3/4 cup chopped walnuts
1 tsp vanilla
1 tbsp butter (for filling)
1  egg
3/4 cup light brown sugar
powdered sugar (for topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. For shells: Mix together cream cheese, flour, and 1/4 lb butter.
2. Roll mixture into small balls, press into ungreased cupcake tins.
3. Chill.
4. For filling: Mix together chopped walnuts, vanilla, 1 tbsp butter, egg, and light brown sugar.
5. Fill tins with the filling mixture.
6. Bake at 325°F for 25-30 minutes.
7. When cool, sprinkle with powdered sugar.
tags: dessert, cookies, nuts, cream cheese pastry, tassies

name: Taco Dip
ingredient_blocks:
1 bag tortilla chips (for serving)
1 8oz pkg cream cheese (softened)
1 can Hormel Chile
1  small onion (grated)
1 can black olives (chopped)
1 8oz pkg Velveeta Taco Cheddar Cheese (shredded)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix cream cheese, chile, onion, shredded taco cheese and chopped olives together.
2. Place in casserole dish and bake at 350° for 30 min.
3. When done, serve and dip with tortilla chips or rounds.
tags: dip, appetizer, Mexican, taco, party food

name: Sweet Pickle Chips
ingredient_blocks:
1 qt dill pickles (drain juice and discard)
1  stick cinnamon (break up)
12  whole cloves
1 3/4 cup sugar
instruction_blocks:
1. Drain juice from dill pickles and discard the juice.
2. Slice pickles 1/4 inch thick.
3. Break up 1 stick cinnamon and add to pickles.
4. Add 12 whole cloves and 1 3/4 cup sugar.
5. Do not stir - let set at room temperature 24 hours.
6. The next 24 hours stir 2 or 3 times.
7. Put in jar and refrigerate.
notes: Recipe attributed to M. Burdon. Total time is approximately 48 hours (24 hours resting without stirring, then 24 hours with occasional stirring).
tags: pickles, sweet pickles, condiment, refrigerator pickles

name: Chicken & Rice Casserole
ingredient_blocks:
4  chicken breasts
1 cup Carolina Long Grain Rice
1/2 cup butter
1 can cream of mushroom soup (undiluted)
1 can cream of celery soup (undiluted)
1 can chicken soup (undiluted)
salt (to taste)
pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix soups and rice in 9" baking pan.
2. Top with chicken and season with salt and pepper.
3. Dot butter on chicken.
4. Bake at 350° for exactly 2 hours.
tags: casserole, chicken, comfort food, one-dish meal

name: Cheese & Apple Spread
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup lite Miracle Whip
8 oz cream cheese (softened)
1/2 cup sharp cheddar cheese (cubed or shredded, I add a little more)
1/3 cup Red Delicious apple (peeled and cubed, I add a little more)
instruction_blocks: 1. Mix together and serve on Triscuits
notes: Recipe writer notes they add a little more cheddar cheese and apple than called for

name: Zesty Marinade for Meat/Poultry
summary: Zesty marinade for meat poultry
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup pineapple juice
1 cup soy sauce
1/2 cup vinegar
1/2 cup brown sugar (firmly packed)
1 tsp garlic powder
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine all ingredients and simmer and stir
2. Use as marinade for meat or poultry

name: Cheese Stollen
ingredient_blocks:
6 cup flour
1 3/4 cup sugar
5 tsp baking powder
4  eggs
1 lb cottage cheese (small curds)
1/2 cup butter or margarine
1/2 cup chopped nuts
1 cup white raisins (rinse in hot water)
1 cup mixed fruit
1 oz almond extract (bottle)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix dough thoroughly.
2. Bake at 335 degrees for 1 hour and 15 minutes (or 1 and 1/4 hours).
3. Makes 2 loaves.
notes: Recipe card indicates 1 hr and 15 min baking time. Some handwriting unclear but best interpretation provided.

name: Asparagus Quiche
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 lb fresh asparagus (cut in 1 inch pieces, or 10 oz pkg frozen cut asparagus)
2 oz cheddar swiss cheese
6  eggs (beaten)
1 cup milk
1/2 cup sour cream
1 tbsp instant minced onion
1/4 tsp white pepper
1  pie shell
instruction_blocks:
1. If fresh asparagus, cook about 3-5 minutes. If frozen, cook according to package.
2. Arrange asparagus and cheese in bottom of pie shell.
3. Beat together remaining ingredients until well blended.
4. Pour mixture over asparagus and cheese.
5. Bake at 375 degrees about 35 to 40 minutes.
notes: Some handwriting was difficult to read - cheese type appears to be 'cheddar swiss' but may be interpreted differently. The amount of cheese (2 oz) is my best interpretation.
tags: quiche, asparagus, brunch, vegetarian, eggs

name: Double Pie Crust
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 cup flour
1/2 tsp baking powder
1/2 tsp salt
2/3 cup shortening
1  egg (beaten)
2 1/2 tbsp ice water
1 tsp vinegar
instruction_blocks:
1. Blend dry ingredients with shortening in mixer.
2. Add egg and liquids.
3. Form into 2 flattened balls.
4. Wrap and chill 15 to 20 minutes.
5. Roll out each ball on a floured board and fit into an 8 inch pie pan.
notes: Recipe from the kitchen of Luci. Makes enough for double crust (2 pie crusts).
tags: pie crust, pastry, baking, basics

name: Foolproof Pie Crust
summary: From the kitchen of Luci
ingredient_blocks:
4 cup flour
1 tbsp sugar
2 tsp salt
1 3/4 cup shortening (must be shortening)
1/2 cup water
1 tbsp vinegar
1  egg
instruction_blocks:
1. Sift flour, sugar & salt with fork. Cut in shortening until crumbly.
2. In small bowl beat together water, vinegar and egg.
3. Add to flour mix and stir until moist.
4. Divide dough into 5 parts, shape each into a patty.
5. Wrap in plastic, and chill at least 1/2 hour before using.
notes: Makes 5 crusts
tags: pie crust, pastry, baking, make-ahead

name: Quick Key Lime Pie
summary: From the kitchen of Jean Spatafore
ingredient_blocks:
1 can sweetened condensed milk
8 oz sour cream
1 cup Cool Whip
1/2 cup key lime juice
1  graham cracker crust
instruction_blocks:
1. Blend all ingredients in a bowl, beat till smooth.
2. Pour into pie shell.
3. Refrigerate at least 4 hours.
4. Add 3-4 drops green food coloring if desired.
notes: No-bake key lime pie recipe. Green food coloring is optional for color.
tags: pie, no-bake, key lime, dessert, quick

name: Parmesan Pastry Twists
ingredient_blocks:
1  frozen patty shells (1-10 oz pkg (6))
1  egg white (slightly beaten)
1/4 cup Parmesan cheese (grated)
2 tsp chives (snipped)
instruction_blocks:
1. On slightly floured surface, roll dills to flatten. Place pieces over, lapping slightly, to form a rectangle.
2. Press all edges together to seal.
3. Roll pastry to 12 x 8" rectangle - 1/8 to 1/4" thick.
4. Brush lightly with egg white.
5. Sprinkle cheese & chives over dough.
6. Cut rectangle in half lengthwise, cut each half crosswise into 12 sticks.
7. Twist ends of sticks several times.
8. Bake 425° oven - ungreased baking sheet, 8-10 min.
notes: Recipe from the kitchen of Claire. Makes 24 sticks (2 dozen).
tags: appetizer, pastry, cheese, party food

name: Turkey Creole
summary: Fresh turkey breast slices served over rice with a tomato-based Creole sauce
ingredient_blocks:
1  pkg (about 1 lb.) Louis Rich fresh turkey breast slices
3 tbsp butter (divided)
1  green pepper (chopped)
2 stalks celery (sliced)
1  medium onion (chopped)
2  tomatoes (chopped)
1 can tomato sauce (15 oz.)
1/4 tsp ground thyme
1/4 tsp black pepper
1/4 tsp paprika
1/4 tsp garlic powder
1/8 tsp cayenne pepper
4 servings rice (cooked)
instruction_blocks:
1. Melt 1 Tbsp. butter on med. in skillet.
2. When butter begins to brown add half of the turkey. Cook 3 minutes; turn. Cook 2 minutes more.
3. Place turkey on platter; cover to keep warm.
4. Repeat with 1 Tbsp. butter and remaining turkey.
5. Add 1 Tbsp. butter, green pepper, celery and onion to skillet. Cook 3 minutes, stirring frequently.
6. Stir in tomatoes, tomato sauce and seasonings. Bring to a boil; turn down heat.
7. Simmer 15 minutes.
8. Serve over turkey and rice.
notes: This is one of five Louis Rich Fresh Turkey Cuts recipes from a vintage promotional recipe card. Other recipes included are Fresh Turkey Wings (Southern Fried Turkey with Gravy), Fresh Turkey Drumsticks (New England Turkey Stew), Fresh Turkey Breast Half (Cranberry Turkey), and Turkey Breast Tenderloins (Turkey with Mushroom-Wine Sauce).
tags: turkey, Creole, Southern, dinner, main dish, skillet meal

name: Buffet Party Mold
summary: A gelatin-based party dessert with cream cheese, pineapple, and walnuts
ingredient_blocks:
1  #2 can crushed pineapple (drained)
1  3 oz pkg lime jello
2  3 oz pkgs cream cheese (softened)
1/2 pt heavy cream (whipped)
1 cup chopped walnuts
instruction_blocks:
1. Boil 1 1/2 cups of water and add to jello.
2. Chill partially or until slightly thick.
3. Then add softened cream cheese and heated undrained pineapple to jello.
4. Fold in heavy cream and nuts.
5. Chill till firm.
notes: The recipe calls for a #2 can of pineapple which is approximately 20 oz. The pineapple is listed as drained in the ingredients but added undrained in the instructions - this may need clarification.
tags: dessert, gelatin, party food, retro, no-bake

name: Tomato Preserves
ingredient_blocks:
2 qt tomatoes
3  lemons
4 cup sugar
instruction_blocks:
1. Scald the tomatoes and peel. Cut into small pieces and drain in colander. (Use juice for soup or sauce.) Measure 2 qts pulp.
2. Place in kettle with thinly sliced lemons.
3. Cook gently uncovered 45 min or until lemons rinds are tender.
4. Add sugar, continue cooking for 15 more minutes.
5. Pour into hot, sterilized jars and seal at once.
notes: Date on recipe card: 7-8-82. Some handwriting was difficult to read, particularly around cooking times and instructions.
tags: preserves, canning, tomato, vintage

name: Chicken Crunch
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup cooked chicken
2  hard cooked eggs (chopped)
1  4 oz jar sliced mushrooms
3/4 cup diced celery
1 tbsp chopped onion
3/4 cup mayonnaise
1 can chicken soup (do not add water)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix all ingredients together.
2. Bake at approximately 30 to 40 minutes.
3. Serve over La Choy Noodles.
notes: Recipe attributed to Betty Morask. Baking temperature not specified in original recipe.
tags: casserole, chicken, comfort food, retro

name: Chicken Croquettes
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp butter or margarine
2 tbsp all-purpose flour
1/2 cup milk
1/4 tsp salt
dash pepper
1 cup minced cooked chicken
1  egg (beaten)
1 tbsp milk
2 tbsp all-purpose flour
1/3 cup soft breadcrumbs
instruction_blocks:
1. Melt butter in a heavy saucepan over low heat; add 2 tablespoons flour and cook 1 minute, stirring constantly.
2. Gradually add milk; cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until sauce is thickened and bubbly.
3. Stir in salt and pepper. Remove from heat; stir in chicken.
4. Cover and chill about 1 hour.
5. Shape chicken mixture into 2 balls or rolls.
6. Combine egg and milk; mix well, and set aside.
7. Dredge each croquette in 2 tablespoons flour; then dip into egg and milk mixture, and coat each croquette with breadcrumbs.
8. Fry croquettes in deep hot oil (370°) for 3 to 5 minutes or until golden brown.
9. Drain on paper towels.
notes: Recipe attributed to Mrs. James L. Twilley, Macon, Georgia.
tags: chicken, fried, Southern, appetizer, comfort food

name: Chicken Pillows
ingredient_blocks:
1  whole chicken breast (halved, skinned, and boned)
1 clove garlic (halved)
2 slice prosciutto or boiled ham (1 ounce)
2 slice mozzarella cheese (1 ounce, thinly sliced)
2 tbsp seasoned breadcrumbs
2 tbsp medium dry sherry
2 tbsp clarified butter
salt (to taste)
freshly ground pepper (to taste)
1 tbsp chopped parsley
instruction_blocks:
1. Pound chicken breast pieces to a thickness of ¼ inch.
2. Rub each chicken breast piece with cut clove garlic.
3. Place prosciutto and cheese over chicken. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs.
4. Roll up, starting at broader end, and secure with wooden picks.
5. Place in a lightly greased shallow baking dish.
6. Combine sherry and butter and heat briefly; pour over chicken.
7. Season with salt and pepper.
8. Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes or until chicken is done.
9. Sprinkle with parsley.
tags: chicken, Italian, baked, stuffed

name: Fettuccine
summary: Creamy fettuccine with butter, eggs, and cheese from Luci's kitchen
ingredient_blocks:
1/2  stick of butter or margarine (melted)
1/3 cup chopped parsley
white pepper (to taste)
2  eggs
1/2 pint heavy cream
1/2 cup grated cheese (about)
1/2 to 1/3  Polly-O ricotta cheese (small container)
fettuccine pasta
instruction_blocks:
1. Cook fettuccine according to directions.
2. Whisk eggs and heavy cream together.
3. After pasta is cooked, pour melted butter over it.
4. Then add the egg and cream mixture over the pasta.
5. Add 1/2 to 1/3 of Polly-O ricotta cheese (small container).
6. Add grated cheese and mix everything well.
7. Add parsley last and toss.
notes: Recipe from Luci's kitchen. The ricotta amount refers to a small container - use 1/2 to 1/3 of it.
tags: pasta, Italian, creamy, fettuccine, quick meal

name: Pear Preserves
summary: Recipe from the kitchen of Luci
ingredient_blocks:
10  large pears (peeled, cored, chopped)
4 1/2 cup sugar
3 cup water
2  lemons (thinly sliced)
instruction_blocks:
1. Place pears in 3-quart Dutch oven and add water to cover. Bring to boil, reduce heat to medium - cover and cook pears for about 15 minutes - drain well.
2. Combine sugar and 3 cups of water in 6-quart Dutch oven - bring to boil and cook for about 10 minutes. Remove from heat and let cool 15 minutes.
3. Stir in pears and lemon slices and bring mixture to rapid boil - boil for about 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Pour mixture into a shallow pan (13x9x2), skim off foam with slotted spoon.
5. Let stand uncovered in cool place for 12 hours.
6. Spoon fruit into sterilized jars. Bring syrup to boil - pour over fruit leaving 1/4" space.
7. Cover with lids, screw and boil in water bath 10 min.
notes: Serves 7 half pints
tags: preserves, canning, fruit, pear, homemade

name: Beef Burgundy
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb sirloin (cut into 1" cubes)
2 tbsp butter or margarine
1 can Campbell's Golden Mushroom Soup
1/4 cup Burgundy or other dry red wine
12  small whole white onions (3/4 lb)
2 tbsp chopped parsley
1/8 tsp pepper
instruction_blocks:
1. In skillet, brown meat in butter.
2. Add remaining ingredients except noodles. Cover.
3. Cook over low heat for 1 hr. or until tender. Stir now and then.
4. Serve with cooked wide noodles.
notes: Serve with cooked wide noodles
tags: beef, comfort food, French, stovetop, main dish

name: Stuffed Peppers
ingredient_blocks:
green peppers
sausage (remove casing)
parsley
1  garlic (chopped)
1  small can black olives (chopped)
salt
bread soaked up
oil (for drizzling)
instruction_blocks:
1. Blend together all filling ingredients with a little oil
2. Wash and clean peppers - cut off tops of pepper
3. Stuff peppers with stuffing
4. Lay peppers in pan on side and drizzle with oil
5. Bake 375 to 400 oven till they roast on all sides
notes: Some handwriting is difficult to read. The filling ingredients (sausage, parsley, garlic, black olives, salt, bread) should be blended together with a little oil before stuffing the peppers.
tags: Italian, stuffed peppers, sausage, main dish

name: Oven Style Sliced Potatoes
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 lb all-purpose potatoes (4 medium potatoes)
1/2  butter or margarine (quarter-pound stick)
1  garlic clove (small, minced)
1/2 tsp dried crushed thyme
1 tbsp grated onion (pulp and juice)
1 tbsp lemon juice
2 tbsp grated Parmesan
instruction_blocks:
1. Peel potatoes and drop in cold water (or potatoes may be left unpeeled); slice each potato 1/4-inch thick, dropping each slice into a bowl of fresh cold water.
2. Line a 15-by-10-by-1-inch jelly roll pan with foil.
3. Place butter, garlic, thyme and onion on foil and heat in 450-degree oven until butter is melted; stir and leave oven on.
4. Drain potatoes and arrange on butter mixture, turning to coat both sides. Overlap slices as necessary.
5. Bake for 15 minutes; take from oven and drizzle with lemon juice and sprinkle with Parmesan.
6. Bake until edges are crisp — 10 to 15 minutes.
7. Serve at once.
tags: potatoes, side dish, oven-baked, vegetarian

name: Stuffed Vinegar Peppers
ingredient_blocks:
1 box raisins
1 pkg figs
2  orange rind
1 lb walnuts
1 loaf hard bread
2 bottles pignolas (pine nuts)
3 bottles grape juice
instruction_blocks:
1. Cut up figs, chop nuts & cut bread in small pieces.
2. Mix all together & add 2 bottles of grape juice.
3. Stuff pepper & cut crust to hold stuffing.
4. Fry pepper then place in white pot.
5. Add another bottle of juice or until covered and let come to a boil & shut off.
notes: 2 gals peppers average about 29 peppers.
tags: Italian, stuffed peppers, appetizer, pickled

name: Stuffed Green Pepper
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 lb Italian sausage (mild or hot, or more)
15 oz spaghetti sauce
16 oz cottage or ricotta cheese
4 oz shredded mozzarella cheese
1 tbsp parmesan cheese (or more)
1  egg
1/4 tsp oregano (if desired)
4-6  green peppers (cut in half)
instruction_blocks:
1. Brown sausage, drain grease, cool slightly.
2. Mix with egg, oregano, cheese.
3. Stuff peppers.
4. Place peppers in baking dish, pour sauce over peppers.
5. Then sprinkle parmesan cheese over peppers.
6. Bake at 350°F for 40-50 minutes.
7. Peppers will be tender crisp.
notes: I prefer ricotta cheese
tags: Italian, stuffed peppers, comfort food, main dish

name: Zucchini
summary: Fried zucchini sticks with honey mustard dipping sauce
ingredient_blocks:
zucchini (do not peel)
egg (for dipping)
flour (for coating)
Dijon mustard (for sauce)
honey (for sauce)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cut zucchini into sticks - do not peel
2. Dip in egg and then in flour
3. Fry until golden
4. Serve with sauce made from Dijon mustard and honey
notes: Sauce is a simple honey mustard dipping sauce. No specific quantities given in original recipe.
tags: appetizer, fried, zucchini, vegetable, side dish

name: Stuffed Cabbage Casserole
summary: A layered casserole with seasoned meat mixture, cabbage, and tomatoes
ingredient_blocks:
3 lb ground beef
1 lb ground pork
2  eggs (beaten)
3/4 cup rice
onion (grated)
3 slices bacon (fried slightly and cubed)
dill weed (to taste)
dill seed (to taste)
salt (to taste)
pepper (to taste)
2  head cabbage (chopped)
1 can crushed tomatoes (large can, drained)
1 can sauerkraut
1 can tomato soup
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix together the ground beef, ground pork, beaten eggs, rice, grated onion, fried bacon pieces, dill weed, dill seed, salt and pepper.
2. Mix together the crushed tomatoes, sauerkraut, and tomato soup.
3. Put meat mixture on bottom of pan, layer with some of the chopped cabbage.
4. Add some of the chopped cabbage on top and put some more tomato mixture.
5. Bake at 350 degrees for 2 hours.
notes: This appears to be a deconstructed stuffed cabbage recipe made as a casserole. The handwriting indicates 'lge' (large) can for crushed tomatoes.
tags: casserole, comfort food, Eastern European, cabbage, ground beef, pork

name: Stuffed Cabbage
ingredient_blocks:
dill seed
3  large heads of cabbage
1  large can crushed tomatoes
1  large can sauerkraut (rinse and drain)
1  can tomato soup
1  large onion (grated)
2 lb ground beef (mix remainder together)
1 lb pork
3  slices bacon (diced)
2  eggs (beaten)
3/4 cup rice
2 tsp salt
1/2 tsp Lowry's salt
1/4 tsp garlic
1/2 tsp pepper
1/4 tsp dill weed
1/2 tsp dill weed
instruction_blocks:
1. Bake at 350° for 3-4 hours.
2. Mix ground beef, pork, diced bacon, beaten eggs, rice, grated onion, and seasonings together.
3. Stuff cabbage leaves with meat mixture.
4. Layer with crushed tomatoes, sauerkraut (rinsed and drained), and tomato soup.
notes: Recipe card indicates 'over' suggesting continuation. Some handwriting unclear - dill weed amount appears twice (1/4 tsp and 1/2 tsp). The instruction details are sparse in original - steps inferred from ingredient notes.
tags: Eastern European, comfort food, casserole, cabbage rolls, ground beef

name: French Fried Onion Rings
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup sifted flour
2 tsp salt
1 1/2 tsp baking powder
1  egg yolk
2/3 cup milk
1 tbsp salad oil
1  egg white
shortening (for frying)
instruction_blocks:
1. Sift flour, salt and baking powder into bowl...set aside
2. Beat egg yolk slightly, stir in milk and salad oil. Add flour mixture, stirring until smooth.
3. Beat egg white, fold into batter
4. Heat skillet to 375°F with shortening.
notes: Recipe card shows stains indicating it was well-used. Instructions do not explicitly mention dipping onion rings in batter and frying, but this is implied.
tags: fried, appetizer, side dish, onion rings, vintage recipe

name: Chicken & Crab Valentine
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup cooked chicken (cut in bite size pieces)
2 cup crabmeat (2-7½ oz cans, S&W)
6 tbsp butter (for sauce)
6 tbsp flour
1½ cup chicken broth
1½ tsp salt
3/4 tsp paprika
1  clove garlic (crushed)
1/4 tsp nutmeg
5-6  drops red pepper
2½ cup sour cream
instruction_blocks:
1. Melt butter in double boiler, stir in flour and chicken broth.
2. Cook and stir until smooth and thick.
3. Add salt, garlic, paprika, nutmeg and liquid pepper.
4. Cover and cook 10 to 15 min, stirring occasionally.
5. Add sour cream - mix well and continue heating over just simmering water.
6. Add chicken & crab & toss lightly.
7. Serve in shells or with rice.
notes: Some handwriting difficult to read, particularly around sauce instructions. Recipe appears to be a creamy chicken and crab dish served in shells or over rice.
tags: seafood, chicken, creamy, entertaining, special occasion

name: Crabmeat Dip
ingredient_blocks:
1 can crabmeat
1 8-ounce package cream cheese
2 tsp chopped onion
1 tsp milk
1/2 tsp horseradish
1/4 tsp salt
1 dash pepper
instruction_blocks:
1. Have the cream cheese at room temperature before you cream it into a bowl.
2. Rinse crabmeat under running water and drain.
3. Add crabmeat to cream cheese and mix.
4. Add all other ingredients and mix well.
5. Bake at 375 degrees for 15 minutes, or until top is slightly browned in an oven-proof dish.
6. Use large fish shaped crackers for dipping.
notes: Recipe by Mary A. Kozlowski, Brewster, N.Y. Serves eight to 10.
tags: appetizer, dip, seafood, crab, baked

name: Strawberry Jello
summary: A creamy strawberry jello salad with fruit and nuts
ingredient_blocks:
2  small strawberry jello (packages)
2  small cream cheese (packages)
3 cup hot water
2  small pkg frozen strawberries
1  small can crushed pineapple
1 cup chopped walnuts or pecans
1  grated apple
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix jello and cream cheese well, add boiling water.
2. When room temp - add balance of ingredients (juice included).
notes: Recipe card indicates to include the juice from the canned/frozen fruits when adding to the jello mixture.
tags: jello salad, dessert, vintage, potluck

name: Star Salad
summary: A molded lime gelatin salad with crushed pineapple, walnuts, and sour cream
ingredient_blocks:
2  lime jello (packages, or 1 double)
1 cup boiling water
1  crushed pineapple with juice (#2 can)
1/2 cup sour cream
1/2 cup chopped walnuts
instruction_blocks:
1. Dissolve jello with water.
2. Add crushed pineapple and chill till slightly thick.
3. Then add chopped walnuts and sour cream.
4. Pour into 2 quart salad mold and chill until firm.
notes: A #2 can is approximately 20 oz or 2.5 cups. This is a vintage molded gelatin salad recipe.
tags: salad, gelatin, vintage, molded salad, retro

name: German Pancakes
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup flour
1 1/4 cup milk
3  eggs
1 pinch baking powder
1 pinch salt
instruction_blocks: 1. No instructions provided on recipe card
notes: This is a brief ingredient list without cooking instructions. German pancakes are typically made by mixing ingredients, then baking in a hot oven in a buttered pan until puffed and golden. The handwriting is somewhat difficult to read - the flour amount could possibly be 3 cups instead of 2 cups.
tags: German, pancakes, breakfast, Dutch baby

name: Sweetheart Salad
summary: A molded gelatin salad with cream cheese, pineapple, and maraschino cherries
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup crushed pineapple
1/2 cup sugar
1.5 tbsp plain Knox gelatin
1/4 cup cold water
6 oz cream cheese (room temp)
2 tbsp lemon juice
2 tbsp cherry juice (from jar of maraschino cherries)
1 cup heavy whipping cream
12  maraschino cherries (chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. Dissolve gelatin in cold water. Add pineapple to sugar.
2. Bring to boiling point and add gelatin. Stir until gelatin is dissolved.
3. Add lemon and cherry juice. Cool.
4. Mash cream cheese and add chopped cherries.
5. Combine with pineapple mixture, adding a small amount at a time.
6. Chill until slightly thickened.
7. Whip the cream til thickened (not stiff) and blend with rest of mixture.
8. Mold and chill. Spray mold with no stick spray.
notes: Recipe from the kitchen of Delores. Some handwriting was difficult to read, particularly measurements.
tags: gelatin salad, vintage, molded salad, dessert salad, retro

name: Jello Ice Cream Pie
ingredient_blocks:
1  9" graham cracker crust
1 pkg Jello (as on package)
1 pint vanilla ice cream (softened but not melted)
instruction_blocks:
1. Make 9" graham cracker crust
2. Make 1 pkg Jello as on package - chill till almost firm
3. Beat with mixer till double in size
4. Add 1 pint vanilla ice cream (softened but not melted)
5. Beat until well blended - pour into crust
6. Add graham cracker crumbs on top
7. Refrigerate til firm
notes: Recipe from the kitchen of Delores. Some handwriting difficult to read with certainty.
tags: dessert, pie, no-bake, Jello, ice cream

name: Lime Mold
summary: A creamy lime gelatin dessert with cream cheese, sour cream, cool whip, and crushed pineapple
ingredient_blocks:
2  3 oz. pkgs lime Jello
12 oz cream cheese
16 oz sour cream
8 oz Cool Whip
8 oz crushed pineapple (canned, with juice)
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat cream cheese, sour cream & Cool Whip together.
2. Then add pineapple with juice.
3. Next boil 3 cups of water and mix with lime Jello.
4. Let cool for a while and then add to the other ingredients.
5. Stir real well & refrigerate.
tags: dessert, gelatin, lime, retro, no-bake

name: Ice Tea
summary: Refreshing iced tea with lemonade
ingredient_blocks:
4 cup water
8 tbsp sugar (heaping)
4  tea bags
1  small can lemonade (frozen concentrate)
instruction_blocks:
1. Boil 4 cups of water with 8 heaping tablespoons of sugar
2. Add 4 tea bags and fix one small can lemonade
3. Add ice to the tea to cool
4. Mix with lemonade
notes: Handwriting somewhat difficult to read - 'fix' may mean 'prepare' the lemonade concentrate according to package directions before mixing
tags: beverage, summer, iced tea, lemonade

name: Barbecue Sauce
summary: Use for Barbecued Chicken or Spareribs. Sauce may be refrigerated and stored for later use.
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup chopped onion
2  cloves garlic (crushed)
3 tbsp oil
1 lb tomato puree (13-oz. can)
1/4 cup lemon juice
2 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
1/2 cup molasses
1 tsp dry mustard
1 tsp thyme
1 tsp oregano
3 tbsp brown sugar
1 tsp salt
instruction_blocks:
1. Saute 1 cup chopped onion and 2 crushed cloves garlic in 3 tbsp. oil in saucepan until tender.
2. Add 1 lb. 13-oz. can tomato puree, 1/4 cup lemon juice, 2 tbsp. Worcestershire sauce, 1/2 cup molasses, 1 tsp. dry mustard, 1 tsp. thyme, 1 tsp. oregano, 3 tbls. brown sugar and 1 tsp. salt.
3. Mix well.
4. Simmer uncovered 15 minutes.
notes: Makes 1 quart. Use for Barbecued Chicken or Spareribs. Sauce may be refrigerated and stored for later use.
tags: barbecue, sauce, condiment, grilling

name: Pesto
summary: From the kitchen of Tucci
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup fresh basil leaves (loosely packed)
5  sprigs of parsley
1 tsp coarse salt
3  medium garlic cloves (peeled and cut in half)
3 tbsp pine nuts (lightly toasted)
2 tbsp walnuts (chopped, lightly toasted)
1 tbsp romano cheese (grated)
1 tbsp parmesan cheese (grated)
1/3 cup olive oil (to 2/3 cups)
instruction_blocks:
1. Place everything but the olive oil in the processor bowl.
2. Process on/off all the ingredients form a very fine mace.
3. Slowly add the olive oil.
4. Add a tbsp unsalted butter in pasta, toss.
notes: Second page appears to be a separate bread/pizza dough recipe with yeast, warm water, olive oil, flour, and coarse salt. Bake at 500°F for 15-18 minutes.
tags: Italian, sauce, pasta, basil, vegetarian

name: Dijon Chicken
summary: Breaded chicken breasts with a Dijon mustard sauce
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup Blue Bonnet Margarine (melted)
1/3 cup Grey Poupon Dijon Mustard
2 tbsp dry sherry (optional)
1/8 tsp ground ginger
2  whole boneless chicken breasts (split (about 1 pound))
1/3 cup dry bread crumbs
instruction_blocks:
1. In small bowl, mix margarine, mustard, sherry and ginger.
2. Dip chicken in margarine mixture, then in bread crumbs to coat.
3. Place on ungreased baking sheet. Bake at 375°F for 35 to 40 minutes or until golden brown.
4. In small saucepan, over medium heat, heat remaining margarine mixture to a boil; simmer 2 to 3 minutes.
5. Serve as a sauce over chicken.
tags: chicken, baked, Dijon mustard, easy, weeknight dinner

name: Bean Salad
summary: Publix Presents A Recipe For Your Pleasure
ingredient_blocks:
3/4 cup sugar
1/3 cup oil
2/3 cup vinegar
1 tsp salt
1 cup string beans
1 cup waxed beans
1 cup kidney beans
1/4 cup chopped onion
2/3 cup chopped celery
pinch pepper (a dash)
instruction_blocks:
1. Bring sugar, oil, vinegar and salt to a boil.
2. Chill.
3. Then add other ingredients and refrigerate for two hours before serving.
notes: Free Take One recipe card from Publix
tags: salad, bean salad, side dish, make ahead, cold salad

name: Alfredo Noodles
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb pasta (noodles)
1/4 cup butter
2  cloves garlic (minced)
3 tbsp chopped parsley
1 dash salt and pepper
1/2 cup half & half
1/4 cup parmesan cheese
instruction_blocks:
1. In a medium size skillet, saute garlic in butter.
2. Add parsley, salt & pepper, half & half and parmesan cheese.
3. Stir over heat but do not boil.
4. Place pasta in heated bowl. Pour sauce over pasta and serve.
tags: pasta, Italian, Alfredo, quick

name: Fresh Apple Cake
summary: A Publix recipe card
ingredient_blocks:
2  eggs (well beaten)
2 cup sugar
2 cup fresh apples (chopped)
3 cup flour
1 tsp soda
1 tsp salt
1 tsp cinnamon
1 cup chopped dates
1 cup chopped nuts
1 1/4 cup cooking oil
2 tsp vanilla
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix together eggs, sugar, and fresh chopped apples. Let stand while preparing other ingredients.
2. Add flour, soda, salt, and cinnamon to the egg mixture.
3. Mix in chopped dates and chopped nuts with the flour mixture.
4. Add cooking oil and vanilla to the mixture.
5. Mix well and pour into tube pan or loaf pan.
6. Cook in 325° oven for 1 hour and 20 minutes.
notes: Recipe card says 'chopped dats' which appears to be a typo for 'chopped dates'. Can be baked in either a tube pan or loaf pan.
tags: cake, apple, dessert, vintage recipe, Publix

name: Creamy Lemon Pie
summary: Today's easy way to homemade.
ingredient_blocks:
1  graham cracker crumb crust (8-inch or 9-inch)
3  egg yolks (Use only Grade A clean, uncracked eggs)
1 can Eagle Brand Sweetened Condensed Milk (14-ounce, NOT evaporated milk)
1/2 cup ReaLemon Lemon Juice from Concentrate
yellow food coloring (few drops, optional)
whipped topping or whipped cream
instruction_blocks:
1. In medium mixing bowl, beat egg yolks; stir in Eagle Brand, ReaLemon, and food coloring if desired.
2. Pour into prepared crust.
3. Chill thoroughly.
4. Top with whipped topping.
5. Garnish as desired.
6. Refrigerate leftovers.
notes: Makes one pie. Recipe from Borden, Inc. 1985. For over 200 other delicious recipes, send for the hardcover, 128-page 'Classic Desserts' recipe book. Send $5.95 by check or money order to: P.F.C., Box 7073-B, Clinton, Iowa 52736. Allow 6 weeks for delivery. Offer good only in USA. Expires 12/31/86.
tags: pie, lemon, no-bake, dessert, cream pie, vintage

name: Noel Cookie Gems
summary: Different jams make colorful gems
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup shortening
1/4 cup sugar
1  egg
1 tsp vanilla extract
2 1/2 cup unsifted flour
1/2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp baking powder
1/4 tsp baking soda
1/2 cup Borden Sour Cream
1 1/4 cup finely chopped nuts
Bama Jams, Jellies or Preserves (for filling)
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat oven to 400°.
2. In large mixer bowl, combine shortening, sugar, egg and vanilla; mix well.
3. Sift together dry ingredients.
4. Add to shortening mixture alternately with sour cream; mix well.
5. Shape into 1 1/4-inch balls; roll in nuts.
6. Place 1 inch apart on greased baking sheets.
7. Press thumb in center of each ball; fill with jam.
8. Bake 10 to 12 minutes or until lightly browned.
notes: TIP: Noel Cookie Gems can be made ahead and frozen.
tags: cookies, Christmas, holiday, thumbprint cookies, jam cookies

name: Apricot Oatmeal Bars
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/4 cup all-purpose flour
1 1/4 cup quick-cooking oats
1/2 cup sugar
3/4 cup Land O Lakes Sweet Cream Butter (melted)
1/2 tsp baking soda
1/4 tsp salt
2 tsp vanilla
10 oz apricot preserves (jar)
1/2 cup flaked coconut
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oven to 350°.
2. In large mixer bowl combine all crumb mixture ingredients. Beat at low speed, scraping bowl often, until mixture is crumbly (1 to 2 min.).
3. Reserve 1 c. crumb mixture; press remaining crumb mixture into greased 13x9" baking pan.
4. Spread apricot preserves to within 1/2" from edge of crumb mixture; sprinkle with reserved crumb mixture and coconut.
5. Bake for 22 to 27 min. or until edges are lightly browned.
notes: Yield: 3 doz.
tags: bars, dessert, oatmeal, apricot, coconut

name: Tomato Soup Cake or Mystery Cake
summary: Is it nostalgia for the good old days or a revived interest in vegetables that has inspired interest in certain old favorites among dessert recipes. Tomato Soup Cake and Carrot Cake are high on the list at the moment.
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp butter (softened)
1 cup sugar
2 cup all-purpose flour (sifted)
1/2 tsp salt
1/2 tsp cloves
1/2 tsp nutmeg
1 tsp cinnamon
1 tsp baking soda
1  condensed tomato soup (10 3/4 ounce can)
1 cup broken nut meats
1 cup raisins
1/2 cup butter or margarine (softened, for icing)
8 oz cream cheese (softened, for icing)
3 1/2 cup confectioners' sugar (approximately, for icing)
2 tbsp milk (for icing)
1 tsp vanilla (for icing)
instruction_blocks:
1. Add sugar gradually to butter and blend well.
2. Re-sift flour with salt, cloves, nutmeg, cinnamon and baking soda.
3. Starting with soup, stir soup into sugar mixture alternately with sifted dry ingredients, using approximately 1/4 of soup and 1/4 of dry ingredients each time.
4. Beat until smooth after each addition.
5. Fold in nuts and raisins.
6. Bake in greased 9-inch tube pan at 350 degrees for 50 to 55 minutes, or until cake tester comes out clean.
7. Spread icing on after cake has cooled.
8. For Cream Cheese Icing: Cream butter and cheese and add other ingredients. Beat until smooth.
notes: Recipe clipping also includes beginning of a Carrot Cake recipe at the bottom (2 cups sugar, 1 1/2 cups salad oil, 4 eggs well-beaten, 2 teaspoons baking soda, 2 cups...) but it is cut off.
tags: vintage, cake, tomato soup, spice cake, cream cheese frosting, tube pan

name: Sirloin Tip Goulash
summary: A hearty beef goulash served over egg noodles with poppyseeds
ingredient_blocks:
1/4 cup flour
dash pepper
1 lb sirloin tip steak (cut into 1-inch cubes)
2 tbsp cooking oil
1  small onion (sliced)
2  cloves garlic (minced)
3  tomatoes (chopped, or 1 (28-oz.) can whole tomatoes, broken up)
1  (6-oz.) can tomato paste
1 cup water
2 tbsp brown sugar
1 tbsp paprika
1 tsp salt
1/4 tsp dried marjoram (crushed)
dash pepper
1 cup fresh halved mushrooms
hot egg noodles with poppyseeds (for serving)
chopped parsley (for topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine flour and pepper; coat meat cubes with flour mixture.
2. In a large skillet brown meat in hot oil. Add onion and garlic to the skillet; cook till onion is tender.
3. Combine tomatoes, tomato paste, water, brown sugar, paprika, salt, marjoram, and pepper; stir into skillet.
4. Spoon meat mixture into a 2-qt. casserole. Bake, covered, in a 350° oven for 1 1/2 hours.
5. Remove from oven and stir in mushroom halves. Cover and bake for 8 to 10 minutes more or till meat is tender.
