6. To serve, spoon goulash over hot egg noodles with poppyseeds; top with chopped parsley.
notes: From The Kroger Co., 1987. Need help? Call 1-800-632-6900.
tags: beef, goulash, Hungarian, comfort food, casserole, dinner

name: Glazed Roast Pork
ingredient_blocks:
3-4 lb pork butt roast
1  garlic clove (split)
1  currant jelly (10-oz jar)
1 tsp shredded orange peel
2 tbsp orange juice
2 tbsp lemon juice
1 tbsp butter or margarine
1 1/2 tsp grated gingerroot (or 1/4 tsp ground ginger)
1 tsp Dijon-style mustard
instruction_blocks:
1. Place roast, fat-side up, on a rack in a shallow roasting pan; rub split garlic over surface. Insert a meat thermometer. Roast in a 325° oven about 1 1/2 hours or till thermometer registers 165°. Let stand 10 minutes, allowing internal temperature to rise to 170°.
2. Meanwhile, in a small saucepan combine currant jelly, orange peel, orange juice, lemon juice, butter, gingerroot, and mustard. Heat till sauce is smooth. Baste roast with currant sauce the last 1/2 hour of roasting. Serve warm sauce with pork.
notes: Makes 4 servings plus leftovers. Need help? Call 1-800-632-6900. © 1987 The Kroger Co.
tags: pork, roast, glazed, main dish, vintage

name: Calzone
summary: A convenient form of calzone, a baked turnover, which in this recipe is made with refrigerated biscuits.
ingredient_blocks:
1 can refrigerated biscuits
1/2 cup pizza sauce
10  thin slices salami
3/4 cup pitted ripe olives (halved lengthwise)
1 cup shredded Mozzarella cheese
1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese
instruction_blocks:
1. For each calzone, press 5 biscuits into a flat cake; roll or pat out to make an 8-inch diameter circle.
2. Spread half the sauce over half the circle to within a half inch of the edge.
3. Top sauce with 5 slices of salami, half the olives, half the Mozzarella and half the Parmesan cheese.
4. Fold the plain half over the filling to resemble a turnover; pinch or crimp to seal.
5. On a greased baking sheet, place each calzone 1 1/2 inches apart.
6. Bake at 500 degrees for 6 minutes, or until golden brown.
notes: Sour salt can be purchased at Publix supermarkets and possibly other grocery stores.
tags: Italian, calzone, quick meal, biscuit dough

name: Golden Turkey Quiche
ingredient_blocks:
1  9-inch deep dish pie shell
1 cup chopped celery
1 tbsp butter or margarine
1 cup diced cooked turkey
2 tbsp chopped pimento
3  eggs
1 cup milk
1/4 cup mayonnaise
2 tbsp prepared mustard
1/2 tsp salt
1 cup shredded Cheddar or Monterey Jack cheese
paprika
instruction_blocks:
1. Bake pie shell at 375 degrees for 10 minutes.
2. Cook celery in butter until tender.
3. Stir in turkey and pimento.
4. Beat together eggs, milk, mayonnaise, mustard and salt.
5. Stir in turkey mixture. Pour into pie shell.
6. Sprinkle with cheese and paprika.
7. Bake at 375 degrees for 25 to 35 minutes, until knife inserted near center comes out clean.
tags: quiche, turkey, leftover turkey, brunch, savory pie

name: All Seasons Pesto Spaghetti
ingredient_blocks:
1/4 cup water
1 tbsp dried basil leaves
1/4 cup Blue Bonnet Margarine
1  clove garlic (crushed)
1 tbsp lemon juice
3/4 cup fresh parsley (finely chopped)
2 oz fresh Parmesan cheese (grated)
1/2  package (8 oz.) spaghetti (cooked and drained)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix water and basil; let set 30 minutes.
2. Heat Blue Bonnet Margarine and garlic until margarine melts. Remove from heat.
3. Whisk in basil mixture, lemon juice, parsley and cheese.
4. Toss with hot cooked spaghetti and serve.
tags: pasta, Italian, pesto, quick, vegetarian

name: Tortoni
summary: A frozen Italian-style dessert made with no-bake cheesecake filling, whipped cream, and candied fruit in graham cracker cups
ingredient_blocks:
1  package Royal No Bake Cheese Cake
3 tbsp sugar
1/4 cup Blue Bonnet Margarine (melted)
1 1/2 cup milk
1 tsp rum extract
1 cup heavy cream (whipped)
1/2 cup candied mixed fruit
maraschino cherries (for garnish, if desired)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix graham cracker crumbs, sugar and margarine. Press 1 tablespoonful mixture into the bottom of each of 12 cupcake pan cups.
2. Pour milk and rum extract into a small mixing bowl. Add Royal No Bake Cheese Cake filling; beat at low speed to blend.
3. Beat at medium speed 3 minutes.
4. Fold in whipped cream and fruit.
5. Fill cups with mixture.
6. Freeze.
7. Let stand at room temperature 5 minutes before removing from cups.
8. Garnish with maraschino cherries if desired.
notes: Handwritten note on back of recipe card indicates graham cracker crust ingredients: 3 tbls sugar, 1/3 cup melted Blue Bonnet Margarine (this appears to be a simplified version or reminder note of the crust portion)
tags: frozen dessert, Italian, no-bake, cheesecake, tortoni, vintage

name: All Seasons Pesto Spaghetti
ingredient_blocks:
1/4 cup water
1 tbsp dried basil leaves
1/4 cup Blue Bonnet Stick
1  clove garlic (crushed)
1 tbsp lemon juice
3/4 cup fresh parsley (finely chopped)
2 oz fresh Parmesan cheese (grated)
1/2  package (8 oz.) spaghetti (cooked and drained)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix water and basil; let set 30 minutes.
2. Heat Blue Bonnet Stick and garlic until Blue Bonnet Stick melts.
3. Remove from heat. Whisk in basil mixture, lemon juice, parsley and cheese.
4. Toss with hot cooked spaghetti and serve.
notes: Recipe is from Blue Bonnet margarine promotional recipe card
tags: pasta, Italian, pesto, quick, vegetarian

name: Tomato and Meat Loaf
ingredient_blocks:
1  29 oz can REDPACK Tomato Puree
2 lb ground beef
1  small onion (chopped fine)
6  hard boiled eggs (sliced)
1 tsp salt
1 tsp pepper
1/2 cup grated Romano Cheese
1/2 lb Monterey Jack Cheese (sliced)
instruction_blocks:
1. In large bowl, mix ground beef, onion, salt, pepper and half of the amount of grated cheese. Let stand 10 minutes.
2. Pour in half the amount of Tomato Puree into bottom of 9"x11"x2" greased loaf pan.
3. Add 1/2 the amount of beef mixture.
4. On top of beef mixture, make row of eggs, then a layer of the sliced cheese.
5. Add remainder of beef mixture, then the remaining Puree.
6. Sprinkle the rest of the grated cheese on top.
7. Bake 40 minutes in 350° oven.
tags: meat loaf, Italian, comfort food, casserole

name: Zucchini Bread
ingredient_blocks:
3  eggs (beaten till frothy)
1 1/2 cup sugar
1 cup salad oil
1 tbsp vanilla
2 cup grated zucchini (loosely packed & coarsely grated, DO NOT PEEL)
3 cup flour
1 tsp salt
2 tsp cinnamon
1 tsp baking soda
1/4 tsp baking powder
1 cup nuts
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat 3 eggs until frothy.
2. Add sugar, oil, and vanilla. Mix well.
3. Add 2 cups grated zucchini (loosely packed and coarsely grated - do not peel).
4. Add flour, salt, cinnamon, baking soda, baking powder, and nuts. Mix well.
5. Batter will be thin. Pour into 2 greased 8x5 loaf pans.
6. Bake at 350° for about 1 hour.
notes: Recipe from the kitchen of Dolores. Batter will be thin.
tags: bread, zucchini, quick bread, baking

name: Mandel Bread
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup oil
1 cup sugar
2  eggs
1 tsp salt
1 tsp baking powder
3 cup flour
1 cup nuts or maraschino cherry slivers
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix all above ingredients (oil, sugar, eggs, salt, baking powder) in a bowl.
2. Add flour and nuts or maraschino cherry slivers to the above, mix well.
3. Form into three loaves on baking sheet.
4. Bake at 350° for 40 minutes.
notes: Traditional Jewish cookie/biscotti. The handwriting reads 'Mandel Bread' (also known as Mandelbrot). Cherry slivers may also read as 'strains' - interpretation uncertain.
tags: Jewish, cookies, biscotti, traditional

name: Shortbread
summary: Joyce Rash's family favorite. Best served warm.
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 lb softened butter
1/2 cup granulated sugar (plus 2 tablespoons)
1 pinch salt
2 1/2 cup flour
instruction_blocks:
1. Add sugar and salt to soft butter.
2. Cream well with hands.
3. Gradually add 2 cups flour, continuing to cream the mixture with hands.
4. Keep adding flour until mixture resembles pie dough.
5. Pull off small balls of dough and roll them out to saucer size, about 1/4 inch thick.
6. Sprinkle with a little sugar, prick all over with fork.
7. Cut into pie-wedge shaped pieces and bake on ungreased cookie sheet at 300 degrees for 15 to 20 minutes or until edges start to brown.
8. Cool on the pan.
notes: Makes about 48 pieces. Also mentioned: Treacle Scones, which are best served warm.
tags: shortbread, cookies, Scottish, butter cookies, family recipe

name: Molasses Raisin Bread
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 cup all purpose flour
1 1/2 cup whole wheat flour
1 1/2 tsp soda
1 tsp salt
1/2 cup sugar
3 tbsp margarine
3/4 cup raisins (soften first in hot water and then dry them before adding)
3/4 cup molasses
1 1/4 cup buttermilk (or whole milk and add 2 tsp white vinegar)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a large bowl - thoroughly blend the 2 flours, soda, salt and sugar.
2. With a pastry blender cut in margarine.
3. Stir in raisins (soften them first in hot water and then dry them before adding).
4. On low speed of mixer blend in molasses and milk to dry ingredients.
5. Mix only until smooth.
6. Grease and flour bottom of 2 loaf pans 8x4x2.
7. Bake 350° - 40-45 min.
8. Remove from pans - cool slightly before slicing.
notes: Makes 2 loaves. Copyright 1980 Conimar, Inc.
tags: bread, molasses, raisin, quick bread, vintage

name: Sweet Potato Casserole
ingredient_blocks:
3 lb sweet potato
3/4 cup light brown sugar (packed)
3 tbsp butter
1/2 tsp cinnamon
1/2 tsp nutmeg
1/4 tsp salt
1 cup milk
instruction_blocks:
1. Mash potatoes, stir in all ingredients except 2 tbsp sugar.
2. Sprinkle 2 tbsp sugar on top.
3. Grease 1 1/2 quart casserole. Cover.
4. Bake at 400° for 30 min.
tags: casserole, sweet potato, side dish, holiday

name: Romanoff Potatoes
ingredient_blocks:
7-8 cup potatoes (cooked, cubed)
1 cup sour cream
3 cup cottage cheese (small curd)
1/3 cup green onions (chopped)
2 cup cheddar cheese (shredded)
salt (to taste)
pepper (to taste)
paprika (for topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine all ingredients except 1 cup cheddar cheese.
2. Put in casserole.
3. Top with cheddar cheese and paprika.
4. Bake 45 minutes at 350°F.
tags: potatoes, casserole, comfort food, side dish, cheesy

name: Peanut Butter Balls
summary: No-bake peanut butter and Rice Krispies balls dipped in chocolate
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 cup peanut butter
1 1/2 cup powdered sugar
4 tbsp oleo (melted)
3 cup Rice Krispies (crushed)
6 oz milk chocolate or semi-sweet chocolate chips (or 3/4 of a large Hershey Bar)
1/4  paraffin wax (square)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix peanut butter, powdered sugar, and melted oleo well.
2. Add 3 cups crushed Rice Krispies.
3. Form into balls and refrigerate.
4. In double boiler, melt 6 oz milk chocolate or semi-sweet chocolate chips (or 3/4 of a large Hershey Bar) with 1/4 square of paraffin wax.
5. Dip cooled balls into chocolate.
6. Place on waxed paper till firm.
notes: Oleo is margarine. Paraffin wax was traditionally used to help chocolate coating set with a nice sheen.
tags: no-bake, candy, peanut butter, chocolate, dessert, holiday

name: Eclair Cake
summary: No-bake layered dessert with graham crackers, pudding, and chocolate topping
ingredient_blocks:
2  small instant French vanilla pudding (packages)
1 box graham crackers
1  large 9oz Cool Whip
3 cup milk (for pudding)
3 tbsp butter (unsweetened chocolate)
3 tbsp light Karo syrup
1 tsp vanilla
1.5 cup powdered sugar
3-5 tbsp milk (for frosting)
instruction_blocks:
1. Butter pan - bottom and sides
2. Line pan with crackers
3. Make pudding with 3 cups milk (2 packages)
4. Mix in Cool Whip
5. Pour 1/2 mixture over crackers
6. Add 1 more layer crackers
7. Pour in other half of mixture and add 1 more layer of crackers
8. Mix and melt 3 tbsp butter (unsweetened chocolate)
9. Add 3 tbsp light Karo syrup
10. Add 1 tsp vanilla
11. Cool awhile, then mix in 1.5 cups powdered sugar and 3-5 tbsp milk
12. Pour over top layer of crackers
13. Refrigerate
notes: Make 1 day ahead. Use 13x9 pan. Serves 12-16. Second page appears to be written upside down and contains the chocolate topping/frosting instructions.
tags: no-bake, dessert, make ahead, eclair, pudding

name: Chocolate Chip Cheesecake
summary: A cheesecake variation with chocolate chips mixed into the filling and crust
ingredient_blocks: 1/2 cup chocolate chips (processed in blender until broken into tiny pieces)
instruction_blocks:
1. Place about 1/2 cup chocolate chips in blender and process until broken into tiny pieces.
2. Add to cheesecake filling ingredients.
3. Instead of the graham cracker crumb crust, butter well the springform pan and press a mixture of graham crackers crumbs and chocolate chip pieces onto pan before adding filling.
4. Alternatively, use graham cracker crumbs and nuts, or vanilla wafer or chocolate cookie crumbs.
5. Decorate top of cooked cheesecake with chocolate shavings.
notes: This appears to be a variation recipe (No. 3) that modifies a base cheesecake recipe. Crust alternatives mentioned: graham cracker crumbs with chocolate chip pieces, graham cracker crumbs and nuts, vanilla wafer crumbs, or chocolate cookie crumbs. The base cheesecake filling recipe is not included on this card.
tags: cheesecake, chocolate, dessert, variation

name: Marble Cheesecake
ingredient_blocks:
1  Graham cracker crumb crust (No. 1 Card)
3  squares unsweetened chocolate
1 tsp vegetable shortening
4  cream cheese (8oz packages)
2 tsp vanilla flavoring
1 1/2 cup sugar
6  large eggs
1 cup sour cream
instruction_blocks:
1. Prepare crust and press into 9 or 10 inch springform pan.
2. Preheat oven to 450 degrees.
3. Melt chocolate & shortening in top of double boiler.
4. In large bowl beat cream cheese until fluffy.
5. Slowly add sugar, then eggs. Add flavoring.
6. Stir in sour cream.
7. Pour about 2 cups of cream cheese mixture into separate bowl and add chocolate mixture. Mix well.
8. Pour plain cream cheese mixture into prepared crust.
9. Add the chocolate mixture by dabbing spots on top of plain mixture in about three different places.
10. Use a knife to swirl the chocolate mixture through the plain in a sigzag motion for a marble effect.
11. Bake 15 min. at 450 degrees; then reduce oven temp. to 300 degrees and bake for another hour.
12. Allow cake to cool in oven for 30 minutes or so, then cool to room temp. and refrigerate.
notes: If desired, substitute mint chocolate, a milk or a dark chocolate candy bar.
tags: cheesecake, chocolate, dessert, baked

name: Chocolate Chip Pudding Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
2 1/4 cup unsifted all-purpose flour
1 tsp baking soda
1 cup butter or margarine (softened)
3/4 cup light brown sugar (firmly packed)
1/4 cup granulated sugar
1  JELL-O Instant Pudding and Pie Filling (4-serving size package; Butter Pecan, Butterscotch, Chocolate, Milk Chocolate, Chocolate Fudge, French Vanilla or Vanilla Flavor)
1 tsp vanilla
2  eggs
1  chocolate chips (12 oz. package)
1 cup chopped nuts (optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix flour with baking soda.
2. Combine butter, the sugars, pudding mix and vanilla in large mixer bowl; beat until smooth and creamy.
3. Beat in eggs.
4. Gradually add flour mixture; then stir in chips and nuts.
5. Batter will be stiff.
6. Drop from teaspoon onto ungreased baking sheets, about 2 inches apart.
7. Bake at 375° for 8 to 10 minutes.
notes: Makes about 7 dozen. In high altitude areas, use large eggs; bake 9 to 11 minutes.
tags: cookies, chocolate chip, dessert, pudding cookies

name: California Pot Roast
ingredient_blocks:
4-5 lb pot roast
3 tbsp fat or salad oil
2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp pepper
1/2 cup water
1  tomato sauce (8 oz. can)
3  medium onions (thinly sliced)
2  cloves garlic (minced)
2 tbsp brown sugar
1/2 tsp dry mustard
1/4 cup lemon juice
1/4 cup vinegar
1/4 cup ketchup
1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
instruction_blocks:
1. Brown roast well on both sides in fat in a heavy pan.
2. Add salt, pepper, water, tomato sauce, onions and garlic.
3. Cover tightly and simmer over low heat about 1½ hours.
4. Combine brown sugar, mustard, lemon juice, vinegar, ketchup and Worcestershire sauce and pour over meat.
5. Continue cooking until meat is fork tender - about 1½ hours.
6. Thicken gravy.
notes: Recipe attributed to 'Carol Makar' (handwritten at top). Total cooking time is approximately 3 hours.
tags: pot roast, beef, comfort food, braised, American

name: Marion's Fruit Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 cup water
1/2 cup raisins
1 1/2 cup sugar
1 cup chopped nuts
1 cup mixed dried fruit
1/8 lb butter
2  eggs (slightly beaten)
2 1/4 cup flour
2 tsp baking powder
1 tsp baking soda
1 tsp cinnamon
1/2 tsp allspice
1/2 tsp ground cloves
1/2 tsp salt
candied cherries (for decoration)
walnuts (for decoration)
instruction_blocks:
1. Put water, raisins, and sugar in pot, bring to a boil, lower flame and simmer 5 minutes.
2. Remove from flame.
3. Add 1 cup chopped nuts, 1 cup mixed dried fruit, and 1/8 lb butter.
4. Let all cool, then add 2 slightly beaten eggs.
5. Sift together: 2 1/4 cup flour, 2 tsp baking powder, 1 tsp baking soda, 1 tsp cinnamon, 1/2 tsp allspice, 1/2 tsp ground cloves, and 1/2 tsp salt.
6. Add wet ingredients to dry mix a little at a time and mix.
7. Butter a tub pan and pour mixture into pan.
8. Bake at 325° for one hour.
9. When 3/4 done, take out quickly and decorate with candied cherries and walnuts - put back in oven and bake remaining time.
10. When done, cool in pan for 10 or 15 min, then take out of pan.
tags: fruit cake, holiday, vintage, dessert

name: Spinach Casserole
summary: Leftovers may be frozen
ingredient_blocks:
2 lb hamburger meat (chop, brown & drain; if served with other meats, omit chop meat)
1  medium onion (diced)
1 clove garlic (crushed, cook until onion & garlic is transparent)
8 oz mushrooms (sliced, canned or jar)
ground pepper
1/2 tsp oregano
2 pkg frozen chopped spinach (cook according to direction on box, drain thoroughly)
2 cans cream of mushroom or celery soup (do not add water)
1 cup minute rice (cooked)
1 pt sour cream
3/4 lb Monterey Jack cheese (shredded, for topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. Brown and drain 2 lbs of hamburger meat (chop if not serving with other meats)
2. Add diced onion and crushed garlic, cook until onion and garlic are transparent
3. Add mushrooms, ground pepper, and oregano
4. Cook frozen chopped spinach according to box directions and drain thoroughly
5. Mix 2 cans of cream of mushroom or celery soup (do not add water)
6. Combine all ingredients including cooked minute rice and sour cream in a large bowl and mix well
7. Put in large pyrex 9 x 13 and top with 3/4 lb of shredded Monterey Jack cheese
8. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes to melt cheese
notes: Serves 6-8. Use half ingredients for 4. Leftovers may be frozen.
tags: casserole, comfort food, spinach, ground beef, freezer-friendly

name: Minestroni
ingredient_blocks:
2 can broth (1 chicken - 1 beef, more if you like)
1/4 cup olive oil
2  medium onions (thinly sliced)
3 cloves garlic (chopped)
3  carrots (sliced)
1/2  head of cabbage (chopped or sliced)
2 stalks celery (sliced)
1 pkg frozen spinach (or canned mustard greens or any kind of greens)
1  large potato (scrubbed and cubed, leave skin on)
1 can peeled tomatoes (medium size, chopped)
1 can beans (white, red or your choice)
1 pkg chopped frozen broccoli (or chopped green beans, or both)
1 sprig fresh rosemary (or 1/2 teaspoon dry)
ground pepper
grated cheese (for serving)
instruction_blocks:
1. In pot sauté onions and garlic in olive oil until limp.
2. Add cabbage, celery, spinach, cubed potato, broccoli and/or beans and cook slowly for about 20 minutes or until limp.
3. If necessary add more olive oil.
4. Add beans, peeled tomatoes, broth, pepper and rosemary.
5. Cook for a couple of hours on low setting.
6. If desired add dry pasta like very thin spaghetti broken or capellini and let the pasta absorb the liquid.
7. Re-heat when ready to serve and serve with grated cheese.
notes: Recipe is flexible - various greens can be substituted for spinach, bean type is your choice, and broccoli and/or green beans can be used together or separately.
tags: soup, Italian, vegetable soup, comfort food, slow-cooked

name: Banana Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1 2/3 cup regular flour
1 1/2 cup sugar
1/2 cup shortening
1 cup banana (or 1/2 cup, crushed)
1/4 cup walnuts
1 tsp vanilla
1 tsp baking soda
1 pinch salt
2  eggs (separated)
1/2 cup sour milk (or sweet milk with 1 tsp. vinegar)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream shortening and sugar. Beat until smooth.
2. Add egg yolks and beat until smooth.
3. Put to medium or slow speed, and add crushed bananas, walnuts and vanilla.
4. Sift flour, baking soda, and salt together, add, alternate, with sour milk and first mixture.
5. Then fold in egg whites.
6. Bake in well-greased pan for 55 minutes at 350 degrees.
7. Let stand in pan a few minutes and turn over on cake rack.
notes: To make sour milk substitute, add 1 tsp vinegar to sweet milk.
tags: cake, banana, vintage, dessert

name: Brownies
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup cocoa
1 cup sugar
1/4 lb butter
2  eggs
vanilla (a little)
1 tsp baking powder
3/4 cup flour
chopped walnuts
instruction_blocks:
1. Melt butter and cocoa over hot water.
2. Beat eggs, all sugar, flour, baking powder, vanilla, mix to first mixture add chopped walnuts.
3. Bake in moderate oven for 30 minutes.
tags: brownies, chocolate, dessert, baked goods

name: Cream Puffs
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup water
1/3 cup Crisco or oil (little more than 1/3 cup)
1 cup presto cake flour
4  eggs (beat after each one)
1 pinch baking powder
1/2 cup sugar (for filling)
3 tbsp flour (less than 1/2 cup, for filling)
4-6  egg yolks (for filling)
1/2 cup milk (about, for filling paste)
2 cups milk (for filling)
1/2 stick butter (for filling)
1/2 tsp vanilla (for filling)
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine water, Crisco or oil, flour, eggs (beating after each one), and baking powder to make the puff dough.
2. Bake in hot oven at 450 degrees for 10 minutes.
3. Reduce to moderate oven at 350 degrees for 20 or 25 minutes.
4. For filling: Make paste with sugar, flour, egg yolks, and about 1/2 cup milk.
5. Add 2 cups milk, cook slow and stir.
6. Add butter and vanilla to filling.
notes: Recipe has two parts: the cream puff pastry and the filling. The filling appears to be a custard/pastry cream.
tags: dessert, pastry, cream puffs, vintage recipe

name: Fruit Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 lb butter
2 cup sugar (1 white, one brown cup)
4  eggs
3 cup cake flour
1 cup milk
1 tsp vanilla
1/4 tsp nutmeg
1 small jar cherries (roll in flour)
1 small can walnuts (or 1/2 cup chopped nuts, roll in flour)
1 small can citrate fruit (roll in flour)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream butter and sugar together.
2. Add egg yolks, sifted flour, add milk.
3. Fold in egg whites.
4. Add fruit that has been rolled in flour. Just fold in, do not beat.
5. Cook for about 1 hour at 350 degrees.
6. Can be decorated with cherries and walnuts.
notes: Roll cherries, walnuts, and citrate fruit in flour before adding to batter. Separate eggs - yolks go in with flour and milk, whites are folded in separately.
tags: fruit cake, holiday, vintage, baking

name: Apple Cake
ingredient_blocks:
4 tbsp butter
1 cup sugar
2  eggs
2 cup flour
3 tsp baking powder
pinch salt (a little)
vanilla
lemon flavoring
apples (cut)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream sugar and butter.
2. Add eggs, flour, baking powder and salt.
3. Mix well, add more flour if necessary.
4. Roll out dough and line cake pan.
5. Cut apples, top with sugar and put rest of dough on top in strips.
6. Bake at 325 degrees.
notes: Also can be used with other fruit
tags: cake, apple, fruit dessert, baked

name: Potato Pancake
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup raw grated white potatoes
2  whole eggs (well beaten)
1 1/2 tbsp enriched flour
1/8 tsp baking powder
1 tsp salt
instruction_blocks:
1. Peel large potatoes and cover with cold water.
2. Let soak several hours before using.
3. Pour out the water and grate potatoes.
4. Drain thoroughly.
5. Add the well-beaten egg to the grated potatoes and mix lightly.
6. Stir in the remaining ingredients.
7. Drop from tsp. on hot well-greased skillet and brown on both sides.
8. Serve with apple sauce.
tags: potato, pancakes, breakfast, side dish, German

name: Pressed Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 lb margarine
2/3 cup sugar
1/2 tsp salt
1  egg
1 tsp vanilla
2 1/2 cup flour
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream butter & sugar add egg, vanilla, and heat well.
2. Add flour and salt. Mix thoroughly.
3. Put through cookie press and bake in hot oven for 5 or 10 minutes.
notes: Could use color in some if you like.
tags: cookies, pressed cookies, dessert

name: Easter Cheese Cake
summary: Traditional Easter cheesecake with ricotta filling and homemade pie crust
ingredient_blocks:
1 can ricotta (drain overnight)
1  orange rind (grated)
1  lemon rind (grated)
1/2 glass whiskey
12  eggs (for filling)
3 cup sugar (for filling)
1 package chocolate chip
4 cup flour (for pie crust)
1 tsp baking powder (for pie crust)
1/2 lb butter (for pie crust)
2 tbsp Crisco (for pie crust)
1 cup sugar (for pie crust)
3  eggs (for pie crust)
instruction_blocks:
1. Overnight drain one can ricotta with one orange rind and one lemon rind.
2. Grate the rinds and put into 1/2 glass of whiskey.
3. Overnight strain ricotta and beat one dozen eggs.
4. Mix eggs with three cups sugar, then add whiskey mixture.
5. Add one package of chocolate chip to the filling.
6. For pie crust: Combine flour, baking powder, butter, Crisco, sugar, and eggs.
7. Knead as a pie crust, then roll out.
8. Line pan at least 2 inches height and pour filling.
9. Bake at 350 degrees for 1-2 hours.
notes: This appears to be a traditional Italian Easter cheesecake (possibly Pastiera-style). The filling requires overnight preparation for draining the ricotta and soaking the citrus rinds in whiskey.
tags: Easter, Italian, cheesecake, holiday, dessert, ricotta

name: Nut Cake
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup cake flour (or 3 cups all purpose flour minus 6 tablespoons)
1 3/4 cup sugar
4  eggs
2 tsp double acting baking powder
1 1/2 tsp salt
1 cup chopped nuts (dredged in flour)
3/4 cup milk
2 tsp vanilla
1 cup Crisco or Spry
instruction_blocks:
1. Sift all dry ingredients in large bowl.
2. Drop in Spry, milk, vanilla and eggs.
3. Beat mixture 300 brisk strokes then add other egg and beat 300 more strokes - use mixing machine.
4. Last - fold in nuts.
5. Grease and flour pan.
6. Bake in oven 375 degrees for 1 hour.
7. Let cool in pan or rack for 15 minutes before removing from pan.
notes: Recipe calls for dredging nuts in flour before folding in. Crisco and Spry are solid vegetable shortenings.
tags: cake, dessert, nuts, vintage

name: Cheese Cake
ingredient_blocks:
17  zwiebacks
2 tbsp butter
2 tbsp sugar (for crust)
1 cup sugar (for filling)
1 tsp vanilla
1 pinch salt
1/2 lb cream cheese
1/2 lb cottage cheese
4  egg yolks
2 tbsp flour
4  egg whites
1 cup milk
instruction_blocks:
1. Roll out 17 zwiebacks with rolling pin and mix with 2 tbsp. of butter and 2 tbsp. of sugar.
2. Line pan with mixture. An eight inch pyrex dish is best.
3. Cream cheese's and sugar together well.
4. Add egg yolks, vanilla, flour, and salt.
5. Cream well together, add milk, fold in stiffen egg whites.
6. Bake in greased pan or pyrex dish 1 hour at 350 degrees.
notes: An eight inch pyrex dish is recommended for best results.
tags: cheesecake, dessert, baked

name: Fryed Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
1 dozen eggs
2 shots liquor
1 3/4 cup sugar
flour (enough to make dough satiny smooth)
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat eggs, add liquor and sugar, add enough flour together.
2. Dough has to be satiny smooth.
3. Divide into 4 balls.
4. Roll real fine, cut in strips.
5. Fry in deep oil.
tags: fried, cookies, deep fried

name: Home-Made Brew
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/8 cup sugar
1/2 qt water (add a little more)
1 bottle flavor
1/3 qt alcohol (1 1/3 cups)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix alcohol and flavor together.
2. Boil sugar and water together until thick.
3. Cool sugar and water and then add alcohol and flavor.
tags: beverage, homemade, alcohol

name: Cannoli
summary: Traditional Italian fried pastry shells with ricotta filling
ingredient_blocks:
12 oz flour
1 pinch sugar
1 pinch salt
1 tbsp honey
2 tbsp shortening
white wine (a little, for making paste)
12 oz sugar (for filling)
2 tbsp water (for filling, to moisten sugar)
1 pinch cream of tartar (for filling)
1.5  ricotta (scarce, for filling)
cannella (cinnamon, powder form, a touch)
pistacchio (a few, previously soaked in warm water)
candied fruit (for garnish)
powdered sugar (for sprinkling)
instruction_blocks:
1. Use bamboo sticks for forms for cannoli shells--at least 10" long & 1" diameter.
2. Make a paste of the flour, sugar, salt, honey, and shortening with a little white wine into a fairly hard consistency. Roll into a ball and let rest at least two hours.
3. Two hours later roll out with rolling pin to a thickness of a quarter (25¢), then cut into squares of 5 inches.
4. Place bamboo on each square diagonally and fold over center the two opposite corners of the squares.
5. Press down a little seam or seal the two corners that are folded over.
6. Place the entire cannolis on tube into hot oil and let fry to a rather dark color.
7. It is best to fry one or two at a time, when the cannoli are fried to the proper coloring you remove it from oil. Make sure bamboo is cold before reusing.
8. For filling: Put into a saucepan about 12 oz. of sugar and moisten with a couple tbsp. of water so the result is a thick paste.
9. Place the pan on the fire, add a pinch of cream tarter. Stir sugar until it reaches boiling point, then let it reach the consistency of carmel. Remove from fire.
10. Place 1½ scarce of recotta in a mixing bowl and stir lightly. Pour the carameled sugar slowly but steadily over ricotta and mix rapidly. Let mixture cool for 1 hour.
11. Add to the mixture a touch of cannella in powder form and a few of the pistacchio that had previously soaked in warm water.
12. Fill cannoli shells with mixture, press cream in with knife, glaze cannoli with candied fruit. Sprinkle with powdered sugar.
notes: Bamboo sticks are used as forms and must be cooled before reusing. The dough should rest at least 2 hours before rolling. Fry to a rather dark color. The filling needs to cool for 1 hour before adding the cinnamon and pistachios. Recipe continues across two pages.
tags: Italian, dessert, fried, pastry, traditional, ricotta

name: Butter Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
3  eggs
1 cup sugar
1 tsp vanilla
1 lb butter or margarine
5 cup flour
2 1/2 tsp baking powder
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix 3 eggs, 1 cup sugar, 1 tsp vanilla together in a bowl.
2. Mix 1 lb butter or margarine, 5 cups of flour, 2 1/2 tsp baking powder in another bowl.
3. Mix all together.
4. When mixed put in cookie press.
5. Bake in pre heat oven at 300 degrees.
tags: cookies, butter cookies, pressed cookies

name: Hermit's
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup seedless raisins
1 cup chopped nuts
2 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
1/4 tsp salt
1/2 tsp soda
1 tsp cinnamon
1/2 tsp nutmeg
1 cup soft butter or margarine
1 cup sugar
3  eggs
instruction_blocks:
1. Grease baking sheets lightly. Start oven 10 minutes before baking. Set at 400 degrees.
2. Plump raisins (cool). Chop nuts.
3. Sift flour, measure, resift 4 times with next 4 ingredients.
4. Cream butter until shiny, add sugar 2 portions mixing until smooth.
5. Add eggs one at a time beating well after each.
6. Add flour in two or three portions mixing well after each.
7. Stir in raisins and nuts.
8. Drop by teaspoonsful onto baking sheet.
9. Bake 10 minutes or until nicely browned.
notes: This page also contains partial instructions for Walnut Bread at the top: Add flour in two additions using first amount necessary to make it easy to handle, knead the dough and let it rise. Knead it again and let it rise. Then roll dough and sprinkle chopped walnuts and vanilla. Spread beaten egg whites. Bake at 375 degrees.
tags: cookies, drop cookies, raisins, spiced, vintage

name: Pineapple Cheese Cake
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp sugar (for crust)
1/2  stick of butter or margarine (for crust)
1  #2 can of crushed pineapple (well drained)
1/2 cup sugar (for filling)
2 tbsp flour
1/4 tsp salt
1 lb cream cheese
1 tsp vanilla
4  eggs (separated)
1 cup light cream
instruction_blocks:
1. Break graham crackers with rolling pin into fine crumb. Blend sugar, butter, and crackers together. Press firmly over the bottom and sides of a 9" spring pan.
2. Carefully spread well drained crushed pineapple over bottom of crumb crust.
3. Combine sugar, flour, and salt. Add this to softened cream cheese and blend well. Add vanilla.
4. Stir in egg yolks (one at a time). Add cream and blend thoroughly.
5. Beat egg whites until stiff, but not dry, and fold into cheese mixture.
6. Carefully pour over pineapple.
7. Bake in slow oven 325 degrees 1 hour and 20 minutes.
8. Cool before removing from pan.
notes: Graham cracker crust requires graham crackers which are not explicitly listed in the ingredient amounts. A #2 can is approximately 20 oz or 2.5 cups.
tags: cheesecake, pineapple, dessert, baking

name: Sweet Bread
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup scalded milk (cooled)
1/2 cup sugar
1 pinch salt
1/2 cup butter or crisco (very soft, not melted)
3  eggs (beaten well)
1  yeast cake (dissolved in 1/4 cup warm water)
5 cup flour
instruction_blocks:
1. Scald 1 cup of milk, cool, and add 1/2 cup of sugar, pinch of salt, 1/2 cup of butter or crisco very soft (not melted).
2. Add 3 eggs beaten well with 1 yeast cake, dissolved in 1/4 cup of warm water.
3. Add this mixture to the milk mixture, then add 5 cups of flour gradually.
4. Make a soft dough and put in greased bowl, and let raise 2 hours.
5. Knead down and put in greased pans, let raise 2 more hours.
6. Place in oven 400 degrees.
7. Add citron if you want.
notes: Optional: Add citron. This page also contains a partial recipe for Anise Cookies below the Sweet Bread recipe.
tags: bread, sweet bread, yeast bread, vintage

name: Italian Chew Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
3/4 cup soft butter
2 tbsp granulated sugar
1 2/3 cup flour
3  egg yolks
2 1/2 cup light brown sugar (packed)
1 cup chopped walnuts
3/4 cup flaked coconuts
3  egg whites (beaten stiff)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream butter and add sugar. Beat until light.
2. Add flour mix well.
3. Pat in pan 13"-9"-2".
4. Bake in oven 15 minutes at 375 degrees.
5. Beat egg yolks, add sugar little at a time.
6. Add nuts and coconuts, then fold in egg whites.
7. Spread on baked mixture.
8. Bake 25-30 minutes longer.
notes: Page also contains a second recipe for Rum Balls: 2 1/2 cups vanilla crumbs (or wafers), 1 cup chopped pecans, 1/2 cup brandy or rum, 1 cup confectioners sugar, 3 tbs. cocoa, 2 tbs. white corn syrup
tags: Italian, cookies, coconut, walnuts, bar cookies

name: Vegetarian Meatballs
summary: An eggplant recipe made on the Mike Douglas Show by a Roman Catholic Priest
ingredient_blocks:
1  medium eggplant
1 cup Romano cheese (more or less)
1 cup bread crumbs (more or less)
1  egg (beaten)
1 tbsp minced onion
oil (for frying)
spaghetti sauce (favorite)
instruction_blocks:
1. Peel and cube eggplant and boil in salted water until soft; drain and mash.
2. Add Romano cheese, bread crumbs, egg, and onion till it's possible to form balls.
3. Brown in oil; drain on toweling.
4. Simmer in sauce 20 minutes.
5. Serve over pasta.
notes: It is delicious and my family loves it, even my two young sons. Recipe submitted by Terry Chappell from Ft. Myers, Fla.
tags: vegetarian, Italian, eggplant, meatless, pasta

name: Grain Pastiere
summary: Traditional Italian grain pie (pastiera) with ricotta filling
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb shelled grain (soak overnight if not pre-soaked)
1 lb flour (for dough)
1/2 lb sugar (for dough)
1/2 lb leaf lard (for dough)
1/4 tsp baking powder (for dough)
4  egg yolks (for dough)
lemon rind (chopped, for dough)
1 jigger cognac or rum (for dough)
4 lb ricotta (for filling)
2 dozen eggs (separated, for filling)
chopped citron (for filling)
chopped lemon peel (for filling)
orange water or extract (for filling)
2-3 cup sugar (for filling)
1 jigger whiskey (for filling)
1 lump butter (for cooking grain)
salt (for cooking grain)
instruction_blocks:
1. If you can't buy the grain already soaked, then you must try to buy the shelled grain. Soak it the night before you use it (put it in an enamel or china container).
2. The next day, in the same water add salt and a lump of butter and cook until tender (1 1/2 or 2 hrs.) low flame turning often, till water is absorbed.
3. For dough: Sift dry ingredients together, add lard, egg yolks, lemon rind and cognac. Handle dough as little as possible.
4. One half of dough is to be rolled out between two sheets of waxed paper and placed in baking pan. The other half is rolled but cut into ribbon strips to be used for the top.
5. For filling: To ricotta add slightly beaten egg yolks, citron, lemon peel, sugar, whiskey, orange water and mix gently not to make it too smooth.
6. Then fold in beaten egg whites (not too stiff) till used up in mixture.
7. Fold in drained grain. Pour in lined pie pan, place strips on top.
8. Put in hot oven 375 degrees for 10 minutes, then lower to 325 degrees for about 1 hour or 1 hr. & 1/2.
notes: Makes 1 large pie. This is a traditional Italian Easter pastiera. The grain should be cooked in an enamel or china container. Handle the dough as little as possible for best results.
tags: Italian, Easter, pie, ricotta, traditional, grain pie, pastiera

name: Toll House Pan Cookie
ingredient_blocks:
2-1/4 cup unsifted flour
1 tsp baking soda (measuring teaspoon)
1 tsp salt (measuring teaspoon)
1 cup butter (softened)
3/4 cup sugar
3/4 cup brown sugar (firmly packed)
1 tsp vanilla extract (measuring teaspoon)
2  eggs
12 oz Nestlé Semi-Sweet Real Chocolate Morsels (one pkg, 2 cups)
1 cup chopped nuts
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F. In small bowl, combine flour, baking soda and salt; set aside.
2. In large bowl, combine butter, sugar, brown sugar and vanilla extract; beat until creamy.
3. Beat in eggs. Gradually add flour mixture; mix well.
4. Stir in Nestlé Semi-Sweet Real Chocolate Morsels and nuts.
5. Spread into greased 15" x 10" x 1" baking pan.
6. Bake at 375°F for 20 minutes.
7. Cool; cut into 2" squares. Makes 35 squares.
notes: For one 6-oz. pkg., recipe may be divided in half. Spread into greased 13" x 9" x 2" baking pan. Bake for 12-15 minutes. Makes 24 squares.
tags: cookies, chocolate, dessert, bar cookies, Toll House

name: Kulich
summary: Traditional Easter bread
ingredient_blocks:
1  package active dry yeast
1/4 cup warm water (not hot)
1 cup scalded milk (cooled to lukewarm)
2/3 cup sugar
1/2 tsp salt
3  egg yolks
3/4 cup soft butter or margarine
4 cup sifted flour (about)
1/2 cup raisins
3/4 cup candied fruit (citron, orange peel, cherries)
1/4 cup toasted almonds (chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. Soften the yeast in the warm, not hot, water.
2. In a large bowl mix the scalded, cooled milk, sugar, salt, egg yolks and soft butter. Beat in half the flour.
3. Add the raisins, citron, orange peel, cherries, almonds, cognac and yeast. Beat in remaining flour.
4. Turn out on a floured board; knead until dough is smooth and elastic.
5. Place in an oiled bowl, lightly oil the top of dough. Cover, let rise until double in bulk.
6. Shape two-thirds of the dough into a ball. Place into a greased and floured shortening can (3 lb. size). The can should be half-filled.
7. Divide remaining dough into two balls. Place each ball into a greased and floured can (#2 size). Let rise until dough reaches the top of the can.
8. Bake in a moderate oven until brown. Remove from can to rack.
9. If desired, frost top of bread with confectioners' sugar mixed with a little warm milk and allow to dribble down the sides.
notes: First rising time: one and one-half hours. Second rising time: two hours. Oven temperature: 350 degrees. Baking time: 30-35 minutes (about). Yield: 1 large & 2 small. Page also includes recipe for Apple Pie Filling (Without Apples) using water, cream of tartar, sugar, and Ritz crackers.
tags: Easter bread, Russian, holiday, yeast bread

name: Italian Easter Ring
ingredient_blocks:
2  packages active dry yeast
1/4 cup warm water (not hot)
1 cup scaled milk (cooled to lukewarm)
1/2 cup soft butter
1/2 cup sugar
3/4 tsp salt
4  egg yolks (slightly beaten)
2 tbsp slivered citron
1 tbsp slivered orange peel
1/2 cup Sultana raisins
4 cup sifted flour (about)
8  hard-cooked eggs (colored)
1 cup confectioners' sugar
1 tbsp milk
instruction_blocks:
1. In a bowl soften the yeast in the warm, not hot water.
2. Scald the milk and cool to lukewarm; combine with the butter, sugar, salt, egg yolks, citron, orange peel and raisins.
3. Beat in one cup flour. Add the softened yeast. Beat in remaining flour.
4. Toss on a floured board, knead until smooth and elastic. Shape into a ball.
5. Place in an oiled bowl; brush top of dough with oil. Cover. Let rise in a warm place until double in bulk.
6. Take two-thirds of the dough and divide into four pieces. Shape each piece into a long roll (18x1 in.). Twist two of the rolls to form a circle of nest. Place on a greased baking pan.
7. Place colored eggs in the dough. Repeat procedure.
8. Shape the remaining dough into six thin long rolls. Place three rolls over the eggs in a criss-cross fashion. Repeat procedure.
9. Brush top of dough with melted butter. Cover. Let rise until double in bulk.
10. Bake in a moderate oven until golden. Remove to racks.
11. While warm, brush top with combined confectioners' sugar and hot milk.
notes: First rising time: 2 hours. Second rising time: 1 hour. Oven temperature: 350 degrees. Yield: 2 rings.
tags: Italian, Easter, bread, yeast bread, holiday

name: Pasta with Carbonara Sauce
ingredient_blocks:
4  eggs
1/4 cup butter or margarine
1/4 cup cream
1 lb fettucini or spaghetti
2  Hormel sliced pepperoni (3 1/2 oz. pkgs)
1 cup Parmesan cheese (grated)
1/4 cup fresh parsley (snipped)
pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Let eggs, butter or margarine and cream stand at room temp. for 2-3 hours.
2. Beat together eggs and cream just until blended.
3. Add pasta to a large amount of boiling, salted water. Cook 10-12 minutes or until tender, but firm. Drain well.
4. Heat an ovenproof serving dish in a 250 degree oven.
5. Turn pasta into heated serving dish. Toss pasta with butter and pepperoni.
6. Pour egg mixture over and toss until pasta is well coated.
7. Add cheese and parsley, toss to mix.
8. Serve immediately.
notes: Makes 10-12 side dishes or 4-5 main servings. This is a variation of carbonara using pepperoni instead of traditional pancetta or guanciale.
tags: Italian, pasta, carbonara, comfort food

name: Pepper Cookies
summary: A savory bread-like cookie made with yeast
ingredient_blocks:
2 1/2 cup flour
1 tsp salt
1 tsp pepper
1 pkg dry yeast (original recipe calls for 2 pkgs, handwritten note says 1 yeast)
1/2 cup warm water
1/2 cup wesson oil (handwritten note says 'use a little less')
instruction_blocks:
1. Add flour, pepper, salt (should be like bread dough).
2. Put in Tupperware with cover to rise so it will double in bulk.
3. Let rise for half hour.
4. Oil the cookie sheet lightly.
5. Bake at 400° for the first 10 minutes.
6. Lower to 350° and bake for 20 to 30 min.
notes: Handwritten modifications suggest using 1 package of yeast instead of 2, and using a little less oil. Despite the name 'cookies', these are more like savory bread rolls.
tags: savory, yeast bread, vintage recipe

name: Broccoli Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp grated onion
1/2 cup butter or margarine (melted)
1/4 cup all-purpose flour
1 cup milk
1 cup half and half
2 cup chicken broth
1/2 tsp salt
1/8 tsp garlic powder
1/4 to 1/2 tsp dried basil
3 cup fresh broccoli (chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. Sauté onion in butter in Dutch oven.
2. Add flour, stirring until smooth. Cook 1 minute, stirring constantly.
3. Gradually add milk, half and half, and chicken broth; cook over medium heat stirring constantly, until the mixture is thickened and bubbly.
4. Stir in salt, garlic powder, and basil.
5. Add broccoli, cover and cook over medium heat 15 min or until broccoli is tender.
notes: Yield: 5 cups. Recipe attributed to Claire.
tags: soup, broccoli, comfort food, creamy

name: Mushroom Soup
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup mushrooms (stems diced, caps sliced)
1/4 cup onion (finely diced)
1/4 cup cooked carrots (diced)
1/4 cup cooked celery (finely diced)
5 tbsp butter
7 tbsp flour
4 cup chicken broth
1 cup light cream or milk
2 tbsp dry vermouth
salt and pepper (to taste)
chopped parsley (optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. Saute mushroom stems and caps in butter with onion about 5 minutes.
2. Add vermouth. Cook 3 minutes.
3. Add flour. Cook stirring and cook till thickened.
4. Cool slightly and add cream or milk and the cooked carrots and celery.
5. Heat through. Do not boil.
6. Add salt and pepper to taste.
7. Sprinkle with parsley if desired.
tags: soup, mushroom, cream soup, comfort food

name: Potato Casserole
ingredient_blocks:
2 lb hashbrown frozen potatoes (or frozen cubed potatoes)
1/2 cup minced onion
1/2 cup oleo or butter (melted)
1 tsp salt
2 cup sour cream (1 pt)
2 cup shredded cheddar cheese
1 can Campbell's mushroom soup (do not dilute)
1 cup crushed corn flakes
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix all but potatoes (let them thaw about 10 min).
2. Grease pan - put potatoes on bottom.
3. Add ingredients to potatoes.
4. Add cornflakes to cover top.
5. Bake at 350° for 45 to 50 min.
tags: casserole, potato, comfort food, side dish

name: Sausage Bread
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 lb sausage
1 cup sifted flour
1/2 tsp baking powder
1/4 tsp soda
1/2 tsp salt
2 tbsp shortening
1/3 cup buttermilk
1 tbsp grated onion
1 cup shredded cheese
instruction_blocks:
1. Crumble sausage in the bottom of a 9" pie plate.
2. Bake in a 425° oven for 10 min.
3. Pour off fat leaving about 1 tbsp.
4. Sift flour with baking powder, soda and salt.
5. Rub in shortening and stir in buttermilk to make a rather soft dough.
6. Mix in onions and cheese.
7. Knead slightly and roll out to a 9" circle.
8. Place on top of sausage and bake in 400° for about 30 min.
notes: Two-stage baking process: first bake sausage at 425°F for 10 minutes, then bake with dough topping at 400°F for about 30 minutes.
tags: bread, sausage, savory, comfort food, cheese

name: Family Star Ring
summary: A star-shaped bread ring with date-nut filling
ingredient_blocks:
5-5 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
2  packages Fleischmann's Active Dry Yeast or RapidRise Yeast
1/2 cup sugar
4 tsp Spice Islands Lemon Peel
3/4 tsp salt
1 cup low fat milk
1/2 cup water
1/2 cup margarine
2  eggs (at room temperature; for low-cholesterol baking, substitute 3 egg whites)
No Cholesterol PAM Cooking Spray
Date-Nut Filling (recipe follows)
Confectioners' sugar icing (optional)
1  8-ounce package Dromedary Pitted Dates (snipped, for filling)
1/2 cup water (for filling)
1 tsp Spice Islands Ground Cinnamon or Pumpkin Pie Spice (for filling)
1/2 cup chopped toasted pecans (for filling)
instruction_blocks:
1. In large bowl mix 2 cups flour, undissolved yeast, sugar, lemon peel and salt.
2. Heat milk, water and margarine until hot to touch (125° to 130°F). Gradually add to dry ingredients; beat 2 min. at medium speed of electric mixer, scraping bowl occasionally. Add eggs and 1 cup flour; beat at high speed 2 min., scraping bowl occasionally. With spoon, stir in enough additional flour to make stiff dough.
3. Knead on lightly floured surface until smooth and elastic, about 6-8 min. Place in bowl coated with PAM; spray top of dough with PAM. Cover; let rise in warm place until doubled in size, about 45 min.
4. Punch dough down. On lightly floured surface, roll dough to 18" x 15". Spread filling evenly on dough, leaving 1/2" border at long edges. Roll up from long side as for jelly roll; pinch seam to seal. Join ends to form ring; pinch to seal. Place in 10" tube pan coated with PAM. Spray top of dough with PAM. Cover; let rise in warm place until almost doubled in size, about 35-45 min.
5. To make star: With sharp knife, make 8 V-shaped cuts in top of ring. Make each "V" 1/2" deep, 1" at wide end and 2" long with points extending to outer edge of ring.
6. Bake at 350°F for 55 to 60 min., until done. Cover with foil after 35 min. Remove from pan; cool on wire rack. Frost with icing, if desired.
7. Date-Nut Filling: Combine 1 (8-ounce) package Dromedary Pitted Dates or Dromedary Chopped Dates (snipped) and 1/2 cup water in saucepan over medium heat. Simmer 5 min., stirring occasionally, until very thick. Stir in 1 teaspoon Spice Islands Ground Cinnamon or Pumpkin Pie Spice and 1/2 cup chopped, toasted pecans.
notes: To save up to 50% rising time, use Fleischmann's RapidRise Yeast. Follow above directions except: reduce first rise, cover kneaded dough and let rest on board 10 minutes. Proceed with recipe. If you have any questions about baking with Fleischmann's Yeast, call our toll free BAKER'S HELP-LINE 1-800-227-6202. Makes 1 Loaf.
tags: bread, yeast bread, breakfast, holiday, dates, ring

name: Baked Doughnuts
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 cup milk (scalded and cooled to lukewarm)
1/3 cup melted butter
1/4 cup sugar
2 tsp salt
2 tsp nutmeg
1/2 tsp cinnamon
2  eggs
2  packages active dry yeast
1/4 cup warm water (not hot)
5 cup sifted flour
melted butter (for brushing)
sugar (for dipping)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a saucepan scald the milk and cool to lukewarm.
2. Add the butter, sugar, salt, nutmeg, cinnamon and eggs; mix until blended.
3. In a cup dissolve the yeast in a warm, not hot, water. Blend into the milk mixture.
4. Add the flour gradually. Toss on a floured board and knead until smooth and elastic.
5. If an electric beater is used add 4 cups of flour and beat until smooth and elastic.
6. Knead in the remaining cup of flour into the dough on the board.
7. The dough is a soft mass. Shape into a ball. Place in a greased bowl; grease the top.
8. Cover. Let stand until double in bulk.
9. Roll out half the dough to 1/2 inch in thickness. Cut with doughnut cutter.
10. Place on a greased cookie sheet one inch apart. Brush with melted butter.
11. Repeat procedure for the remaining dough. Let it rise until double in bulk.
12. Bake in a hot oven until golden.
13. Remove to racks. Brush with melted butter; dip in sugar.
notes: First rising time: 1 hour. Second rising time: 40 minutes. Oven temperature: 425 degrees. Baking time: 10 minutes (about). Yield: 48 doughnuts.
tags: baked, doughnuts, yeast, breakfast, dessert

name: Blue Berry Cake
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup flour
1 cup sugar
2  sticks of butter
3 tsp baking powder (level)
1 pinch salt
3  eggs (beat lightly)
instruction_blocks:
1. Work flour, sugar, butter, baking powder, and salt together.
2. Add 3 lightly beaten eggs and work all together.
3. Leave a little (3 tbs.) for strips.
4. Grease pan and pat dough in pan.
5. Pour filling and make strips.
6. Bake at 350 degrees for 1/2 an hour.
notes: This appears to be a blueberry cake with a lattice top made from dough strips. The filling (presumably blueberry) is not detailed in this recipe - may be on another page or assumed to be a standard blueberry filling.
tags: cake, blueberry, fruit cake, dessert

name: Nun's Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 lb butter or margarine
2 cup sugar (mix well)
4  eggs (mix well)
1 pint sour cream
3 cup flour (sift once alone)
2 tsp baking powder
2 tsp baking soda
2 tsp vanilla (or 1 vanilla and 1 lemon)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix butter or margarine with sugar well.
2. Add eggs and mix well.
3. Add sour cream.
4. Sift flour once alone, then add with baking powder and baking soda.
5. Add vanilla (or 1 vanilla and 1 lemon).
6. Bake at 350 degrees for one hour.
notes: This page also contains a second recipe for Italian Cookies: 1 dozen eggs, 1 lb sugar (2 cups), 1/2 cup orange juice, 1 cup milk, 2 tsp vanilla, 12 tsp baking powder, 1 tsp baking soda, 2 tsp anise flavor, 1 lb butter, about 4-5 cups flour (or use about four cups of flour or more). Bake at 375 for gas and at 400 for electric.
tags: cake, sour cream, vanilla, baking

name: Spinach Soup
ingredient_blocks:
1/4 cup chopped onion
1/2  clove garlic (minced)
1 tbsp butter or margarine
1 can chicken broth (46 oz)
1/2 cup Ronzoni Acini Pepe (pasta)
1/4 tsp nutmeg
1/8 tsp pepper
1 pkg chopped spinach (10 oz, frozen)
grated Parmesan cheese (for topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cook and stir onion and garlic in hot butter until tender but not browned.
2. Add chicken broth and bring to a boil.
3. Add pasta, nutmeg and pepper; simmer about 5 minutes.
4. Add chopped spinach (frozen) and simmer 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Season to taste with salt, if desired.
6. Sprinkle with cheese.
notes: Salt to taste is optional. The pasta used is Acini di Pepe (small round pasta).
tags: soup, spinach, Italian, pasta soup, quick

name: Hungarian Paprikas
summary: Traditional Hungarian chicken paprikash with sour cream gravy
ingredient_blocks:
3 lb chicken
2  onions (finely chopped)
3 tbsp Crisco
1 tbsp paprika
1 tsp salt
1 pt sour cream
7 tbsp flour
instruction_blocks:
1. Brown onions lightly in Crisco.
2. Add chicken and paprika. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
3. Cover and cook slowly until tender.
4. Remove chicken from pan.
5. Add enough water or chicken broth to drippings to make 3 cups.
6. Mix flour with sour cream - add to boiling stock.
7. Boil until thickened.
8. Put chicken back into sour cream gravy.
9. Serve with dumplings.
notes: Classic Hungarian comfort dish. Pepper is mentioned in instructions but not in ingredient list - add to taste.
tags: Hungarian, chicken, comfort food, Eastern European, main dish

name: New England Donuts
ingredient_blocks:
4  medium eggs
1 cup sugar
4 tsp melted butter
1 cup milk
1 tsp vanilla
1 tsp lemon extract
3-4  drops almond extract
4 cup flour
1 tsp nutmeg
3 tsp baking powder
1 tsp salt
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream eggs and sugar together.
2. Cream butter in and add milk.
3. Blend all the ingredients together.
4. Put in refrigerator for two hours or over night.
notes: Recipe does not include frying instructions. Dough requires refrigeration before shaping and frying.
tags: donuts, New England, breakfast, fried, dessert

name: Kattbullar (Swedish Meatballs)
ingredient_blocks:
1/4 lb butter (divided, 3 tbsp for sauteing onion, remaining for sauce)
1  medium onion (chopped fine)
1 lb lean beef (ground together with pork and veal)
1 lb pork (ground together)
1/2 lb veal (ground together)
2  eggs
1 cup bread crumbs (soaked in 1 cup light cream)
2 tbsp parsley (chopped fine)
1 tbsp salt (about, to taste)
3/4 tsp pepper (about, to taste)
1 tbsp flour (for sauce)
1.5 cup heavy cream (for sauce)
instruction_blocks:
1. Take 3 tbsp butter and saute 1 medium onion chopped fine.
2. Mix all together (ground meats, eggs, soaked bread crumbs, parsley, salt and pepper) and form in small balls.
3. Cook slowly in butter until brown. Keep shaking pan gently to move meatballs.
4. Put cooked meatballs in covered casserole to keep warm while cooking.
5. For sauce: Into frying pan put remaining butter and 1 tbsp flour, enough salt and pepper to flavor sauce and 1 1/2 cups of heavy cream.
6. Boil gently 5 min. Pour over warm meatballs and serve.
notes: This page also contains a recipe for Vanilla Cookies
tags: Swedish, meatballs, comfort food, main dish, cream sauce

name: Barbecue Sauce
summary: Sauce for cooking hot dogs
ingredient_blocks:
2  onions (chopped)
3/4 cup ketchup
1 tbsp vinegar
1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
2/3 cup water
1 tsp salt
1 tbsp chile powder
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine all ingredients and boil for 1/2 hour.
2. Cook hot dogs in sauce.
notes: This page contains multiple recipes including Easter Cheese Cake and Filling for Prosuit Pie
tags: sauce, barbecue, hot dogs

name: Lentil Soup
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup Blue Bonnet Margarine (1 stick)
2 cup chopped onions
1 lb dried lentils
10 cup water
2 tbsp chopped parsley
2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp ground black pepper
2  bay leaves
instruction_blocks:
1. Melt Blue Bonnet Margarine in a large heavy saucepan.
2. Add onions and saute until tender, about 5 minutes.
3. Stir in lentils, water, parsley, salt and pepper.
4. Add bay leaves.
5. Bring to a boil.
6. Cover; reduce heat and simmer 1½ to 2 hours, or until lentils are tender.
7. Discard bay leaves.
8. Serve hot.
9. If desired, puree in blender or processor before serving.
notes: Can be pureed in blender or processor before serving for a smoother texture.
tags: soup, lentils, vegetarian, comfort food

name: Pasteria Easter Cake
summary: Traditional Italian Easter ricotta pie with a lattice crust
ingredient_blocks:
1/3 cup minute tapioca
1/3 cup sugar (for tapioca mixture)
1/4 tsp salt (for tapioca mixture)
2 1/2 cup milk
1  egg (slightly beaten, for tapioca mixture)
1/4 tsp cinnamon (for tapioca mixture)
3 cup pastry flour
1/2 tsp cinnamon (for pastry dough)
1/2 cup sugar (for pastry dough)
1 pinch salt (for pastry dough)
2  eggs (slightly beaten, for pastry dough)
1/2 cup loaf lard
1/2 tsp lemon rind (grated)
2 1/2 lb ricotta
2 cup sugar (for filling, or sweeten to desire)
10  egg yolks
1/2 tsp cinnamon (for filling)
1/4 tsp lemon rind (grated, for filling)
1/2 cup candied citron and orange peel (dried fine)
8  egg whites
instruction_blocks:
1. Step I - Tapioca Mixture: Blend tapioca, sugar, salt, milk, egg, and cinnamon. Let stand five minutes. Cook over medium heat, stir constantly. Cook 2 minutes past boiling. Remove from heat, add cinnamon and cool.
2. Step II - Pastry Dough: Sift dry ingredients into pastry board, make a well in center and place eggs, lard and lemon rind, work with hands until smooth. Chill for 30 minutes.
3. Step III - Ricotta Filling: Place ricotta in bowl and stir until very smooth, add sugar and egg yolk one at a time. Stir well after each egg. Add cinnamon, lemon peel, citron and orange peel. Blend well, add tapioca and ricotta mixture.
4. Step IV - Assembly: Take 2/3 of pastry and roll to thickness of a little more than 1/4 inch. Place in deep pie plate which has been buttered and floured. Cut rest of dough in strips and lay criss cross over pie. Pressing ends firmly, bake in 375 degree oven for 1 hour or until firm.
notes: This is a traditional Italian Easter pie (Pastiera). The recipe uses loaf lard for the pastry. Sweeten ricotta filling to taste if desired.
tags: Italian, Easter, holiday, dessert, pie, ricotta, traditional

name: Duncan Hines Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1 pkg Duncan Hines Yellow Cake Mix
1 pkg vanilla instant pudding
4  eggs
1 cup water
1/2 cup Wesson oil
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat in electric mixer for four (4) minutes.
2. Bake in an angel food pan for one (1) hour in 350° oven.
tags: cake, yellow cake, box mix

name: Chiffon Cake
ingredient_blocks:
7  egg whites
1/2 tsp cream of tartar
2 cup flour
3 tsp baking powder
1 pinch salt
1 1/2 cup sugar
7  egg yolks
1/2 cup oil
3/4 cup orange juice
1 tsp vanilla
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat egg whites with cream of tartar until stiff peaks form.
2. Combine flour, baking powder, salt, sugar, egg yolks, oil, orange juice, and vanilla.
3. Fold egg white mixture into batter.
4. Pour into ungreased 10" tube pan or large square pan.
5. Bake at 325°F for 1 hour 20 minutes.
notes: Page also contains a separate Fudge recipe: Combine 3c. sugar, 2/3 c. (6 oz. can) evaporated milk or 2/3 c. light cream, and 3/4 c. Parkay Margarine; bring to boil. Cook 5 minutes to softboil stage (238°), stirring frequently. Remove from heat; stir in two 6 oz. pkgs. semi-sweet chocolate morsels. Add 1 jar Kraft Marshmallow Creme, 1 tsp. vanilla and 1 c. chopped nuts. Beat until well blended. Pour into greased 13x9x2" pan. Cool. Cut into squares.
tags: cake, chiffon, orange, vintage

name: Polish Bows
summary: Two variations of traditional Polish bow-shaped fried pastries (chrusciki/faworki)
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup all-purpose flour (Marge's version)
2  egg yolks (Marge's version)
1  whole egg (Marge's version)
1/2 container sour cream (Marge's version)
1 pinch salt (Marge's version)
1-1/2 cup flour (Ella's version)
6  egg yolks (Ella's version)
1/4 tsp salt (Ella's version)
3 tbsp vanilla (Ella's version)
1-1/2 cup flour (Ella's version - second addition)
1 jigger brandy, rum or rock and rye (Ella's version)
instruction_blocks:
1. Marge's version: Beat eggs and sour cream. Add flour. Knead until very smooth.
2. Roll out and cut in 6" strips 1" wide.
3. Fry in deep oil.
4. Sprinkle with powdered sugar when cool.
5. Ella's version: Mix egg yolks, vanilla, and rum with 1-1/2 cups flour.
6. Add sour cream. Mix other 1-1/2 c. flour with salt and add to first batter.
7. Knead, shape and roll (not too thin).
notes: Two family recipe variations attributed to Marge and Ella. Ella's version appears to be missing sour cream in the ingredients list but mentions it in the instructions. 'Rock and rye' is a traditional whiskey-based liqueur.
tags: Polish, fried, dessert, pastry, holiday, traditional, chrusciki, faworki

name: Boston Baked Beans
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb pea beans (boiled)
1/2 lb salt pork (sliced)
2 tbsp salt
1/2 cup molasses
2  small onions (or 1 large onion cut in half)
2 tbsp brown sugar (about)
instruction_blocks: 1. Bake in oven at 250 degrees for about 8 hours.
notes: Original temperature appears to have been crossed out and changed to 250 degrees.
tags: beans, baked beans, slow cooked, American

name: Nut & Poppyseed Roll
ingredient_blocks:
4 cup all purpose flour
1 cup butter
1 tsp salt
2 tbsp sugar
2  egg yolks
2  cakes of yeast
1 cup lukewarm water or milk
1 lb shelled nuts (for nut filling)
3/4 cup brown & white sugar (mixed, for nut filling)
1 tbsp lemon or almond extract (for nut filling)
1 cup bread or graham cracker crumbs (toasted in 2 tablespoons of oleo, for nut filling)
1 cup heated milk (just to make a paste, for nut filling)
instruction_blocks:
1. Crumble yeast into large cup or bowl and add lukewarm milk or water and set aside.
2. Cut butter into flour, salt & sugar with pastry blender till like coarse corn meal.
3. Make a well and add unbeaten yolks, then add yeast mixture and mix well until it leaves bowl clean, this takes about 5 minutes.
4. Put dough on floured pastry cloth. Divide in 4 portions and let rise while you make the filling.
5. This dough doesn't have to rise double like other dough before filling.
6. Roll one section of dough into rectangle about 1/4 inch thick.
7. Spread filling and roll like for a jelly roll.
8. Place on lightly greased pan 12x15 or on cookie sheet.
9. Brush with egg. Let rest for 20 min.
10. Bake 30 min. at 425 F.
notes: Makes 4 rolls - 2 poppyseed and 2 nut. Nut filling recipe included. The recipe page also mentions a poppyseed filling which is not detailed on this page.
tags: pastry, Eastern European, holiday, nut roll, poppyseed, yeast bread

name: Dough for Proscutto Pie
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup flour
1 pinch salt
3  eggs (save 1 yolk)
1 tbsp crisco
2 tbsp baking powder (handwritten addition)
2/3 cup lukewarm water
instruction_blocks:
1. Put flour, salt, baking powder, and crisco.
2. Make hole and place eggs inside.
3. Use water as needed.
4. Knead well.
5. Place dough between 2 dishes for about 15-20 minutes.
6. Fill and bake at 375° for 1 hour.
notes: Handwritten note adds '2 tbs. baking powder' next to the crisco line. Page also contains a second recipe for Connoli Shells: 3 cups sifted all purpose flour, 1 tbs sugar, 1/4 tsp cinnamon, 3/4 cup port wine, 1 egg yolk slightly beaten, salad oil or shortening for deep fry.
tags: Italian, pie, savory, prosciutto

name: Swedish Speed Meatballs
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb ground veal or beef
1 lb ground pork
1 cup milk
2  eggs
1 cup soft bread crumbs
2 tsp salt
1 tsp nutmeg
1 tsp cardamom
1 tsp thyme
1/2 cup butter or margarine
4  cans beef gravy (12oz cans)
2 cup sour cream
2  whole cloves
1  bay leaf
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix ground veal or beef and ground pork together.
2. Beat milk with eggs then stir in soft bread crumbs, salt, nutmeg, cardamom, and thyme.
3. Add to combined meat and stir in lightly but thoroughly. Mixture will be soft.
4. Using 1 measuring tablespoon of mixture, shape into little meatballs (makes approximately 4½ dozen).
5. Saute meatballs a few at a time in ½ cup butter or margarine until they are lightly browned.
6. Remove sauteed meatballs from skillet to sauce pan.
7. Pour beef gravy into skillet and heat, stirring to lift all browned bits from bottom.
8. Stir in sour cream and add whole cloves and bay leaf.
9. Pour over meatballs and simmer slowly and cover for 20 to 30 minutes.
10. Remove cover and simmer 10 minutes longer.
11. Serve with buttered noodles.
notes: Serves 6 to 8
tags: Swedish, meatballs, comfort food, dinner, Scandinavian

name: Cranberry Relish Mold
ingredient_blocks:
1  9-ounce can crushed pineapple
1  3-ounce package cherry-flavored gelatin
1/2 cup sugar
1 tbsp lemon juice
1 cup ground fresh cranberries
1  small unpeeled orange (seeds removed, ground)
1 cup chopped celery
1/2 cup chopped walnuts
instruction_blocks:
1. Drain pineapple. Reserve syrup, add water to make 1/2 cup.
2. Dissolve gelatin, sugar in 1 cup hot water. Add syrup lemon juice.
3. Chill till partially set.
4. Add fruits, celery, nuts.
5. Chill in 5-cup ring overnight.
notes: Serves 8 to 10
tags: holiday, gelatin mold, cranberry, side dish, vintage

name: Pastel Candied Fruit Peel
ingredient_blocks:
3  large grapefruit, halved
2  packages (3 oz each) JELL-O Brand Strawberry or Lime Flavor Gelatin
2 cup water
1 cup sugar
1  large stick cinnamon
1/2 tsp whole cloves
4 cup sugar
instruction_blocks:
1. Remove grapefruit sections, use as desired. Cover grapefruit shells with water in saucepan; bring to boil. Boil 15 minutes. Drain.
2. Carefully remove any remaining pulp and membrane. Cut peel into thin strips, about 1/4 inch wide. Again place in saucepan and cover with water. Boil 15 minutes, or until tender; drain.
3. Mix gelatin with 2 cups water and 1 cup sugar in heavy skillet. Add prepared peels and spices. Bring to boil; reduce heat and continue cooking, stirring occasionally until peels are translucent and syrup is almost absorbed, 35 to 40 minutes. Remove from heat; lift pieces from skillet with slotted spoon. Sprinkle with 1 cup sugar; toss lightly. Arrange pieces in single layer on waxed paper-lined trays; let dry about 12 hours or overnight. Store in tightly covered container. Makes about 1 pound.
notes: This document also contains recipes for Coffee Butterball Cookies and Easy Coffee Fudge
tags: candy, citrus, grapefruit, Jell-O, holiday

name: Hot Milk Sponge Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup sifted flour
1 tsp baking powder (sifted together with flour three times)
3  eggs
1 cup sugar
2 tsp lemon juice
6 tbsp hot milk
instruction_blocks:
1. Sift flour with baking powder together three times.
2. Beat 3 eggs until very thick and light and nearly white.
3. Add 1 cup sugar gradually, beating constantly.
4. Add 2 teaspoons lemon juice.
5. Fold in flour alternately with 6 tablespoons of hot milk, mixing quickly until batter is smooth.
6. Bake at once in moderate oven at 350° for 45 minutes.
7. Remove from oven and invert pan for 1 hour or until cool.
notes: Some words were slightly difficult to read due to handwriting. The instruction to invert the pan suggests using a tube or angel food cake pan.
tags: cake, sponge cake, vintage, dessert

name: Chicken Crepes Conquistador
summary: Aunt Jemima Crepe Carnival Recipe - savory chicken crepes with a tomato-chili sauce
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup coarsely chopped onion (for sauce)
1 tbsp vegetable oil (for sauce)
12 oz Sacramento Tomato Juice (one can, for sauce)
4 oz chopped green chilies (one can, drained, for sauce)
1/2 tsp garlic salt (for sauce)
1  Aunt Jemima Frozen Crepe Batter (for crepes)
9 oz Hoffman's Super Sharp Pasteurized Process Cheese (shredded, one pkg)
1 1/2 cup chopped cooked chicken
1/2 cup dairy sour cream
1/4 tsp salt
instruction_blocks:
1. For sauce, saute onion in oil. Add tomato juice, chilies and garlic salt; mix well.
2. Bring mixture to a boil; reduce heat. Simmer 15 minutes.
3. Prepare 8 crepes according to package directions.
4. Combine cheese, chicken, sour cream and salt; mix well.
5. Fill each crepe with about 1/4 cup cheese mixture; place in 13 x 9-inch baking dish.
6. Bake in preheated moderate oven (350°F.) about 10 minutes.
7. Spoon warm sauce over crepes to serve.
notes: This recipe card also includes two additional recipes: Fruited Ambrosia Crepes (dessert crepes with fruit cocktail, frozen dessert topping, banana, marshmallows, and coconut) and Hot Ham and Cheese Crepes (with ham, Swiss cheese with caraway, celery, mayonnaise, pickle relish, and mustard).
tags: crepes, chicken, Mexican-inspired, vintage, Aunt Jemima, savory

name: Aunt Jemima Crepe Carnival Recipes
summary: Three crepe recipes from Aunt Jemima: Choco-Cherry Crepes, Tuna Supreme Crepes, and Hot Apple Crepe A' La Mode
notes: This document contains 3 separate recipes. See individual recipes below.
tags: crepes, vintage, Aunt Jemima, dessert, savory

name: Barbacue Sauce
ingredient_blocks:
1/4 cup salad oil
1/2 cup wine vinegar
1/4 cup catsup
1/4 cup chili sauce
1 tbsp lemon juice
1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
1 tsp accent (MSG seasoning)
1 tsp dry mustard
1  small onion (chopped)
1  clove garlic (chopped fine)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a sauce pan put all ingredients, bring to a boil over medium heat.
2. Lower heat and cover pan and simmer for about 30 minutes.
tags: sauce, barbecue, condiment

name: Pignoli Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
4  egg whites
1 cup sugar
2 cup almond paste
1 cup confectioner sugar
pignoli nuts (for sprinkling on top)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix together.
2. Drop by teaspoon fulls on ungreased cookie sheet.
3. Sprinkle pignoli nuts on cookies.
4. Cook until light brown.
notes: Temperature appears to be 325°F or 350°F (handwritten corrections visible). Page 41 of what appears to be a family recipe collection.
tags: cookies, Italian, nuts, almond

name: Quiche Lorraine
summary: Cheese Custard Pie
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup unsifted flour
1 tsp salt
1/2 cup Blue Bonnet Margarine
3-4 tbsp ice water
1/4 cup finely chopped onion
1 tbsp Blue Bonnet Margarine
6  bacon slices (crisply fried and crumbled)
1/4 lb Swiss cheese (diced)
4  eggs (beaten)
2 cup light cream
1/4 tsp white pepper
1/4 tsp ground nutmeg
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine flour and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Cut in 1/2 cup margarine until mixture resembles coarse meal.
2. Gradually stir in ice water until mixture forms a ball.
3. Roll dough out on lightly floured board to fit a 9-inch pie plate. Transfer to plate and shape edge.
4. Saute onion in 1 tablespoon margarine until tender. Set aside to cool.
5. Line prepared crust with crumbled bacon and diced cheese.
6. Combine eggs, cream, remaining salt and spices and sauteed onion. Pour into crust.
7. Bake at 375°F, about 35 minutes, or until knife inserted in center comes out clean.
8. Serve hot.
notes: Recipe appears to be from Blue Bonnet Margarine packaging
tags: quiche, French, breakfast, brunch, cheese, bacon, custard, pie

name: Ritz Apple Pie
summary: A mock apple pie made with Ritz crackers instead of apples
ingredient_blocks:
36  Ritz crackers
2 cup sugar
2 tbsp lemon juice
butter or margarine
pastry for two crust 9-inch pie
2 cup water
2 tsp cream of tartar
grated rind of one lemon
cinnamon
instruction_blocks:
1. Roll out bottom crust of pastry and fit into 9-inch pie plate.
2. Break Ritz crackers coarsely into pastry-lined plate.
3. Combine water, sugar and cream of tartar in sauce pan; boil gently for 15 minutes.
4. Add lemon juice and rind. Cool.
5. Pour syrup over crackers, dot generously with butter or margarine and sprinkle with cinnamon.
6. Cover with top crust. Trim and flute edges together.
7. Cut slits in top crust to let steam escape.
8. Bake in a hot oven until crust is crisp and golden.
9. Serve warm. Cut into 6 to 8 slices.
notes: Bake at 425°F for 30 to 35 minutes
tags: dessert, pie, mock apple pie, vintage recipe

name: Cheese Cake (Sue)
summary: A classic cheesecake with a cookie-style crust, baked and cooled slowly in the oven
ingredient_blocks:
1/4 cup margarine (melted and cooled)
1/2 cup sugar (for crust)
1 tsp vanilla (for crust)
1 1/4 cup flour (for crust)
1/4 tsp baking soda
1/4 tsp salt
3 tbsp cold water
1 lb ricotta
1 lb cream cheese
1 pint sour cream
1 1/2 cup sugar (for filling)
3  eggs (beaten well)
3 tbsp flour (for filling)
3 tbsp corn starch
1 1/2 tsp vanilla (for filling)
1  lemon (juice of 1 whole lemon)
instruction_blocks:
1. Make the crust by combining margarine, 1/2 cup sugar, 1 tsp vanilla, 1 1/4 cup flour, baking soda, salt, and cold water. Spread on a 10 inch spring pan and put aside.
2. In a large bowl place ricotta, cream cheese, sour cream, and 1 1/2 cups sugar in order.
3. Beat 3 eggs well and add to mixture.
4. Add 3 tbsp flour, 3 tbsp corn starch, 1 1/2 tsp vanilla, and juice of 1 whole lemon to the filling mixture.
5. Pour filling over crust in spring pan.
6. Bake at 325 degrees for 1 hour.
7. Turn off oven and let cheesecake sit in oven for 2 hours.
notes: This is a New York style cheesecake with ricotta. The slow cooling in the oven helps prevent cracking.
tags: cheesecake, dessert, baked, family recipe

name: Swedish Meatballs in Burgundy
ingredient_blocks:
3/4 lb chopped chuck
3/4 cup packaged bread crumbs
1  small onion (minced)
3/4 tsp cornstarch
1 dash allspice
1  egg (beaten)
3/4 cup light cream
1/4 cup salad oil (hot, for frying)
3 tbsp flour
2 cup water
1 cup burgundy
2  beef bouillon cubes
1 1/4 tsp salt
1/8 tsp pepper
1 1/2 tsp sugar
bottled gravy sauce (enough to give color)
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine chuck, bread crumbs, onion, cornstarch, allspice, egg, and light cream. Shape into 25 balls.
2. Drop meatballs into 1/4 cup hot salad oil. Brown and remove meatballs from fat. Set aside.
3. Slowly stir flour into hot oil.
4. Add water, burgundy, beef bouillon cubes, salt, pepper, and sugar.
5. Add enough bottled gravy sauce to give it color.
6. Cook stirring until smooth.
7. Add meatballs. Simmer 30 minutes.
tags: Swedish, meatballs, beef, burgundy, wine sauce, comfort food

name: Potatoes, Onions, Mushrooms Au Gratin
ingredient_blocks:
5 lb potatoes (cooked in their jackets, cooled, peeled and cut into 1/2 inch cubes)
1/2 cup butter or margarine
1 lb mushrooms (sliced)
1/2 cup flour
1 quart milk
1 cup cream or evaporated milk
1  condensed consomme (10.5 oz can)
1 lb sharp Cheddar cheese (grated)
1 tbsp seasoned salt
1/4 tsp pepper
1 tsp Worcestershire sauce
2  small white onions (15 oz cans, drained)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cook 5 lbs. potatoes in their jackets. Cool, peel and cut into 1/2 inch cubes.
2. Heat 1/2 cup butter or margarine in a large kettle or pot.
3. Saute 1 lb. sliced mushrooms a few minutes until lightly browned.
4. Stir in 1/2 cup flour.
5. Stir in 1 quart milk, 1 cup cream or evaporated milk and 1 (10.5 oz.) condensed consomme.
6. Add 1 lb. sharp Cheddar cheese, grated, 1 tablespoon seasoned salt, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, and 1 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce.
7. Cook, stirring until smooth and thickened.
8. Remove from heat, add the potatoes and 2 (15 oz.) cans small, white onions, drained, mixing carefully.
9. Spoon mixture into a shallow 4 quart casserole or two smaller ones.
10. Cover casserole with saran or aluminum foil and refrigerate until 1 hour before serving.
11. Bake in a moderate oven (350°F) for about 1 hour, or until golden on top and bubbly.
notes: Can be prepared ahead and refrigerated until 1 hour before serving. Can use one 4 quart casserole or two smaller ones.
tags: casserole, comfort food, make ahead, potatoes, cheese, side dish

name: Spice Cake
summary: Grace Granzio's spice cake recipe with raisins and nuts
ingredient_blocks:
3/4 box raisins
3 1/2 cup water (for boiling raisins)
1/2 lb butter
2 cup sugar
2  eggs
4 cup flour
2 tsp baking soda
1 tsp salt
2 tsp cloves
3 tsp cinnamon
2 cup raisin liquid (reserved from boiling)
1 cup chopped nuts
powdered sugar (for glaze)
vanilla (for glaze)
milk (for glaze)
instruction_blocks:
1. Boil 3/4 box raisins in 3 1/2 cups water, make come to a boil.
2. Drain and keep liquid. Let cool.
3. Mix and cream together 1/2 lb butter and 2 cups sugar.
4. Add 2 eggs.
5. Add 4 cups flour, 2 tsp baking soda, 1 tsp salt, 2 tsp cloves, and 3 tsp cinnamon.
6. Add raisins and 2 cups of the liquid.
7. Add 1 cup chopped nuts.
8. Bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour.
9. Make glaze for top with powdered sugar, vanilla, and milk.
notes: Recipe attributed to Grace Granzio. Glaze ingredients for topping do not have specific measurements - mix to desired consistency.
tags: spice cake, raisins, vintage recipe, family recipe, dessert, cake

name: Anise Biscuits
ingredient_blocks:
3-3 1/2 cup flour (1 pound)
1/2 tsp cream of tartar
1 tsp bicarbonate of soda
3 tbsp anise seed
1/2 lb butter or Crisco (1 stick)
1 cup sugar
2 tbsp anise extract
2  eggs
1/2 cup milk (less)
instruction_blocks:
1. Sift dry ingredients together and set aside.
2. Place butter in large bowl and beat until smooth with sugar.
3. Add eggs and extract. Add dry ingredients alternately with milk.
4. Divide into 3 or 4 equal parts. Make them 1/2 inch low and long and narrow.
5. Bake middle shelf 400° for about 20 minutes.
6. Slice after cooling for a minute or so, place slices side down on cookie sheet and brown for about 5 to 10 minutes.
7. You can brown them without turning them over.
notes: Handwritten annotations suggest using 1/4 stick butter and baking at 375°. The milk amount notation 'less' suggests using slightly less than 1/2 cup. Some handwriting difficult to read.
tags: cookies, biscotti, anise, Italian

name: Stuffed Mushrooms
ingredient_blocks:
3 lb large mushrooms (about 30 mushrooms)
4 tbsp olive oil
4 tbsp butter
1  clove garlic (minced)
1 tbsp flour
1/4 cup dry white wine or dry vermouth (not sweet)
1 tsp salt
1/4 tsp pepper
1  egg yolk (beaten)
1/2 cup bread crumbs
instruction_blocks:
1. Remove stems from mushrooms and chop stems.
2. Heat 1 tbsp oil and 1 tbsp butter in skillet and saute chopped stems with garlic for 3 minutes.
3. Blend flour, wine, salt and pepper and cook over medium heat 5 minutes, stirring.
4. Cool 10 minutes. Mix in egg yolk and stuff mushroom caps with mixture.
5. Put remaining oil in baking dish. Sprinkle with bread crumbs and remaining butter.
6. Bake at 375 for 20 minutes.
7. Serve hot or cold.
notes: Some handwriting unclear - bread crumbs amount appears to be 1/2 cup but could be different. The word after 'bread crumbs and' on page 1 appears to be 'remaining' referring to butter.
tags: appetizer, mushrooms, stuffed, party food

name: Zuppa Inglese (English Soup)
summary: An Italian layered dessert with rum-soaked sponge cake, cream sauce, and meringue topping
ingredient_blocks:
1  sponge cake (cut into 1/2" slices)
1/2 jigger rum
1/2 jigger Creme de Cocoa
1  vanilla cream sauce
1 tbsp candied fruit (chopped fine)
3  egg whites
4 tbsp sugar
instruction_blocks:
1. Soak half the sponge cake slices in rum, the other half in the Creme de Cocoa.
2. Line a 10 inch, heat-resistant plate with the rum-soaked cake slices.
3. Top with alternate layers of vanilla cream sauce, the Creme de Cocoa-soaked slices, ending with a rum layer.
4. Pour cream sauce over entire cake.
5. Beat egg whites and add sugar. Beat again to a stiff meringue.
6. Pile on cake in an attractive design.
7. Bake in 300° oven for 20 minutes.
8. Cool and serve at room temperature.
notes: This is an Italian trifle-style dessert. The name 'English Soup' is a traditional Italian name for this layered cake dessert.
tags: Italian, dessert, cake, rum, meringue, Zuppa Inglese

name: Tottoes
ingredient_blocks:
5 cup flour
3  sticks of butter or oleo (melted)
1/2 doz eggs (6 eggs)
5 tsp baking powder
1 tsp vanilla
1 cup sugar
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix ingredients together.
2. Bake at 400 degrees for about 8 to 10 minutes, with rack placed in middle of oven.
