notes: Title may also be read as 'Tuttles' or 'Tottles' - handwriting is slightly unclear. The '11 min' notation at top may indicate total time or a timing note. This appears to be a cookie recipe. No specific mixing instructions were provided in the original recipe.
tags: cookies, baking, family recipe, vintage

name: Christmas Tree Skirt
summary: Crocheted granny square Christmas tree skirt in red, white, and green
ingredient_blocks:
36  G Granny Squares (1st rnd green center, 2nd rnd red, 3rd rnd white, 4th rnd green)
24  R Granny Squares (1st rnd red center, 2nd rnd white, 3rd rnd red, 4th rnd green)
instruction_blocks:
1. Make 36 G Granny Squares with: 1st round green center, 2nd round red, 3rd round white, 4th round green
2. Make 24 R Granny Squares with: 1st round red center, 2nd round white, 3rd round red, 4th round green
3. Put together following diagram - arrange squares in diamond/tree skirt shape with G squares on outside and R squares creating pattern, leaving one wedge open
4. Crochet all pieces together
5. Single crochet around outside, up seam, around open square and down seam
notes: This is a crochet pattern, not a food recipe. The diagram shows a diamond-shaped layout with alternating G and R squares arranged in rows, with an opening marked for the tree trunk. G = green outer round squares, R = red outer round squares (lowercase 'e' or 'r' between squares indicates joining method).
tags: crochet, Christmas, tree skirt, granny squares, craft pattern

name: Pastiera di Grano (Easter Wheat Pie)
summary: Traditional Italian Easter Wheat Pie made with ricotta and soaked wheat
ingredient_blocks:
1 tsp sugar
1 1/2 lb POLLY-O RICOTTA
1 1/2 cup sugar
6  egg yolks (beaten)
1 dash cinnamon (optional)
1  grated rind of one lemon
1-2 tbsp orange flower water (or 1/2 teaspoon orange extract)
1/4 cup minced glace citron
1/4 cup orange peel
4  egg whites (beaten stiff)
1 tsp vanilla
2 cup sifted flour (for Pasta Frolla pastry)
1/2 cup sugar (for pastry)
1 pinch salt (for pastry)
1/4 cup POLLY-O BUTTER (for pastry)
3  egg yolks (for pastry)
1 tbsp milk (for pastry)
3/4 lb soaked wheat (drained, this is the weight after soaking)
1/2 cup hot scalded milk
1 tsp salt
instruction_blocks:
1. PREPARE WHEAT: Drain soaked wheat, cover with 3 cups of fresh water, add 1 teaspoon salt and boil 1 hour in a covered saucepan, stirring frequently to prevent sticking. Drain water, add 1/2 cup scalded milk, 1 teaspoon sugar, and boil for additional five minutes.
2. Remove from heat, add citron and orange peel; mix and cool. If soaked wheat is not readily available, cover dry wheat and allow to soak for 24 hours. Then prepare soaked wheat as above.
3. PREPARE PASTA FROLLA: Sift flour, salt and sugar into a bowl; cut in butter with a pastry blender or with finger tips, to distribute the butter evenly through the flour. Stir in egg yolks one at a time, mixing with a wooden spoon the flour with hands until dough is manageable and clears the bowl. Work a little milk, if necessary, to hold together. Turn onto a lightly floured board and knead quickly until smooth. Form into a ball and chill for 30 minutes. Then divide into two parts, one larger than the other. Roll large piece on lightly floured board into a large disc, 1/8 inch thick, large enough to lightly line a deep 10-inch pie plate. Butter pie plate and line with pastry, leaving a 1/2-inch overhang. Roll out the smaller piece of dough 1/8 inch thick and cut into 3/4 inch strips, for lattice work topping.
4. PREPARE FILLING: Beat ricotta and sugar together, adding 6 beaten egg yolks, vanilla, cinnamon, lemon rind and orange water, blending well. Stir prepared wheat and fold in egg whites. Turn into pie shell; arrange strips criss-cross over filling, to edge; roll into bottom overhang up over strips at edge and flute heavy. Bake in a preheated moderate 350°F. oven for about 1 hour, or until firm in center and crust nicely browned. Let cool in oven. Serve sprinkled with confectioners' sugar.
notes: VARIATIONS: Substitute Pearl Tapioca for wheat. Follow soaking directions on Pearl Tapioca package; and then drain, add 1 pint scalded milk, pinch of salt, teaspoon of sugar and peel of 1 lemon. Cook over a moderate heat until Tapioca has thoroughly absorbed the milk. Remove lemon peel, turn into a bowl, add citron and orange peel, mix and allow to cool. Then proceed with preparation of Pasta Frolla. Soaked wheat may be substituted with Wheat Germ, as per following recipe: 1 1/2 pounds POLLY-O RICOTTA, 1 1/2 fruit (1-3 ounce jar mixed citron), 1 1/4 tablespoons orange flower water or 1 tablespoon grated orange rind and 1 teaspoon vanilla, 6 egg whites beaten stiff, 1 3/4 cups sugar, 2 1/2 cups wheat germ drained through cheesecloth, 2 cups scalded milk, 6 egg yolks beaten.
tags: Italian, Easter, holiday, dessert, pie, ricotta, traditional

name: Rum Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup chopped nuts (pecans)
1 pkg Duncan Hines butter recipe golden cake mix
1 pkg vanilla instant pudding mix
1/2 cup light rum
1/2 cup water
1/2 cup vegetable cooking oil
4  eggs
1 cup sugar (for glaze)
1 stick butter (for glaze)
1/4 cup light rum (for glaze)
1/4 cup water (for glaze)
instruction_blocks:
1. Grease and flour bundt or tube pan (10x4).
2. Crumble nuts in bottom of pan.
3. In large bowl put cake and pudding mixes. Add rum, water, oil and eggs.
4. Mix 2 min.
5. Bake at 325 deg. for 50-60 min.
6. Remove cake from oven and pour on hot rum glaze while cake is still in pan.
7. Don't be alarmed if glaze seems to affect the cake. It will shrivel up. It is helping cake to settle.
8. For glaze: Mix sugar, butter, rum and water and boil for 2-3 min. Pour over hot cake.
9. Let stand at least 30 min. before taking out of pan onto cake plate.
notes: Will keep for weeks in refrigerator.
tags: dessert, cake, rum, bundt cake

name: Proscuitto Pie
summary: Italian savory pie with prosciutto and ricotta filling
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup flour (for dough)
1 pinch salt (for dough)
3  eggs (save 1 yolk, for dough)
1 tbsp crisco (for dough)
2 tbsp baking powder (for dough)
2/3 cup lukewarm water (for dough)
2 lb ricotta (1 can, for filling)
pepper (for filling, salt crossed out)
3/4 cup grated cheese (for filling)
1/4 cup parsley (for filling)
4  eggs (for filling)
1/2 lb proscuitto (diced, for filling)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix flour, salt, baking powder and crisco.
2. Make hole, place eggs, use water as needed.
3. Knead well.
4. Place between 2 dinner dishes for 15 to 20 minutes.
5. Fill with proscuitto pie filling.
6. Bake at 350 degrees for 1 to 1½ hours on cookie sheet.
notes: Original recipe had temperature crossed out from 375 to 350. Handwritten notes indicate: use 2 dinner dishes for resting dough, bake on cookie sheet. Salt was crossed out from the filling ingredients. Proscuitto should be diced (written as 'piced').
tags: Italian, savory pie, Easter, prosciutto, ricotta

name: Sweet Dough for Walnut Rolls or Poppyseed Rolls
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup milk (scalded)
3/4 cup sugar
1 1/2 tsp salt
2  eggs (beaten)
2 pkg dry yeast
6 cup flour
1/3 cup oleo (softened)
butter (for spreading)
filling (walnut or poppyseed)
instruction_blocks:
1. Scald 2 cups milk with 3/4 cups sugar, 1 1/2 tsp salt - cool.
2. Add 2 eggs beaten and 2 pkg dry yeast.
3. In large bowl put 6 cups flour and make well.
4. Add above mixture and knead lightly.
5. Add 1/3 cup oleo (softened) and knead till smooth.
6. Let rise 3/4 hour and punch down and let rise again another 3/4 hour.
7. Divide in six and roll out.
8. Spread butter and add filling.
notes: Be sure not to add too much flour when kneading. If too sticky add a little flour. The word 'coo' at end of first line appears to be 'cool' cut off.
tags: sweet dough, rolls, walnut rolls, poppyseed rolls, yeast bread, Eastern European

name: Skillet Chicken Mozzarella
ingredient_blocks:
1  medium green pepper
1  small onion
2  medium zucchini (1 lb)
2  large chicken breasts (skinned & boned)
2 tbsp all-purpose flour
1/4 tsp salt
1/8 tsp pepper
3/4  15 oz jar spaghetti sauce (1 cup)
1/2 tsp sugar
1/2  8 oz pkg mozzarella (sliced)
2 tbsp oil
instruction_blocks:
1. Cut green pepper into bite-size pieces. Slice onion. Cut zucchini lengthwise into quarters; cut each quarter into 1/4" chunks. Set vegetables aside.
2. Cut each breast in half. With mallet, pound each piece to 1/4" thickness. On waxed paper combine flour, salt & pepper; coat chicken with mixture.
3. In 10" skillet, heat oil over medium-high heat; cook chicken until brown on both sides. Remove chicken. Using the drippings cook onion & pepper until crisp, stirring frequently.
4. Return chicken to skillet, add sauce, sugar & zucchini; over high heat, heat to boiling. Reduce heat, cover & simmer 15 minutes until chicken & vegetables are tender. Top with mozzarella, cover & simmer about 2 minutes.
tags: chicken, Italian, skillet, one-pan, cheese

name: Fluffy Dilled Carrots and Potatoes
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 cup sliced carrots
3/4 cup peeled and sliced potato or parsnip
1-2 tbsp skim milk
2 tsp margarine
1/4 tsp dried dillweed
1/8-1/4 tsp onion powder
1 dash pepper
instruction_blocks:
1. In a saucepan bring 2 ins of water to boiling. Add carrot and potato. Return to boiling, reduce heat. Cover and simmer about 25 min or till very tender.
2. Drain. With an electric mixer on low speed, or potato masher, add milk, margarine, salt, onion powder, and pepper. Beat or mash till nearly smooth.
notes: Recipe page also contains a second recipe for 'Vegetable and Pasta' which includes corkscrew pasta, broccoli florets, cauliflowerets, frozen artichoke halves, sliced carrots, sliced green onions, and reduced calorie Italian dressing. Some handwriting was difficult to read.
tags: side dish, vegetables, mashed, comfort food

name: Oven Fried Vegetables
ingredient_blocks:
1/4 cup fine dry bread crumbs
1 tbsp grated Parmesan cheese
1/8 tsp paprika
2 cup zucchini slices, onion rings, or cauliflower florets (1/4 inch thick)
2 tbsp reduced calorie Italian salad dressing
instruction_blocks:
1. Spray a cold baking sheet with non-stick spray coating. Set aside.
2. In a 9 inch pie plate stir together bread crumbs, Parmesan cheese and paprika till well mixed.
3. In medium mixing bowl, place zucchini, onion rings or cauliflower. Drizzle vegetables with salad dressing. Toss till coated.
4. Then roll vegetables in crumb mixture till coated. Place the coated vegetables in a single layer on the prepared baking sheet.
5. Bake vegetables in a 450 oven for 9 to 11 min. or till golden.
notes: Makes 4 (1/2 cup) servings
tags: vegetables, oven-fried, healthy, low-calorie, side dish

name: Vegetable and Pasta
ingredient_blocks:
3/4 cup corkscrew pasta
1 cup broccoli flowerets
1 cup cauliflowerets
1  9 oz pkg frozen artichoke (thawed and halves)
1/2 cup thinly sliced carrots
1/4 cup sliced green onions
1/2 cup reduced calorie Italian dressing
instruction_blocks:
1. Cook pasta according to pkg.
2. Add broccoli and cauliflowerets to boiling pasta for the last min of cooking.
3. Drain. Rinse with cold water. Drain well.
4. In large mixing bowl, combine pasta mixture, artichoke halves, carrots & green onions.
5. Add the Italian dressing, toss to coat.
6. Cover and refrigerate for 2 to 24 hrs.
notes: Some handwriting was difficult to read, particularly crossed out sections. This appears to be a cold pasta salad.
tags: pasta salad, vegetable, cold salad, make ahead

name: Chocolate Chip Cheesecake Brownies
ingredient_blocks:
12 oz Nestle's chocolate chips (pkg)
2  cream cheese (8 oz pkgs, softened)
3/4 cup sugar
2  eggs
1 cup pecans (chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. Follow directions on back of Nestle's pkg to make chocolate chip cookies.
2. Divide chocolate chip batter in half and spread one half in bottom of lightly greased 13x9 baking pan.
3. Combine cream cheese with sugar and eggs in mixer or food processor and blend until very creamy.
4. Pour cream cheese batter on top of chocolate chip batter in sprinkle with nuts.
5. Spread remaining chocolate chip batter carefully over cheese filling.
6. Bake 350° oven for 45 min.
7. Cut into squares.
notes: Yields 36 squares
tags: brownies, cheesecake, chocolate chip, dessert, bars

name: Beef Barley Soup
ingredient_blocks:
3/4 lb ground beef
1/3 cup barley
1 tbsp salt
3  celery stalks (diced)
16 oz canned tomatoes (with juice)
1 tsp pepper
1  medium onion
1 1/2 quarts water
1 tbsp oregano
3  carrots (sliced)
1 tsp A-1 sauce
2 cans beef bouillon soup
grated cheese (for serving)
1 cup elbow macaroni
instruction_blocks:
1. Brown meat and onions. Drain fat.
2. Add barley, salt, tomatoes, pepper, carrots, A-1 sauce and beef soup.
3. Simmer 1 hour or till carrots are cooked.
4. Boil elbow macaroni then add to soup and serve.
5. Sprinkle with grated cheese.
tags: soup, beef, comfort food, hearty

name: Holiday Squares
ingredient_blocks:
2  large eggs
1/3 cup milk
2 tbsp butter (melted and cooled)
1/3 cup unbleached all purpose flour
1 tsp baking powder
10 oz frozen chopped spinach (thawed and squeezed dry)
3  tiny scallions (finely chopped (3 tbs))
1/3 lb monterey jack cheese (shredded med fine)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a medium bowl with an electric beater, scraping the sides of the bowl often, beat together until smooth the eggs, milk, butter, flour, baking powder.
2. Thoroughly stir in the spinach, scallion and cheese.
3. Pour into buttered 8x8 by 2 in cake smooth top.
4. Bake in a preheated 350 degree oven until browned, 35 min.
5. Let stand about 10 min, cut in small squares.
6. Remove with metal spatula.
notes: Use an 8x8x2 inch baking pan, buttered. Some handwriting was difficult to read but best interpretation provided.
tags: appetizer, holiday, spinach, cheese, vegetarian

name: Quiche Lorraine
summary: A cheese pie appetizer suitable for Christmas party entertaining
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 cup grated Swiss cheese
8  slices fried bacon, crumbled
1  9-inch unbaked pie shell
3  eggs
1 cup heavy cream
1/2 cup milk
1/4 tsp salt
1/4 tsp pepper
1/2 tsp powdered mustard
dash Cayenne pepper
instruction_blocks:
1. Sprinkle grated cheese and bacon into pie shell.
2. Beat remaining ingredients together and pour over cheese.
3. Bake in oven pre-heated to 375 degrees or until firm and browned.
4. Let cool, slice like pie and serve.
notes: From a newspaper article by Debby Wood about Christmas party entertaining with alcohol and non-alcoholic beverages. Article also includes recipes for Chicken Liver Pate, Winning Tea Punch, Sour Cream Dip, Pickled Eggs, and Sausage Balls.
tags: appetizer, Christmas, party food, cheese, bacon, quiche

name: Sour Cream Dip
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup sour cream
1/2 cup blue cheese
1/4 lb minced onion (dry)
1/4 tsp salt
1 tsp lemon juice
1 tbsp horseradish
1  dash Worcestershire sauce
1  dry parsley to taste
1  paprika to garnish
instruction_blocks: 1. Combine all ingredients.
notes: Makes 1 gallon or 25 five-ounce servings. This appears to be from a newspaper clipping by Debby Wood with multiple party recipes including Winning Tea Punch, Chicken Liver Pate, Quiche Lorraine, Sausage Balls, and Pickled Eggs.
tags: dip, appetizer, party food, sour cream

name: Golden Freeze
summary: A frozen orange dessert made with ice cubes in a food processor
ingredient_blocks:
1  can (6 ounces or 168 g) frozen orange juice concentrate
2 cup milk (500 ml)
1/2 cup sugar (125 ml)
1 tsp vanilla (5 ml)
8-10  ice cubes
instruction_blocks:
1. Assemble blender. Put all ingredients except ice cubes into blender container. Cover and process at STIR. Increase speed to FRAPPE (LIQUEFY) and drop ice cubes through feeder cap until ice is completely chopped and mixture is smooth and thick.
2. Yield: 1 quart (1 liter)
tags: frozen dessert, orange, beverage, food processor

name: Fresh Apple Bread Pudding
ingredient_blocks:
3  slices fresh white bread
2 tbsp milk
1 cup butter (250 ml)
1 tsp sugar (60 ml)
1/2  Dash salt (2 ml cinnamon)
2  thin strips lemon peel
2  egg yolks (2" x 1/2" (5 x 1 cm))
1 1/2 cup apple pieces (375 ml, pared tart)
1/2 cup raisins
4 tbsp walnuts (60 ml sugar)
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Coarsely blend crumb bread and empty into large mixer bowl.
3. Put butter, milk, sugar, cinnamon, salt, lemon peel and egg yolks into blender container, cover and process at MIX until smooth.
4. Stop blender, add apple cycles and GRATE. Pour over bread crumbs and add raisins.
5. Assemble Kitchen Center Mixer. Beat egg whites in small mixer bowl at #11 (#9) until soft peaks form, then add sugar gradually while continuing to beat on whites.
6. Fold pudding mixture at #1 and turn into an ungreased 1 1/2-quart (1.50 liter) casserole.
7. Bake 40-50 minutes, until a knife inserted into center comes out clean.
8. Cool slightly before serving with vanilla sauce or cream.
notes: This is a recipe from an 'Ultimate in Food Processing' booklet for a Kitchen Center appliance.
tags: dessert, bread pudding, apple, vintage

name: Date & Nut Loaf
ingredient_blocks:
2 pkg pitted dates
3 cup boiling water
2 tsp baking soda
3 cup sugar
3 tbsp butter
2  eggs
4 1/2 cup flour
1 tsp baking powder
1 lb walnuts
1 tbsp vanilla
instruction_blocks:
1. Soak dates in boiling water and baking soda. Let stand. Mix following in large bowl.
2. Cream sugar with butter, then add eggs, flour and baking powder. Mix well.
3. Take wet mixture of dates and add to dry ingredients, while stirring add walnuts.
4. Then a good tablespoon vanilla.
5. Use large pan and 1 small one. Grease pan and bake in oven at 350 for 1 hour or 1 1/2 hours.
notes: Recipe makes enough batter for one large pan and one small pan. Baking time varies between 1 hour to 1.5 hours depending on pan size.
tags: date bread, nut bread, quick bread, dessert, vintage recipe

name: Tomato Soup Cake
summary: Good
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup butter (cream with sugar)
1 cup sugar
2 cup flour (sifted)
1 tsp cloves
1/2 tsp nutmeg
1/2 tsp cinnamon
1 cup raisins
1 cup nuts
1 can condensed tomato soup
1 tsp baking soda (dissolved in 1/2 cup warm water)
1/2 tsp salt
1/2 tsp allspice
1 pkg cream cheese (8 oz, for frosting)
1 tsp vanilla (for frosting)
1/2 pkg confectioners sugar (for frosting)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream together 1/2 cup butter and 1 cup sugar.
2. Sift together 2 cups flour, 1 teaspoon cloves, 1/2 teaspoon nutmeg, 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/2 teaspoon allspice.
3. Add 1 cup raisins and 1 cup nuts.
4. Add 1 can condensed tomato soup.
5. Dissolve 1 teaspoon baking soda in 1/2 cup warm water and add to mixture.
6. Bake in moderate oven.
7. When cool, frost with cream cheese frosting.
8. For frosting: Mix 8 oz cream cheese, 1 teaspoon vanilla, and 1/2 package confectioners sugar.
notes: Recipe marked as 'Good'. Moderate oven typically means 350°F (175°C). Some handwriting was slightly unclear but ingredients appear standard for this classic Depression-era cake.
tags: cake, vintage, tomato soup cake, Depression era, cream cheese frosting, spice cake

name: Spinach, Sausage, Mozzarella & Ham
summary: A filled bread or pastry with spinach, Italian sausage, ham, and mozzarella cheese
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup all-purpose flour (for the dough)
1  egg (at room temperature, for the dough)
2 tbsp unsalted butter (melted, for the dough)
pinch coarse salt (for the dough)
1 oz compressed yeast (or 2 pkg active dry yeast)
1/2 cup lukewarm water (plus 1 to 2 tablespoons additional water if necessary)
corn oil (for oiling pan)
2 lb fresh spinach (or 2 pkg (each 10 oz) frozen spinach, for filling)
3 tbsp unsalted butter (for filling)
coarse salt and freshly ground black pepper (for filling)
7 oz boiled ham (thinly sliced)
8 oz mozzarella cheese (coarsely grated)
2  hot Italian sausages (skinned, crumbled and cooked in 2 tablespoons water)
1  egg (lightly beaten for painting crust)
instruction_blocks:
1. Dough: Combine the flour, egg yolk, butter and a pinch of salt in a bowl. Dissolve the yeast in 1/2 cup warm water, and add to the other dough ingredients.
2. Stir with a wooden spoon until well mixed, adding 1 to 2 tbls water, if necessary to obtain a soft dough.
3. Turn out on a marble or wooden surface, and knead for a few minutes, until the dough is smooth and elastic.
4. Place in a floured bowl, cover with a tea cloth and put in a warm, draft free place until the dough doubles in size between 1 and 2 hours.
5. After the dough has doubled, remove from bowl, and knead for a minute or two. Return to bowl, and let rise a second time until double in bulk, 1 to 1.5 hours.
6. Meanwhile preheat oven to 375°F.
7. Filling: Sauté the spinach and salt and cook about 4 minutes. Drain and squeeze. Melt butter in a skillet and add the spinach, cook over med. heat for 3 min. Add season with salt and pepper.
8. Oil the bottom of a loaf pan 13 by 9 inches. Press the dough with 1/2 inch (not over all the way).
9. Spread at 3 inches around the center edge of the dough, place the filling of the spinach and half the remaining slices of ham.
10. Sprinkle on the mozzarella as follows: Arrange half the ham slices over top of the ham crust. Arrange half the grated mozzarella, then place half the spinach on top. Take all of the sausage meat and any juices of the mozzarella and place over the spinach.
11. Add the spinach and ham remaining. Now press the dough over the filling to seal.
12. Bake 40 to 50 minutes. After 30 minutes remove from oven and paint top crust with beaten egg, and finish baking.
notes: Some portions of the handwriting on page 2 were difficult to read as the page appears to be written in reverse/mirror image. The assembly instructions may need interpretation. Total baking time is 40-50 minutes at 375°F, with an egg wash applied after the first 30 minutes.
tags: Italian, stuffed bread, spinach, sausage, ham, cheese, yeast bread

name: Zucchini Salad
summary: Food World Presents A Recipe For Your Pleasure
ingredient_blocks:
3  medium zucchini (thinly sliced)
2/3 cup cider vinegar
1/2 cup green pepper (shredded)
1/2 cup celery (shredded)
1/2 cup yellow onion (shredded)
1/2 cup sugar
1/3 cup oil
1/4 cup burgundy
2 tbsp red wine vinegar
1 tsp salt
1/2 tsp black pepper (freshly ground)
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine all ingredients in large bowl and mix thoroughly.
2. Cover and refrigerate at least six hours before serving.
notes: Salad serves six. Keeps up to two weeks.
tags: salad, zucchini, make ahead, vegetable, marinated

name: Pine Nut Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
3 1/2 lb almond paste
1 1/2 lb confectioners sugar
1 1/2 tsp baking powder
6  egg whites (stiff beaten)
pine nuts (for rolling)
instruction_blocks:
1. Make little balls and roll in nuts
2. Bake at 375 until golden brown
3. Keep in refrigerator overnight
notes: Additional notes at bottom of recipe card appear to reference variations or another recipe with 2 cups almond paste, granulated sugar, 3 egg whites beaten, 15 min at 325, 3/4 sugar, 3/4 BP (baking powder). Some text is crossed out. Attribution appears to be 'Mrs. A. Bodine' (partially crossed out).
tags: cookies, Italian, pine nuts, almond

name: Sausage Pie
ingredient_blocks:
3 lb pot cheese
2 lb sausage
4-6  eggs (depends on size)
tapioca
pie crust (note in margin says 3 bot pie crust for)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cook sausage and drain off fat. Cool.
2. Mix pot cheese, eggs and add cool sausage.
3. Roll pie crust and line pan. Let it go over side of pan.
4. Sprinkle tapioca at bottom of pie crust.
5. Then add filling and add other crust on top.
6. Roll first crust and 2 crust to so filling will not come out.
7. Prick and put in 350° oven and cook until golden brown.
notes: Pot cheese is similar to dry cottage cheese or farmer's cheese. The tapioca helps absorb moisture from the filling.
tags: Italian, savory pie, Easter, breakfast

name: Rice Pudding
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup Carolina Rice
1 qt milk
3  eggs (beat all together with sugar, salt, vanilla, and cornstarch)
1 cup sugar (beat all together)
1/4 tsp salt (beat all together)
1 tsp vanilla (beat all together)
2 tbsp corn starch (beat all together)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cook rice in 2 quarts water.
2. Do not throw water away, add milk until it starts to boil.
3. Then add other ingredients that have been mixed together.
4. Let it come to a full boil and take off stove.
5. Stir once or twice more and serve cold.
notes: The ingredients marked 1 and 2 on the recipe indicate groupings - group 2 (eggs, sugar, salt, vanilla, cornstarch) should be beaten all together before adding to the rice mixture.
tags: dessert, pudding, rice, comfort food, cold dessert

name: Key Lime Pie
ingredient_blocks:
1 can condensed milk
4  egg yolks
1/2 cup lime juice
1  egg white (for folding into mixture)
3  egg whites (for meringue)
6 tbsp sugar (for meringue)
1/2 tsp cream of tartar
1  baked pie shell
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat 1 egg white stiff
2. Fold into above mixture (condensed milk, egg yolks, and lime juice)
3. Beat 3 egg whites and gradually add 6 tbsp sugar and 1/2 tsp cream of tartar
4. Pour into baked pie shell and top with meringue
5. Bake at 350°F till egg whites are golden brown
notes: The filling appears to be made by combining condensed milk, egg yolks, and lime juice before folding in the beaten egg white. Meringue topping is made separately.
tags: pie, key lime, dessert, meringue

name: Keylime Pie
ingredient_blocks:
1 can Eagle Sweeten Condensed Milk
1/2 cup lime juice
1 can crushed pineapple (drain overnight)
few drops green food color
1  graham cracker crust
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix altogether (except crust) pour in pie shell
2. Refrigerate about 4 hrs
3. Decorate with slivers of lime + cool Whip or any thing you desire for decoration
notes: This is a no-bake key lime pie variation with pineapple. Cool Whip and lime slivers suggested for decoration.
tags: pie, no-bake, dessert, key lime, refrigerator pie

name: Irish Soda Bread
ingredient_blocks:
4 cup flour
4 tsp baking powder
1 cup sugar
1/4 cup margarine (or 1/2 cup)
1 cup milk
2  eggs (slightly beaten)
1/2 tsp salt
1 tsp caraway seeds
1 cup raisins (seedless)
1 cup currants (optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. Sift flour, baking soda, sugar and salt together.
2. Sprinkle a little flour over raisins and currants then mix well into flour mixture.
3. Cut in margarine with knife or with your hands, crumble well.
4. Add beaten eggs and milk, mix all ingredients together.
5. Turn on floured board, mold round shape to fit into baking mold bowl.
6. Make sure to grease bowl well.
7. Bake at 350° for about 1 hr. or more.
notes: Recipe mentions both baking powder in ingredients and baking soda in instructions - this may be an inconsistency in the original handwritten recipe. Some handwriting was difficult to read, particularly around amounts.
tags: Irish, bread, soda bread, baked goods, traditional

name: Sausage Pie
summary: Katie's Sausage Pie - an Italian-style savory meat and cheese pie
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup shortening
2  beaten eggs (for crust)
2 cup flour
1/2 tsp salt
1 tsp black pepper
1/2 lb prosciutto (for filling)
1/2 lb salami (for filling)
pepperoni slices (for filling)
1 doz hard boiled eggs (cut up, for filling)
1/2 cup grated cheese (for filling)
1 lb pot cheese (for filling)
1/2 tsp black pepper (for filling)
8-10  beaten eggs (for filling)
instruction_blocks:
1. Sift dry ingredients. Crumble shortening in flour mixture.
2. Add 2 eggs, add water if not moist enough.
3. Roll out not too thick. Place lining in glass baking dish.
4. For filling: Beat together 1/2 lb prosciutto, 1/2 lb salami, pepperoni slices, 1 dozen hard boiled eggs (cut up), 1/2 cup grated cheese, 1 lb pot cheese, and 1/2 tsp black pepper.
5. Add 8 to 10 beaten eggs. Taste for salt.
6. Pour all ingredients into glass dish and bake at 350° for 1 hour.
notes: This is Katie's recipe. Pot cheese is similar to ricotta. Some handwriting was difficult to read but best interpretation was made.
tags: Italian, savory pie, Easter, meat pie, comfort food

name: Chicken Cordon Bleu
ingredient_blocks:
1/2-1 lb medium egg noodles
3  chicken breasts (split, boned, skinned and pounded)
6 slices swiss cheese
6 slices cooked ham
6 tbsp butter
1/2 lb fresh mushrooms (sliced)
1  medium onion (sliced)
3 tbsp flour
2 cup milk
1/3 cup brandy
instruction_blocks:
1. Top each half chicken breast with a piece of cheese, then ham; roll up and secure with skewers or toothpicks.
2. In large skillet, melt butter and brown chicken, remove from pan.
3. Cook mushrooms and onion until tender; blend in flour, gradually add milk and brandy, stirring constantly, until thickened.
4. Return chicken to pan and simmer covered, stirring occasionally, 20 minutes or until tender.
5. Meanwhile cook noodles according to package directions; drain.
6. Turn noodles out onto serving platter and arrange chicken and sauce on top.
tags: chicken, French, comfort food, dinner, entertaining

name: Peach Cobbler
ingredient_blocks:
1 box yellow cake mix
1 can sliced peaches
1 cup walnuts
1 cup coconut
1/2 cup melted butter or oleo
instruction_blocks:
1. Put peaches on bottom of pan
2. Cover with yellow cake mix
3. Sprinkle nuts over cake mix
4. Then coconut, last drizzle butter or marg over it
5. Bake 40 min at 350°F
6. Check on it half way, if it browns cover with foil loosely
notes: Recipe written on The Rye Town Hilton Inn stationery (Penfields - The Den - The Tulip Tree). Some handwriting difficult to read - 'oleo' may also read as 'or oleo' indicating butter substitute option.
tags: dessert, cobbler, peach, dump cake, easy

name: Cheese Spread
summary: Appetizer cheese spread with beer and nuts
ingredient_blocks:
12 oz cheddar cheese (softened)
16 oz cream cheese (softened)
1/3 cup beer (warm)
2 tbsp parsley (chopped)
1 tsp paprika
instruction_blocks:
1. Soften cheeses in microwave oven.
2. Combine cheeses, beer, parsley and paprika in food processor.
3. Process until creamy.
4. Place in serving dishes or roll in nuts.
5. Remove from refrigerator 30 minutes before serving.
notes: Can be rolled in nuts for presentation. Recipe appears to be from a digital retrieval system.
tags: appetizer, cheese, spread, party food

name: Lina's Cookies
summary: Anise or vanilla flavored cookies from The Rye Town Hilton Inn
ingredient_blocks:
1 stick margarine (melted)
1/2 cup sugar
3  eggs
2 3/4 cup flour
5 tsp baking powder
1/2 bottle anise or vanilla extract
instruction_blocks:
1. Melt margarine
2. Mix eggs, sugar, and margarine well
3. Add vanilla and half bottle of anise or vanilla
4. Then add baking powder
5. Then add flour
6. Bake at 375°F for 8 to 10 minutes
notes: Recipe written on The Rye Town Hilton Inn stationery (Penfields - The Den - The Tulip Tree). Name 'Lina' written at top, possibly the recipe source.
tags: cookies, anise, vanilla, Italian

name: Bordelaise Sauce
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp butter
2 tbsp shallots (fine minced)
3/4 cup dry red wine
1 1/2 cup brown sauce or canned beef gravy
2 tbsp lemon juice
2 tbsp parsley (minced)
salt (to taste)
cayenne pepper (to taste)
3/4 cup sliced mushrooms (cooked in little butter)
instruction_blocks:
1. Melt the butter in a saucepan and cook the shallots until they are transparent.
2. Add the wine and simmer until reduced one half.
3. Add the remaining ingredients and heat thoroughly.
notes: Second recipe on page 2 is for Mustard Sauce: 2 tbsp butter, 2 tbsp flour, 1 cup milk, salt & freshly ground pepper, nutmeg optional, 1 tsp dry mustard added to the flour.
tags: sauce, French, steak sauce, wine sauce

name: Cheese Cake No Crust
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb cottage cheese (small curds)
1 lb cream cheese
1 1/2 cup sugar
4  eggs (beaten light)
1 1/2 tbsp lemon juice
1 tsp vanilla
3 tbsp cornstarch
3 tbsp flour
1/4 lb butter (melted)
1 pint sour cream
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream the cheese in a bowl and gradually add the sugar, stirring.
2. Add the eggs; beat well.
3. Stir in the lemon juice, vanilla and mixed cornstarch and flour, add the melted butter and last, the sour cream.
4. Pour into a greased spring form pan (9 in.).
5. Bake in a moderately slow oven until firm.
6. Turn off oven; let the cake remain in the oven two hours longer.
7. Serve cold.
notes: This cheese cake has no crust. Slow oven suggested at 300 deg. Turn oven off at end of one hour. If oven is too hot, cake over cooks. Also a well insulated oven takes longer to cool off. I use a teaspoon to open the oven just a crack at cooling off period. Baking time: 1 hour. Standing time: 2 hours. Oven temp: 325 deg.
tags: cheesecake, dessert, no crust, baked

name: Pistachio Pudding Salad
summary: A fluffy dessert salad made with pistachio pudding, pineapple, cool whip and marshmallows
ingredient_blocks:
1 pkg pistachio pudding (3 1/2 oz)
1  crushed pineapple (16 oz)
1  cool whip (8 oz)
1 cup mini marshmallows
instruction_blocks:
1. Put pineapple in mixing bowl (electric mixer)
2. Sprinkle pudding from box on top of pineapple
3. Add cool whip and then marshmallows
notes: Note: I put on 'slow', just to mix ingredients. Recipe from Ann R. Heatley on Conoco letterhead.
tags: dessert salad, no-bake, pistachio, retro

name: Lentil Soup with Frankfurters
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 cup lentils
6 cup cold water
2  stalks celery
1  medium onion
2  small carrots
1 1/2 cup cooked tomatoes
2 tbsp bacon fat
3  frankfurters
salt (to taste)
pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cook lentils for about 45 minutes
2. Add vegetables and simmer 30 minutes
notes: Recipe found on personal stationery of G. J. Makar, White Plains, N.Y. Some handwriting was difficult to read - 'tbs' interpreted as tablespoons for bacon fat.
tags: soup, lentils, comfort food, frankfurters

name: French Bread
summary: Gourmet French bread recipe
ingredient_blocks:
1  package granular yeast or one cake compressed yeast (granular 110°-115°, compressed 80°-86°)
1/4 cup water (lukewarm)
1/2 cup milk (scalded)
1 tbsp sugar
1 tbsp soft butter
1 1/2 tsp salt
1 1/4 cup lukewarm water
5 cup flour (sifted, approximately)
cornmeal (for sprinkling on baking sheet)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a warm bowl dissolve one package granular yeast or one cake compressed yeast in 1/4 cup water (granular 110°-115°, compressed 80°-86°).
2. Scald 1/2 cup milk, add one tablespoon each of sugar and soft butter, and 1 1/2 teaspoons salt and let mixture cool to lukewarm.
3. Add 1 1/4 cups lukewarm water and stir in the dissolved yeast.
4. Mix in gradually about five cups sifted flour, or enough to make a dough that is light but does not stick to the hands.
5. Turn the dough out on a lightly floured board and let rest 10 minutes.
6. Flour the hands lightly and knead the dough for 8 to 10 minutes, or until it is smooth, evenly textured and elastic.
7. Put the dough in a lightly buttered bowl and turn it until the entire surface is coated.
8. Cover the dough with a dry towel and let it rise in a warm place until it doubles in bulk, about 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
9. Press two fingertips into the dough. If the imprint of the fingertips remains, the dough has risen enough.
10. Punch down the dough, work it lightly, cover it, and let it rise until it doubles in bulk, about 40 to 50 minutes.
11. Turn the dough out on a smooth surface and knead it for a minute.
12. Halve the dough and shape each half into a long, cylindrical loaf.
13. Put the loaves on a buttered baking sheet sprinkled with cornmeal, cover them, and let them rise in a warm place until they double in bulk, about 1 hour.
14. With sharp pointed scissors, cut 1/4-inch-deep diagonal slits in the top of the loaves when they are half risen.
15. Before baking, brush the surfaces with dorure.
16. Bake the loaves in a hot oven (400°F) for 40 to 50 minutes, or until they test done and the crusts are golden brown.
notes: For a crustier loaf, put a large flat pan filled with boiling water on the rack below the loaves during the baking period. For a sweeter loaf, add 1 tablespoon honey to the basic mixture. Dorure: Beat 1 egg yolk with 1 tablespoon milk or water. For glazing breads and pastries.
tags: bread, French, yeast bread, Gourmet

name: French Bread
summary: From Gourmet magazine
ingredient_blocks:
1  package granular yeast or one cake compressed yeast
1/4 cup water (granular 110-115°, compressed 80-86°)
1/2 cup milk (scalded)
1 tbsp sugar
1 tbsp soft butter
1 1/2 tsp salt
1 1/4 cup lukewarm water
5 cup flour (sifted, approximately)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a warm bowl dissolve one package granular yeast or one cake compressed yeast in 1/4 cup water (granular 110-115°, compressed 80-86°).
2. Scald 1/2 cup milk, add one tablespoon each of sugar and soft butter, and 1 1/2 teaspoons salt and let mixture cool to lukewarm.
3. Add 1 1/4 cups lukewarm water and stir in the dissolved yeast.
4. Mix in gradually about five cups sifted flour, or enough to make a dough that is light but does not stick to the hands.
5. Turn the dough out on a lightly floured board and let rest 10 minutes.
6. Flour the hands lightly and knead the dough for 8 to 10 minutes, or until it is smooth, evenly textured and elastic.
7. Put the dough in a lightly buttered bowl and turn it until the entire surface is coated.
8. Cover the dough with a dry towel and let it rise in a warm place until it doubles in bulk, about 1 1/2 to 2 hours. Press two fingertips into the dough. If the imprint of the fingertips remains, the dough has risen enough.

name: Ryan's Irish Cream
ingredient_blocks:
3  eggs
1 can sweetened condensed milk (8 oz)
1 tsp vanilla
3 tbsp chocolate syrup (regular Hershey's syrup will do)
1 cup half & half
1 cup rye whiskey
instruction_blocks:
1. Blend with mixer or blender
2. Refrigerate
notes: P.S. The longer it sets the better it gets. Will keep 2-3 weeks in refrigerator.
tags: beverage, Irish cream, alcohol, homemade liqueur

name: Cherry Pie Supreme
summary: Two all-time American favorites—cherry pie and cheesecake—are subtly mingled in this applause-winning recipe. The result is a delightfully different dessert that is as elegant and exciting as it is easy-to-make.
ingredient_blocks:
1  9-inch unbaked pie shell
1 can cherry pie filling (1 lb, 5 oz)
4 pkg soft cream cheese (3 oz each)
1/2 cup sugar
2  eggs
1/2 tsp vanilla extract
1 cup dairy sour cream
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat oven to 425F.
2. Prepare pie shell. Spread half of cherry pie filling in bottom; set rest of filling aside.
3. Bake shell 15 minutes, or just until crust is golden. Remove from oven.
4. Reduce oven temperature to 350F.
5. Meanwhile, in small bowl, with portable electric mixer, beat cheese with sugar, eggs, and vanilla until smooth.
6. Pour over hot cherry pie filling; bake 25 minutes. (Filling will be slightly soft in center.)
7. Cool completely on wire rack.
8. To serve: Spoon sour cream around edge of pie. Fill center with remaining cherry pie filling.
notes: Variation: BLUEBERRY PIE SUPREME - Follow above recipe, substituting 1 can (1 lb, 5 oz) blueberry pie filling for cherry pie filling. Recipe from McCall's Great American Recipe Card Collection, Copyright 1973 by The McCall Publishing Co.
tags: dessert, pie, cheesecake, cherry, Midwest, American

name: Company Chicken Cordon Bleu
summary: A delectable, kitchen-tested recipe from The Southern Living Cookbook
ingredient_blocks:
8  chicken breast halves (skinned and boned)
1/4 tsp salt
1/4 tsp white pepper
2  eggs (beaten)
1 cup milk
4  cooked ham slices (1-ounce each, cut in half)
4  Swiss cheese slices (1-ounce each, cut in half)
1/3 cup all-purpose flour
1 1/3 cup fine dry breadcrumbs
vegetable oil (for frying)
Mushroom Sauce (optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. Place each piece of chicken between 2 sheets of wax paper; flatten to 1/4 inch thickness using a meat mallet or rolling pin. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
2. Combine eggs and milk; brush both sides of chicken pieces with milk mixture. Place a piece of ham and a piece of cheese in center of each chicken piece. Brush top of cheese slices with milk mixture.
3. Fold ends over ham and cheese; roll up, beginning with long side. Secure with wooden picks.
4. Dredge chicken in flour; dip in remaining milk mixture, and coat well with breadcrumbs. Cover and chill 1 hour.
5. Heat 1/2 inch oil in a heavy skillet; add chicken, and pan fry over medium heat 20 minutes or until golden brown, turning frequently.
6. Drain well. Serve with Mushroom Sauce, if desired.
notes: Mushroom Sauce recipe included: Combine 1 (10 3/4-ounce) can mushroom soup undiluted, 1 (8-ounce) carton commercial sour cream, 1 (4-ounce) can sliced mushrooms drained, and 1/3 cup dry sherry in a saucepan; cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until thoroughly heated. Yields 2 1/2 cups sauce.
tags: chicken, French, entertaining, Southern Living, pan-fried, stuffed chicken

name: Caesar Salad
ingredient_blocks:
1  large head romaine lettuce
3/4 cup olive oil
3 tbsp red wine vinegar
1 tsp Worcestershire sauce
1/2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp dry mustard
1  large clove garlic (crushed)
1  egg
1  lemon (halved)
freshly ground pepper
1/4 cup Parmesan cheese (grated)
Garlic Croutons (recipe follows)
1  can anchovy fillets (2-ounce, optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. Wash romaine under cold running water. Trim core, and separate stalk into leaves; discard wilted or discolored portions. Shake leaves well to remove moisture. Place romaine in a large plastic bag; chill at least 2 hours.
2. Combine olive oil, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, salt, mustard, and garlic in a jar. Cover tightly, and shake vigorously. Set aside.
3. Pour water to a depth of 2 inches in a medium saucepan; bring water to a rapid boil, and turn off heat.
4. Carefully lower egg into water using a slotted spoon; let stand 1 minute. Remove egg from water, and set aside to cool.
5. Cut coarse ribs from large leaves of romaine; tear leaves into bite-size pieces, and place in a large salad bowl. Pour dressing over romaine; toss gently until well coated.
6. Break coddled egg over romaine; then squeeze juice from lemon halves over salad. Grind a generous amount of pepper over salad, and sprinkle with cheese. Toss lightly. Top with croutons; garnish with anchovies, if desired. Serve immediately.
notes: Yield: 4 to 6 servings. Includes recipe for Garlic Croutons: Spread 3 tablespoons softened butter or margarine over both sides of 3 (3/4-inch-thick) slices French bread; sprinkle with 1/4 teaspoon garlic powder. Cut slices into 3/4-inch cubes. Place on a baking sheet, and bake at 350° for 15 minutes or until croutons are crisp and dry. Let croutons cool; then store in an airtight container. Yield: about 1 1/2 cups.
tags: salad, classic, appetizer

name: Marinated Chicken
ingredient_blocks:
parsley
oregano
salt
pepper
2 cloves garlic
lemons (cut in half)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cut lemons in half, squeeze juice from lemons
2. Throw in the lemon halves with chicken
notes: This appears to be an incomplete recipe card listing marinade ingredients. No quantities given for parsley, oregano, salt, pepper, or number of lemons. The handwriting suggests this is a quick reference note rather than a complete recipe.
tags: chicken, marinade, lemon, herbs

name: Fruit Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 lb butter
1 cup brown sugar
1 cup white sugar
4  eggs
3 cup flour
1 cup milk
1 tsp vanilla
1/4 tsp nutmeg
1  small jar of cherries (cut small, rolled in flour)
1/2 cup raisins
1 cup dried fruit
nuts (cut small, rolled in flour)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream butter, sugar, add egg yolks.
2. Sift flour, add with milk, mix well.
3. Fold in egg whites.
4. Add all fruits and nuts.
5. Put some fruit designs on top of cake.
6. Bake at 350° till done.
notes: Recipe marked 'Edie's' at top. Some handwriting unclear - 'nuts cut small rolled in flour' appears to apply to cherries and possibly other ingredients.
tags: fruit cake, holiday, vintage, baking

name: New Orange Cooler Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1  package Duncan Hines Deluxe Orange Cake (cake mix)
3  eggs
1/3 cup Crisco or Puritan Oil
1 tbsp grated lemon peel
1 tbsp grated orange peel
2 tbsp lemon juice
2 tbsp orange juice
1/3 cup sugar
1-1/4 cup water (for cake)
2 tbsp lemon juice (for glaze)
2 tbsp orange juice (for glaze)
1/3 cup sugar (for glaze)
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oven to 350 degrees. Grease and flour a 10-inch tube or bundt pan.
2. Combine in large mixer bowl and mix at medium speed for 2 minutes: cake mix, 1-1/4 cups water, 3 eggs, 1/3 cup oil, 1 tbsp grated lemon peel, 1 tbsp grated orange peel.
3. Bake for 45 to 55 minutes. Cake is done when toothpick inserted in center comes out clean.
4. Immediately turn cake out on rack and set over shallow pan.
5. GLAZE: Combine in small saucepan and bring to a boil: 2 tbsp lemon juice, 2 tbsp orange juice, 1/3 cup sugar. Simmer for 3 minutes. Drizzle over warm cake.
6. Cool completely before serving.
notes: Serves 12 to 16
tags: cake, citrus, orange, bundt cake, dessert

name: Chicken Divan
ingredient_blocks:
1 stick butter
6  boneless chicken breast
2  small pkg. frozen broccoli
2 oz cubed swiss cheese
2 cans cream of chicken soup
1 cup mayonnaise
1 tsp lemon juice
2 cups Pepperidge Farms Herb Seasoned Stuffing
instruction_blocks:
1. Cook frozen broccoli just enough to thaw.
2. Grease large pan and layer bottom with broccoli.
3. Next make a layer of chicken.
4. In a bowl combine: soup, mayonnaise, curry powder and lemon juice.
5. Spread soup mixture evenly over chicken and sprinkle swiss cheese cubes.
6. Topping: Melt one stick of butter in pan and add stuffing. Stir until butter is absorbed.
7. Spread topping over Divan evenly.
8. Cover with foil and cook at 350 deg. for 45 min.
9. Remove foil and cook another 5 to 10 minutes to brown topping.
notes: Recipe mentions curry powder in instructions but not in ingredients list. HAPPY EATING
tags: casserole, chicken, comfort food, broccoli

name: Granny's Green Apple Cake
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup unsifted all purpose flour
2 cup sugar
1 tsp baking soda
1 tsp salt
1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
1 cup vegetable oil
1/2 cup milk
3  eggs
1 tsp pure vanilla extract
3  Granny Smith green apples (peeled, cored and diced)
1 cup chopped walnuts
1/2 cup raisins
1 cup confectioners sugar (for lemon glaze)
2 tbsp lemon juice (for lemon glaze)
instruction_blocks:
1. In large bowl of electric mixer blend flour, sugar, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, oil, milk, eggs and vanilla.
2. Beat on low speed of electric mixer 3 minutes.
3. Fold in diced apples, nuts and raisins.
4. Pour batter into greased and floured 9 or 10 inch tube pan.
5. Bake in preheated 350 deg. oven 1 hour and 15 minutes or until cake tester inserted comes out clean.
6. Cool cake in pan on wire rack for 10 minutes.
7. Remove from pan and cool completely.
8. For Lemon Glaze: Blend confectioners sugar and lemon juice smoothly, drizzle over top of cake.
tags: cake, apple, dessert, bundt cake, tube pan

name: Biscotti
ingredient_blocks:
5 1/2 cup flour
1 tbsp baking powder
2 sticks butter or margarine
2 tsp water
2 cup sugar
6  eggs (room temp.)
1/4 cup anisette seeds
1/4 cup anisette
2 tsp vanilla
1 cup walnuts (coarsely chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a bowl mix flour and baking powder.
2. In another bowl mix sugar, anisette, vanilla, anisette seeds, water and walnuts.
3. Add eggs beating one at a time.
4. Add melted butter or margarine and mix well.
5. Add flour mixture and mix well.
6. Let ingredients rest in refrigerator for 3 hrs. with plastic wrap cover.
7. Divide in 5 parts.
8. Use a lightly greased cookie sheet and form loafs 2 in. wide by 1/2 in. high.
9. Bake for 15 to 20 min. in preheated oven 375 deg.
10. Let cool for 30 min.
11. Maintain oven temp.
12. Cut loafs and place slices facing up.
13. Brown for 7 to 10 min. on each side.
tags: Italian, cookies, biscotti, anise

name: Filled Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup butter
1/2 cup margarine
1 cup granulated sugar
1 tsp vanilla
3  egg yolks
2 cup all purpose flour (sifted)
1/2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp baking soda
1 cup apricot preserve (or 2 pkgs. (8 oz.) semi-sweet chocolate bits)
1/3 cup sugar (for meringue)
3  egg whites
1/2 cup walnuts (finely chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat the butter, margarine, sugar, vanilla, until light fluffy.
2. Beat in the egg yolks.
3. Mix in the combined flour, salt and baking soda which has been sifted.
4. With the palm of the hand press out the dough to cover the surface of a shallow oiled pan (10x15).
5. Chill for 30 min. or if you wish, you can keep in refrigerator until ready to use.
6. When ready to bake spread the apricot jam over the top. (or chocolate bits)
7. Beat the egg whites and 1/3 cup of sugar until stiff and satiny.
8. Spread over the top of jam or bits - sprinkle with chopped walnuts.
9. Bake in a moderate oven until meringue is firm (350 degrees) for 20 to 30 minutes. (Use own judgment)
10. Remove to rack to cool and cut into squares.
notes: Makes 6 dozen squares. Can substitute apricot preserve with 2 packages (8 oz.) semi-sweet chocolate bits.
tags: cookies, bar cookies, meringue, dessert

name: Cheese Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 pint sour cream
7  eggs
4 oz cream cheese (4-8 oz)
4 tsp vanilla extract
2 cup sugar
graham crackers (crushed, for crust)
instruction_blocks:
1. Butter bottom and sides of spring pan and line with a layer of crushed graham crackers to form crust.
2. Pour in filling mixture and bake in 375 deg. oven until top turns brown (about 55 minutes).
notes: Recipe attributed to Sue Talsano. The cream cheese amount appears to be 4-8 oz. The filling preparation is not explicitly detailed - likely combine sour cream, eggs, cream cheese, vanilla extract, and sugar.
tags: dessert, cheesecake, baked

name: Watergate Salad
ingredient_blocks:
1  small pkg. Pistachio Pudding mix (dry)
1  #2 can crushed pineapple
1 lb cottage cheese (1 lb container)
1  small container Cool Whip
instruction_blocks:
1. Pour pineapple over dry pudding and mix.
2. Add cottage cheese and mix.
3. Add Cool Whip and mix.
4. Chill approx. 1 hr. before serving.
notes: This page also contains a second recipe: Lime Pineapple Cheese Mold
tags: salad, dessert, no-bake, retro

name: Lentil Soup
summary: Traditional lentil soup served with grilled cheese sandwich
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 cup lentils (sorted & washed)
6 cup cold water
3  celery stalks (diced)
1  medium onion (diced)
2  small carrots (diced)
16 oz tomato sauce (2 8oz cans)
5  hot dogs (diced, Pearl brand)
salt (to taste)
pepper (to taste)
1  red pepper (diced, jalapeno)
instruction_blocks:
1. Sort & drain lentils. Wash in cold water.
2. Place lentils, water and vegetables in pot, cover and simmer for 90 minutes or until lentils are soft.
3. When lentils are soft then add hot dogs and simmer for an additional 30 minutes.
4. Serve with Grilled Cheese sandwich.
notes: Handwritten annotations indicate: 1 bag = 1.5 cups lentils, 8-5 hot dogs, red pepper/jalapeno diced as optional addition. Document also contains a second recipe for '30 minute Lentil Soup' with different ingredients including olive oil, shallots, chicken stock, red potato, and ham, served with parsley puree.
tags: soup, lentils, comfort food, family recipe

name: Pasta Fagiola
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb navy beans (sorted and washed)
2  pork chops (large, on the bone)
2  smoked ham hocks (Harrington Ham Hocks are excellent)
1 1/2 tbsp wine vinegar
6 cup water
2  celery stalks
3  garlic cloves
3  onion (large, chopped)
1  red pepper (diced)
2  jalapeno pepper (diced)
3  carrots (diced)
8 oz tomato sauce (1 can)
1 dash salt
1 lb ditalini pasta (1 box)
instruction_blocks:
1. Sort and soak beans in cold water overnight. Drain.
2. Add all ingredients in large pot. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 3 hours, stirring frequently until cooked.
3. When completed, dice the pork chop and ham hock meat and put back in soup.
4. Cook ditalini's until done.
5. Mix pasta and soup and serve.
notes: Prepare beans Method 1: Wash and sort 1 pound of navy or great northern beans. Soak beans in a large pot with 3 quarts of water overnight. Method 2: Wash and sort 1 pound of navy or great northern beans. Boil the beans for 1 hour in 8 cups of water and 1 tbsp of vinegar (de-snapper) and drain. Let stand overnight. Source: Mom 2
tags: soups, Italian, comfort food, beans, pasta

name: Beef Barley and Vegetable Soup
ingredient_blocks:
3 tbsp olive oil
1 3/4 lb sirloin (before trimming, cut into 1/2" cubes)
2  beef bones (meaty beef bones)
3  marrow bones
3  celery stalks
1 1/2  large onions (diced)
1/2 cup pearl barley
4 cup water
2  beef broth (14 1/2 oz cans)
1  diced tomatoes (28 oz can)
1  corn (11 oz can)
2  bay leaf (small)
2 tsp garlic powder
salt (to taste)
pepper (to taste)
1  frozen peas (package, optional)
1 1/2 to 2 tsp hot pepper sauce (optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oil in large pot over medium-high heat. Add beef and bones and sauté until beef is dark brown, about 8 minutes.
2. Transfer beef and bones to a dish plate.
3. Add celery, onion and barley to pot. Sauté until onion is golden, about 15 minutes.
4. Then add 4 cups water, beef broth, tomatoes with juices, corn, bay leaves and garlic powder.
5. Return beef and bones to pot and bring to a boil.
6. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer uncovered until beef is almost tender, about 1 hour.
7. Add peas and hot pepper sauce to soup. Cover and simmer until beef is tender and 30 minutes more.
8. Season with salt and pepper.
notes: Peas and hot pepper sauce are optional additions
tags: soup, beef, comfort food, hearty, barley

name: Split Pea Soup
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb split peas
3 quarts water
1  onion (medium, diced)
1  carrot (diced)
1  smoked ham hock
1/2 lb smoked ham (diced)
instruction_blocks:
1. Sort and rinse split green peas in cold water.
2. Place in kettle with remaining ingredients except smoked ham and bring to a boil.
3. Let simmer for about 2 1/2 hours.
4. Add more water from time to time to maintain desired consistency.
5. Remove ham hock and cut off meat, chop and return to pot.
6. Add diced ham smoked ham (optional).
7. Serve hot.
tags: soups, comfort food, ham, split pea

name: Chicken Pot Pie
ingredient_blocks:
3/4 lb Chicken Breast
1  Potatoes (medium, peeled and chopped)
1/2  Onion (chopped)
1 can Peas and Carrots
1 can Cream of Chicken Soup
1/4 cup Milk
1 can Flake Rolls (by Eggs)
Salt and Pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Turn on the oven to 375 degrees.
2. Trim all the fat from the breasts and put on microwave dish. Season with salt and pepper, cover with Saran wrap and cook on hi for 4 to 5 minutes until juice runs clear. Do not overcook.
3. While chicken is cooking, peel and dice 1 medium sized potatoes. Then chop a 1/2 of an onion and mix together in a microwaveable dish, season with a little salt and pepper. And cover with saran wrap.
4. When the chicken is done, then cook the potatoes about 3 to 4 minutes until they are fork tender.
5. Drain the can of peas and carrots.
6. Add the diced chicken, potatoes, peas and carrots, and the can of cream of chicken soup to a bowl and mix together.
7. Then cover the mixture with a single layer of the flake rolls.
8. Cook in the oven for 30 minutes until the crust is lightly browned and the center is hot.
tags: comfort food, casserole, chicken, pot pie

name: Italian Biscotti
ingredient_blocks:
2 1/2 lb flour (about 10 cups)
6  eggs
1 1/2 cup sugar
1 1/2 tsp anise seed
3/4 cup milk
3/4 cup oil
1/2 stick butter
6 tsp baking powder
instruction_blocks:
1. Sift flour in large bowl - add baking powder and anise seed.
2. Make well - sift sugar in center - add eggs - beat eggs and sugar by hand.
3. Then add milk which has been warmed with the butter.
4. Add oil, then start mixing all ingredients.
5. Make 3 small rolls and let rest for 10 min.
6. Mix again and start rolling till piece is as thick as your thumb.
7. Cut in small pieces and roll pieces a little thinner and make round or braids.
8. Place on cookie sheet and bake approx. 20 min. Oven temp. 350 deg.
tags: Italian, biscotti, cookies, anise

name: Country Butter Cake
summary: A yeast-based butter cake with cinnamon sugar topping
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup sugar (generous, 225g)
1 cup warm milk (250ml, 110°F/43°C)
3  pkgs active dry yeast
4 cup all-purpose flour (450g)
1 pinch salt
1 cup butter (225g)
1  egg
1 tsp ground cinnamon
instruction_blocks:
1. Stir 1 teaspoon sugar into warm milk and sprinkle with yeast. Let stand 5 minutes or until the surface is frothy. Stir gently to moisten any dry particles remaining on top.
2. Sift flour, 1/4 cup (50g) sugar and salt into a large bowl. Melt half the butter; cool slightly. Refrigerate remaining butter.
3. Lightly beat melted butter and egg into yeast mixture. Beat into flour mixture to make a dough.
4. On a floured surface, knead dough until smooth and elastic. Cover and let rise in a warm place 1 hour.
5. Grease two 13x9-inch (33x23-cm) cake pans.
6. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Roll out dough to fit greased pans; place in pans.
7. Make small hollows in the surface of dough. Cut refrigerated butter into small pieces and place in hollows.
8. Mix remaining sugar with cinnamon; sprinkle over cakes.
9. Bake 25 minutes. Cool in pans then cut into slices.
notes: This is a yeast-risen cake topped with butter pieces and cinnamon sugar. Makes two 13x9-inch cakes.
tags: cake, yeast, butter cake, cinnamon, German, everyday baking

name: Dresden Slices
summary: A yeast dough cake with cream cheese filling and almond topping
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp sugar (25g, for yeast dough)
1/2 cup warm milk (125ml, 110°F/43°C)
1 pkg active dry yeast
1-1/2 cup all-purpose flour (225g)
1 pinch salt
2 tbsp margarine (25g)
1  egg
12 oz cream cheese (350g, for filling)
1 cup sugar (200g, for filling)
3  eggs (for filling)
3 tbsp all-purpose flour (20g, for filling)
1  lemon (grated peel only)
8 tbsp butter (100g, softened, for topping)
1 cup sliced almonds (100g, for topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. To make yeast dough, stir a pinch of sugar into milk; sprinkle with yeast. Let stand 5 minutes or until frothy. Stir gently.
2. Sift flour, remaining sugar and salt into a large bowl. Melt margarine; cool slightly. Lightly beat margarine and egg into yeast mixture.
3. Pour into flour mixture, combining to make a dough. On a floured surface, knead dough lightly. Cover and let rise in a warm place 1 hour.
4. Grease a 13x9-inch (33x23-cm) cake pan.
5. To make filling and topping, beat cream cheese, half the sugar, 1 egg, half the flour and lemon peel in a medium bowl.
6. Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C). Lightly knead dough; roll out to fit pan. Place in pan.
7. Spread filling over dough. Cream butter and remaining sugar until fluffy. Add remaining flour. Beat in 2 eggs one at a time.
8. Spread topping over cream cheese filling. Sprinkle with almonds.
9. Bake 30 to 40 minutes or until golden. Cool slightly in pan. Cut into slices.
notes: This recipe appears in a section called 'Cakes for Everyday'. Uses a 13x9-inch baking pan.
tags: German, yeast cake, cream cheese, almonds, dessert, Dresden

name: Italian Panettone
summary: Holiday Baking Begins Early - Traditional Italian sweet bread with candied fruit and raisins
ingredient_blocks:
2/3 cup warm milk (150 ml, 110°F, 43°C)
1/4 cup sugar (50 g)
2  pkgs. active dry yeast
2-2/3 cup all-purpose flour (400 g)
1 tsp salt
8 tbsp butter (100 g)
3  egg yolks
1 pinch grated nutmeg
grated peel of 1/2 lemon
2/3 cup chopped mixed candied peel (100 g)
1/3 cup raisins (50 g)
1  egg yolk, beaten (for brushing)
instruction_blocks:
1. Put warm milk into a medium bowl; stir in a pinch of sugar. Sprinkle with yeast. Let stand 5 minutes or until the surface is frothy. Stir gently to moisten any dry particles remaining on top.
2. Sift flour, salt and remaining sugar into a large bowl. Melt butter; cool slightly. Stir butter into yeast mixture; lightly beat in 3 egg yolks, nutmeg and lemon peel. Pour into flour mixture, combining to make a soft dough.
3. On a floured surface, knead dough about 10 minutes. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size, about 1 hour.
4. Line a deep 7-inch (18-cm) cake pan with waxed paper; grease paper.
5. Turn out dough onto a lightly floured surface; knead in candied peel and raisins. Form dough into a ball; place in prepared pan. Cover and let stand in a warm place until dough reaches top of pan. Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C).
6. Brush risen dough with egg yolk. Using a sharp knife, cut a cross on the top. Bake 20 minutes. Reduce heat to 350°F (175°C) and bake 45 minutes longer or until cake is a rich brown.
7. Cool slightly in pan before turning out onto a rack to cool completely.
8. Remove lining paper just before cutting cake.
notes: Use a 7-inch (18-cm) deep cake pan lined with waxed paper. Total baking time is approximately 65 minutes (20 minutes at 400°F, then 45 minutes at 350°F).
tags: Italian, holiday, bread, yeast bread, Christmas, sweet bread

name: Chicken Escarole Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup low sodium chicken broth
3 cup water
1 lb skinned and boned chicken breast (about 1/3 lb)
2  medium size carrots (peeled and chopped)
2  medium size stalks celery (chopped)
1  medium size yellow onion (chopped)
1/4 cup minced parsley
1/8 tsp black pepper
1/2 lb escarole, spinach or Swiss chard (trimmed)
1  large ripe tomato (peeled, cored, seeded and chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. In large heavy saucepan over moderate heat, bring the chicken broth and water to a simmer.
2. Add chicken, carrots, celery, onion, parsley and pepper. Cover and cook until the chicken is tender, about 30 min.
3. Remove the chicken and set aside. Stir in escarole and tomato into the saucepan and simmer uncovered for 5 to 10 min or until the escarole is tender.
4. When chicken is cool enough to handle, cut it into 1/2 in cubes. Return it to the saucepan and bring the soup to serving temp.
notes: This page also contains a second recipe for Zucchini, Carrot and Onion Quiche. Some handwriting was difficult to read, particularly measurements and some ingredient details.
tags: soup, chicken, Italian, healthy, low sodium

name: Hearty 9-Bean Soup
summary: A highly nutritious, low calorie meal. Excellent for a cold winter night.
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup soup mix (9-bean soup mix)
1/4 tsp freshly ground pepper
1  large onion (chopped)
2-3  cloves garlic (minced)
2 cup broccoli (chopped)
4 cup beef/chicken stock
4 tbsp olive oil
salt (to taste)
1/2 cup pasta (orzo or alphabet - optional)
1  chili pepper (coarsely chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. Measure soup mix - rinse well and soak in pot, with water 2" above mixture, overnight. Rinse well again and add 4 cups of water to clean beans. Cook until beans are tender 2½-3 hrs. Do not drain water from beans.
2. Saute chopped onion and garlic in olive oil. Add salt and pepper to taste.
3. Add the cooked mixture of onion and garlic to bean pot. Add broccoli, uncooked, stock and chili pepper.
4. Simmer for 2 hrs. If you like, add pasta for the last ½ hour of simmering.
5. The soup will be thick and hearty. Adjust for thickness by increasing/decreasing liquid.
6. Serve with flavored bread croutons or crusty bread and freshly grated Romano cheese.
notes: Recipe by Luci. David & I have enjoyed it. Hope you will too.
tags: soup, bean soup, hearty, low calorie, nutritious, winter, comfort food

name: Black Bean Soup
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb dried black beans
1/2 cup diced salt pork (1/4 lb)
1 cup chopped onions
1/2 cup chopped green pepper
1/2 tsp minced garlic
2  bay leaves
2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp black pepper
1/4 cup wine vinegar
1/4 cup minced raw onion (for garnish)
instruction_blocks:
1. Wash the beans. Cover with water in a large pan. Soak overnight.
2. In the morning, add more water if necessary, to a depth of 1/2 inch above the beans.
3. Cook the salt pork over low heat in a 10 inch skillet until lightly brown. Add the browned pork to the beans. Place onion, green pepper, and garlic in the remaining fat, adjust heat to moderately high, and cook, stirring often until the onions turn pale gold. Add the vegetables and fat to the beans. Add bay leaves, salt, and pepper and 1 tablespoon of the vinegar. Cover tightly and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to very low and slowly simmer until beans are tender (from 1/2 to 2 1/2 hours).
4. When beans are tender and liquid has thickened slightly, add remaining vinegar.
5. To serve, spoon 1/2 cup boiled rice into a heated soup plate. Ladle 1 cup beans with juice over rice. Garnish with 2 tbsp. of minced onion.
notes: Optional: Offer cruets of olive oil and additional wine vinegar. May be frozen.
tags: soup, black beans, Cuban-style, comfort food, freezer-friendly

name: Cream Soup
summary: A base cream soup recipe with variations for Broccoli Cream Soup and Split Pea Soup
ingredient_blocks:
1/4 cup finely chopped onion
1/4 cup finely chopped celery
1/4 cup margarine or butter
3 tbsp flour
1/4 tsp salt
1/8 tsp pepper
1 1/2 cup milk
chicken broth (amount unclear)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a medium saucepan saute onion and celery in margarine until tender.
2. Stir in flour, salt, pepper. Cook 1 min. stirring constantly until smooth and bubbly.
3. Gradually stir in chicken broth and milk.
4. Cook until slightly thickened, stir constantly. Do not boil.
notes: 30 min - Broccoli Cream Soup variation: Add 2 cups Green Giant frozen broccoli (cooked & drained), 2 tsp lemon juice, 1/4 tsp garlic powder - pour into cream soup. Split Pea Soup variation also included with ingredients: 2 pkg dry split peas, 12 cups water, 2 chicken flavor bouillon cubes or 2 teaspoons instant chicken bouillon, 1/2 tsp oregano leaves, 1 cup finely chopped onions, 1/4 to 1/2 tsp pepper, 1/3 tsp garlic powder, 1 lb ham shank, 1 bay leaf, 1 1/2 cups thinly sliced carrots, 1 cup celery. Instructions: Wash peas in 5qt pan, combine peas, water, bouillon cubes, onions, garlic, oregano, pepper, ham shanks and bay leaf. Reduce heat, simmer uncovered for 1 1/2 hrs. Remove ham for soup, cut into little pieces, put back in soup. Stir in carrots, celery, simmer uncovered for 1 1/2 hrs.
tags: soup, cream soup, comfort food, broccoli soup, split pea soup

name: Homemade Sausage
ingredient_blocks:
5-6 lb pork butt or fresh shoulder (boned, without hide)
coarse black pepper (to taste)
salt (imported, to taste)
paprika (to taste)
fennel seeds (to taste)
2  garlic kernels (chopped very fine, optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. Put pork through grinder.
2. Season to taste with coarse black pepper, salt (imported), paprika, fennel seeds, and garlic (chopped very fine, optional).
3. Make day before. Keep refrigerated overnight.
notes: Some handwriting is difficult to read. 'Imported' likely refers to imported salt. Recipe recommends making the day before and refrigerating overnight to allow flavors to meld.
tags: sausage, pork, homemade, Italian, make ahead

name: Pork Gulyas
summary: A Hungarian-style pork stew with sauerkraut and sour cream
ingredient_blocks:
1.5 lb cubed pork
2  medium sized onions (sliced)
1 can sauerkraut (washed out a bit)
0.5 cup water or sauerkraut juice (save some sauerkraut water to add to stew if necessary)
1 container sour cream
1 tsp paprika
salt (small amount)
pepper (small amount)
instruction_blocks:
1. Place pork in pan, add paprika and sliced onions.
2. Simmer for about 1 hour until almost cooked.
3. Add sauerkraut which has been washed out a bit and cook for ½ hour more.
4. Add sour cream and heat thoroughly but do not boil.
5. Serve immediately.
notes: Save some of the sauerkraut water to add to the stew if necessary, as the fluid may cook off. Do not boil after adding sour cream.
tags: Hungarian, pork, stew, sauerkraut, Eastern European, comfort food

grated Italian cheese

name: Black Bean Soup
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup black beans (soaked 4 to 8 hrs and drained)
3 tbsp butter
3 cup onions (chopped)
1 cup celery (chopped)
1  bouquet garni (composed of sprigs of parsley, 3 sprigs of fresh thyme or 1/4 tsp dried thyme, and 1 bay leaf)
1  smoked ham hock
6 cup brown stock or beef broth
4 cup water (or use 10 cups water if desired)
1 tsp salt
1/4 tsp pepper
1/3 cup dark rum
lemon juice (to taste)
salt and pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Soak 3 cups black beans in water to cover for 4 to 8 hours and drain them.
2. In heavy kettle melt 3 tbsp butter, add 3 cups chopped onions, 1 cup chopped celery and a bouquet garni composed of sprigs of parsley, 3 sprigs of fresh thyme or 1/4 tsp dried thyme, and 1 bay leaf.
3. Cook the vegetables over low heat for 10 minutes.
4. Add 1 smoked ham hock, the beans, 6 cups brown stock or beef broth, 4 cups water, 1 tsp salt and 1/4 tsp pepper (or use 10 cups water if desired).
5. Bring the soup to a boil, reduce the heat and simmer it, adding more liquid if necessary to keep beans covered, for 3 to 4 hours.
6. Put soup through food mill. Return to kettle, add 1/3 cup dark rum.
7. Reheat soup and season with lemon juice, salt and pepper to taste.
notes: Some handwriting was difficult to read, particularly around the bouquet garni ingredients. The recipe appears to be a traditional black bean soup with ham hock and rum.
tags: soup, black beans, comfort food, Southern

name: Biscotti
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup all purpose flour
2 tsp baking powder
2 tsp aniseed (crushed)
1 tsp lemon peel (finely shredded)
1/4 cup margarine
1/2 cup sugar
2  eggs
instruction_blocks:
1. In a mixing bowl stir together flour, baking powder, aniseed and lemon peel. Set aside.
2. In a small mixer bowl beat margarine with an electric mixer on med. speed till softened.
3. Add sugar and eggs, beat well.
4. Stir in flour mixture.
5. On waxed paper shape dough into two 12 in long logs. Place on prepared baking sheet, flatten log slightly.
6. Bake in a 375° oven for 15 to 20 min or till lightly browned.
7. Cool completely on wire racks about 1 hr.
8. Cut each log into 1/2 in slices. Arrange slices one cut side down on the baking sheet.
9. Bake in a 300° oven for 10 min, turn over, bake 5 to 10 min longer or till crisp & dry.
10. Cool completely on wire racks.
notes: Makes 48 cookies. Phone number on recipe: 813-574-7897
tags: Italian, cookies, biscotti, anise

name: Proscuitto Pie
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup flour
1 pinch salt
3  eggs (save 1 yolk)
1 tbsp Crisco
2 tbsp baking powder
1 cup lukewarm water
1  2-pound container ricotta cheese
pepper (to taste)
3/4 cup grated parmesan cheese
1/2 cup chopped parsley
1-2 lb diced proscuitto
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix flour, salt, baking powder and Crisco. Make hole, place eggs in hole, add water as needed.
2. Knead well. Place between two dinner plates for 15 to 20 minutes.
3. Roll out dough to size of cookie sheet. Fill half; fold over the other half to form a pillow.
4. Place on a greased cookie sheet and bake at 350° for 1 to 1½ hours.
5. For filling: Mix ingredients together and fill dough as explained above.
tags: Italian, pie, savory, proscuitto, ricotta

name: Prosciutto Bread
ingredient_blocks:
1 pkg dry yeast
1/8 tsp sugar
3/4 cup warm water (105 to 115 degrees)
2  eggs (beaten)
2 tsp fennel seed
1 tsp salt
3 to 3-1/2 cup all purpose flour
1 cup minced onion
2 tbsp olive oil
1/2 lb prosciutto (cut into julienne strips)
8 oz provolone cheese (cut into julienne strips)
1  egg yolk
1 tsp water
instruction_blocks:
1. Dissolve yeast and sugar in 3/4 cup warm water in a large bowl. Let stand 6 min.
2. Add eggs, fennel seed, and salt; stir well.
3. Gradually stir in enough flour to make a soft dough.
4. Turn dough out onto a well floured surface, and knead until smooth and elastic (8 to 10 min).
5. Place in a well greased bowl, turning to grease top. Cover and let rise in a warm place (85°F) free from drafts 1 hr. or until double in bulk.
6. Saute minced onion in olive oil until tender. Let cool slightly.
7. Add onion, prosciutto and cheese to dough. Knead gently to combine.
8. Shape into a 7 in round loaf. Place on a greased baking sheet.
9. Let raise in a warm place free from drafts, 30 min. or until double in bulk.
10. Combine egg yolk and 1 tsp water; brush on loaf.
11. Bake at 350 for 35 to 40 min or until golden. Let cool 10 min.
notes: Some handwriting was difficult to read, particularly in the instructions section. Temperature for warm water specified as 105-115°F. Rising temperature specified as 85°F.
tags: Italian, bread, yeast bread, prosciutto, savory bread

name: Rum Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup chopped Pecan nuts
1  Duncan Hines butter recipe golden cake mix (package)
1  vanilla instant pudding (package)
1/2 cup light rum
1/2 cup water
1/2 cup vegetable cooking oil
4  eggs
1 cup sugar (for glaze)
1 stick butter (for glaze)
1/4 cup light rum (for glaze)
1/4 cup water (for glaze)
instruction_blocks:
1. Grease and flour bundt or tube pan (10" x 4"). Crumble nuts in bottom of pan.
2. In a large bowl, put cake mix and instant pudding mix. Add rum, water, oil and eggs.
3. Mix for two (2) minutes. Bake at 325° oven for 50 to 60 minutes.
4. Remove cake from oven and pour hot rum glaze on cake while cake is still in the pan. Do not be alarmed if the glaze seems to affect the cake. It will shrivel up. It is helping the cake to settle.
5. This cake will keep for week in the refrigerator.
6. For glaze: Mix all ingredients in a pot and boil for 2-3 minutes.
7. Pour over hot cake; let stand at least 30 minutes before taking out of the pan. Place on cake plate and sprinkle with confectioner's sugar.
notes: This cake can also be made using a chocolate cake mix and chocolate instant pudding.
tags: dessert, bundt cake, rum, pecans

3 cup flour

3  eggs (save 1 yolk)

1 cup lukewarm water
1  2-pound container ricotta cheese
pepper (to taste)

2. Knead well. Place between two dinner plates for 15 to 20 minutes.
3. Roll out dough to size of cookie sheet. Fill half; fold over the other half to form a pillow.
4. Place on a greased cookie sheet and bake at 350° for 1 to 1½ hours.

1  2-pound container ricotta cheese
pepper (to taste)
