name: Proscuitto Pie
summary: Grandma Capece's recipe, from Aunt Lil's handwritten recipe
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup flour
1 pinch salt
3  eggs (save 1 yolk)
1 tbsp Crisco
2 tbsp baking powder
2/3-1 cup lukewarm water
2 lb ricotta
3/4 cup parmesan (grated)
1/4 cup parsley (chopped)
4  eggs
1 1/2-2 lb Proscuitto (slice thick, then dice)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix flour, salt, baking powder and Crisco. Make a well.
2. Add eggs and use water as needed to make dough come together.
3. Knead well. Place dough between two floured dinner dishes for 15 to 20 minutes.
4. Roll dough out, pour filling over 1/2 the dough.
5. Fold other end over the top making an envelope. Seal ends.
6. With a fork, prick holes in the top crust.
7. Place pie on a greased cookie sheet.
8. Bake at 350° for 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
9. For filling: Mix all ingredients together, then place on half the rolled out dough.
notes: This is a family recipe from Grandma Capece, preserved from Aunt Lil's handwritten recipe. The document shows some wear and staining but the recipe text is legible.
tags: Italian, pie, savory, proscuitto, ricotta, family recipe, Easter

name: Spinach Soup
ingredient_blocks:
1/4 cup chopped onion
1/2 clove garlic (minced)
1 tbsp butter or margarine
1 can chicken broth (46 ounces)
1/2 cup Ronzoni Acini Pepe
1/4 tsp nutmeg
1/8 tsp pepper
1  frozen chopped spinach (10 ounce package)
Grated Parmesan cheese
salt (if desired)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cook and stir onion and garlic in hot butter until tender, but not browned.
2. Add chicken broth and bring to a boil.
3. Add pasta, nutmeg and pepper; simmer about 5 minutes.
4. Add frozen chopped spinach and simmer 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Season to taste with salt if desired.
6. Sprinkle with cheese.
notes: Some text was difficult to read due to staining/damage on the scanned document
tags: soup, spinach, Italian, pasta

name: Sausage Bread
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 lb sausage
1 cup sifted flour
1/2 tsp baking powder
1/4 tsp baking soda
1/2 tsp salt
2 tbsp shortening
1/2 cup buttermilk
1 tbsp grated onion
1 cup shredded cheese
instruction_blocks:
1. Crumble sausage in the bottom of 9" pie plate. Bake in a 425 degree oven for 10 minutes.
2. Pour off fat, leaving about 1 tablespoon. Sift flour with baking powder, baking soda and salt. Cut in shortening and stir in buttermilk to make a rather soft dough. Mix in onions and cheese. Knead slightly and roll out to a 9" circle. Place on top of sausage and bake in 400 degree oven for about 20 minutes.
tags: bread, sausage, savory

name: Mushroom Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup mushrooms - stems diced, caps sliced
1/4 cup onion (finely diced)
1/4 cup cooked carrots (finely diced)
1/4 cup cooked celery (finely diced)
5 tbsp butter
7 tbsp flour
4 cup chicken broth
1 cup light cream or milk
2 tbsp dry Vermouth
salt and pepper to taste
chopped parsley (optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. Sauté mushroom stems and caps in butter with onion about 5 minutes.
2. Add Vermouth. Cook 3 minutes.
3. Add flour. Cook stirring until thickened.
4. Cool slightly and add cream or milk and the cooked carrots and celery.
5. Heat through. Do not boil.
6. Add salt and pepper to taste.
7. Sprinkle with parsley if desired.
notes: This page also contains a second recipe for Pepper Cookies
tags: soup, mushroom, comfort food

name: Broccoli Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp grated onion
1/2 cup butter or margarine (melted)
1/4 cup all-purpose flour
1 cup milk
1 cup half and half
2 cup chicken broth
1/2 tsp salt
1/8 tsp garlic powder
1/4 tsp dried basil (to 1/2 teaspoon)
3 cup chopped fresh broccoli
instruction_blocks:
1. Sauté onion in butter in Dutch oven.
2. Add flour, stirring until smooth. Cook 1 minute, stirring constantly.
3. Gradually add milk, half and half, chicken broth. Cook over medium heat stirring constantly until the mixture is thickened and bubbly.
4. Stir in salt, garlic powder and basil.
5. Add broccoli, cover and cook over medium heat 15 minutes or until broccoli is tender.
notes: Recipe attributed to Elaine. Yield: 5 cups
tags: soup, broccoli, creamy, comfort food

name: Lentil Soup
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup margarine (1 stick)
2 cup chopped onions
1 lb dried lentils
10 cup water
2 tbsp chopped parsley
2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp ground black pepper
2  bay leaves
instruction_blocks:
1. Melt margarine in a large heavy saucepan. Add onions and sauté until tender, about 5 minutes.
2. Stir in lentils, water, parsley, salt and pepper. Add bay leaves. Bring to a boil.
3. Cover; reduce heat and simmer 1 1/2 to 2 hours, or until lentils are tender. Discard bay leaves.
4. Serve hot. If desired, puree in blender or processor before serving.
tags: soup, lentils, vegetarian

name: Pasta With Carbonara Sauce
ingredient_blocks:
4  eggs
1/4 cup butter or margarine
1/4 cup cream
1 lb fettucini or spaghetti
2-3 oz Hormel packages (1/2 ounce packages)
2  sliced pepperoni
1 cup Parmesan cheese (grated)
1/4 cup fresh parsley (snipped)
pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Let eggs, butter or margarine and cream stand at room temperature for 2 to 3 hours.
2. Beat together the eggs and cream just until blended.
3. Add pasta to a large amount of boiling, salted water. Cook 10-12 minutes or until tender, but firm. Drain well.
4. Heat an ovenproof serving dish in a 250 degree oven.
5. Turn pasta into heated serving dish. Toss pasta with butter and pepperoni.
6. Pour egg mixture over and toss until pasta is well coated.
7. Add cheese and parsley, toss to mix. Serve immediately.
notes: Makes 10 to 12 side dishes or 4 to 5 main servings. Some text on page appears overlapped/unclear.
tags: Italian, pasta, carbonara

name: Black Bean Soup
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb dried black beans
1/2 cup salt pork (diced, 1/4 pound)
1 cup onions (chopped)
1/2 cup green pepper (chopped)
1/2 tsp garlic (minced)
2  bay leaves
2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp black pepper
1/4 cup wine vinegar
6 cup cooked rice
12 tbsp raw onion (minced)
instruction_blocks:
1. Wash beans. Cover with water in a large pan. Soak overnight. In the morning, add more water, if necessary, to a depth of 1/2 inch above the beans.
2. Cook the salt pork over low heat in a 10 inch skillet until lightly brown. Add the browned pork to the beans.
3. Place onion, green pepper, and garlic in the remaining fat, adjust heat to moderately high, and cook, stirring often until onions turn pale gold.
4. Add the vegetables and fat to the beans. Add bay leaves, salt, and pepper and 1 tablespoon of the vinegar. Cover tightly and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to very low and slowly simmer until beans are tender (from 1 1/2 to 2 1/2 hours).
5. When beans are tender and liquid has thickened slightly, add remaining vinegar.
6. To serve, spoon 1/2 cup boiled rice into a heated soup plate. Ladle 1 cup beans with puree over rice. Garnish with 2 tablespoons of minced onion.
notes: Optional: Offer cruets of olive oil and additional wine vinegar. May be frozen.
tags: soup, beans, Cuban, comfort food

name: Rose's Lentil Vegetable Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup lentils
2  slices of Pancetta (Italian bacon)
1/2 cup chopped onion
1/2 cup celery
1/2 cup carrots
3 tbsp snipped parsley
1  clove garlic, chopped
salt and pepper to taste
1/4 tsp dried oregano, crushed
2 cup tomatoes
2  cans chicken broth and 1 can of water
1/2 cup red wine (optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. Rinse lentils, drain. Add chicken broth and water. Bring to a boil. Lower heat and simmer for 1 hour.
2. Make roux with onions, bacon, parsley and vegetables until they are soft, about 10 minutes. Add tomatoes, oregano and cook for 20 minutes. If desired add 1/2 cup of red wine. Add all to lentils and simmer for 30 minutes.
3. I like my soup to be thick. I puree about a cup or more of the mixture and return to the pot.
tags: soup, lentils, Italian, vegetable

name: Luci's Minnestroni
summary: A hearty Italian minestrone soup with vegetables, beans, and pasta
ingredient_blocks:
1 can chicken broth
1 can beef broth
1/4 cup olive oil
2  medium onions (thinly sliced)
3 cloves garlic (chopped)
3  carrots (sliced)
1/2 head cabbage (chopped or sliced)
2 stalks celery (sliced)
1 package frozen spinach (or canned mustard greens or any kind of greens)
1  large potato (scrubbed and cubed)
1  medium-sized can of peeled tomatoes (chopped)
1 can beans (white, red, or your choice)
1 package chopped frozen broccoli or chopped green beans (or both)
1 sprig fresh Rosemary (or 1/2 teaspoon dry Rosemary)
ground pepper (to taste)
grated cheese (for serving)
instruction_blocks:
1. In pot, sauté onions and garlic in olive oil until limp.
2. Add cabbage, celery, spinach, cubed potato, broccoli and/or beans and cook slowly for about 20 minutes or until limp. If necessary, add more olive oil.
3. Add beans, peeled tomatoes, broth, pepper and Rosemary.
4. Cook for a couple of hours on low setting.
5. If desired add dry pasta like very thin spaghetti broken or capellini and let the pasta absorb the liquid.
6. Reheat when ready to serve.
7. Serve with grated cheese.
notes: This page also contains a second recipe called 'Capanota' - an eggplant dish
tags: Italian, soup, minestrone, vegetable soup, comfort food

name: Capanota
summary: Italian eggplant dish that can be served hot or cold with Italian bread or crackers
ingredient_blocks:
1  medium eggplant
6 tbsp olive oil
1 clove garlic (minced)
1  onion (coarsely chopped)
1  small can tomato sauce
1/2 cup celery (chopped)
1/2 cup green pepper (diced)
2 tbsp capers (optional)
12  large stuffed green olives (sliced)
2 tbsp wine vinegar
1 tbsp sugar
salt (to taste)
pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Peel eggplant and cut into slices 1/2" thick. Cut slices into cubes measuring 1/2".
2. In large heavy skillet, heat 5 tablespoons of oil and sauté eggplant until brown. Remove eggplant and set aside.
3. Add remaining oil to pan and sauté garlic and onion. Add tomato sauce, celery and green pepper.
4. Simmer, covered, for 15-20 minutes, adding water if necessary.
5. Return eggplant to skillet with capers and olives. Heat vinegar and sugar together and pour over vegetables.
6. Season and simmer 15 minutes longer, stirring occasionally.
7. Chill. Serve hot or cold with Italian bread or crackers.
tags: Italian, eggplant, appetizer, vegetable, make ahead

ground pepper (to taste)

instruction_blocks:

name: Luci's Hearty 9-Bean Soup
summary: High nutritious, low calorie. Excellent on a cold winter night.
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup mixed beans
1/4 tsp freshly ground pepper
1  large onion (chopped)
2-3  cloves garlic (minced)
2 cup broccoli (chopped)
4 cup beef/chicken stock
4 tbsp olive oil
salt (to taste)
1/2 cup pasta (orzo or alphabet, optional)
1  chili pepper (coarsely chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. Measure soup mix. Rinse well and soak overnight in pot with water 2" above mixture.
2. Rinse well again and add 4 cups of water to clean beans. Cook until beans are tender, about 2 1/2 to 3 hours. Do not drain water from beans.
3. Sauté chopped onion and garlic in olive oil. Add salt and pepper to taste. Add the cooked mixture of onion and garlic to bean pot. Add uncooked broccoli, stock and chili pepper. Simmer for 2 hours. If you like, add pasta for the last 1/2 hour of simmering.
4. The soup will be thick and hearty. Adjust for thickness by increasing/decreasing liquid.
5. Serve with flavored bread croutons or crusty bread and freshly grated Romano cheese.
tags: soup, beans, hearty, winter, healthy, low calorie

name: Chicken Escarole Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup low sodium chicken broth
2 cup water
1/2 lb skinned and boned chicken breast
2  medium carrots (peeled and chopped)
2  medium stalks celery (chopped)
1  medium yellow onion (chopped)
1/4 cup minced parsley
1/8 tsp black pepper
1/2 lb escarole, spinach or Swiss chard (trimmed)
1  large ripe tomato (peeled, cored, seeded and chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a large heavy sauce pan, over moderate heat, bring the chicken broth and water to a simmer.
2. Add chicken, carrots, celery, onion, parsley and pepper. Cover and cook until chicken is tender, about 20 minutes.
3. Remove the chicken and set aside.
4. Stir in the escarole and tomato into the sauce pan and simmer uncovered for 5 to 10 minutes or until the escarole is tender.
5. When chicken is cool enough to handle, cut it into 1/2 inch cubes, return to sauce pan and bring the soup to serving temperature.

name: Creamy Broccoli Au Gratin Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp butter or margarine
1/2 cup chopped onion
1 3/4 cup water
1  Uncle Ben's Country Inn Broccoli Rice Au Gratin (4.5 ounces package)
1/4 cup flour
1/4 tsp dry mustard
1 3/4 cup chicken broth or bouillon
2 cup milk
1/2 cup shredded American cheese
1  green onion (cut into short, thin strips)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cook onion in butter in large saucepan until tender.
2. Add water and contents of rice and seasoning packets. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover and simmer 20 minutes.
3. Combine flour and dry mustard in a small bowl. Gradually add about 1/2 cup of the broth, stirring until smooth.
4. Stir remaining broth, flour mixture and milk into rice mixture.
5. Heat to a simmer, stirring occasionally. Continue simmering until slightly thickened.
6. Stir in cheese.
7. Sprinkle with green onion.
notes: Makes 4 to 6 servings
tags: soup, broccoli, cheese, comfort food, creamy

name: Black Bean Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup black beans
3 tbsp butter
2 cup onions (chopped)
1 cup celery (chopped)
1  bouquet garni (parsley, 2 sprigs fresh thyme or 1/4 teaspoon dried thyme, 1 bay leaf)
1  smoked ham hock
6-10 cup brown stock or beef broth
4 cup water
1 tsp salt
1/4 tsp pepper
1/3 cup dark rum
instruction_blocks:
1. Soak black beans in water to cover for 4 to 6 hours, then drain.
2. In a heavy kettle melt butter and, add chopped onions, chopped celery and bouquet garni. Cook vegetables over low heat for 10 minutes.
3. Add ham hock, beans and brown stock or beef broth, water, salt and pepper.
4. Bring the soup to a boil, reduce the heat and simmer adding more liquid if necessary to keep beans covered. Cook for 3 to 4 hours.
5. Put soup through food mill.
6. Return to kettle. Add rum. Reheat soup and season with lemon juice, salt and pepper to taste.
notes: Soup is pureed through a food mill before finishing. Lemon juice is used to season at the end but not listed in ingredients.
tags: soup, black beans, comfort food, ham

name: Pepper Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
2 1/2 cup flour
1 tsp salt
1 tsp pepper
2  packages dry yeast
1/2 cup warm water
1/4 cup Wesson Oil (I use a little less)
instruction_blocks:
1. Add flour, pepper, salt. Consistency should be like bread dough.
2. Put in Tupperware with cover to rise so it will double in bulk (about 30 minutes).
3. Oil the cookie sheet lightly.
4. Roll out dough into long strings about 1/2" thick, slice into 3" pieces and knot.
5. Bake at 400 degrees for the first 10 minutes.
6. Lower temperature to 350 degrees and bake for 20 to 30 minutes.
notes: These are savory yeast-based cookies/bread knots, not sweet cookies.

name: Potato Casserole
ingredient_blocks:

1/2 cup oleo or butter (melted)
1 tsp salt

instruction_blocks:

2. Grease pan. Put potatoes on bottom.
3. Add remainder of ingredients.

instruction_blocks:

name: Spinach Soup
ingredient_blocks:

1  frozen chopped spinach (10 ounce package)

instruction_blocks:

4. Add frozen chopped spinach and simmer 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.

name: Clams with Quinoa
summary: From Too Hot Tamales Show #TH 6219
ingredient_blocks:
24  littleneck clams
3 cup shucked cherrystone clams (with their liquor)
4 cup clam juice or water (as needed)
3/4 cup quinoa
3 tbsp olive oil
1  onion (finely chopped)
4  cloves garlic (finely chopped)
2  jalapeno chiles (seeded and finely chopped)
1  bay leaf
1 tbsp ground cumin
1/4 tsp freshly ground black pepper
1 tbsp tomato paste
8  small red rose potatoes (quartered)
1 cup white wine
2  cloves garlic (slivered)
1 tsp salt
1/2 tsp red pepper flakes
1  red bell pepper (stemmed, seeded, and cut into fine julienne)
1/2 bunch cilantro (leaves only, finely chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. Wash and vigorously scrub the littlenecks under cold running water. Place them on a tray, cover with a damp cloth, and set aside until ready to serve.
2. Finely chop the shucked cherrystones and set them aside, refrigerate. Pour the clam liquor through a strainer lined with a double thickness of dampened cheesecloth. Add enough additional clam juice or water to make the liquid up to 5 1/2 cups. Set aside, refrigerate.
3. Place the quinoa in a bowl and add enough cold water to cover by about 2 inches. Let soak for 5 minutes, then massage the grain between the palms of your hands for a moment. Strain through a fine strainer, rinse with cold running water, and repeat the soaking and rubbing process. Set the quinoa aside to drain.
4. In a large casserole with a cover, preferably earthenware, heat the olive oil over medium-low heat. Add the onion and saute until it is totally translucent but not browned, about 8 minutes. Increase the heat to medium and add the garlic, chiles, bay leaf, cumin, and pepper. Stir over the heat for 2 minutes more, or until the aroma of the garlic is released. Add the tomato paste, chopped clams and potatoes and stir together to mix. Pour in 4 1/2 cups of the reserved clam juice, bring up to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes. Stir in the quinoa and cook for 15 minutes more, stirring occasionally.
5. While the quinoa is cooking, in another large saucepan reduce the white wine by half over medium heat. Add the garlic, salt, pepper flakes, julienned red pepper, and the remaining 1/2 cup clam juice and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add the littlenecks, cover the pan and reduce the heat to low, and cook for 10 to 15 minutes, or until the clams have barely opened, gently stirring the mixture once during the cooking time. Mound the quinoa mixture onto a large platter and spoon the littlenecks, with all of their juice and the peppers, over the top. Scatter the cilantro over and serve immediately.
notes: Yield: 6-8 servings. Document also contains partial recipe for Tuna Steaks Chorrilos-Style.
tags: seafood, clams, quinoa, Too Hot Tamales, Latin

name: Salsa Alla Bolognese - Tomato and Meat Sauce
summary: Traditional Italian meat sauce for pasta
ingredient_blocks:
3 tbsp sweet unsalted butter
1  small white or yellow onion (chopped fine)
1  small celery stalk (chopped fine)
1/2  small carrot (chopped fine)
1/4 lb lean good quality beef (preferably chuck, ground twice)
1/4 lb lean good quality pork (preferably belly, ground twice)
1/4  lean good quality veal (ground twice)
1/8 lb prosciutto crudo (thicksliced, chopped)
1/2 tsp salt (or to taste)
freshmilled pepper (to taste)
1/2 cup dry white wine (good quality)
2/3 cup milk
1/8 tsp nutmeg (preferably freshgrated)
21/2 cup Italian plum tomatoes (1 28ounce can, peeled, seeded and chopped, in puree)
1/2 cup cream (optional)
1  white truffle (thinly sliced at the end, optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a large, wide Dutch oven or large, deep skillet, saute the butter, chopped onion, celery, and carrot until they are soft but not browned.
2. Add the ground meats and prosciutto, turning the flame very low. It is very important that the meat heat very gently, only enough to color it on the outside. Not searing the meat allows it to absorb the other flavors of the sauce, and will also make it more delicate and creamy.
3. Break up and stir the meat as you heat it. You should get it to a uniform pink, not brown color.
4. Add the salt, pepper and wine. Simmer very gently for several minutes until the alcohol evaporates and the liquid begins to be absorbed by the meat and vegetables.
5. Stir in milk and nutmeg. It is important to add the milk before adding the tomatoes for it to be absorbed directly by the meat.
6. Cook gently for 10 minutes. Add the tomatoes. When the sauce begins to simmer, turn the flame down as low as you can.
7. If your burner cannot be regulated low enough, insert a "flame tamer." Simmer in this way, partially covered, for about 4 hours, stirring it gently now and then. Check for seasonings and add an additional pinch of nutmeg if necessary.
8. If the sauce is being used on fresh noodles (not lasagne), and you have a mind to be extravagant, add the cream and the finely sliced truffle when it is finished.
9. Stir and pour over cooked fresh pasta.
notes: This recipe is sufficient for saucing 1 pound of pasta. Ahead of time note: Make the sauce up to 2 hours in advance. Cover and leave at room temperature until ready to serve. Yield: Sauce for 1 pound of pasta. Recipe also mentions a Hass avocado preparation at the top of the page which appears to be for a different dish.
tags: Italian, meat sauce, Bolognese, pasta sauce, slow-cooked

name: Shrimp and Saffron Risotto
ingredient_blocks:
1 tbsp olive oil
1/2 cup chopped onions
2 tbsp minced shallots
2 tbsp minced garlic
2 cup Arborio rice
4 cup shrimp stock
1 tbsp unsalted butter
2 tsp salt
1 tsp white pepper
1/2 cup chopped green onions
1/2 cup chopped tomatoes (peeled and seeded)
3  threads of saffron
1 tsp Essence
1/2 lb peeled medium shrimp (tail off and halved)
3/4 cup grated Parmesan Reggiano Cheese (plus 1/4 cup for garnish)
6  fried spinach leaves
Essence
instruction_blocks:
1. In a saute pan, heat the olive oil. When the pan is smoking hot, saute the onions, shallots and garlic for 1 minute.
2. Using a wooden spoon, stir in the rice and saute for 1 minute. Stir in 1/2 cup of the stock at a time until all of the liquid is incorporated, about 3 minutes.
3. Season with salt and pepper. Fold in the green onions and tomatoes. Season with Essence and bring up to boil, about 10 minutes.
4. Fold in the shrimp and simmer for 2 minutes.
5. Spoon the risotto into a shallow bowl. Garnish with fried spinach leaves, Parmesan Reggiano Cheese and Essence.
notes: From Essence of Emeril Show #EE2318
tags: seafood, Italian, risotto, shrimp, saffron

name: Grilled Beef, Roasted Pepper, and Raisin Risotto
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb top round
2 tbsp olive oil
1 tbsp Essence
1 tbsp olive oil (for risotto)
1 cup julienne yellow onions
2 tbsp minced shallots
1 tbsp minced garlic
2 1/2 cup Arborio rice
2 cup veal reduction
1/4 cup red wine
1/3 cup Dry Marsala
8 cup meat stock
1/2 cup julienne roasted green peppers
1/2 cup julienne roasted red peppers
1/2 cup julienne roasted yellow peppers
1/2 cup golden raisins
1/2 cup Romano Cheese
1 tbsp finely diced red peppers
1 tbsp finely diced yellow peppers
2 tbsp chopped green onions
3 oz block of Romano Cheese
3  grilled whole green onions
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat the grill. Season the top round with the olive oil and Essence. Place on the grill. Grill for 3-4 minutes on each side for medium-rare.
2. For the risotto: In a saute pan, heat the olive oil. When the pan is smoking hot, add the onions, shallots and garlic. Saute the vegetables for 1 minute.
3. Using a wooden spoon, stir in the rice, saute for 1 minute. While stirring constantly, add the veal reduction, wine, Marsala, and meat stock, one cup at a time.
4. Cook the risotto for 10-12 minutes, stirring constantly. Fold in the peppers, cheese and raisins. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Remove the round from the grill and slice on the bias into 2 ounce portions.
6. To assemble, mound the risotto in the center of the platter. Fan the meat around the risotto. Garnish with the brunoiséd peppers, grilled green onion, and with a peeler, cut off thin slices of the cheese over the top of the risotto.
tags: Italian, risotto, grilled beef, main course

name: Farfalle Pasta
summary: Homemade bow-tie pasta with butter, pine nuts, cheese, and marjoram
ingredient_blocks:
pasta dough (prepared and rolled through machine)
butter (for sauce)
pine nuts
1/2 cup cheese (plus additional parmigiano for serving)
marjoram
pepper (to taste)
salt (for boiling water)
instruction_blocks:
1. Roll out pasta dough, freeing it of folds. Set the strip aside and cover with clean kitchen towels. Roll out each portion completely in this same manner, covering them well until all the dough is passed through the machine.
2. To form the farfalle, using a fluted pastry wheel, cut each sheet of pasta in half lengthwise. Then cut every 2 inches across the width of each strip to form rectangles about 2 inches long and 1 inch wide.
3. Using your thumb and forefinger, press two opposite long sides together, pinching firmly in the middle so that a bow is formed.
4. Line several baking sheets with clean kitchen towels. Place the farfalle on the towels and allow them to dry for 10 minutes. (At this point, they can be covered with dry kitchen towels and set aside for up to several hours before cooking, if desired.)
5. To cook the pasta, bring the water to a rolling boil in a large pot. Pick up each of the towels holding the farfalle by two opposite corners, so the contents can be easily and quickly dumped into the boiling water. Dump all of the farfalle into the boiling water as quickly as possible so all the pasta begins cooking at the same time.
6. Stir immediately and add the salt to the water. Stir well again and cover. When the pasta rises to the surface, it is cooked, about 1 minute from the time the water returns to the boil. Be certain not to overcook; the entire cooking process should take no longer than 3 to 4 minutes.
7. Meanwhile, to make the sauce, melt the butter in a skillet large enough to accommodate the pasta later.
8. Drain the farfalle, transfer them to the skillet, and toss with the butter. Using two large forks and keeping the gentlest possible flame under the skillet, distribute the sauce well through the pasta.
9. Add the pine nuts, the 1/2 cup cheese, the marjoram, and pepper to taste and serve immediately. Pass additional parmigiano at the table.
notes: Yield listed as 4 to 6 servings. This is page 2 of a recipe - page 1 with full ingredient quantities may be missing. The farfalle can be made ahead and dried for up to several hours before cooking.
tags: Italian, pasta, homemade pasta, farfalle, bow-tie pasta

name: Oyster Suzette
ingredient_blocks:
12  shucked oysters, leaving in their shell
1 cup diced bacon
1/2 cup chopped onions
2 tbsp minced shallots
1 tbsp minced garlic
1/4 cup small diced green peppers
1 tbsp Crystal Hot sauce
1 cup bread crumbs
Essence
1/2 cup grated Parmesan Reggiano Cheese
1 cup prepared Hollandaise sauce, warm
2 tbsp chopped chives
Rock salt for the platter
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees. In a hot saute pan, render the bacon until crisp, about 2-3 minutes. Add the onions, shallots, garlic, and peppers. Saute for 2-3 minutes.
2. Remove the mixture from the heat and allow to cool. In a mixing bowl, combine the bacon mixture, Crystal sauce, bread crumbs and cheese together.
3. Season with Essence. Place 2 tablespoons of the bread crumb mixture on top of each oyster.
4. Bake for 6-8 minutes or until golden brown. Remove from the oven.
5. Place the oysters on a platter with the rock salt. Drizzle each oyster with the Hollandaise Sauce. Garnish with chives.
notes: From Essence of Emeril Show #EE2307
tags: seafood, oysters, appetizer, Cajun, Emeril

name: Crab Meat Imperial
ingredient_blocks:
1 tbsp olive oil
3/4 cup yellow onions (small dice)
2 tbsp shallots (minced)
1/4 cup red peppers (small dice)
1/4 cup yellow peppers (small dice)
1/4 cup celery (small dice)
3/4 tsp salt
1/4 tsp cayenne
1 lb crab meat
1 tbsp garlic (minced)
2 tbsp parsley (chopped)
1/4 cup green onions (chopped)
1 cup mayonnaise (homemade or prepared)
2 tbsp Creole Mustard
1/4 tsp Tabasco
1/4 cup bread crumbs (dried fine)
1/2 tbsp Rustic Rub
2 tbsp chives (chopped, garnish)
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees. In a large saute pan, heat the olive oil. When the pan is smoking hot, add the onions, shallots, peppers, celery, salt and cayenne. Saute for 5 minutes or until the vegetables are wilted. Add the crab meat, garlic, parsley and green onions, saute for 1-2 minutes.
2. Remove from the heat and cool for about 30 minutes. In a mixing bowl, combine the crab meat mixture with 3/4 cup of the mayonnaise, the mustard and Tabasco. Mix until thoroughly incorporated.
3. Spoon the mixture into 4 coquille shells. In a mixing bowl, combine the breadcrumbs, the remaining mayonnaise and the Rustic Rub together.
4. Spread the top of the crab mixture with the bread crumb mixture. Bake for 20 minutes, or until bubbly and brown.
5. Place the shells on a platter and garnish with chopped chives.
tags: seafood, crab, Creole, baked

name: Jewish Style Corned Beef
ingredient_blocks:
1-12 lb whole brisket
4 quarts water
2 cup Kosher salt
1 tsp saltpeter
5  bay leaves
7  garlic cloves (smashed)
1/4 tsp ground cloves
2 tsp whole peppercorns
1 tsp allspice berries
1 tsp mustard seeds
1 cup white vinegar
1/2 cup white sugar
1/4 tsp cloves (for steamer pot)
2 tsp peppercorns (for steamer pot)
1 tsp allspice berries (for steamer pot)
1 tsp mustard seeds (for steamer pot)
3  garlic cloves (for steamer pot)
1/4 cup salt (for steamer pot)
instruction_blocks:
1. Place all ingredients except garlic and brisket in a large pan and bring to a boil and cool. Place garlic and brisket in non-reactive pot and cover with brining liquid, cover with a plate and weight for 3 weeks, turning after 1 1/2 weeks.
2. Add to a steamer pot: 1/4 teaspoon cloves, 2 teaspoons peppercorns, 1 teaspoon allspice berries, 1 teaspoon mustard seeds, 3 cloves garlic, 1/4 cup salt.
3. Remove meat from the brine and rinse. Add enough water to the steamer pot to reach just below the bottom of the steamer. Place meat in steamer.
4. Steam for 3 hours, until tender.
notes: Suggested wine: 1988 Erdener Treppchen Riesling Kabinett, Elisabeth Christoffel - Berres. Recipe from TASTE SHOW#TS4838.
tags: Jewish, corned beef, brisket, steamed, brined, traditional

name: Coconut Custard Pie
ingredient_blocks:
1  9 inch pie crust, prepared or home made
2 1/2 cup heavy cream
3 cup grated fresh coconut
4  large eggs
1/3 cup sugar
2 tsp vanilla
1/2 cup grated toasted coconut
lightly whipped cream and additional toasted coconut for garnish
instruction_blocks:
1. Trim the edge of the shell, leaving a 1/2 inch overhang. Crimp the edge over the shell decoratively. Prick the dough with a fork and chill for 30 minutes.
2. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees. Line with wax paper, weight and bake the shell in the lower third of the oven for 15 minutes.
3. Remove the wax paper and rice. Reduce the oven temperature to 375 degrees and bake the shell for 10 minutes more or until golden. Transfer the shell to a rack and let cool.
4. Reduce the oven temperature to 350 degrees and butter the second pie plate. In a saucepan bring the half-and-half to a simmer, stir in 2 cups of the fresh coconut, remove from the heat and let the mixture stand for 20 minutes, or until it is room temperature.
5. Pour mixture into the pie plate. Put the pie plate in a larger pan, add enough hot water to the pan to come halfway up the sides of the plate and bake in the middle of the oven for twenty minutes or until the edges of the custard are set. The center of the custard will still be a bit loose. Add additional toasted coconut to the top of the custard and bake for another 10 minutes.
notes: Yield: 1 9-inch pie. Suggested wine: 1986 Chateau Climens (Sauternes - Barsac)
tags: pie, coconut, custard, dessert

name: Classic Scrambled Eggs
summary: Creamy scrambled eggs with smoked salmon and dill, cooked in a double boiler
ingredient_blocks:
9  eggs
1/4 cup sour cream
2 tbsp unsalted butter
salt and white pepper (to taste)
1/4 cup chopped dill
4 oz smoked salmon (diced)
fresh dill sprigs (for garnish)
instruction_blocks:
1. Bring water to boil in bottom of double boiler.
2. In top stir in eggs, sour cream and unsalted butter. Beat lightly to break up eggs.
3. Turn heat down and over simmering water, stir eggs constantly until very lightly set, but still creamy - about 8 minutes.
4. Stir in salt and white pepper to taste. Add dill and smoked salmon. Mix well.
5. Eggs will be creamy looking. Serve immediately with dill.
notes: From Food In A Flash Show #FF2062. Uses double boiler method for extra creamy eggs.
tags: breakfast, eggs, salmon, quick

name: Barbecued Endives in Tarragon Oil with Pepato
ingredient_blocks:
4  large Belgian endives
1 tbsp virgin olive oil (plus 1/2 cup)
1 tbsp red wine vinegar
1 tbsp freshly ground black pepper
1/2 cup fresh tarragon leaves
1/4 lb pecorino pepato (sheep's milk cheese with peppercorns)
salt (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat barbecue or grill. Bring 1 quart water to a boil and prepare an ice bath on the side.
2. Cut the endives in half lengthwise. Mix 1 tablespoon virgin olive oil, vinegar and black pepper together.
3. Dredge the endives in vinegar mixture and place on hot grill. Cook until slightly charred (8 to 10 minutes) and remove to serving platter.
4. Meanwhile, drop tarragon leaves into boiling water with 1/2 cup virgin olive oil and blend until smooth.
5. Spoon tarragon oil over endives, shave pepato over in long shards with a peeler and serve at room temperature.
notes: This recipe is from Molto Mario show #MB5654
tags: Italian, grilled, vegetable, appetizer

name: Linguine with Prosciutto, Radicchio and Caraway Goat Cheese
ingredient_blocks:
4 tbsp virgin olive oil
1  large head radicchio (halved and thinly sliced (3 cups))
1/4 lb prosciutto di parma (thinly sliced)
1/2 lb linguine
1/4 lb caraway seed goat cheese (preferably Coach Farm)
salt and pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Bring 6 quarts water to a boil and add 2 tablespoons of salt.
2. In a 12" to 14" skillet, heat olive oil until smoking. Toss in radicchio, stirring quickly until just wilted (1 minute).
3. Julienne prosciutto slices and toss into pan with radicchio.
4. Cook pasta until well mixed and pour into heated serving bowl.
5. Shave caraway goat cheese over pasta and serve immediately.
notes: This appears to be page 2 of a document. Some text at bottom of instructions was partially cut off or unclear.
tags: Italian, pasta, prosciutto, goat cheese, radicchio

name: Crostini Toscane
summary: Italian appetizer with chicken liver spread on toasted peasant bread
ingredient_blocks:
3 tbsp virgin olive oil
1  medium red onion (cut into 1/8th inch dice)
1 tbsp capers
2 tbsp anchovy paste (or 4 anchovy fillets, rinsed and patted dry)
1/2 lb chicken livers
1/2 cup dry red wine (Chianti)
2 tbsp tomato paste
1 tbsp balsamic vinegar
salt and pepper (to taste)
8  1-inch thick slices of Italian peasant bread
instruction_blocks:
1. In a 12-inch skillet, heat 3 tablespoons olive oil until smoking over medium heat.
2. Add onion, capers, anchovy paste and cook until golden brown, about 8 to 10 minutes.
3. Add chicken livers and stir until lightly browned, about 4 to 5 minutes.
4. Add wine, tomato paste and balsamic vinegar and bring to boil. Lower heat and simmer 15 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Place mixture in food processor and pulse 5 or 6 times until chopped but not paste-like (There should be some lumps). Remove from processor and set aside.
6. Toast bread under broiler and spread liver mixture over one side of each piece and serve.
notes: From Molto Mario Show #MB5651. Yield: 4 servings as antipasto.
tags: Italian, Tuscan, appetizer, antipasto, chicken liver, crostini

name: Basic Tomato Sauce
ingredient_blocks:
1  Spanish onion (cut into 1/4-inch dice)
4 cloves garlic (thinly sliced)
3 oz virgin olive oil
4 tbsp fresh thyme (or 2 tablespoons dried)
1/2  medium carrot (finely shredded)
2  28-ounce cans of tomatoes (crushed and mixed well with their juices)
salt (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Saute the onion and garlic in the olive oil over medium heat until translucent, but not brown (about 10 minutes).
2. Add the thyme and carrot and cook 5 minutes more.
3. Add the tomatoes. Bring to a boil, lower the heat to just bubbling, stirring occasionally for 30 minutes.
4. Season with salt to taste. Serve immediately, or set aside for further use.
notes: The sauce may be refrigerated for up to one week.
tags: Italian, sauce, basic, tomato

name: Carrot Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
3/4 cup all purpose flour
1/2 tsp baking powder
1/2 tsp salt
1 tsp grated orange zest
1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
1/3 cup vegetable oil
1  large egg
1/3 cup sugar
1/3 cup cooked and pureed carrots
1/4 cup golden raisins
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Lightly grease a cookie sheet.
2. In a mixing bowl whisk together the flour, baking powder, salt, orange zest, and cinnamon. Mix the vegetable oil, egg and sugar together and beat in the pureed carrots and raisins. Combine the wet with the dry ingredients.
3. Drop teaspoonfuls of dough onto the cookie sheet and space well apart. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes or until golden brown.
notes: From Cooking Monday to Friday Show # MF 6659. Yield: 2 1/2 dozen
tags: cookies, carrot, baking, dessert

name: Oatmeal Cookies
summary: Orange and almond flavored oatmeal cookies
instruction_blocks:
1. Lightly butter a cookie sheet. Preheat the oven to 325 degrees.
2. Beat together the butter, sugar, egg, orange zest and almond extract.
3. With your fingers press the oatmeal into the butter until the dough just holds together.
4. Form the dough into tiny balls and with the tines of a fork press them down.
5. Bake for 10 to 15 minutes or until golden brown.
notes: This appears to be page 2 of a recipe - the ingredient list is likely on page 1. Yield: 2 1/2 dozen cookies.
tags: cookies, oatmeal, orange, almond

name: Chicken Fajitas
summary: From How To Boil Water Show #BW8340
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 lb boneless, skinless chicken breasts
2 tbsp olive oil
3 tbsp lime juice
2  cloves garlic
1 cup packed cilantro leaves
1/2  jalapeno (seeded)
salt and pepper
1 tbsp olive oil
1  large onion (peeled and cut into 1/2 inch slices)
1  green bell pepper (seeded and sliced)
2 tbsp olive oil
3  potatoes (cut into 1/2 inch cubes)
salt and pepper
cayenne
10  flour tortillas
instruction_blocks:
1. Flatten chicken breasts to 1/2 inch thickness between two sheets of plastic wrap with a mallet or rolling pin.
2. Place chicken breasts in glass baking dish. In a blender combine oil, lime juice, garlic, cilantro, jalapeno and salt and pepper. Blend until smooth and pour over chicken.
3. Cover with plastic wrap and let chicken marinate for 30 minutes. In a large skillet over medium heat oil.
4. Add onions and peppers and cook until they are tender and begin to brown.
5. In a 9x13 glass baking dish toss potatoes with oil and seasonings, bake in 375 degree oven until crispy, about 30 minutes. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
6. Heat a cast iron grill pan or an outdoor grill. Toast flour tortillas for about 30 seconds per side and keep warm in an aluminum foil pocket.
7. Grill the chicken on each side for 45 minutes. To serve slice chicken into even strips.
notes: The document includes a margarita recipe: Mix 1 oz fresh lime juice, 1/2 cup tequila, 23 tablespoons triple sec, and 1/2 cup ice cubes. Rub rim of glass with wedge of lime and dip into salt. Add ice cubes to glass and pour in margarita. Serve immediately. Yield: 2 margaritas. Note: The chicken grilling time of 45 minutes appears to be a typo and should likely be 4-5 minutes per side.
tags: Mexican, fajitas, chicken, grilled, Tex-Mex

name: Pumpernickel Rolls
ingredient_blocks:
2 tsp active dry yeast
1 cup warm water
1 tbsp melted butter
1 tsp sugar
6 tbsp dark molasses
1 tbsp caraway seeds
1/2 cup mashed potatoes
3 tbsp melted semi-sweet chocolate
2 cup rye flour
1/2 cup whole wheat flour
1/4 cup cornmeal
1/4 cup flour
1  egg (slightly beaten)
2 tbsp poppy seeds
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. In a large bowl dissolve the yeast in the water.
2. Add the butter, sugar, and molasses and mix well.
3. Stir in the caraway seeds, potatoes, chocolate, rye and wheat flours, and the cornmeal.
4. Using your hands, mix the ingredients together, do not over handle. Form into a smooth ball.
5. Grease a bowl, turn the dough into the bowl and cover with plastic wrap.
6. Allow the dough to rise for 1 1/4 hours or until double in size.
7. Flour the work surface and turn the dough out onto the floured surface and punch down. This will allow any gases to escape.
8. Using your hands flatten the dough into a circle about 1 inch thick.
9. Cut the dough into pieces and form each into a roll about 3 inches in diameter.
10. Place the rolls on a parchment lined baking sheet.
11. Brush the rolls with the egg wash and sprinkle with the poppy seeds.
12. Bake for 25 minutes or until golden brown.
13. Remove from the oven and allow to cool.
notes: From Essence of Emeril, Show #EE2304
tags: bread, rolls, pumpernickel, yeast bread, baking

name: Chocolate and Roasted Poblano Sauce
summary: A savory-sweet sauce combining roasted poblanos, chocolate, and cream, served with sweet potatoes
ingredient_blocks:
1 tbsp olive oil
1/2 cup roasted poblanos (peeled, seeded, and chopped)
1/2 cup roasted onions (peeled and chopped)
2 tbsp shallots (minced)
1 tbsp garlic (minced)
Essence (seasoning)
3 cup chicken stock
1/4 cup semi-sweet chocolate (melted)
3 tbsp heavy cream
instruction_blocks:
1. In a sauce pan, heat the olive oil. When the oil is hot, saute the poblanos, onions, shallots, and garlic, for 2-3 minutes.
2. Season with Essence. Stir in the stock, bring to a boil and cook over high heat for 15 minutes.
3. Whisk in the chocolate and cream, continue simmering for 2 minutes.
4. Remove from the heat and puree in a blender. Return sauce to the heat and season with salt and pepper.
5. To plate: spoon the sauce on the bottom of the plate. Place the julienned sweet potato in the center of the sauce. Lay the chops against the potatoes with their bones crossing. Garnish with green onions, red and yellow peppers.
notes: Yield: 2 cups of sauce. This sauce appears to be served with lamb or pork chops coated in melted chocolate and nut crust, seared and cooked in oven for 10-12 minutes. Served with deep-fried julienned sweet potatoes.
tags: Mexican-inspired, sauce, chocolate, poblano, savory-sweet

name: Sweet-Baked White Beans with Pork
ingredient_blocks:
2 1/4 cup small white or cannellini beans (picked over and soaked overnight)
2 tsp dry mustard
1/4 cup cider vinegar
1 tbsp fresh ginger (grated)
1/2 cup dark brown sugar (firmly packed)
1  yellow onion (diced)
3 cup chicken stock
4 oz salt pork (in one piece)
2  large unsmoked pork hocks
1 tsp salt
1/4 tsp black pepper (freshly ground)
1/2  small red onion (very finely diced, for salsa garnish)
1/2  small bunch flat-leafed parsley (leaves only, coarsely chopped, for salsa garnish)
2  plum tomatoes (coarsely chopped, for salsa garnish)
1 tsp salt (for salsa garnish)
1/4 tsp black pepper (freshly ground, for salsa garnish)
2 tbsp olive oil (for salsa garnish)
2 tbsp cider vinegar (for salsa garnish)
1 tsp soy sauce (for salsa garnish)
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat oven to 300 degrees.
2. Drain the soaked beans in a colander and rinse them with cold running water. In a medium heavy casserole, combine the mustard, vinegar, ginger, brown sugar, onion and chicken stock. Whisk together until evenly blended and add the beans.
3. Trim any tough outer skin from the salt pork and pork hocks and add them to the casserole, placing the bacon in the center and 1 hock on either side. Bake in the warm oven for 3 hours, or until the beans have absorbed most of the liquid and the remaining sauce is thick. Stir occasionally to keep the beans evenly moistened, and add up to 1 cup water if necessary, to keep the beans from scorching. About 1 hour before the beans will be done, combine all the ingredients for the garnish in a small glass bowl and toss together well. Pull the meat off the bones, cut the bacon into chunks and serve each bowl garnished with a spoonful of the vegetable salsa.
notes: Salt pork is from the same cut of pork as bacon, but whereas bacon is usually cured in brine and smoked, salt pork is cured in coarse salt, but not smoked.
tags: pork, beans, baked, comfort food

name: Fruit Salad with Lemon-Lime Dressing
ingredient_blocks:
1  papaya (peeled, seeded and cut into 1-inch chunks)
2  bananas (peeled and sliced 1/2 inch thick)
1  mango (peeled, stoned and cut into 1-inch chunks)
1 cup pineapple (cut into 1 inch chunks)
1 pint strawberries (hulled and halved)
2 tbsp fresh lemon juice
2 tbsp fresh lime juice
1 tbsp dark rum
2 tbsp sugar (or to taste)
1 bunch mint leaves (coarsely chopped)
tiny sprigs of mint (for garnish)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a large glass serving bowl or picnic container, combine all the fruits and toss very gently to mix.
2. Sprinkle the lemon and lime juices, rum (if using), sugar and mint over the top and again toss gently to mix.
3. Garnish with tiny mint sprigs before serving.

name: Chocolate and Roasted Poblano Sauce
summary: A rich sauce with roasted poblanos, chocolate, and cream, served with chops and julienned sweet potatoes
ingredient_blocks:
1 tbsp olive oil
1/2 cup roasted poblanos (peeled, seeded, and chopped)
1/2 cup roasted onions (peeled and chopped)
2 tbsp minced shallots
1 tbsp minced garlic
Essence (seasoning blend)
3 cup chicken stock
1/4 cup semi-sweet chocolate (melted)
3 tbsp heavy cream
instruction_blocks:
1. Rub each chop with the melted chocolate and cover with the nut crust, covering the entire chop completely. In a saute pan, heat the remaining olive oil. When the pan is smoking hot, add the chops. Sear on one side for 3 minutes. Turn the chops over and place in the oven. Cook for 10-12 minutes for medium.
2. In a deep fryer, cook julienned sweet potatoes until crispy and golden, about 1 minute. Set aside for garnish.
3. In a sauce pan, heat the olive oil. When the oil is hot, saute the poblanos, onions, shallots, and garlic, for 2-3 minutes. Season with Essence.
4. Stir in the stock, bring to a boil and cook over high heat for 15 minutes.
5. Whisk in the chocolate and cream, continue simmering for 2 minutes.
6. Remove from the heat and puree in a blender. Return sauce to the heat and season with salt and pepper.
7. To plate: spoon the sauce on the bottom of the plate. Place the julienned sweet potato in the center of the sauce. Lay the chops against the potatoes with their bones crossing. Garnish with green onions, red and yellow peppers.
notes: Yield for sauce is 2 cups. Recipe appears to be for nut-crusted chops with chocolate poblano sauce. Essence is likely Emeril's Creole seasoning blend.
tags: sauce, Mexican-inspired, chocolate, poblano, fine dining, pork chops

name: Chicken Fajitas
summary: From HOW TO BOIL WATER SHOW #BW8340
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 lb boneless, skinless chicken breasts
2 tbsp olive oil
3 tbsp lime juice
2  cloves garlic
1 cup packed cilantro leaves
1/2  jalapeno (seeded)
salt and pepper
1 tbsp olive oil
1  large onion (peeled and cut into 1/2 inch slices)
1  green bell pepper (seeded and sliced)
2 tbsp olive oil
3  potatoes (cut into 1/2 inch cubes)
salt and pepper
cayenne
10  flour tortillas
instruction_blocks:
1. Flatten chicken breasts to 1/2 inch thickness between two sheets of plastic wrap with a mallet or rolling pin.
2. Place chicken breasts in glass baking dish. In a blender combine oil, lime juice, garlic, cilantro, jalapeno and salt and pepper. Blend until smooth and pour over chicken.
3. Cover with plastic wrap and let chicken marinate for 30 minutes.
4. In a large skillet over medium heat oil. Add onions and peppers and cook until they are tender and begin to brown.
5. In a 9x13 glass baking dish toss potatoes with oil and seasonings, bake in 375 degree oven until crispy, about 30 minutes. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
6. Heat a cast iron grill pan or an outdoor grill. Toast flour tortillas for about 30 seconds per side and keep warm in an aluminum foil pocket.
7. Grill the chicken on each side for 45 minutes. To serve slice chicken into even pieces.
notes: The cook time for grilling chicken appears to say '45 minutes' but this is likely a typo - should probably be 4-5 minutes per side. Recipe also includes a margarita recipe on the same page.
tags: Mexican, fajitas, chicken, grilling, Tex-Mex

name: Pumpernickel Rolls
summary: From Essence of Emeril Show #EE2304
ingredient_blocks:
2 tsp active dry yeast
1 cup warm water
1 tbsp melted butter
1 tsp sugar
6 tbsp dark molasses
1 tbsp caraway seeds
1/2 cup mashed potatoes
3 tbsp semi-sweet chocolate (melted)
2 cup rye flour
1/2 cup whole wheat flour
1/4 cup cornmeal
1/4 cup flour
1  egg (slightly beaten)
2 tbsp poppy seeds
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
2. In a large bowl dissolve the yeast in the water. Add the butter, sugar, and molasses and mix well.
3. Stir in the caraway seeds, potatoes, chocolate, rye and wheat flours, and the cornmeal. Using your hands, mix the ingredients together, do not over handle.
4. Form into a smooth ball. Grease a bowl, turn the dough into the bowl and cover with plastic wrap.
5. Allow the dough to rise for 1 1/4 hours or until double in size.
6. Flour the work surface and turn the dough out onto the floured surface and punch down. This will allow any gases to escape.
7. Using your hands flatten the dough into a circle about 1 inch thick.
8. Cut the dough into pieces and form each into a roll about 3 inches in diameter. Place the rolls on a parchment lined baking sheet.
9. Brush the rolls with the egg wash and sprinkle with the poppy seeds.
10. Bake for 25 minutes or until golden brown.
11. Remove from the oven and allow to cool.
notes: Yield: 18 rolls
tags: bread, rolls, pumpernickel, Emeril, baking

name: Carrot Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
3/4 cup all purpose flour
1/2 tsp baking powder
1/2 tsp salt
1 tsp grated orange zest
1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
1/3 cup vegetable oil
1  large egg
1/3 cup sugar
1/3 cup cooked and pureed carrots
1/4 cup golden raisins
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Lightly grease a cookie sheet.
2. In a mixing bowl whisk together the flour, baking powder, salt, orange zest, and cinnamon. Mix the vegetable oil, egg and sugar together and beat in the pureed carrots and raisins. Combine the wet with the dry ingredients.
3. Drop teaspoonfuls of dough onto the cookie sheet and space well apart. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes or until golden brown.
notes: Yield: 2 1/2 dozen. From Cooking Monday to Friday Show # MF 6659.
tags: cookies, carrot, baking, dessert

name: Fine Swiss Weisswurst
summary: A fine Swiss-style white sausage recipe from The Culinary Institute of America
ingredient_blocks:
3/4 lb veal (trimmed, cubed)
3/4 lb jowl fat (cubed)
11 oz ice
1 tbsp salt
1 tsp sugar
1 tsp white pepper
1 tsp dry mustard
1/4 tsp mace
1/2 tsp ginger
1 1/2 tsp lemon zest (blanched, chopped superfine)
2 tbsp non-fat dry milk powder
hog casings for stuffing
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine veal with salt and sugar.
2. Grind meat and jowl fat through the fine plate of heavy duty grinder.
3. Keep meat and jowl fat separate. Chill well. Place ground meat in food processor, add ice. Sprinkle spice mixture over ice.
4. Process mixture until very cold, 30 degrees on thermometer.
5. Stop machine, scrape down sides.
6. Continue processing until temperature rises to 40 degrees (the longer it takes, the finer the sausage). Mixture should resemble cake batter.
7. Add jowl fat and process until mixture reaches 45 degrees (emulsion occurs now).
8. Add non-fat milk powder and process until mixture reaches 58 degrees.
9. To stuff casings: Fill a commercial sausage stuffer evenly with mixture, making sure there are no air-pockets. Stuff hog casings, not too tight, tie off with fine kitchen string.
10. Poach sausages in a shallow pan of simmering water (water should be 165 degrees), until internal temperature of meat is 155 degrees. (Don't boil, casings can split)
11. Remove sausages to ice bath when cooked. When internal temperature reaches 60 degrees remove from water.
12. Cook, fry or grill and serve.
notes: Recipe from Chef-Instructor Lyde Buchtenkirch at The Culinary Institute of America. Recommended Wine: 1992 Niersteiner Rosenberg Silvaner Trocken, Weingut Mathildenhof Freiherr Heyl zu Herrnsheim
tags: German, Swiss, sausage, charcuterie, pork, veal

name: Baby Lima Beans Braised in Lemon
ingredient_blocks:
3 tbsp unsalted butter (at room temperature)
1 1/2 tsp lemon zest (grated)
1/3 cup olive oil
1  jalapeño pepper (seeded and minced)
3/4 cup pine nuts (toasted (see Note))
6 cup baby lima beans (fresh or frozen (about 2 pounds))
1 1/4 cup water
juice of 1 lemon
3/4 tbsp coarse sea salt
1/2 tsp black pepper (freshly ground)
8  green onions (thinly sliced)
1/2 bunch fresh mint (leaves only, cut into chiffonade)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mash the butter together with the lemon zest and set aside.
2. In a large heavy skillet heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the jalapeño, pine nuts, lima beans, water, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
3. Bring to a boil, stirring occasionally, then reduce the heat so that the liquid is simmering.
4. Simmer uncovered for about 8 minutes, until the beans have absorbed all the liquid. If the beans are still firm, add more water and continue cooking.
notes: To toast pine nuts, preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Spread the nuts on a cookie sheet and toast in the oven until golden.
tags: vegetarian, side dish, lima beans, lemon

name: Unknown Recipe
instruction_blocks: 1. Cook for about 15 minutes. Set aside to cool.
notes: This appears to be page 3 of a recipe. Most of the page content is illegible or heavily faded/degraded. Only the final instruction fragment is clearly visible at the top of the page.

name: Stuffed Roasted Pepper Sandwiches
summary: Roasted bell peppers stuffed with prosciutto and Fontina cheese
ingredient_blocks:
6  large red or yellow bell peppers
6  thin slices of prosciutto
6  thin slices of Fontina
olive oil (for brushing on peppers)
instruction_blocks:
1. Arrange the peppers on a grill rack above a charcoal fire, on wire racks positioned over the burners of a gas or electric stove, 2 to 3 inches under a preheated broiler, or in an oven preheated to 400 degrees F. Roast them until they are charred all over and tender inside, turning them frequently to insure they cook evenly, about 25 minutes in the oven, but less time by the other methods.
2. Remove them from the oven but leave the oven on. When the peppers are cool enough to handle, using a sharp knife, cut them in half vertically, like a sandwich. Care should be taken not to break the skin. Carefully remove the stem and lift off the skin. Remove all of the seeds.
3. Preheat an oven to 400 degrees F.
4. Place a slice each of prosciutto and fontina on the inside of one half of each pepper. Replace the matching half over the filling. Brush lightly with olive oil and place on a foil-lined baking sheet. Use two baking sheets if necessary. Place the peppers in the oven, placing the baking sheet(s) on the middle rack. Cook until the cheese is melted and the peppers are heated through, about 15 minutes.
5. Serve hot or warm.
notes: This document also contains a second recipe for Meat Loaf Stuffed with Ham and Parsley Frittata (Polpettone)
tags: Italian, appetizer, roasted peppers, sandwich, prosciutto, cheese

name: Dried Figs Stuffed with Roasted Almonds, Chocolate, and Fennel Apulia Style (Fichi Mandorlati)
summary: Soft, moist figs stuffed with roasted almonds, chocolate, and fennel seeds
ingredient_blocks:
50  soft, moist, unstrung dried figs (preferably Barese figs)
50  whole unblanched almonds (or twice that amount if figs are large)
2 tbsp fennel seeds
6 oz semi-sweet chocolate (broken up into small pieces)
25  bay leaves (approximately)
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat an oven to 350 degrees F. Place the almonds on baking sheet on middle rack. Roast until browned, about 15 minutes, stirring twice to ensure even roasting.
2. If almonds are still not roasted, leave them in longer, but check them every few minutes; because of their oily nature, they burn easily.
3. Remove almonds from oven and allow them to cool. Keep the oven on while you stuff the figs.
4. Make a slit in each fig large enough to allow passage of the almond. Place a pinch of fennel seeds, one or two almonds, depending on the size of the figs, and a sliver or two of chocolate in the center of each fig; press the opening closed.
5. When all of the figs have been stuffed, place the figs on a baking sheet. Slide the sheet onto the middle rack of the oven and allow the figs to bake until heated through, about 20 minutes.
6. Remove the figs from the oven and allow them to cool.
notes: Yield: 50 stuffed figs. This is an Apulia style Italian recipe.
tags: Italian, Apulian, dessert, figs, stuffed figs, holiday, appetizer

name: Pomarola (Pummarola) - Neapolitan Tomato Sauce with Carrot, Celery, Onion, Garlic, and Herbs
summary: A classic Neapolitan tomato sauce with vegetables and herbs
ingredient_blocks:
21/2 cup canned peeled plum tomatoes in juice (or 21/2 pounds fresh, sweet, mature vineripened plum tomatoes, peeled, seeded, and chopped)
4 tbsp extravirgin olive oil
2  large cloves garlic (bruised)
1  small red onion (finely chopped)
1  medium celery stalk (including leaves, finely chopped)
1  small carrot (scraped and finely chopped)
1 tbsp fresh Italian parsley (chopped)
1  small handful fresh basil (chopped)
2 tbsp tomato paste
1/2 tsp salt (scant, or to taste)
freshly milled black or white pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. If using canned tomatoes, drain the tomatoes in a colander, reserving their juice. Strain the captured tomato juice to hold back the seeds. Using your fingers, push out excess seeds from each tomato. Chop the tomatoes and set aside. If using fresh tomatoes, bring a large saucepan three-fourths full of water to a boil. Slip in the tomatoes and blanch for 30 to 45 seconds. Drain the tomatoes and immediately plunge them into cold water. Using a paring knife, lift off the skins and cut out the tough area around the core of each tomato. Cut the tomatoes into quarters lengthwise and, using your fingers, push out the excess seeds. Chop the tomatoes.
2. In a saucepan over medium-low heat, warm 3 tablespoons of the olive oil, the garlic, and onion together, and saute until the garlic is golden and the onion is translucent, 4 to 5 minutes. Then add the celery, carrot, parsley, and basil, and saute until the vegetables are soft, about 10 minutes. Press down on the vegetables with the back of a wooden spoon to release their flavors. Add the tomato paste and continue to saute for 3 minutes. Then add the tomatoes and their juice, cover partially, and simmer gently until thickened, about 45 minutes. Remove and discard the garlic cloves and season to taste with salt and pepper. Remove from the heat and stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil. If a smooth sauce is desired, allow it to cool somewhat. Position a food mill over a clean saucepan and pass the sauce through it, being sure to press out as much of the pulp as possible. Place over medium heat just long enough for the sauce to heat through, about 3 minutes. Remove from the heat and stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil.
notes: Yield: approximately 2 cups. This recipe is sufficient for saucing 1 pound of pasta. Ahead of time note: The sauce can be made 4 to 5 days in advance of using and stored tightly covered in the refrigerator, or it can be frozen for up to 3 months. Whether storing it in the refrigerator or freezer, leave out the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil. Stir it into the sauce after reheating. Also includes a second recipe: Salsa di Pomodoro a Crudo di Flavia (Flavia's Uncooked Tomato and Avocado Sauce) with ingredients: 11/2 pounds fresh, sweet, mature vineripened tomatoes or sweet, ripe cherry tomatoes; 4 large fresh basil leaves, torn into very small pieces or chopped; 1 large clove garlic, finely chopped or passed through a garlic press; 5 tablespoons extravirgin olive oil; Generous 1/2 teaspoon salt, or to taste; Freshly milled black or white pepper to taste.
tags: Italian, Neapolitan, tomato sauce, pasta sauce, vegetarian

name: Orange Almond Oatmeal Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
butter
sugar
egg
orange zest
almond extract
oatmeal
instruction_blocks:
1. Lightly butter a cookie sheet. Preheat the oven to 325 degrees.
2. Beat together the butter, sugar, egg, orange zest and almond extract.
3. With your fingers press the oatmeal into the butter until the dough just holds together.
4. Form the dough into tiny balls and with the tines of a fork press them down.
5. Bake for 10 to 15 minutes or until golden brown.
notes: This appears to be page 2 of a recipe - the ingredient amounts are missing as they were likely on page 1. Yield is 2 1/2 dozen cookies.
tags: cookies, oatmeal, orange, almond

name: Basic Tomato Sauce
ingredient_blocks:
1  Spanish onion (cut into 1/4-inch dice)
4  cloves garlic (thinly sliced)
3 oz virgin olive oil
4 tbsp fresh thyme (or 2 tablespoons dried)
1/2  medium carrot (finely shredded)
2  28-ounce cans of tomatoes (crushed and mixed well with their juices)
salt (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Saute the onion and garlic in the olive oil over medium heat until translucent, but not brown (about 10 minutes).
2. Add the thyme and carrot and cook 5 minutes more.
3. Add the tomatoes. Bring to a boil, lower the heat to just bubbling, stirring occasionally for 30 minutes.
4. Season with salt to taste.
5. Serve immediately, or set aside for further use. The sauce may be refrigerated for up to one week.
notes: The sauce may be refrigerated for up to one week. Document also contains a partial second recipe for clementines with balsamic vinegar (8 sweet clementines, 1 ounce best quality balsamic vinegar, 1 teaspoon sugar, fresh black pepper - served in martini glasses).
tags: Italian, sauce, tomato, basic, vegetarian

name: Crostini Toscane
summary: Tuscan-style crostini with chicken liver spread
ingredient_blocks:
3 tbsp virgin olive oil
1  medium red onion (cut into 1/8th inch dice)
1 tbsp capers
2 tbsp anchovy paste (or 4 anchovy fillets, rinsed and patted dry)
1/2 lb chicken livers
1/2 cup dry red wine (Chianti)
2 tbsp tomato paste
1 tbsp balsamic vinegar
salt and pepper (to taste)
8  1-inch thick slices of Italian peasant bread
instruction_blocks:
1. In a 12-inch skillet, heat 3 tablespoons olive oil until smoking over medium heat.
2. Add onion, capers, anchovy paste and cook until golden brown, about 8 to 10 minutes.
3. Add chicken livers and stir until lightly browned, about 4 to 5 minutes.
4. Add wine, tomato paste and balsamic vinegar and bring to boil.
5. Lower heat and simmer 15 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
6. Place mixture in food processor and pulse 5 or 6 times until chopped but not paste-like (There should be some lumps). Remove from processor and set aside.
7. Toast bread under broiler and spread liver mixture over one side of each piece and serve.
notes: From Molto Mario Show #MB5651. Yield: 4 servings as antipasto.
tags: Italian, Tuscan, antipasto, appetizer, chicken liver, crostini

name: Linguine with Prosciutto, Radicchio and Caraway Goat Cheese
ingredient_blocks:
4 tbsp virgin olive oil
1  large head radicchio (halved and thinly sliced (3 cups))
1/4 lb prosciutto di parma (thinly sliced)
1/2 lb linguine
1/4 lb caraway seed goat cheese (preferably Coach Farm)
salt and pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Bring 6 quarts water to a boil and add 2 tablespoons of salt.
2. In a 12" to 14" skillet, heat olive oil until smoking. Toss in radicchio, stirring quickly until just wilted (1 minute).
3. Julienne prosciutto slices and toss into pan with radicchio.
4. Cook pasta according to package directions.
5. Toss until well mixed and pour into heated serving bowl.
6. Shave caraway goat cheese over pasta and serve immediately.
notes: Some text in the original document appears partially obscured or unclear. The recipe specifies Coach Farm brand for the caraway seed goat cheese.
tags: Italian, pasta, prosciutto, quick meal

name: Classic Scrambled Eggs
summary: Creamy scrambled eggs with smoked salmon and dill, cooked in a double boiler
ingredient_blocks:
9  eggs
1/4 cup sour cream
2 tbsp unsalted butter
salt and white pepper (to taste)
1/4 cup chopped dill
4 oz smoked salmon (diced)
fresh dill sprigs (for garnish)
instruction_blocks:
1. Bring water to boil in bottom of double boiler.
2. In top stir in eggs, sour cream and unsalted butter. Beat lightly to break up eggs.
3. Turn heat down and over simmering water, stir eggs constantly until very lightly set, but still creamy - about 8 minutes.
4. Stir in salt and white pepper to taste.
5. Add dill and smoked salmon. Mix well.
6. Eggs will be creamy looking. Serve immediately with dill.
notes: This recipe is from Food In A Flash Show #FF2062. The document also contains partial recipes for a potato dish with bacon and an apple coffee cake, but they are incomplete.
tags: breakfast, brunch, eggs, smoked salmon, creamy

name: Barbecued Endives in Tarragon Oil with Pepato
summary: Grilled Belgian endives with tarragon oil and pecorino pepato cheese
ingredient_blocks:
4  large Belgian endives
1 tbsp virgin olive oil (plus 1/2 cup)
1 tbsp red wine vinegar
1 tbsp freshly ground black pepper
1/2 cup fresh tarragon leaves
1/4 lb pecorino pepato (sheep's milk cheese with peppercorns)
salt (to taste)
pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat barbecue or grill. Bring 1 quart water to a boil and prepare an ice bath on the side.
2. Cut the endives in half lengthwise. Mix 1 tablespoon virgin olive oil, vinegar and black pepper together.
3. Dredge the endives in vinegar mixture and place on hot grill. Cook until slightly charred (8 to 10 minutes) and remove to serving platter.
4. Meanwhile, drop tarragon leaves into boiling water. Blanch with 1/2 cup virgin olive oil and blend until smooth.
5. Spoon tarragon oil over endives, shave pepato over in long shards with a peeler and serve at room temperature.
notes: From Molto Mario Show #MB5654. Document also contains recipes for Po-Style Veal Saltimbocca and Basic Tomato Sauce.
tags: Italian, grilled, vegetable, appetizer, Molto Mario

name: Coconut Custard Pie
ingredient_blocks:
1  9 inch pie crust, prepared or home made
2 1/2 cup heavy cream
3 cup grated fresh coconut
4  large eggs
1/3 cup sugar
2 tsp vanilla
1/2 cup grated toasted coconut
lightly whipped cream and additional toasted coconut for garnish
instruction_blocks:
1. Trim the edge of the shell, leaving a 1/2 inch overhang. Crimp the edge over the shell decoratively. Prick the dough with a fork and chill for 30 minutes.
2. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees. Line with wax paper, weigh the shell in the lower third of the oven for 15 minutes. Remove the wax paper and rice. Reduce the oven temperature to 375 degrees and bake the shell for 10 minutes more or until golden. Transfer the shell to a rack and let cool.
3. Reduce the oven temperature to 350 degrees and butter the second pie plate. In a saucepan bring the half-and-half to a simmer, stir in 2 cups of the fresh coconut. Remove from the heat and let the mixture stand for 20 minutes, or until it is room temperature.
4. Put the pie plate in a larger pan, add enough hot water to the pan to come halfway up the sides of the plate and bake in the middle of the oven for twenty minutes or until the edges of the custard are set. The center of the custard will still be a bit loose. Add additional toasted coconut to the top of the custard and bake for another 10 minutes.
notes: Yield: 1 9-inch pie. Suggested wine - 1986 Chateau Climens (Sauternes - Barsac). Some portions of the scanned recipe are difficult to read due to image quality.
tags: pie, custard, coconut, dessert

name: Jewish Style Corned Beef
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb whole brisket (1-12 pound)
4 quart water
2 cup Kosher salt
1 tsp saltpeter
5  bay leaves
7 cloves garlic (smashed)
1/4 tsp ground cloves
2 tsp whole peppercorns
1 tsp allspice berries
1 tsp mustard seeds
1 cup white vinegar
1/2 cup white sugar
1/4 tsp cloves (for steamer pot)
2 tsp peppercorns (for steamer pot)
1 tsp allspice berries (for steamer pot)
1 tsp mustard seeds (for steamer pot)
3 cloves garlic (for steamer pot)
1/4 cup salt (for steamer pot)
instruction_blocks:
1. Place all ingredients except garlic and brisket in a large pot and bring to a boil and cool. Place garlic and brisket in non-reactive pot and cover with brining liquid, cover with a plate and weight for 3 weeks, turning after 1 1/2 weeks.
2. Add to a steamer pot: 1/4 teaspoon cloves, 2 teaspoons peppercorns, 1 teaspoon allspice berries, 1 teaspoon mustard seeds, 3 cloves garlic, 1/4 cup salt.
3. Remove meat from the brine and rinse. Add enough water to the steamer pot to reach just below the bottom of the steamer. Place meat in steamer.
4. Steam for 3 hours, until tender.
notes: Suggested wine: 1988 Erdener Treppchen Riesling Kabinett, Elisabeth Christoffel - Berres
tags: Jewish, corned beef, brisket, brined, steamed

name: Crab Meat Imperial
ingredient_blocks:
1 tbsp olive oil
3/4 cup yellow onions (small dice)
2 tbsp shallots (minced)
1/4 cup red peppers (small dice)
1/4 cup yellow peppers (small dice)
1/4 cup celery (small dice)
3/4 tsp salt
1/4 tsp cayenne
1 lb crab meat
1 tbsp garlic (minced)
2 tbsp parsley (chopped)
1/4 cup green onions (chopped)
1 cup mayonnaise (homemade or prepared)
2 tbsp Creole Mustard
1/4 tsp Tabasco
1/4 cup bread crumbs (dried fine)
1/2 tbsp Rustic Rub
2 tbsp chives (chopped, for garnish)
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees.
2. In a large saute pan, heat the olive oil. When the pan is smoking hot, add the onions, shallots, peppers, celery, salt and cayenne.
3. Saute for 5 minutes or until the vegetables are wilted. Add the crab meat, garlic, parsley and green onions, saute for 1-2 minutes.
4. Remove from the heat and cool for about 30 minutes.
5. In a mixing bowl, combine the crab meat mixture with 3/4 cup of the mayonnaise, the mustard and Tabasco. Mix until thoroughly incorporated.
6. Spoon the mixture into 4 coquille shells.
7. In a mixing bowl, combine the breadcrumbs, the remaining mayonnaise and the Rustic Rub together.
8. Spread the top of the crab mixture with the bread crumb mixture.
9. Bake for 20 minutes, or until bubbly and brown.
10. Place the shells on a platter and garnish with chopped chives.
tags: seafood, crab, baked, Creole, appetizer

name: Oyster Suzette
ingredient_blocks:
12  shucked oysters, leaving in their shell
1 cup diced bacon
1/2 cup chopped onions
2 tbsp minced shallots
1 tbsp minced garlic
1/4 cup small diced green peppers
1 tbsp Crystal Hot sauce
1 cup bread crumbs
Essence
1/2 cup grated Parmesan Reggiano Cheese
1 cup prepared Hollandaise sauce (warm)
2 tbsp chopped chives
Rock salt for the platter
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees. In a hot saute pan, render the bacon until crisp, about 2-3 minutes. Add the onions, shallots, garlic, and peppers. Saute for 2-3 minutes.
2. Remove the mixture from the heat and allow to cool. In a mixing bowl, combine the bacon mixture, Crystal sauce, bread crumbs and cheese together. Season with Essence.
3. Place 2 tablespoons of the bread crumb mixture on top of each oyster. Bake for 6-8 minutes or until golden brown.
4. Remove from the oven. Place the oysters on a platter with the rock salt. Drizzle each oyster with the Hollandaise Sauce. Garnish with chives.
notes: From Essence of Emeril Show #EE2307
tags: seafood, oysters, Cajun, appetizer

name: Farfalle Pasta
summary: Homemade farfalle (bow-tie) pasta with sauce
ingredient_blocks:
pasta dough (prepared and rolled through machine)
butter (for sauce)
1/2 cup cheese
pine nuts
marjoram
pepper (to taste)
parmigiano (additional for serving)
salt (for pasta water)
instruction_blocks:
1. Set the strip aside and cover with clean kitchen towels. Roll out each portion completely in this same manner, covering them well until all the dough is passed through the machine.
2. To form the farfalle, using a fluted pastry wheel, cut each sheet of pasta in half lengthwise. Then cut every 2 inches across the width of each strip to form rectangles about 2 inches long and 1 inch wide.
3. Using your thumb and forefinger, press two opposite long sides together, pinching firmly in the middle so that a bow is formed.
4. Line several baking sheets with clean kitchen towels. Place the farfalle on the towels and allow them to dry for 10 minutes. (At this point, they can be covered with dry kitchen towels and set aside for up to several hours before cooking, if desired.)
5. To cook the pasta, bring the water to a rolling boil in a large pot. Pick up each of the towels holding the farfalle by two opposite corners, so the contents can be easily and quickly dumped into the boiling water.
6. Dump all of the farfalle into the boiling water as quickly as possible so all the pasta begins cooking at the same time. Stir immediately and add the salt to the water. Stir well again and cover.
7. When the pasta rises to the surface, it is cooked, about 1 minute from the time the water returns to the boil. Be certain not to overcook; the entire cooking process should take no longer than 3 to 4 minutes.
8. Meanwhile, to make the sauce, melt the butter in a skillet large enough to accommodate the pasta later.
9. Drain the farfalle, transfer them to the skillet, and toss with the butter. Using two large forks and keeping the gentlest possible flame under the skillet, distribute the sauce well through the pasta.
10. Add the pine nuts, the 1/2 cup cheese, the marjoram, and pepper to taste and serve immediately. Pass additional parmigiano at the table.
notes: This appears to be page 2 of a longer recipe. The beginning instructions for making the pasta dough are on a previous page. Yield is 4 to 6 servings.
tags: Italian, pasta, homemade pasta, farfalle, bow-tie pasta

name: Grilled Beef, Roasted Pepper, and Raisin Risotto
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb top round
2 tbsp olive oil
1 tbsp Essence
1 tbsp olive oil (for risotto)
1 cup julienne yellow onions
2 tbsp minced shallots
1 tbsp minced garlic
2 1/2 cup Arborio rice
2 cup veal reduction
1/4 cup red wine
1/3 cup Dry Marsala
8 cup meat stock
1/2 cup julienne roasted green peppers
1/2 cup julienne roasted red peppers
1/2 cup julienne roasted yellow peppers
1/2 cup golden raisins
1/2 cup Romano Cheese
1 tbsp finely diced red peppers
1 tbsp finely diced yellow peppers
2 tbsp chopped green onions
3 oz block of Romano Cheese
3  grilled whole green onions
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat the grill. Season the top round with the olive oil and Essence. Place on the grill. Grill for 3-4 minutes on each side for medium-rare.
2. For the risotto: In a saute pan, heat the olive oil. When the pan is smoking hot, add the onions, shallots and garlic. Saute the vegetables for 1 minute.
3. Using a wooden spoon, stir in the rice, saute for 1 minute. While stirring constantly, add the veal reduction, wine, Marsala, and meat stock, one cup at a time.
4. Cook the risotto for 10-12 minutes, stirring constantly. Fold in the peppers, cheese and raisins. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Remove the round from the grill and slice on the bias into 2 ounce portions.
6. To assemble, mound the risotto in the center of the platter. Fan the meat around the risotto. Garnish with the brunoised peppers, grilled green onion, and with a peeler, cut off thin slices of the cheese over the top of the risotto.
tags: beef, risotto, Italian, grilled, peppers

name: Shrimp and Saffron Risotto
ingredient_blocks:
1 tbsp olive oil
1/2 cup chopped onions
2 tbsp minced shallots
2 tbsp minced garlic
2 cup Arborio rice
4 cup shrimp stock
1 tbsp unsalted butter
2 tsp salt
1 tsp white pepper
1/2 cup chopped green onions
1/2 cup chopped tomatoes (peeled and seeded)
3  threads of saffron
1 tsp Essence
1/2 lb medium shrimp (peeled, tail off and halved)
3/4 cup grated Parmesan Reggiano Cheese (plus 1/4 cup for garnish)
6  fried spinach leaves
Essence
instruction_blocks:
1. In a saute pan, heat the olive oil. When the pan is smoking hot, saute the onions, shallots and garlic for 1 minute.
2. Using a wooden spoon, stir in the rice and saute for 1 minute. Stir in 1/2 cup of the stock at a time until all of the liquid is incorporated, about 3 minutes.
3. Season with salt and pepper. Fold in the green onions and tomatoes. Season with Essence and bring up to boil, about 10 minutes.
4. Fold in the shrimp and simmer for 2 minutes.
5. Spoon the risotto into a shallow bowl. Garnish with fried spinach leaves, Parmesan Reggiano Cheese and Essence.
notes: From Essence of Emeril Show #EE2318. Page also contains a second recipe for Oven Roasted Tomatoes with Mushroom Risotto and Fresh Mozzarella Cheese.
tags: risotto, seafood, shrimp, Italian, saffron

name: Salsa Alla Bolognese (Tomato and Meat Sauce)
summary: Traditional Italian meat sauce for pasta
ingredient_blocks:
3 tbsp sweet (unsalted) butter
1  small white or yellow onion (chopped fine)
1  small celery stalk (chopped fine)
1/2  small carrot (chopped fine)
1/4 lb lean beef (good quality, preferably chuck, ground twice)
1/4 lb lean pork (good quality, preferably belly, ground twice)
1/4 lb lean veal (good quality, ground twice)
1/8 lb prosciutto crudo (thicksliced, chopped)
1/2 tsp salt (or to taste)
fresh pepper (to taste)
1/2 cup dry white wine (good quality)
2/3 cup milk
1/8 tsp nutmeg (preferably freshgrated)
21/2 cup Italian plum tomatoes (1 28ounce can, peeled, seeded and chopped, in puree)
1/2 cup cream (optional)
1  white truffle (thinly sliced at the end, optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a large, wide Dutch oven or large, deep skillet, saute the butter, chopped onion, celery, and carrot until they are soft but not browned.
2. Add the ground meats and prosciutto, turning the flame very low. It is very important that the meat heat very gently, only enough to color it on the outside. Not searing the meat allows it to absorb the other flavors of the sauce, and will also make it more delicate and creamy.
3. Break up and stir the meat as you heat it. You should get it to a uniform pink, not brown color.
4. Add the salt, pepper and wine. Simmer very gently for several minutes until the alcohol evaporates and the liquid begins to be absorbed by the meat and vegetables.
5. Stir in milk and nutmeg. It is important to add the milk before adding the tomatoes for it to be absorbed directly by the meat.
6. Cook gently for 10 minutes. Add the tomatoes. When the sauce begins to simmer, turn the flame down as low as you can. If your burner cannot be regulated low enough, insert a "flame tamer." Simmer in this way, partially covered, for about 4 hours, stirring it gently now and then.
7. Check for seasonings and add an additional pinch of nutmeg if necessary. If the sauce is being used on fresh noodles (not lasagne), and you have a mind to be extravagant, add the cream and the finely sliced truffle when it is finished.
8. Stir and pour over cooked fresh pasta.
notes: This recipe is sufficient for saucing 1 pound of pasta. Ahead of time note: Make the sauce up to 2 hours in advance. Cover and leave at room temperature until ready to serve. Yield: Sauce for 1 pound of pasta.
tags: Italian, meat sauce, pasta sauce, Bolognese, slow-cooked

name: Pomarola (Pummarola) - Neapolitan Tomato Sauce with Carrot, Celery, Onion, Garlic, and Herbs
summary: A classic Neapolitan tomato sauce sufficient for saucing 1 pound of pasta
ingredient_blocks:
21/2 cup canned, peeled plum tomatoes in juice (seeded and chopped, or 21/2 pounds fresh, sweet, mature vineripened plum tomatoes, peeled)
4 tbsp extravirgin olive oil
2  large cloves garlic (bruised)
1  small red onion (finely chopped)
1  medium celery stalk (including leaves, finely chopped)
1  small carrot (scraped and finely chopped)
1 tbsp fresh Italian parsley (chopped)
1  small handful of fresh basil (chopped)
2 tbsp tomato paste
1/2 tsp salt (scant, or to taste)
freshly milled black or white pepper (to taste)
