instruction_blocks:
1. If using canned tomatoes, drain the tomatoes in a colander, reserving their juice. Strain the captured tomato juice to hold back the seeds. Using your fingers, push out excess seeds from each tomato. Chop the tomatoes and set aside. If using fresh tomatoes, bring a large saucepan three-fourths full of water to a boil. Slip in the tomatoes and blanch for 30 to 45 seconds. Drain the tomatoes and immediately plunge them into cold water. Using a paring knife, lift off the skins and cut out the tough area around the core of each tomato. Cut the tomatoes into quarters lengthwise and, using your fingers, push out the excess seeds. Chop the tomatoes.
2. In a saucepan over medium-low heat, warm 3 tablespoons of the olive oil, the garlic, and onion together, and sauté until the garlic is golden and the onion is translucent, 4 to 5 minutes. Then add the celery, carrot, parsley, and basil, and sauté until the vegetables are soft, about 10 minutes. Press down on the vegetables with the back of a wooden spoon to release their flavors. Add the tomato paste and continue to sauté for 3 minutes. Then add the tomatoes and their juice, cover partially, and simmer gently until thickened, about 45 minutes. Remove and discard the garlic cloves and season to taste with salt and pepper. Remove from the heat and stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil. If a smooth sauce is desired, allow it to cool somewhat. Position a food mill over a clean saucepan and pass the sauce through it, being sure to press out as much of the pulp as possible. Place over medium heat just long enough for the sauce to heat through, about 3 minutes. Remove from the heat and stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil.
notes: Yield: approximately 2 cups. This recipe is sufficient for saucing 1 pound of pasta. Ahead of time note: The sauce can be made 4 to 5 days in advance of using and stored tightly covered in the refrigerator, or it can be frozen for up to 3 months. Whether storing it in the refrigerator or freezer, leave out the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil. Stir it into the sauce after reheating. A second recipe for Salsa di Pomodoro a Crudo di Flavia (Flavia's Uncooked Tomato and Avocado Sauce) is also included on this page.
tags: Italian, Neapolitan, tomato sauce, pasta sauce, vegetarian

name: Dried Figs Stuffed with Roasted Almonds, Chocolate, and Fennel Apulia Style (Fichi Mandorlati)
summary: Italian-style stuffed dried figs with roasted almonds, chocolate, and fennel seeds
ingredient_blocks:
50  soft, moist, unstrung dried figs (preferably Barese figs)
50  whole unblanched almonds (or twice that amount if figs are large)
2 tbsp fennel seeds
6 oz semi-sweet chocolate (broken up into small pieces)
25  bay leaves (approximately)
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat an oven to 350 degrees F. Place the almonds on baking sheet on middle rack. Roast until browned, about 15 minutes, stirring twice to ensure even roasting. If almonds are still not roasted, leave them in longer, but check them every few minutes; because of their oily nature, they burn easily. Remove almonds from oven and allow them to cool. Keep the oven on while you stuff the figs.
2. Make a slit in each fig large enough to allow passage of the almond. Place a pinch of fennel seeds, one or two almonds, depending on the size of the figs, and a sliver or two of chocolate in the center of each fig; press the opening closed.
3. When all of the figs have been stuffed, place the figs on a baking sheet. Slide the sheet onto the middle rack of the oven and allow the figs to bake until heated through, about 20 minutes.
4. Remove the figs from the oven and allow them to cool.
notes: This appears to be page 2 of a larger recipe document that also contains instructions for polpettone (meatloaf). The polpettone recipe mentions: placing in an ungreased baking dish, adding half of reserved bouillon cube liquid, baking at middle rack for 50 minutes, basting several times, and adding wine to the pan when done. That recipe yields 15-18 servings.
tags: Italian, Apulian, dessert, figs, stuffed figs, holiday, Christmas

name: Stuffed Roasted Pepper Sandwiches
summary: Roasted bell peppers stuffed with prosciutto and Fontina cheese
ingredient_blocks:
6  large red or yellow bell peppers
6  thin slices of prosciutto
6  thin slices of Fontina
olive oil (for brushing on peppers)
instruction_blocks:
1. Arrange the peppers on a grill rack above a charcoal fire, on wire racks positioned over the burners of a gas or electric stove, 2 to 3 inches under a preheated broiler, or in an oven preheated to 400 degrees F. Roast them until they are charred all over and tender inside, turning them frequently to insure they cook evenly, about 25 minutes in the oven, but less time by the other methods.
2. Remove them from the oven but leave the oven on. When the peppers are cool enough to handle, using a sharp knife, cut them in half vertically, like a sandwich. Care should be taken not to break the skin. Carefully remove the stem and lift off the skin. Remove all of the seeds.
3. Preheat an oven to 400 degrees F.
4. Place a slice each of prosciutto and fontina on the inside of one half of each pepper. Replace the matching half over the filling.
5. Brush lightly with olive oil and place on a foil-lined baking sheet. Use two baking sheets if necessary. Place the peppers in the oven, placing the baking sheet(s) on the middle rack. Cook until the cheese is melted and the peppers are heated through, about 15 minutes.
6. Serve hot or warm.
notes: This recipe is from Chef Du Jour, Julia Della Croce, Show #DJ9213
tags: Italian, appetizer, roasted peppers, sandwich, prosciutto, cheese

name: Unknown Recipe
instruction_blocks: 1. Cook for about 15 minutes. Set aside to cool.
notes: This appears to be only a partial page (page 3) of a recipe. The only legible text visible is a cooking instruction mentioning '15 minutes' and 'Set aside to cool.' The rest of the page contains very faded or illegible text that cannot be reliably transcribed.

name: Baby Lima Beans Braised in Lemon
ingredient_blocks:
3 tbsp unsalted butter (at room temperature)
1 1/2 tsp lemon zest (grated)
1/3 cup olive oil
1  jalapeno pepper (seeded and minced)
3/4 cup pine nuts (toasted (see Note))
6 cup baby lima beans (fresh or frozen (about 2 pounds))
1 1/4 cup water
juice of 1 lemon
3/4 tbsp coarse sea salt
1/2 tsp black pepper (freshly ground)
8  green onions (thinly sliced)
1/2 bunch fresh mint (leaves only, cut into chiffonade)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mash the butter together with the lemon zest and set aside.
2. In a large heavy skillet heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the jalapeno, pine nuts, lima beans, water, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
3. Bring to a boil, stirring occasionally, then reduce the heat so that the liquid is simmering.
4. Simmer uncovered for about 8 minutes, until the beans have absorbed all the liquid. If the beans are not fully cooked, add more liquid and continue cooking.
notes: To toast pine nuts, preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Spread the nuts on a cookie sheet and toast in the oven until golden.
tags: vegetarian, lima beans, lemon, side dish

name: Clams with Quinoa
ingredient_blocks:
24  littleneck clams
3 cup shucked cherrystone clams (with their liquor)
4 cup clam juice or water (as needed)
3/4 cup quinoa
3 tbsp olive oil
1  onion (finely chopped)
4 cloves garlic (finely chopped)
2  jalapeno chiles (seeded and finely chopped)
1  bay leaf
1 tbsp ground cumin
1/4 tsp freshly ground black pepper
1 tbsp tomato paste
8  small red rose potatoes (quartered)
1 cup white wine
2 cloves garlic (slivered)
1 tsp salt
1/2 tsp red pepper flakes
1  red bell pepper (stemmed, seeded, and cut into fine julienne)
1/2 bunch cilantro (leaves only, finely chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. Wash and vigorously scrub the littlenecks under cold running water. Place them on a tray, cover with a damp cloth, and set aside until ready to serve.
2. Finely chop the shucked cherrystones and set them aside, refrigerate. Pour the clam liquor through a strainer lined with a double thickness of dampened cheesecloth. Add enough additional clam juice or water to make the liquid up to 5 1/2 cups. Set aside, refrigerate.
3. Place the quinoa in a bowl and add enough cold water to cover by about 2 inches. Let soak for 5 minutes, then massage the grain between the palms of your hands for a moment. Strain through a fine strainer, rinse with cold running water, and repeat the soaking and rubbing process. Set the quinoa aside to drain.
4. In a large casserole with a cover, preferably earthenware, heat the olive oil over medium-low heat. Add the onion and saute until it is totally translucent but not browned, about 8 minutes. Increase the heat to medium and add the garlic, chiles, bay leaf, cumin, and pepper. Stir over the heat for 2 minutes more, or until the aroma of the garlic is released. Add the tomato paste, chopped clams, and potatoes and stir together to mix. Pour in 4 1/2 cups of the reserved clam juice, bring up to a simmer and cook for 15 minutes. Stir in the quinoa and cook for 15 minutes more, stirring occasionally.
5. While the quinoa is cooking, in another large saucepan reduce the white wine by half over medium heat. Add the garlic, salt, pepper flakes, julienned red pepper, and the remaining 1/2 cup clam juice and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add the littlenecks, cover the pan and reduce the heat to low, and cook for 10 to 15 minutes, or until the clams have barely opened, gently stirring the mixture once during the cooking time. Mound the quinoa mixture onto a large platter and spoon the littlenecks, with all of their juice and the peppers, over the top. Scatter the cilantro over and serve immediately.
notes: From Too Hot Tamales Show #TH 6219
tags: seafood, clams, quinoa, Mexican-inspired

name: Fine Swiss Weisswurst
summary: A professional Swiss-style white sausage recipe from The Culinary Institute of America
ingredient_blocks:
3/4 lb veal (trimmed, cubed)
3/4 lb jowl fat (cubed)
11 oz ice
1 tbsp salt
1 tsp sugar
1 tsp white pepper
1 tsp dry mustard
1/4 tsp mace
1/2 tsp ginger
1 1/2 tsp lemon zest (blanched, chopped superfine)
2 tbsp non-fat dry milk powder
hog casings for stuffing
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine veal with salt and sugar.
2. Grind meat and jowl fat through the fine plate of heavy duty grinder.
3. Keep meat and jowl fat separate. Chill well. Place ground meat in food processor, add ice. Sprinkle spice mixture over ice.
4. Process mixture until very cold, 30 degrees on thermometer.
5. Stop machine, scrape down sides.
6. Continue processing until temperature rises to 40 degrees (the longer it takes, the finer the sausage.) Mixture should resemble cake batter.
7. Add jowl fat and process until mixture reaches 45 degrees (emulsion occurs now).
8. Add non-fat milk powder and process until mixture reaches 58 degrees.
9. To stuff casings: Fill a commercial sausage stuffer evenly with mixture, making sure there are no air-pockets. Stuff hog casings, not too tight, tie off with fine kitchen string.
10. Poach sausages in a shallow pan of simmering water (water should be 165 degrees), until internal temperature of meat is 155 degrees. (Don't boil, casings can split)
11. Remove sausages to ice bath when cooked. When internal temperature reaches 60 degrees remove from water.
12. Cook, fry or grill and serve.
notes: Recommended Wine: 1992 Niersteiner Rosenberg Silvaner Trocken, Weingut Mathildenhof Freiherr Heyl zu Herrnsheim. Recipe from Chef-Instructor Lyde Buchtenkirch, The Culinary Institute of America. TASTE SHOW #TS4839.
tags: German, Swiss, sausage, charcuterie, professional, CIA recipe

name: Spinach Soup
ingredient_blocks:
1/4 cup chopped onion
1/2  clove garlic (minced)
1 tbsp butter or margarine
1 can chicken broth (46 ounces)
1/2 cup Ronzoni Acini Pepe
1/4 tsp nutmeg
1/8 tsp pepper
1  frozen chopped spinach (10 ounce package)
Grated Parmesan cheese (for topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cook and stir onion and garlic in hot butter until tender, but not browned.
2. Add chicken broth and bring to a boil.
3. Add pasta, nutmeg and pepper; simmer about 5 minutes.
4. Add frozen chopped spinach and simmer 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Season to taste with salt if desired.
6. Sprinkle with cheese.
tags: soup, spinach, pasta, Italian

name: Hungarian Paprikash
ingredient_blocks:
3 lb chicken
2  onions (finely chopped)
3 tbsp Crisco
1 tbsp paprika
1 tsp salt
1 pint sour cream
7 tbsp flour
instruction_blocks:
1. Brown onions lightly in Crisco.
2. Add chicken and paprika. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
3. Cover and cook slowly until tender.
4. Remove chicken from pan.
5. Add enough water or chicken broth to drippings to make 3 cups.
6. Mix flour with sour cream.
7. Add to boiling stock. Boil until thickened.
8. Put chicken back into sour cream gravy.
9. Serve with dumplings.
tags: Hungarian, chicken, comfort food, Eastern European

name: Sausage Bread
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 lb sausage
1 cup sifted flour
1/2 tsp baking powder
1/4 tsp baking soda
1/2 tsp salt
2 tbsp shortening
1/2 cup buttermilk
1 tbsp grated onion
1 cup shredded cheese
instruction_blocks:
1. Crumble sausage in the bottom of 9" pie plate. Bake in a 425 degree oven for 10 minutes.
2. Pour off fat, leaving about 1 tablespoon.
3. Sift flour with baking powder, baking soda and salt.
4. Cut in shortening and stir in buttermilk to make a rather soft dough.
5. Mix in onions and cheese.
6. Knead slightly and roll out to a 9" circle.
7. Place on top of sausage and bake in 400 degree oven for about 20 minutes.
tags: bread, sausage, savory, cheese

name: Potato Casserole
ingredient_blocks:
2 lb frozen hashbrown potatoes or frozen cubed potatoes
1/2 cup minced onion
1/2 cup oleo or butter (melted)
1 tsp salt
2 cup sour cream (1 pint)
2 cup shredded cheddar cheese
1 can Campbell's mushroom soup (do not dilute)
1 cup crushed corn flakes
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix all ingredients except the potatoes.
2. Let potatoes thaw about 10 minutes.
3. Grease pan. Put potatoes on bottom.
4. Add remainder of ingredients.
5. Add corn flakes to cover top.
6. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 to 50 minutes.
tags: casserole, potato, comfort food, side dish

name: Mushroom Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup mushrooms (stems diced, caps sliced)
1/4 cup onion (finely diced)
1/4 cup cooked carrots (finely diced)
1/4 cup cooked celery (fined diced)
5 tbsp butter
7 tbsp flour
4 cup chicken broth
1 cup light cream or milk
2 tbsp dry Vermouth
salt (to taste)
pepper (to taste)
chopped parsley (optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. Sauté mushroom stems and caps in butter with onion about 5 minutes.
2. Add Vermouth. Cook 3 minutes.
3. Add flour. Cook stirring until thickened.
4. Cool slightly and add cream or milk and the cooked carrots and celery.
5. Heat through. Do not boil.
6. Add salt and pepper to taste.
7. Sprinkle with parsley if desired.
tags: soup, mushroom, creamy, comfort food

name: Pepper Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
2 1/2 cup flour
1 tsp salt
1 tsp pepper
2  packages dry yeast
1/2 cup warm water
1/2 cup Wesson Oil (I use a little less)
instruction_blocks:
1. Add flour, pepper, salt. Consistency should be like bread dough.
2. Put in Tupperware with cover to rise so it will double in bulk (about 30 minutes).
3. Oil the cookie sheet lightly.
4. Roll out dough into long strings about 1/2" thick, slice into 3" pieces and knot.
5. Bake at 400 degrees for the first 10 minutes.
6. Lower temperature to 350 degrees and bake for 20 to 30 minutes.
notes: Total baking time is 30-40 minutes. Start at 400°F for 10 minutes, then reduce to 350°F for remaining 20-30 minutes.
tags: cookies, yeast, savory, pepper

name: Broccoli Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp grated onion
1/2 cup butter or margarine (melted)
1/4 cup all-purpose flour
1 cup milk
1 cup half and half
2 cup chicken broth
1/2 tsp salt
1/8 tsp garlic powder
1/4 to 1/2 tsp dried basil
3 cup fresh broccoli (chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. Sauté onion in butter in Dutch oven.
2. Add flour, stirring until smooth. Cook 1 minute, stirring constantly.
3. Gradually add milk, half and half, chicken broth. Cook over medium heat stirring constantly until the mixture is thickened and bubbly.
4. Stir in salt, garlic powder and basil.
5. Add broccoli, cover and cook over medium heat 15 minutes or until broccoli is tender.
notes: Recipe attributed to Elaine. Yield: 5 cups
tags: soup, broccoli, comfort food, vegetable

name: Black Bean Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup black beans
3 tbsp butter
2 cup onions (chopped)
1 cup celery (chopped)
1  bouquet garni (parsley, 2 sprigs fresh thyme or 1/4 teaspoon dried thyme, 1 bay leaf)
1  smoked ham hock
6-10 cup brown stock or beef broth
4 cup water
1 tsp salt
1/4 tsp pepper
1/3 cup dark rum
instruction_blocks:
1. Soak black beans in water to cover for 4 to 6 hours, then drain.
2. In a heavy kettle melt butter and add chopped onions, chopped celery and bouquet garni. Cook vegetables over low heat for 10 minutes.
3. Add ham hock, beans and brown stock or beef broth, water, salt and pepper.
4. Bring the soup to a boil, reduce the heat and simmer adding more liquid if necessary to keep beans covered.
5. Cook for 3 to 4 hours.
6. Put soup through food mill. Return to kettle.
7. Add rum. Reheat soup and season with lemon juice, salt and pepper to taste.
notes: Lemon juice is mentioned for final seasoning but not listed in ingredients
tags: soup, black beans, comfort food

name: Lentil Soup
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup margarine (1 stick)
2 cup chopped onions
1 lb dried lentils
10 cup water
2 tbsp chopped parsley
2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp ground black pepper
2  bay leaves
instruction_blocks:
1. Melt margarine in a large heavy saucepan. Add onions and sauté until tender, about 5 minutes.
2. Stir in lentils, water, parsley, salt and pepper. Add bay leaves. Bring to a boil.
3. Cover, reduce heat and simmer 1 1/2 to 2 hours, or until lentils are tender. Discard bay leaves.
4. Serve hot. If desired, puree in blender or processor before serving.
tags: soup, lentils, vegetarian

name: Creamy Broccoli Au Gratin Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp butter or margarine
1/2 cup chopped onion
1 3/4 cup water
1 package Uncle Ben's Country Inn Broccoli Rice Au Gratin (4.5 ounces)
1/4 cup flour
1/4 tsp dry mustard
1 3/4 cup chicken broth or bouillon
2 cup milk
1/2 cup shredded American cheese
1  green onion (cut into short, thin strips)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cook onion in butter in large saucepan until tender.
2. Add water and contents of rice and seasoning packets. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover and simmer 20 minutes.
3. Combine flour and dry mustard in a small bowl. Gradually add about 1/2 cup of the broth, stirring until smooth.
4. Stir remaining broth, flour mixture and milk into rice mixture. Heat to a simmer, stirring occasionally. Continue simmering until slightly thickened.
5. Stir in cheese. Sprinkle with green onion.
tags: soup, broccoli, cheese, comfort food

name: Pasta With Carbonara Sauce
ingredient_blocks:
4  eggs
1/4 cup butter or margarine
1/4 cup cream
1 lb fettucini or spaghetti
2 oz Hormel sliced pepperoni (3 1/2 ounce packages)
1 cup Parmesan cheese (grated)
1/4 cup fresh parsley (snipped)
pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Let eggs, butter or margarine and cream stand at room temperature for 2 to 3 hours.
2. Beat together the eggs and cream just until blended.
3. Add pasta to a large amount of boiling, salted water. Cook 10-12 minutes or until tender, but firm. Drain well.
4. Heat an ovenproof serving dish in a 250 degree oven.
5. Turn pasta into heated serving dish. Toss pasta with butter and pepperoni.
6. Pour egg mixture over and toss until pasta is well coated.
7. Add cheese and parsley, toss to mix. Serve immediately.
notes: Makes 10 to 12 side dishes or 4 to 5 main servings
tags: Italian, pasta, carbonara, comfort food

name: Chicken Escarole Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup low sodium chicken broth
2 cup water
1/2 lb chicken breast (skinned and boned)
2  medium carrots (peeled and chopped)
2  medium stalks celery (chopped)
1  medium yellow onion (chopped)
1/4 cup minced parsley
1/8 tsp black pepper
1/2 lb escarole, spinach or Swiss chard (trimmed)
1  large ripe tomato (peeled, cored, seeded and chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a large heavy sauce pan, over moderate heat, bring the chicken broth and water to a simmer.
2. Add chicken, carrots, celery, onion, parsley and pepper. Cover and cook until chicken is tender, about 20 minutes. Remove the chicken and set aside.
3. Stir in the escarole and tomato into the sauce pan and simmer uncovered for 5 to 10 minutes or until the escarole is tender.
4. When chicken is cool enough to handle, cut it into 1/2 inch cubes, return to sauce pan and bring the soup to serving temperature.
tags: soup, chicken, healthy, Italian

name: Black Bean Soup
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb dried black beans
1/2 cup diced salt pork (1/4 pound)
1 cup chopped onions
1/2 cup chopped green pepper
1/2 tsp minced garlic
2  bay leaves
2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp black pepper
1/4 cup wine vinegar
6 cup cooked rice
12 tbsp minced raw onion (for garnish)
instruction_blocks:
1. Wash beans. Cover with water in a large pan. Soak overnight. In the morning, add more water, if necessary, to a depth of 1/2 inch above the beans.
2. Cook the salt pork over low heat in a 10 inch skillet until lightly brown. Add the browned pork to the beans.
3. Place onion, green pepper, and garlic in the remaining fat, adjust heat to moderately high, and cook, stirring often until onions turn pale gold.
4. Add the vegetables and fat to the beans. Add bay leaves, salt, and pepper and 1 tablespoon of the vinegar.
5. Cover tightly and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to very low and slowly simmer until beans are tender (from 1 1/2 to 2 1/2 hours).
6. When beans are tender and liquid has thickened slightly, add remaining vinegar.
7. To serve, spoon 1/2 cup boiled rice into a heated soup plate. Ladle 1 cup beans with puree over rice. Garnish with 2 tablespoons of minced onion.
notes: May be frozen. Optional: Offer cruets of olive oil and additional wine vinegar.
tags: soup, black beans, Cuban, comfort food, freezer-friendly

name: Lentil Vegetable Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup lentils
2  slices of Pancetta (Italian bacon)
1/2 cup onion (chopped)
1/2 cup celery
1/2 cup carrots
3 tbsp parsley (snipped)
1  clove garlic (chopped)
salt and pepper (to taste)
1/2 tsp dried oregano (crushed)
2 cup tomatoes
2  cans chicken broth and 1 can of water
instruction_blocks:
1. Rinse lentils, drain. Add chicken broth and water. Bring to a boil. Lower heat and simmer for 1 hour.
2. Make roux with onions, bacon, parsley and vegetables until they are soft, about 10 minutes. Add tomatoes, oregano and cook for 20 minutes. If desired add 1/2 cup of red wine. Add all to lentils and simmer for 30 minutes.
3. I like my soup to be thick. I puree about a cup or more of the mixture and return to the pot.
notes: May be frozen.
tags: soup, lentils, vegetable, Italian

name: Luci's Hearty 9-Bean Soup
summary: High nutritious, low calorie. Excellent on a cold winter night.
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup mixed beans
1/4 tsp freshly ground pepper
1  large onion (chopped)
2-3  cloves garlic (minced)
2 cup broccoli (chopped)
4 cup beef/chicken stock
4 tbsp olive oil
salt (to taste)
1/2 cup pasta (orzo or alphabet, optional)
1  chili pepper (coarsely chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. Measure soup mix. Rinse well and soak overnight in pot with water 2" above mixture.
2. Rinse well again and add 4 cups of water to clean beans. Cook until beans are tender, about 2 1/2 to 3 hours. Do not drain water from beans.
3. Sauté chopped onion and garlic in olive oil. Add salt and pepper to taste. Add the cooked mixture of onion and garlic to bean pot. Add uncooked broccoli, stock and chili pepper. Simmer for 2 hours. If you like, add pasta for the last 1/2 hour of simmering.
4. The soup will be thick and hearty. Adjust for thickness by increasing/decreasing liquid.
5. Serve with flavored bread croutons or crusty bread and freshly grated Romano cheese.
tags: soup, beans, comfort food, winter, healthy, low calorie

name: Luci's Minnestroni Soup
ingredient_blocks:
1 can chicken broth
1 can beef broth
1/4 cup olive oil
2  medium onions (thinly sliced)
3 cloves garlic (chopped)
3  carrots (sliced)
1/2  head of cabbage (chopped or sliced)
2 stalks celery (sliced)
1 package frozen spinach (or canned mustard greens or any kind of greens)
1  large potato (scrubbed and cubed)
1 can medium-sized peeled tomatoes (chopped)
1 can beans (white, red, or your choice)
1 package chopped frozen broccoli (or chopped green beans or both)
1 sprig fresh Rosemary (or 1/2 teaspoon dry Rosemary)
ground pepper (to taste)
grated cheese (for serving)
instruction_blocks:
1. In pot, sauté onions and garlic in olive oil until limp.
2. Add cabbage, celery, spinach, cubed potato, broccoli and/or beans and cook slowly for about 20 minutes or until limp. If necessary, add more olive oil.
3. Add beans, peeled tomatoes, broth, pepper and Rosemary.
4. Cook for a couple of hours on low setting.
5. If desired add dry pasta like very thin spaghetti broken or capellini and let the pasta absorb the liquid.
6. Reheat when ready to serve. Serve with grated cheese.
tags: soup, Italian, minestrone, vegetable soup

name: Capanotasoni
ingredient_blocks:
1  medium eggplant
6 tbsp olive oil
1  clove garlic (minced)
1  onion (coarsely chopped)
1  small can tomato sauce
1/2 cup celery (chopped)
1/2 cup green pepper (diced)
2 tbsp capers (optional)
12  large stuffed green olives (sliced)
2 tbsp wine vinegar
1 tbsp sugar
salt and pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Peel eggplant and cut into slices 1/2" thick. Cut slices into cubes measuring 1/2".
2. In large heavy skillet, heat 5 tablespoons of oil and sauté eggplant until brown. Remove eggplant and set aside.
3. Add remaining oil to pan and sauté garlic and onion. Add tomato sauce, celery and green pepper.
4. Simmer, covered, for 15-20 minutes, adding water if necessary.
5. Return eggplant to skillet with capers and olives. Heat vinegar and sugar together and pour over vegetables.
6. Season and simmer 15 minutes longer, stirring occasionally.
7. Chill. Serve hot or cold with Italian bread or crackers.
notes: Title spelling unclear in scan - appears to be 'Capanotasoni' or similar. Recipe appears to be a type of caponata (Italian eggplant dish).
tags: Italian, eggplant, appetizer, vegetable, make-ahead

name: Luci's Minnestroni
ingredient_blocks:
1 can chicken broth
1 can beef broth
1/4 cup olive oil
2  medium onions (thinly sliced)
3 cloves garlic (chopped)
3  carrots (sliced)
1/2  head of cabbage (chopped or sliced)
2 stalks celery (sliced)
1 package frozen spinach (or canned mustard greens or any kind of greens)
1  large potato (scrubbed and cubed)
1  medium-sized can of peeled tomatoes (chopped)
1 can beans (white, red, or your choice)
1 package chopped frozen broccoli (or chopped green beans, or both)
1 sprig fresh Rosemary (or 1/2 teaspoon dry Rosemary)
ground pepper (to taste)
grated cheese (for serving)
instruction_blocks:
1. In pot, sauté onions and garlic in olive oil until limp.
2. Add cabbage, celery, spinach, cubed potato, broccoli and/or beans and cook slowly for about 20 minutes or until limp.
3. If necessary, add more olive oil.
4. Add beans, peeled tomatoes, broth, pepper and Rosemary.
5. Cook for a couple of hours on low setting.
6. If desired add dry pasta like very thin spaghetti broken or capellini and let the pasta absorb the liquid.
7. Reheat when ready to serve.
8. Serve with grated cheese.
notes: This page also contains a second recipe called 'Capanota' featuring eggplant, olive oil, garlic, onion, tomato sauce, celery, green pepper, capers, olives, wine vinegar, and sugar.
tags: soup, Italian, vegetable soup, minestrone

name: Rose's Lentil Vegetable Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup lentils
2  slices of Pancetta (Italian bacon)
1/2 cup onion (chopped)
1/2 cup celery
1/2 cup carrots
3 tbsp parsley (snipped)
1  clove garlic (chopped)
salt and pepper (to taste)
1/4 tsp dried oregano (crushed)
2 cup tomatoes
2  cans chicken broth and 1 can of water
instruction_blocks:
1. Rinse lentils, drain. Add chicken broth and water. Bring to a boil. Lower heat and simmer for 1 hour.
2. Make roux with onions, bacon, parsley and vegetables until they are soft, about 10 minutes. Add tomatoes, oregano and cook for 20 minutes. If desired add 1/2 cup of red wine. Add all to lentils and simmer for 30 minutes.
3. I like my soup to be thick. I puree about a cup or more of the mixture and return to the pot.
tags: soup, lentils, Italian, vegetable

name: Black Bean Soup
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb dried black beans
1/2 cup diced salt pork (1/4 pound)
1 cup chopped onions
1/2 cup chopped green pepper
1/2 tsp minced garlic
2  bay leaves
2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp black pepper
1/4 cup wine vinegar
6 cup cooked rice
12 tbsp minced raw onion (for garnish)
instruction_blocks:
1. Wash beans. Cover with water in a large pan. Soak overnight. In the morning, add more water, if necessary, to a depth of 1/2 inch above the beans.
2. Cook the salt pork over low heat in a 10 inch skillet until lightly brown. Add the browned pork to the beans. Place onion, green pepper, and garlic in the remaining fat, adjust heat to moderately high, and cook, stirring often until onions turn pale gold. Add the vegetables and fat to the beans. Add bay leaves, salt, and pepper and 1 tablespoon of the vinegar. Cover tightly and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to very low and slowly simmer until beans are tender (from 1 1/2 to 2 1/2 hours).
3. When beans are tender and liquid has thickened slightly, add remaining vinegar.
4. To serve, spoon 1/2 cup boiled rice into a heated soup plate. Ladle 1 cup beans with puree over rice. Garnish with 2 tablespoons of minced onion.
notes: Optional: Offer cruets of olive oil and additional wine vinegar. May be frozen.
tags: soup, black beans, comfort food, Cuban

name: Pasta With Carbonara Sauce
ingredient_blocks:
4  eggs
1/4 cup butter or margarine
1/4 cup cream
1 lb fettucini or spaghetti
2-3 oz Hormel packages
2  sliced pepperoni
1 cup Parmesan cheese (grated)
1/4 cup fresh parsley (snipped)
pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Let eggs, butter or margarine and cream stand at room temperature for 2 to 3 hours. Beat together the eggs and cream just until blended.
2. Add pasta to a large amount of boiling, salted water. Cook 10-12 minutes or until tender, but firm. Drain well.
3. Heat an ovenproof serving dish in a 250 degree oven. Turn pasta into heated serving dish. Toss pasta with butter and pepperoni. Pour egg mixture over and toss until pasta is well coated.
4. Add cheese and parsley, toss to mix. Serve immediately.
notes: Makes 10 to 12 side dishes or 4 to 5 main servings. Some text on the scanned page was partially obscured or unclear.
tags: Italian, pasta, carbonara

name: Lentil Soup
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup margarine (1 stick)
2 cup chopped onions
1 lb dried lentils
10 cup water
2 tbsp chopped parsley
2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp ground black pepper
2  bay leaves
instruction_blocks:
1. Melt margarine in a large heavy saucepan. Add onions and sauté until tender, about 5 minutes.
2. Stir in lentils, water, parsley, salt and pepper. Add bay leaves. Bring to a boil.
3. Cover, reduce heat and simmer 1 1/2 to 2 hours, or until lentils are tender. Discard bay leaves.
4. Serve hot. If desired, puree in blender or processor before serving.
tags: soup, lentils, vegetarian

name: Broccoli Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp grated onion
1/2 cup butter or margarine (melted)
1/4 cup all-purpose flour
1 cup milk
1 cup half and half
2 cup chicken broth
1/2 tsp salt
1/8 tsp garlic powder
1/4 tsp dried basil (to 1/2 teaspoon)
3 cup chopped fresh broccoli
instruction_blocks:
1. Sauté onion in butter in Dutch oven.
2. Add flour, stirring until smooth. Cook 1 minute, stirring constantly.
3. Gradually add milk, half and half, chicken broth. Cook over medium heat stirring constantly until the mixture is thickened and bubbly.
4. Stir in salt, garlic powder and basil.
5. Add broccoli, cover and cook over medium heat 15 minutes or until broccoli is tender.
notes: Recipe attributed to Elaine. Yield: 5 cups
tags: soup, broccoli, creamy, comfort food

name: Mushroom Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup mushrooms - stems diced, caps sliced
1/4 cup onion (finely diced)
1/4 cup cooked carrots (finely diced)
1/4 cup cooked celery (finely diced)
5 tbsp butter
7 tbsp flour
4 cup chicken broth
1 cup light cream or milk
2 tbsp dry Vermouth
salt and pepper to taste
chopped parsley (optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. Sauté mushroom stems and caps in butter with onion about 5 minutes.
2. Add Vermouth. Cook 3 minutes.
3. Add flour. Cook stirring until thickened.
4. Cool slightly and add cream or milk and the cooked carrots and celery.
5. Heat through. Do not boil.
6. Add salt and pepper to taste.
7. Sprinkle with parsley if desired.
notes: Page also contains a second recipe for Pepper Cookies which includes: 2 1/2 cups flour, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon pepper, 2 packages dry yeast, 1/2 cup warm water, 1/2 cup Wesson Oil. Instructions mention consistency should be like bread dough, put in Tupperware with cover to rise about 30 minutes, oil cookie sheet lightly, roll out dough into long strings about 1/2 inch thick, slice into 3 inch pieces and knot, bake at 400 degrees for first 10 minutes then lower temperature to 350 degrees and bake for 20 to 30 minutes.
tags: soup, mushroom, cream soup, comfort food

name: Sausage Bread
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 lb sausage
1 cup sifted flour
1/2 tsp baking powder
1/4 tsp baking soda
1/2 tsp salt
2 tbsp shortening
1/2 cup buttermilk
1 tbsp grated onion
1 cup shredded cheese
instruction_blocks:
1. Crumble sausage in the bottom of 9" pie plate. Bake in a 425 degree oven for 10 minutes.
2. Pour off fat, leaving about 1 tablespoon. Sift flour with baking powder, baking soda and salt. Cut in shortening and stir in buttermilk to make a rather soft dough. Mix in onions and cheese. Knead slightly and roll out to a 9" circle. Place on top of sausage and bake in 400 degree oven for about 20 minutes.
tags: bread, sausage, savory

name: Spinach Soup
ingredient_blocks:
1/4 cup chopped onion
1/2 clove garlic (minced)
1 tbsp butter or margarine
1 can chicken broth (46 ounces)
1/2 cup Ronzoni Acini Pepe
1/4 tsp nutmeg
1/8 tsp pepper
1  frozen chopped spinach (10 ounce package)
grated Parmesan cheese
salt (if desired)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cook and stir onion and garlic in hot butter until tender, but not browned.
2. Add chicken broth and bring to a boil.
3. Add pasta, nutmeg and pepper; simmer about 5 minutes.
4. Add frozen chopped spinach and simmer 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Season to taste with salt if desired.
6. Sprinkle with cheese.
notes: Document also contains a second recipe for Hungarian Paprikash which is partially visible/damaged
tags: soup, spinach, pasta, Italian

name: Proscuitto Pie
summary: Grandma Capece's recipe, from Aunt Lil's handwritten recipe
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup flour
1 pinch salt
3  eggs (save 1 yolk)
1 tbsp Crisco
2 tbsp baking powder
2/3-1 cup lukewarm water
2 lb ricotta
3/4 cup parmesan (grated)
1/4 cup parsley (chopped)
4  eggs
1 1/2-2 lb Proscuitto (slice thick, then dice)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix flour, salt, baking powder and Crisco. Make a well.
2. Add eggs and use water as needed to make dough come together. Knead well.
3. Place dough between two floured dinner dishes for 15 to 20 minutes.
4. Roll dough out, pour filling over 1/2 the dough.
5. Fold other end over the top making an envelope. Seal ends.
6. With a fork, prick holes in the top crust.
7. Place pie on a greased cookie sheet.
8. Bake at 350° for 1 1/2 hours.
9. For filling: Mix all ingredients together, then place on half the rolled out dough.
notes: This is Grandma Capece's recipe, preserved from Aunt Lil's handwritten version. The dough resting technique uses two floured dinner dishes.
tags: Italian, savory pie, family recipe, Easter, prosciutto, ricotta

name: Anise Biscuits
summary: Traditional anise-flavored cookies from Josie Lanzetti
ingredient_blocks:
3 1/2 cup flour
1/2 tsp cream tartar
1 tsp bicarb of soda (baking soda)
3 tbsp anise seed
1 stick butter
1 cup sugar
2 tbsp anise extract
2  eggs
1/2 cup milk (may use less than 1/2 cup)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix together and set aside the first four ingredients (flour, cream tartar, bicarb of soda, anise seed).
2. Place butter and sugar in large bowl and beat until smooth.
3. Add eggs and extract.
4. Add dry ingredients alternately with milk. (May use less than 1/2 cup of milk.)
5. Divide into 3 or 4 equal parts. Make them 1/2" low and long and narrow.
6. Bake on middle shelf at 400 degrees for about 20 minutes.
7. Slice after cooling for a minute or so.
8. Place slices side down on cookie sheet and brown for about 5 to 10 minutes.
notes: Recipe from Josie Lanzetti. This appears to be a biscotti-style cookie that is baked twice.
tags: cookies, Italian, anise, biscotti, family recipe

name: Anisette
summary: Homemade anise-flavored liqueur
ingredient_blocks:
5 lb sugar
5 quart cold water
65 drop anise oil
1 gallon alcohol (changed to 1 quart per handwritten note)
instruction_blocks:
1. In an enamel or stainless pot, pour sugar into cold water.
2. Bring sugar and water to boiling point slowly, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon.
3. Turn off heat and let mixture cool by itself.
4. Add 65 drops of anise oil to one quart of alcohol.
5. Shake gallon well.
6. Add to sugar and water mixture and mix well.
7. Filter, bottle and cap.
8. Let stand approximately six months in a cool, dark place.
notes: Handwritten corrections indicate: use stainless steel pot instead of enamel, and use 1 quart of alcohol instead of 1 gallon. Word 'Change' written at top right of recipe.
tags: liqueur, anise, homemade alcohol, Italian

name: Homemade Star Anise Liqueur
summary: From a collection of untried recipes from books. The authors have tried them, so they're not deadly or anything.
ingredient_blocks:
2/3 tbsp star anise (broken and crushed)
1 1/2 cup vodka
1/2 cup sugar syrup
instruction_blocks:
1. Steep herb in vodka for 2 weeks.
2. Filter, add syrup.
3. Liqueur is ready to be consumed or bottled.
notes: For effect: when completed, place a whole star intact in clear glass bottle before adding liqueur. Recipe from Homemade Liqueurs by Dona and Mel Meilach.
tags: liqueur, homemade, anise, infusion, beverage

name: Homemade Anise Liqueur (Meilach)
summary: A homemade anise liqueur recipe from the book Homemade Liqueurs by Dona and Mel Meilach
ingredient_blocks:
2/3 tbsp anise seed
1/2 tsp fennel seed
1/2 tsp coriander seed
1 1/2 cup vodka
1/2 cup sugar syrup
instruction_blocks:
1. Grind the seeds in a mortar.
2. Steep in vodka for 1 week, shaking frequently.
3. Strain and filter.
4. Add sugar syrup, and it's ready to be bottled or consumed.
notes: This recipe is from a collection of untried recipes. The website author notes they haven't tried it themselves yet, but the book authors have tried them, so they're not deadly or anything.
tags: liqueur, homemade, anise, beverage, alcohol

name: Homemade Anisette (djbec2000)
summary: A homemade anise-flavored liqueur made with brandy
ingredient_blocks:
5 tsp crushed aniseed
1 1/2 tsp crushed fennel seed
1 1/2 tsp crushed coriander
1  750ml bottle of brandy (cheap $6 a bottle stuff is fine)
instruction_blocks:
1. Grind all the seeds in a bowl with the back of a spoon and add them to the brandy.
2. Soak for 4 weeks then strain out, allow the haze to settle for a week then pour off the sediment.
3. No sugar syrup is really needed, the flavors come out to be sweet tasting on their own. Enjoy!
notes: From correspondent 'djbec2000'. Total time is approximately 5 weeks (4 weeks soaking plus 1 week settling). Source: guntheranderson.com/liqueurs/anisette.htm dated 12/12/2002
tags: liqueur, anisette, homemade, brandy, infusion

name: Beef Barley and Vegetable Soup
ingredient_blocks:
3 tbsp olive oil
2.5 lb sirloin (before trimming, cut into 1/2" cubes)
2  meaty beef bones
3  marrow bones
3  celery stalks
1.5  large onions (diced)
1/2 cup pearl barley
4 cup water
2  beef broth (14.5 oz cans)
1  diced tomatoes (28 oz can)
2  corn (11 oz can)
2  bay leaf (small)
2 tsp garlic powder
salt (to taste)
pepper (to taste)
1  frozen peas (package, optional)
1.5-2 tsp hot pepper sauce (optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oil in large pot over medium-high heat. Add beef and bones and sauté until beef is dark brown, about 8 minutes.
2. Transfer beef and bones to a dish plate.
3. Add celery, onion and barley to pot. Sauté until onion is golden, about 15 minutes.
4. Then add 4 cups water, beef broth, tomatoes with juices, corn, bay leaves and garlic powder.
5. Return beef and bones to pot and bring to a boil.
6. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer uncovered until beef is almost tender, about 1 hour.
7. Add peas and hot pepper sauce to soup. Cover and simmer until beef is tender and 30 minutes more.
8. Season with salt and pepper.
notes: The original recipe shows some handwritten corrections - 'Beef bones' was changed to 'Meaty beef bones'. Peas and hot pepper sauce are listed as optional ingredients.
tags: soup, beef, barley, comfort food, hearty

name: Beef Burgundy
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb beef sirloin (cut into 1" cubes)
2 tbsp butter
1 can golden mushroom soup
1/4 cup dry red wine
1  onion (large, sliced)
3 tbsp parsley (chopped)
1/8 tsp pepper
1/2 lb wide noodles (cooked)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a skillet, brown meat in butter.
2. Add remaining ingredients except noodles.
3. Cover and cook over low heat for 1 hour or until meat is tender. Stir occasionally.
4. Serve over cooked wide noodles.
notes: The '#' symbol in the original recipe represents pounds (lb)
tags: beef, comfort food, French, slow-cooked, main dish

name: Blackberry Brandy
ingredient_blocks:
1/3 cup water
1 cup sugar
1 cup alcohol
3 cup blackberry juice
instruction_blocks:
1. Boil sugar and water.
2. Allow syrup to cool.
3. Add to juice and then add alcohol.
4. Bottle and cap.
tags: beverage, brandy, blackberry, homemade liqueur

name: Whole Grain Cookbook - Black Beans with Rice
summary: Complete whole grain cooking recipe featuring black beans served over white rice
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp olive oil
2 tbsp cured olives (Carol Gelles)
1 1/2 cup onion (chopped)
1 cup green pepper (chopped)
2 cloves garlic (minced)
2/3 cup water
2  bay leaves
1/2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp cumin
1/4 tsp sugar
1/8 tsp black pepper
2 cans black beans (16 oz, undrained)
3 cup cooked long grain white rice
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oil in 3-quart saucepan over medium heat. Add onion and green pepper. Cook, stirring until onion is soft.
2. Stir in water, bay leaves, salt, cumin, sugar, and pepper.
3. Add beans with liquid. Stir and bring to a boil.
4. Reduce heat and simmer. Discard bay leaves.
5. Serve over rice.
6. Uncover for 1 hour.
notes: Recipe from Carol Gelles. Some handwriting unclear - 'cured olives' listing at top may be a separate note or variation. The instruction 'Uncover for 1 hour' may refer to simmering time.
tags: beans, rice, Cuban, vegetarian, whole grain, comfort food

name: Baklava
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb butter (unsalted or clarified)
2 cup sugar (for syrup)
1 cup water (for syrup)
1  cinnamon stick (mashed down, for syrup)
7  whole cloves (for syrup)
2 splash lemon juice (fresh cut in half and squeezed)
4 cup walnuts (chopped fine)
2 tbsp sugar (for nut mixture)
1+ tsp ground cinnamon (for nut mixture)
1 lb phyllo dough (frozen)
instruction_blocks:
1. Defrost Phyllo dough in refrigerator over night.
2. Melt unsalted butter in sauce pan.
3. In 2nd saucepan add sugar, water cloves and crushed cinnamon stick. Bring to a boil then reduce temperature until you get to a slow rolling boil. Cook about 7 to 8 minutes until dipped spoon in syrup is sticky when touched. Remove from heat and add 2 squirts of lemon juice. Let rest until cooking is complete.
4. In a large bowl add 4 cups of finely chopped walnuts. Mix in 2 tablespoons of sugar and 1+ teaspoon of ground cinnamon.
5. In a large METAL (or Enamel) 9X13 pan butter the bottom of the pan good. Then unfold the Phyllo dough and take 4 sheets of dough and lay them so the bottom of the pan is covered. Each should overlap the sides for rollup later. Then add 1 plain folded in half and 1 scrunched piece of dough and brush with the butter mixture. Then add 1 plain piece of dough and 1/2 the nut mixture evening the nut later out. Add butter mixture especially around the edges.
6. Then add one plain piece of dough and one scrunchy and add butter. Then add the remaining nut mixture evening it out. Add butter mixture.
7. Add another plain and scrunchy pieces of dough then fold in the overlapping dough and cover with butter mixture.
8. Add remaining dough plain over the top and cover with butter mixture. Make sure the top layer is completely covered with butter especially the edges.
9. Sprinkle the top with cold water. Then with a serrated knife diagonally cut the dough through to the bottom of the pan. Be careful not to move the dough around. Then cut the dough diagonally from the other side creating diamond shape pieces of baklava.
10. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees and bake in the middle of the oven for 40 to 45 minutes. When the top is nice and brown and crusty remove from the oven.
11. REMOVE THE CLOVES FROM THE SUGAR MIXTURE. Then liberally spoon the syrup over the top of the dough making sure the entire top is covered with syrup.
notes: Use a 9x13 metal or enamel pan. The phyllo sheets should overlap the sides for rolling up later. Make sure to remove cloves from syrup before pouring over baklava.
tags: Greek, Middle Eastern, dessert, pastry, nuts, phyllo

name: Boston Baked Beans
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb navy beans (or great northern beans)
2  Harrington Smoked Pork Chops (diced)
2 tsp salt
1/3 cup molasses
1/3 cup maple syrup
1  onion (chopped)
2 tbsp brown sugar
4  garlic cloves (chopped)
1 tsp cider vinegar
1 quart water (boiling)
instruction_blocks:
1. Prepare beans using Method 1 or Method 2.
2. Method 1: Wash and sort 1 pound of navy or great northern beans. Soak beans in a large pot with 3 quarts of water overnight.
3. Method 2: Wash and sort 1 pound of navy or great northern beans. Boil the beans for 1 hour in 8 cups of water and 1 tbsp of vinegar (de-snapper) and drain.
4. Add all ingredients into the baked bean pot. If the slow method of preparing beans was used then add 2 tbsp cider vinegar to bean pot.
5. Cover with 1 quart of boiling water, mix well and bake in a 250 Degree oven Approximately 8 hours (overnight).
6. Stir occasionally. If the beans are a little wet, then remove the top of the bean pot and cook for another hour.
7. Then have baked beans for breakfast!
notes: Optional: Instead of adding the maple syrup add 1/2 cup of molasses. If using the slow overnight soaking method, add 2 tbsp cider vinegar instead of 1 tsp.
tags: Boston, baked beans, comfort food, slow cooked, overnight

name: Boston Baked Beans
summary: American side dish
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb navy pea beans (boiled)
3/4 lb ham steak (smoked)
2 tsp salt
1/2 cup molasses
2 small onions (quartered)
2 tbsp brown sugar
1 clove garlic
1 tsp vinegar
1 cup water (boiling)
instruction_blocks:
1. Wash and sort 1 pound on Navy pea beans.
2. Boil the beans for 1 hour in 8 cups water and 1 tsp of vinegar.
3. Add remaining ingredients in the baked bean pot, with the 1 cup of boiling water, mix well and bake in oven at 250 degrees for approximately 8 hours (overnight).
4. Stir occasionally. If the beans get a little dry, then add more boiling water to the pot stir and continue cooking.
notes: This appears to be a printout from an early computer recipe database system (MODE: RETRIEVAL visible at bottom). Recipe requires overnight cooking.
tags: American, side dish, beans, comfort food, slow-cooked

name: Cranberry-Pecan Bread
summary: Recipe courtesy Emeril Lagasse, 2002
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup orange flavored liqueur
1/2 cup sugar
1/4 cup water
1/2 tsp whole cloves
1  cinnamon stick (halved)
1 1/2 cup fresh cranberries (or defrosted if frozen)
3/4 cup dark brown sugar
1 stick unsalted butter (at room temperature)
2  large eggs
2 cup flour
1 tsp baking soda
1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
1/2 tsp salt
3/4 cup buttermilk
1/4 cup fresh orange juice
1 cup chopped, toasted pecans
1 tsp orange zest
1 tsp vanilla
instruction_blocks:
1. In a saucepan, combine the orange liqueur, sugar, water, cloves, and cinnamon stick and bring to a boil. Stir to dissolve the sugar. Add the cranberries. Reduce the heat to a simmer and cook until the berries burst, about 8 minutes. Remove from the heat and let the berries cool in the liquid. Remove the cinnamon stick and cloves and discard.
2. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F. Lightly grease a 9 1/4 by 5 1/4 by 2 1/2-inch loaf pan.
3. In a mixer bowl, using the mixer on high speed (or using a hand mixer), cream the sugar and butter. Add the eggs and beat until light and fluffy. Into a bowl, sift together the flour, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt. In a small bowl, combine the buttermilk and orange juice. Alternately add the dry ingredients and buttermilk mixture to the creamed butter, beating after the addition of each. Fold in the reserved cranberry mixture, pecans, orange zest and vanilla. Pour into the prepared loaf pan. Bake until a tester comes out clean, 55 to 60 minutes.
4. Turn out onto a wire rack to cool. Serve warm or at room temperature.
notes: From Emeril Live on Food Network
tags: bread, cranberry, pecan, quick bread, holiday, Emeril Lagasse

name: Cranberry-Pecan Bread
notes: This appears to be page 2 of 2 of the recipe, which only contains timing information and storage instructions. The actual ingredients and instructions are on page 1 which is not provided. name: Oven Beef Stew
ingredient_blocks:
2 lb beef stew meat
1  medium onion
1 stalk celery
6  carrots
2 tsp salt
1 tbsp sugar
2 tbsp tapioca
1/2 cup tomato juice (use more if needed)
instruction_blocks:
1. Place meat (cut into 1-1/2 inch cubes) in an 11 x 7 x 1-1/2 pan.
2. Peel and slice onion. Diagonally slice stalk of celery, pare carrots and cut into chunks.
3. Add vegetables to pan.
4. Blend salt with sugar and tapioca and sprinkle over ingredients.
5. Add tomato juice (enough to cover meat and vegetables).
6. Cover pan with heavy cut aluminium foil and crimp edges to seal tightly.
7. Bake in slow oven (250 degrees) for 4 hours.
notes: Serve with rice, noodles or mashed potatoes. Recipe from Lillian D'Agostino.
tags: beef, stew, comfort food, slow cooked, one pot

name: Black Beans and Rice
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp olive oil
1 1/2 cup onions (chopped)
1 cup red pepper (chopped)
2  jalapeno (small peppers w/o seeds, chopped)
3  garlic cloves (minced)
1/3 cup water
2  bay leaves
1/4 tsp cumin
1/8 tsp black pepper
16 oz black beans (canned)
2 cup brown rice (cooked)
1/2 tsp salt (optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oil in 3-quart saucepan over medium heat.
2. Add onion, pepper and garlic, stirring until onion is soft.
3. Stir in water, bay leaves, salt, cumin, sugar and pepper.
4. Add black beans with liquid and stir.
5. Bring to a boil, reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 1 hour.
6. Discard bay leaves.
7. Serves over cooked Brown Rice.
notes: Sugar (1/4 teaspoon) appears crossed out in the original recipe
tags: beans, rice, Cuban, vegetarian, comfort food

name: Bruschetta with Tomato and Basil
summary: Recipe courtesy Rachael Ray
ingredient_blocks:
1/2  baguette or crusty long loaf bread (sliced (12 pieces))
2  garlic cloves (large, cracked away from skin)
extra-virgin olive oil (for drizzling)
3  small plum tomatoes (halved and seeded)
20  fresh basil leaves
coarse salt
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat broiler to high, (if you prefer you can also grill the bread).
2. Place bread slices on a broiler pan. Char bread on each side under hot broiler, keep an eye on it!
3. Rub toasts with cracked garlic and drizzle with oil.
4. Chop seeded tomatoes and place in a small bowl.
5. Pile basil leaves on top of one another and roll into a log. Thinly slice basil into green confetti and loosely combine with tomatoes.
6. Add a drizzle of oil and a little coarse salt to the bowl and gently toss tomatoes and basil to coat.
7. Pile toasts around the bowl of topping. Place a spoon to scoop topping in bowl and serve.
notes: Difficulty: Easy
tags: Italian, appetizer, quick, easy, vegetarian, 30 Minute Meals

name: Chateaubriand with Bearnaise Sauce and Chateau Potatoes
summary: Season with salt and pepper. Strain the sauce through a chinois and set aside.
instruction_blocks:
1. Season with salt and pepper.
2. Strain the sauce through a chinois and set aside.
notes: This appears to be page 2 of 2 of the recipe, so the full ingredient list and complete instructions from page 1 are not available. For more of Emeril's recipes, go to Emerils.com. This episode also featured Escargots in a Pastry Dome, Orange Crepes, and Sazerac Cocktail.

name: Chateaubriand with Bearnaise Sauce and Chateau Potatoes
summary: Recipe from Everyday Is A Party Cookbook, by Emeril Lagasse, with Marcelle Bienvenu and Felicia Willett, published by William Morrow, 1999
ingredient_blocks:
1  whole beef tenderloin (5 pounds or under, trimmed)
2 tbsp butter
salt
freshly ground black pepper
8 tbsp butter (for potatoes)
20  tourneed small white potatoes
1 tbsp finely chopped parsley
3 tbsp white vinegar (for the sauce)
3 tbsp white wine (for the sauce)
10  peppercorns (crushed)
2 tbsp finely chopped shallots
1 tbsp chopped tarragon
1 tbsp water
3  egg yolks
1 cup unsalted butter (melted)
1 tbsp finely chopped parsley leaves
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F. Season the beef with salt and pepper.
2. In a large skillet, over high heat, melt the butter. Add the beef and sear for a couple of minutes on each side.
3. Place in the oven and roast for 20 to 25 minutes for medium rare. Remove from the oven and rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
4. Serve with potatoes and chateaubriand sauce.
5. For the potatoes: In a saute pan, over medium heat, melt 8 tablespoons of butter. Add the potatoes and season with salt and pepper.
6. Saute the potatoes for 3 to 4 minutes. Place the potatoes in the oven and roast the potatoes until golden brown and tender, about 20 minutes, shaking the pan every 5 minutes.
7. Remove from the oven and add the parsley.
8. For the Sauce: In a saucepan, combine the vinegar, wine, peppercorns, shallots, and tarragon. Bring the liquid to a boil and reduce to 1 tablespoon.
9. Add 1 tablespoon of water. Combine the reduced liquid and egg yolks in a stainless bowl, over simmering water.
10. Whisk until frothy. In a steady stream, add the butter until the sauce thickens.
notes: From Emeril Live. Tourneed potatoes are small potatoes carved into an oblong, seven-sided football shape.
tags: beef, French, steakhouse, special occasion, Emeril Lagasse

name: Caesar Salad
summary: Recipe courtesy Emeril Lagasse
ingredient_blocks:
4  garlic cloves
4  egg yolks (raw - see warning)
2  anchovies
1 tbsp Dijon mustard
2  fresh lemons (juiced)
1 cup olive oil
salt (to taste)
cracked black pepper (to taste)
2  large heads of romaine lettuce (washed, ribbed and patted dry)
3 oz Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese
12 oz salad croutons
instruction_blocks:
1. Rub the inside of a large wooden bowl with the garlic cloves.
2. Remove the garlic cloves, and finely chop.
3. Add the egg yolks, remaining garlic, anchovies and mustard to the bowl.
4. Whisk until all of the ingredients are incorporated.
5. Whisk in the lemon juice.
6. Slowly drizzle in the olive oil, while whisking constantly. Whisk until all of the ingredients are incorporated.
7. Season the dressing with salt and pepper.
8. Gently tear the lettuce into bit-size portions and add to the salad bowl.
9. Using a hand-held grater, grate the cheese into the bowl.
10. Add the croutons.
11. Toss the salad completely and reseason if necessary.
12. Serve the salad on cold salad plates.
notes: RAW EGG WARNING: The American Egg Board states: 'There have been warnings against consuming raw or lightly cooked eggs on the grounds that the egg may be contaminated with Salmonella, a bacteria responsible for a type of food poisoning.... Healthy people need to remember that there is a very small risk and treat eggs and other raw animal foods accordingly. Use only properly refrigerated, clean, sound-shelled, fresh, grade AA or A eggs. Avoid mixing yolks and whites with the shell.' Yield: 6 to 8 servings. From Emeril Live, aired 01/24/2003.
tags: salad, Caesar, Emeril Lagasse, classic, raw egg

name: Cavatelli
ingredient_blocks:
1.5 lb ricotta cheese
1/3 cup farina
3  eggs
6 cup flour
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix all ingredients in mixer using the dough hook until dough no longer sticks to finger. May have to finish kneading by hand.
2. Let stand about 10 minutes.
3. On the countertop using the glass rack, roll the dough into 1/2" strands. DO NOT USE FLOUR TO ROLL THEM OUT.
4. After finished rolling out strand, then roll them in flour and let stand until ALL the dough is rolled out.
5. Then put through cavatelli machine. OR by using the Binford 2000 Cavatelli Extruder you can crank out three pounds of cavatelli in 4.35 seconds.
notes: Utensils: Cavatelli machine or the Binford 2000 Cavatelli Extruder. Source: Josie Lanzetti. Recipe dated Tuesday, March 06, 2001.
tags: Italian, pasta, homemade pasta

name: Cavatelli
ingredient_blocks:
1.5 lb ricotta cheese
0.33 cup farina
3  eggs
6 cup flour
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix all ingredients in a large bowl.
2. Roll dough into long 1/2" strands.
3. Let air dry for several minutes then put through cavatelli machine.
notes: Utensils: Cavatelli machine. Source: Josie Lanzetti

name: Chicken Casserole
ingredient_blocks:
6  chicken breasts (cooked)
2 cup celery (diced)
1 cup almonds (slivered)
3 can cream of chicken soup
1 can water chestnuts (or potatoes)
1 can mushrooms (sliced)
1 cup cheddar cheese (shredded, for topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix all the ingredients in a bowl and place in a 9" x 13" pyrex dish. Pam the Pyrex before adding mixture.
2. Prepare topping and place on top of chicken.
3. Place in a preheated oven set at 350 degrees and cook for 45 minutes.
notes: Original recipe called for 4 cups cooked chicken, salt (1 tsp), mayonnaise (1 cup), and potato chips (2 cups crushed) for topping - these items appear to be crossed out or modified. Handwritten notes indicate '6 Breasts Cook 1 pkg frozen peas (cubed potatoes)'
tags: dinner, casserole, chicken, comfort food

name: Best-Ever Chocolate Chip Cookies
summary: These are about as good as cookies get, so make several batches and watch them disappear fast.
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 cup all-purpose flour (sifted)
1 tsp baking soda
1 tsp ground cinnamon
1 cup butter (2 sticks, softened)
1/2 cup light brown sugar (firmly packed)
1 cup granulated sugar
1  large egg
1 tsp vanilla extract
1 1/2 cup old-fashioned rolled oats
1 cup semisweet chocolate chips
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix together flour, baking soda, and cinnamon.
2. Beat together butter, brown sugar, and granulated sugar at medium speed until light and fluffy. Beat in egg and vanilla.
3. At low speed, beat in flour mixture until blended. Fold in oats and chocolate chips. Cover with plastic wrap; chill for 1 hour.
4. Preheat oven to 350° F. Grease 2 baking sheets.
5. Shape dough into 1-inch balls. Place cookies, 2 inches apart, on prepared baking sheets. Flatten each cookie slightly.
6. Bake cookies until lightly browned around edges, 10 to 12 minutes. Transfer baking sheets to wire racks to cool slightly. Transfer cookies to racks to cool completely.
notes: Baking Tips: Make dough ahead of time and freeze it. Later, use defrosted dough to make homemade cookies in minutes.
tags: cookies, chocolate chip, oatmeal, dessert, baking

name: Chocolate-Pecan Brownies
summary: Ready in less than an hour, these fudgy treats are delightfully sweet and rich.
ingredient_blocks:
3/4 cup all-purpose flour
1/4 tsp baking soda
3/4 cup granulated sugar
1/3 cup butter or margarine
2 tbsp water
1  package semisweet chocolate morsels (12 ounces)
1 tsp vanilla extract
2  large eggs
1/2 cup chopped pecans
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat oven to 325° F. Grease a 9-inch square baking pan.
2. In a small bowl, mix together flour and baking soda.
3. In a small saucepan, combine sugar, butter, and water. Bring to a boil over medium heat; remove immediately from heat.
4. Stir in 1 cup of chocolate morsels and vanilla extract until chocolate is melted and mixture is smooth.
5. Transfer mixture to a medium bowl. Cool completely.
6. Stir in eggs, 1 at a time, beating well after each addition. Gradually stir in flour mixture until smooth.
7. Stir remaining chocolate morsels and nuts into batter. Pour batter into prepared pan.
8. Bake until a toothpick inserted in center comes out clean, 30 to 35 minutes. Transfer pan to a wire rack to cool completely. Cut into squares.
notes: Baking Tips: For light, moist brownies, cool chocolate mixture completely before combining it with other ingredients; test for doneness 2 to 3 minutes before the suggested baking time.
tags: brownies, chocolate, cookies & candies, dessert, pecans, fudgy

name: Chicken Pot Pie
summary: Using leftover cooked chicken cuts the preparation time in this flavorful pot pie.
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 cup all-purpose flour (for pastry)
1 tsp salt (for pastry)
1/2 cup chilled butter (cut into pieces)
1  large egg (for pastry)
2-3 tbsp ice water
4 cup cubed cooked chicken
1 tbsp butter (for filling)
1 lb fresh mushrooms (sliced)
1/4 cup dry white wine or water
1 1/2 cup whipping cream
2 tbsp all-purpose flour (for filling)
1 1/2 tsp paprika
1/2 tsp salt (for filling)
1/4 tsp black pepper
3/4 cup reduced-sodium chicken broth
1  large egg (lightly beaten, for glaze)
instruction_blocks:
1. To prepare pastry, in a medium bowl, mix together flour and salt. Using a pastry blender or 2 knives, cut butter into flour until coarse crumbs form.
2. In a small bowl, beat together egg and water. Add to the flour mixture; mix lightly until a soft dough forms. Shape into a disk, wrap in plastic wrap, and chill in the refrigerator for 1 hour.
3. To prepare the filling, place chicken in a 2-quart casserole.
4. In large skillet, melt butter over low heat. Add mushrooms; increase the heat to medium-high; and sauté until browned and the liquid evaporates, about 5 minutes. Add the wine; cook until almost evaporated, about 2 minutes. Add mushroom mixture to chicken; stir to combine.
5. In a medium saucepan, whisk together cream, flour, paprika, salt, and pepper, over low heat; cook until thickened, about 5 minutes. Whisk in broth. Pour sauce over chicken mixture.
6. Preheat oven to 400° F.
7. On a lightly floured surface, using a lightly floured rolling pin, roll the pastry to fit the top of the casserole. Place on top of pie; trim and seal the edges.
8. Roll out trimmings. Cut out leaves and flowers. Brush pastry with glaze; add the decorations; brush again with glaze.
9. Bake until filling is bubbly and crust is browned, 25 to 30 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack to cool slightly. Serve warm.
notes: Baking Tips: If using fresh rather than leftover or delicatessen chicken, you will need from a 3 1/2-pound fryer. Chilling time is 1 hour in addition to prep time.
tags: comfort food, main dish, chicken, pot pie, savory pie, baking

name: Bacon and Double Cheese Quiche
summary: Paired with a tossed green salad, this full-flavored quiche makes an excellent lunch.
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
1/4 tsp salt
1/2 cup chilled butter (1 stick, cut into small pieces)
2-3 tbsp cold water
10  strips lean bacon
4  large eggs
1 1/2 cup light cream
1/4 tsp dried thyme
1/8 tsp white pepper
1/2 cup shredded Gruyère cheese (about 2 ounces)
1/2 cup shredded white Cheddar cheese (about 2 ounces)
instruction_blocks:
1. To prepare the crust, in a large bowl, mix together the flour and salt. Using a pastry blender or 2 knives, cut in the butter until coarse crumbs form.
2. Add water, 1 tablespoon at a time, tossing with a fork, until a dough forms. Shape into a disk, wrap in plastic wrap, and chill in refrigerator for 30 minutes.
3. Preheat oven to 375° F. On a lightly floured surface, using a lightly floured rolling pin, roll the dough into 11-inch circle. Fit into 9-inch pie pan. Trim edge, leaving 1/4-inch overhang. Fold under to form standup edge. Prick dough with a fork. Line with foil and fill with pie weights or dried beans.
4. Bake for 10 minutes. Remove the foil and weights. Bake until lightly golden, about 5 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack to cool.
5. Meanwhile, prepare filling. In a medium skillet, cook the bacon over medium heat until crisp, 8 to 10 minutes. Transfer to a paper towel to drain.
6. In a small bowl, whisk together the eggs, cream, thyme, and pepper. Pour into crust. Crumble bacon. Sprinkle the egg mixture with the bacon, Gruyère cheese, and Cheddar cheese. Bake until golden and custard is set, about 30 minutes. Serve warm.
notes: Baking Tips: To freeze: Cool baked quiche completely, wrap airtight, and freeze for up to 1 month. Total time includes 30 minutes chilling time.
tags: quiche, brunch, bacon, cheese, main dish, baking

name: Successful Cheesecakes
summary: Life is too short not to eat cheesecake. Here, we show you how to produce perfect results every time.
instruction_blocks:
1. Before you begin baking, be sure all the ingredients are at room temperature. They will mix more easily and the finished cake will have a smoother texture.
2. Combine the cream cheese or ricotta and eggs thoroughly before adding any liquid extracts, heavy cream, or sour cream. Lumps are impossible to remove once the liquid ingredients that thin the batter have been added.
3. Use the paddle attachment of an electric mixer for mixing the batter. Regular whipping beaters incorporate too much air into the batter, which can lead to cracks in the finished cake.
4. If too much air is worked into the batter, the cake will be less creamy. If you must use regular whipping beaters, set the mixer at low or medium-low speed so only a minimum amount of air is whipped into the batter.
5. Fold in whipped cream and beaten egg whites with a wire whisk or a rubber spatula. Fold gently and slowly, taking care not to deflate the volume of the whipped ingredients.
notes: Cream Cheese: Cream cheese comes in many different varieties. Always use regular cream cheese for cheesecakes, unless the recipe says otherwise. Base Ingredients: Cheesecake bases are generally made from cookie crumbs mixed with softened or melted butter. Although many recipes call for graham cracker crumbs, almost any cookie will do, including cream-filled sandwich cookies. A serving of creamy cheesecake is one of life's little pleasures. This appears to be a technique guide rather than a complete recipe with specific ingredient measurements.
tags: cheesecake, dessert, baking tips, techniques

name: Cheesecake
summary: Baking school tips for making cheesecake with graham cracker crust
ingredient_blocks:
cookies (graham crackers) (crushed in food processor, plastic bag with rolling pin, or metal blade)
butter (for crust)
sugar (for crust)
cream cheese (for filling)
instruction_blocks:
1. Grease the bottom and sides of a 10-inch springform pan. Combine the crumbs with butter and sugar and press evenly into the bottom and up sides of the pan. In warm weather, refrigerate the pan until ready to use.
2. Use the paddle attachment of an electric mixer to beat the cream cheese smooth before adding any other ingredients. Regular whipping beaters can lead to cracks in the surface of the finished cake.
3. To ensure that the batter has no lumps and no ingredients stuck to the bottom of the bowl, stop the mixer several times while making the batter and scrape down the paddle and sides of the bowl.
4. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and set the pan on a baking sheet. Bake as directed. Cover with aluminum foil partway through baking if the top browns too quickly.
5. The finished cheesecake will have a dull, not shiny, finish. The center should be soft, but it should not wobble. Run a knife around the sides of the cooled cake to loosen it from the pan.
6. Release the spring and remove the pan sides. Leave the cake on the pan bottom for serving. Decorate with fresh fruit or sour cream, or serve plain.
notes: What Went Wrong? Cracks in the surface can occur because cheesecakes release a considerable amount of steam while they bake and during cooling time. Too much steam released too quickly causes the cheesecake to crack. Extremes of temperature can also lead to surface cracks. That is why baking temperatures for cheesecakes are relatively low, and bakers are warned not to set cheesecakes in cold or drafty places to cool. If possible, cool the cheesecake in a turned-off oven. Use a wooden spoon to keep the door slightly ajar. Deep cracks mean the egg white structure has collapsed. The cheesecake will be wet, more like a pudding than a cake. Shallow cracks often occur despite all efforts to prevent them. Accept them as part of a cheesecake's home-baked charm or cover them with fruit. This is Card 9 from Great American Home Baking series.
tags: cheesecake, dessert, baking tips, springform pan

name: Caponata
ingredient_blocks:
1  medium eggplant
6 tbsp olive oil
1  clove garlic (minced)
1  onion (coarsely chopped)
6 tbsp tomato sauce (used small can)
1/2 cup chopped celery
1/2 cup diced pepper
2 tbsp capers
12  large stuffed green olives (sliced)
2 tbsp wine vinegar
1 tbsp sugar
salt & pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Peel eggplant & cut into slices 1/2" thick. Cut slices into cubes measuring 1/2".
2. In large heavy skillet, heat 5 tablespoons of oil & saute eggplant until brown. Remove eggplant & set aside.
3. Add remaining oil to pan & saute garlic & onion.
4. Add tomato sauce, celery & green pepper. Simmer, covered, for 15-30 minutes, adding water if needed.
5. Return eggplant to skillet with capers & olives.
6. Heat vinegar & sugar together & pour over vegetables.
7. Season & simmer 15 minutes longer, stirring occasionally.
8. Chill - serve hot or cold with Italian bread or crackers.
tags: Italian, appetizer, vegetable, eggplant, make ahead

name: Cavatelli
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 lb ricotta
1/3 cup farina (may need more)
3  eggs
1 1/2 lb flour (six cups, may use less)
notes: Recipe only contains ingredient list with no instructions. Flour measurement noted as six cups equivalent.

name: Rum Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup chopped nuts (pecans)
1  package of Duncan Hines butter recipe golden cake mix
1  package of vanilla instant pudding mix
1/2 cup light rum
1/2 cup water
1/2 cup vegetable cooking oil
4  eggs
1 cup sugar (for glaze)
1 stick butter (for glaze)
1/4 cup light rum (for glaze)
1/4 cup water (for glaze)
instruction_blocks:
1. Grease and flour bundt or tube pan (10 x 4).
2. Crumble nuts in bottom of pan.
3. In large bowl put cake and pudding mixes.
4. Add rum, water, oil and eggs. Mix two minutes.
5. Bake at 325 degrees for 50-60 minutes.
6. For glaze: Mix sugar, butter, rum, and water and boil for 2-3 minutes.
7. Remove cake from oven and pour on hot rum glaze while cake is still in pan. Don't be alarmed if glaze seems to affect the cake. It will shrivel up. It is helping cake to settle.
8. Let stand at least 30 minutes before taking out of pan.
notes: Will keep for weeks in refrigerator.
tags: bundt cake, rum, dessert, make-ahead

name: Cheese Cake
ingredient_blocks:
17  Zwieback cookies (rolled out with a rolling pin)
2 tbsp butter (for crust)
2 tbsp sugar (for crust)
1 cup sugar (for filling)
8 oz cream cheese
8 oz cottage cheese
4  egg yolks
4  egg whites (stiffened)
1 cup milk
2 tbsp flour
1 tsp vanilla
1 pinch salt
instruction_blocks:
1. Roll out 17 Zwieback cookies with a rolling pin and mix with two tablespoons of butter and two tablespoons of sugar.
2. Using an eight inch pyrex dish, first grease pan, then line with the mixture and set aside.
3. Cream cheeses and sugar together well.
4. Add egg yolks, vanilla, flour and salt. Cream well and add milk.
5. Fold in stiffened egg whites.
6. Bake at 350 degrees for one hour.
tags: cheesecake, dessert, baked

name: Granny's Green Apple Cake
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup unsifted all purpose flour
2 cup sugar
1 tsp baking soda
1 tsp salt
1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
1 cup vegetable oil
1/2 cup milk
3  eggs
1 tsp pure vanilla extract
3  granny smith green apples (peeled, cored and diced)
1 cup chopped walnuts
1/2 cup raisins
1 cup confectioners sugar (for lemon glaze)
2 tbsp lemon juice (for lemon glaze)
instruction_blocks:
1. In large bowl of electric mixer blend flour, sugar, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, oil, milk, eggs and vanilla. Beat on low speed for three minutes.
2. Fold in diced apples, nuts and raisins.
3. Pour batter into greased and floured 9 inch or 10 inch tube pan.
4. Bake in preheated 350 degree oven for one hour and 15 minutes or until cake tester inserted comes out clean.
5. Cool cake in pan on wire rack for 10 minutes.
6. Remove from pan and cool completely.
7. For lemon glaze: Blend confectioners sugar and lemon juice until smooth.
8. Spoon lemon glaze over cake.
notes: Recipe from Eleanor Cinelli. Use a 9 or 10 inch tube pan.
tags: cake, apple, dessert, bundt cake, family recipe

name: Granny's Green Apple Cake
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup unsifted all purpose flour
2 cup sugar
1 tsp baking soda
1 tsp salt
1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
1 cup vegetable oil
1/2 cup milk
3  eggs
1 tsp vanilla extract
3  Granny Smith green apples (peeled, cored and diced)
1 cup chopped walnuts
1/2 cup raisins
1 cup confectioners sugar (for lemon glaze)
2 tbsp lemon juice (for lemon glaze)
instruction_blocks:
1. In large bowl of electric mixer blend flour, sugar, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, oil, milk, eggs and vanilla.
2. Beat on low speed for three minutes.
3. Fold in diced apples, nuts and raisins.
4. Pour batter into greased and floured 9 or 10 inch tube pan.
5. Bake in preheated 350 degree oven one hour and 15 minutes or until cake tester inserted comes out clean.

7. Remove from pan and cool completely.
8. For Lemon Glaze: Blend confectioners sugar and lemon juice until smooth.
9. Drizzle over top of cake.

name: Egg Nog
ingredient_blocks:
1 dozen egg yolks
1/2 gallon whole milk
1 1/2 cup sugar
1 1/2 cup Meyers dark rum
1 pint heavy cream
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat egg yolks in KitchenAid mixer until light.
2. Add sugar and continue beating until thick and pale.
3. Stir in milk and rum until well mixed.
4. Chill 3 hours or overnight.
5. Whip up heavy cream until nice and thick.
6. Pour eggnog mixture into punch bowl and fold in the whipped cream.
7. Stir before serving and ladle into glasses.
8. Top with a dash of cinnamon or nutmeg, if desired.
tags: holiday, drink, Christmas, dessert beverage

name: Easter Pie
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup flour (for dough)
1 pinch salt (for dough)
3  eggs (save 1 yolk, for dough)
1 tbsp Crisco (for dough)
2 tsp baking powder (for dough)
2/3 cup luke warm water (for dough)
1 1/2 cup grated cheese (for filling)
1  can of pot cheese (for filling)
1 lb prosciutto (for filling)
4  eggs (for filling)
pepper (for filling)
instruction_blocks:
1. Make well with dry ingredients, put liquid in, then knead.
2. Put between 2 dishes for about 15 min.
3. Prepare filling with grated cheese, pot cheese, prosciutto, eggs, and pepper.
notes: This is a traditional Italian Easter Pie recipe. 'Pot cheese' likely refers to ricotta or a similar fresh cheese. The dough resting instructions are somewhat unclear but appear to say rest between 2 dishes for about 15 minutes.
tags: Easter, Italian, holiday, pie, savory

name: Eggplant Rolatini
ingredient_blocks:
2  eggplant (Large, NOT teardrop shape)
9  eggs
20 oz bread crumbs (Italian flavored)
26 oz Italian tomato sauce (Good store sauce)
2 lb ricotta cheese (Arthur Ave or Polly-O)
1 lb mozzarella (Arthur Ave or Polly-O)
parmesan cheese (Grated)
1/2 lb prosciutto ham (thinly sliced)
1 tbsp canola oil
salt (For light seasoning)
instruction_blocks:
1. Peel eggplant, then slice lengthwise into 1/4 inch strips.
2. METHOD 1 - Frying Eggplant: ALWAYS Prepare a pie plate with a mixture of Flour and SALT. Then Place the eggs in a pie plate and mix. Place flavored breadcrumbs in a third pie plate (You may want to add some parmesan cheese to bread crumbs). Dip eggplant in the FLOUR MIXTURE FIRST, then in the Egg mixture then cover with breadcrumbs.
3. Heat canola oil in frying pan until hot. (Put control at medium) Heat until dark brown then turn and do the same. Drain on paper towels. This method keeps the eggplant softer for rolling.
4. METHOD 2 - Frying Eggplant: Prepare the flour mixture and the egg mixture with 1 tbs of oil in a pie plate and mix. Place flavored breadcrumbs in another pie plate (You may want to add some parmesan cheese to bread crumbs). Dip eggplant in the Egg mixture then cover with breadcrumbs.
5. Place on a cookie sheet and bake in a 350 degree oven for 20 minutes. Then turn and continue baking for 15 minutes. Place on paper towels when done.
6. Sauce: Sauté 1 large onion, seasoned. When lightly browned add 1 pound of chopped meat. When cooked add 1 jar of Classico sauce, preferably 5 cheese and heat.
7. Preparing dish: Cover bottom of Pyrex dish with meat sauce, then add eggplant. Top each eggplant layer with scoops of Ricotta cheese, Parmesan cheese, Mozzarella cheese, and Prosciutto (optional) then a layer of plain tomato sauce and cover with shredded mozzarella.
8. Add another layer exactly the same but use meat sauce. On the top layer do not add ricotta cheese.
9. Bake in oven at 425 degrees, covered for 45 minutes.
notes: Inspiration: Tony Capece. Method 1 (frying) keeps the eggplant softer for rolling. You may add parmesan cheese to breadcrumbs. Prosciutto is optional. Recipe recommends Arthur Ave or Polly-O brand cheeses and Classico 5 cheese sauce.
tags: Italian, eggplant, baked, cheese

name: Nut Cups
ingredient_blocks:
3 oz cream cheese (for shells)
1/4 lb butter (for shells)
1 cup flour (for shells)
3/4 cup chopped walnuts (for filling)
1  egg (for filling)
1 tsp vanilla (for filling)
3/4 cup light brown sugar (for filling, do not pack down)
1 tbsp butter (for filling)
powdered sugar (for topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. For shells: Mix together cream cheese, butter, and flour.
2. Roll mixture into small balls.
3. Press into small ungreased cupcake tins.
4. Chill.
5. For filling: Mix together chopped walnuts, egg, vanilla, brown sugar, and butter.
6. Fill tins with the filling mixture.
7. Bake at 325 degrees for 25-30 minutes.
8. When cool, sprinkle with powdered sugar.
notes: Page also contains a crossed-out recipe title 'Granny's Green Apple Cake' at the top.
