name: Kapusta
summary: Christmas eve soup
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup split peas
3  onions (medium, chopped)
2  sauerkraut (bag or can)
0.25 lb margarine
6 tbsp flour
3 quart water
pepper (fresh ground)
3  beef bouillon cubes
3 tbsp soy sauce
3  smoked ham hock (or use 4 Tbl of bacon)
instruction_blocks:
1. Drain the sauerkraut well and add to 3 quarts of water.
2. Add the 3 beef bouillon cubes and the smoked ham hocks, and fresh ground pepper, and soy sauce and cook over medium heat.
3. In a separate pot add the split peas to 2 cups of water. Cook at low heat until mushy. Add to sauerkraut.
4. Fry the onions in margarine until very lightly browned or golden brown.
5. Add flour and cook over medium heat until flour starts to brown (5 minutes). Stir constantly.
6. Add to sauerkraut.
7. Simmer soup for 40 minutes.
notes: Source: Mom. Traditional Christmas Eve soup.
tags: soup, Christmas, holiday, Polish, dinner

name: Chicken in White Wine
ingredient_blocks:
4  large chicken breasts (halved, skinned and boned)
flour (for coating)
2 tbsp olive oil
2 tbsp butter
salt (to taste)
freshly ground black pepper (to taste)
1 cup dry white wine
6  scallions (white and green parts thickly sliced, OR substitute 2 small leeks)
2  small leeks (white and green parts thinly sliced, alternative to scallions)
1  lemon zest (grated zest of one lemon)
instruction_blocks:
1. Trim chicken breasts and remove any loose or visible fat. Coat lightly with flour shaking off excess.
2. Heat olive oil and butter in frying pan. Cook the chicken until golden brown on all sides.
3. Season with salt and pepper and add the wine. Cook for about five minutes or until half the wine has evaporated.
4. Sprinkle the scallions or leeks over the top of the chicken. Cover lightly and cook over low to medium heat about ten minutes or until tender.
5. Check liquid occasionally. If necessary add a little more wine to prevent sticking. There should be about one or two tablespoons of sauce for each piece.
6. Sprinkle the grated lemon zest over the chicken and serve.
notes: Recipe from Ann Marie Daniel
tags: chicken, white wine, pan-fried, lemon, easy dinner

name: Lemon Chicken Almondine
summary: Could be veal
ingredient_blocks:
6  skinless, boneless chicken breast halves (pounded to 1/4-inch thickness)
1/3 cup lemon juice
3 tbsp Dijon mustard
2  garlic cloves (finely chopped)
1/4 tsp pepper (freshly ground)
1/3 cup olive oil
1 package sliced almonds (2 1/2 ounces)
2 tbsp butter or margarine
1 cup chicken broth
1 tsp cornstarch (dissolved in 1 tablespoon water)
2 tbsp fresh parsley (chopped)
1/4 tsp cayenne pepper
lemon slices (for garnish)
instruction_blocks:
1. Place chicken in a large shallow baking dish. Combine lemon juice, mustard, garlic, and pepper in a small bowl. Beat in oil. Pour over chicken. Marinate 1 hour at room temperature.
2. Preheat oven to 350°. Place almonds in a small baking pan. Bake until lightly browned, about 10 minutes.
3. Drain chicken, reserving marinade. In a large frying pan, melt butter over medium heat. Add chicken and cook about 4 minutes a side, until lightly browned. Remove to a dish.
4. Add reserved marinade and chicken broth to pan. Boil over high heat, stirring, until the sauce reduces by half, about 5 minutes. Stir in cornstarch mixture and cook, stirring, until thickened and smooth. Add parsley and cayenne pepper. Reduce heat, return chicken to pan, and heat through. Transfer chicken to a serving platter and pour sauce over all. Sprinkle toasted almonds over chicken and garnish with lemon slices.
notes: Handwritten notes on document indicate: 'What wine', 'mushrooms', 'No - almonds'. Marinate time is 1 hour (not included in prep/cook times). Recipe suggests it could also be made with veal.
tags: chicken, lemon, almonds, main dish, elegant

name: Lemon-Wine Chicken Cutlets
ingredient_blocks:
6  chicken breasts (with bones and skin removed)
flavored breadcrumbs (Italian style)
2  eggs (beaten with a little milk)
salt
pepper
2  large cloves garlic
2  chicken bouillon cubes
2  lemons (or six tablespoons of lemon juice)
3/4 cup dry white wine
3-4 tbsp olive oil
3-4 tbsp butter
3-4 tbsp grated Romano cheese
instruction_blocks:
1. Arrange the chicken breasts into medium thin, veal cutlet size pieces.
2. Beat eggs (with a little milk added). Dip chicken into egg batter, then breadcrumbs, seasoned with salt and pepper until nicely coated.
3. Heat olive oil and butter gently. Slightly brown garlic and remove to paper towel.
4. Dissolve bouillon cubes in 3/4 cup boiling water, add juice of two lemons and pour over chicken casserole dish.
5. Bake at 325 degrees, covered with tin foil for 20 minutes.
6. Then add wine and bake uncovered for 15 minutes.
7. Sprinkle with Romano cheese before serving.
notes: From: A Taste of Palm Springs, Dick Van Patten

name: Chicken Chili in Bread Bowls
summary: Chicken chili served in hollowed sourdough loaves or over cornbread
ingredient_blocks:
beans (type not specified)
8 cup cold water
onion
chili peppers
garlic
chicken broth
cumin
oregano
ground red pepper
chicken
salt
dairy sour cream (topping)
shredded Monterey Jack cheese (topping)
shredded colby cheese (topping)
chopped green onions (topping)
sourdough loaves or cornbread (for serving)
salsa (topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. Rinse beans. In a covered large kettle or Dutch oven, combine beans and 8 cups cold water. Bring to boiling; reduce heat. Simmer for 2 minutes. Remove from heat. Cover and let stand for 1 hour. (Or omit simmering and soak beans in cold water overnight in a covered pan.)
2. Drain and rinse beans. In the same pan, cook onion, chili peppers and garlic in hot oil until onion is tender.
3. Stir in beans, chicken broth, cumin, oregano and ground red pepper. Bring to boiling; reduce heat. Cover and simmer for 1-1/2 to 2 hours or until beans are tender. Mash beans slightly to thicken.
4. Add chicken. Cover and simmer for 10 to 15 minutes more or until chicken is no longer pink. Season with salt. Add additional red pepper, if you like.
5. Hollow out sourdough loaves or split squares of cornbread. Spoon chili into bread bowls or over cornbread. Top with salsa, sour cream and shredded cheeses. Garnish with green onions. Makes 6 to 8 servings.
notes: This recipe appears to be from a Recipe Center printout dated 11/9/2002. Some ingredient amounts are not specified in the visible portion of the recipe. The recipe offers an overnight soaking option as an alternative to the quick-soak method for the beans.

summary: A fat-free sour cream-mushroom sauce makes this low-fat chicken dish extra rich. For a colorful sauce, use half of a green and half of a red sweet pepper. Then accent the dish with a colorful sprinkling of fresh edible flowers.
ingredient_blocks:

1 tsp olive oil
2 cup sliced fresh mushrooms
1  medium red or green sweet pepper (cut into 3/4-inch squares)
1  clove garlic (minced)
1/2 cup reduced-sodium chicken broth
Salt
Pepper
1/2 cup fat-free dairy sour cream
1 tbsp all-purpose flour
1/8 tsp black pepper
1 tbsp dry sherry

name: Chicken with Mushrooms and Sweet Pepper
summary: Good Food, Good Health Eating Plan recipe
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup hot cooked white or brown rice (optional)
snipped fresh parsley
fresh chives (optional)
edible flowers (optional)
mushrooms
sweet pepper
garlic
chicken
salt
black pepper
sour cream (for sauce)
flour (for sauce)
1/8 tsp black pepper (for sauce)
sherry (optional, for sauce)
instruction_blocks:
1. Lightly coat an unheated large nonstick skillet with cooking spray. Preheat skillet over medium heat. Cook chicken for 3 to 5 minutes or until browned, turning once. Remove chicken from skillet.
2. Add oil to hot skillet. Cook the mushrooms, sweet pepper, and garlic in hot oil until tender. Remove vegetables; keep warm. Carefully stir broth into skillet. Return chicken to skillet. Sprinkle lightly with salt and black pepper. Bring to boiling; reduce heat. Simmer, covered, for 5 to 7 minutes or until chicken is tender and no longer pink. Transfer chicken to a platter; keep warm.
3. For sauce, in a bowl stir or whisk together sour cream, flour, and the 1/8 teaspoon black pepper until smooth. If desired, stir in sherry. Stir into mixture in skillet. Cook and stir until bubbly. Cook and stir 1 minute more. Serve chicken, vegetables, and sauce over hot cooked rice tossed with parsley. If desired, garnish with chives and flowers.

name: Pepper Mixture with Couscous or Rice
summary: A stir-fry dish with peppers, zucchini, corn, and black beans served over couscous or rice
instruction_blocks:
1. Pour cooking oil into a wok or 12-inch skillet. (Add more oil as necessary during cooking.) Preheat over medium-high heat. Stir-fry garlic in hot oil for 15 seconds. Add sweet peppers and onions; stir-fry for 2 minutes. Remove pepper mixture from the wok.
2. Add zucchini and corn to the hot wok; stir-fry for 2 minutes. Add chili powder; stir-fry for 2 minutes more or until vegetables are crisp-tender.
3. Return pepper mixture to the wok. Add undrained tomatoes, black beans, tomato sauce, jalapeno pepper, salt, and pepper. Cook and stir until bubbly. Cover and cook about 5 minutes more. Serve immediately over hot cooked couscous or rice.
notes: Make-Ahead Tip: Prepare vegetables; cover and chill up to 4 hours. This appears to be page 2 of 3 from a recipe, so the ingredients list is missing from this page. Ingredients mentioned in directions include: cooking oil, garlic, sweet peppers, onions, zucchini, corn, chili powder, undrained tomatoes, black beans, tomato sauce, jalapeno pepper, salt, pepper, and couscous or rice for serving.

name: Creme Brulee
summary: Recipe courtesy Tyler Florence
ingredient_blocks:
9  egg yolks
3/4 cup superfine white sugar (plus 6 tablespoons)
1 quart heavy cream
1  vanilla bean
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees F. In a large bowl, cream together egg yolks and sugar with a whisk until the mixture is pale yellow and thick.
2. Pour cream into a medium saucepan over low heat. Using a paring knife, split the vanilla bean down the middle, scrape out the seeds and add them to saucepan. Bring cream to a brief simmer, do not boil or it will overflow.
3. Remove from heat and temper the yolks by gradually whisking the hot vanilla cream into yolk and sugar mixture. Do not add hot cream too quickly or the eggs will cook.
4. Divide custard into 6 (6-ounce) ramekins, about 3/4 full. Place ramekins in a roasting pan and fill pan with enough water to come halfway up the sides of the ramekins.
5. Bake until barely set around the edges, about 40 minutes. You may want to cover loosely with foil to prevent browning.
6. Remove from oven and cool to room temperature. Transfer the ramekins to the refrigerator and chill for 2 hours.
7. Sprinkle 1 tablespoon of sugar on top of each chilled custard. Hold a kitchen torch 2 inches above surface to brown the sugar and form a crust.
8. Garnish with cookies and fresh fruit. Serve at once.
notes: Variation: Before dividing into ramekins: add 3 ounces of shaved dark chocolate for chocolate creme brulee; add 4 slices of crystallized ginger for ginger creme brulee; add 3 slices of orange peel for orange creme brulee. Let steep 20 minutes to infuse the flavor. Strain out the ginger and orange peel before baking.
tags: dessert, French, custard, classic

name: Pumpkin Creme Brulee
summary: Recipe courtesy Gale Gand, from "Butter Sugar Flour Eggs" by Gale Gand, Rick Tramonto, Julia Moskin, Clarkson N. Potter Publishers, 1999
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 cup heavy cream
1/2 cup whole milk
1/8 tsp cinnamon
2 pinch nutmeg
1 pinch ginger
1 pinch ground cloves
4  egg yolks
1/2 cup granulated sugar
1/4 cup canned pumpkin puree
1/3 cup coarse sugar or raw sugar (for topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat the oven to 300 degrees F.
2. In a medium saucepan, heat the cream, milk, cinnamon, nutmeg, ginger, and cloves over medium heat, stirring occasionally, just until it comes to a boil. Immediately turn off the heat and set aside to infuse at least 15 minutes.
3. In a large bowl, whisk the egg yolks with the granulated sugar. Whisking constantly, gradually pour in the hot cream mixture.
4. Whisk in the pumpkin puree. Pour the mixture into 4 ovenproof ramekins and arrange in a hot water bath.
5. Bake in the center of the oven until almost set but still a bit soft in the center, 30 to 40 minutes. The custard should "shimmy" a bit when you shake the pan; it will firm up more as it cools.
6. Remove from the water bath and let cool 15 minutes. Tightly cover each ramekin with plastic wrap, making sure the plastic does not touch the surface of the custard.
7. Refrigerate at least 2 hours, and up to 24 hours.
8. When ready to serve, preheat a broiler to very hot (or fire up your kitchen torch). Uncover the chilled custards. Pour as much coarse sugar as will fit onto the top of 1 of the custards.
9. Pour off the remaining sugar onto the next custard. Repeat until all the custards are coated. Discard any remaining sugar.
10. Place the ramekins on a baking sheet or in a roasting pan and broil until the sugar is melted and well browned, 1 to 2 minutes. Let cool 1 minute before serving.
notes: Episode #: SD1A32
tags: dessert, pumpkin, creme brulee, custard, fall, French

name: Cranberry Vanilla Creme Brulee
summary: A cranberry-topped vanilla creme brulee from Ming Tsai
ingredient_blocks:
16 tbsp cranberry sauce
1 cup half-and-half
1  vanilla bean (preferably Tahitian)
1/2 tsp pure vanilla extract
1  egg
8  egg yolks
2/3 cup granulated sugar
1 1/2 cup heavy cream (cold)
2 tbsp superfine sugar (see tip)
instruction_blocks:
1. Spoon 2 tablespoons of cranberry sauce into each of 8 (4-ounce) ramekins.
2. Preheat the oven to 325 degrees. In a small saucepan, combine the half-and half, vanilla bean, and vanilla extract. Heat over medium heat just until scalded; do not allow the mixture to boil.
3. Fill a large bowl with water and add ice. In a medium mixing bowl, combine the egg, egg yolks, and granulated sugar and mix. Gradually stir in the scalded half-and-half mixture and place the smaller bowl in the bowl of ice water to cool completely. Stir in the heavy cream and top equally the cranberry prepped ramekins.
4. Place in a baking dish just large enough to hold them and add enough hot water to the pan to come halfway up the sides of the ramekins. Cover the baking dish tightly with foil and bake until the custard is set but still quivers in the center, about 35 minutes. Remove the ramekins from the water and refrigerate to cool completely.
5. Preheat the broiler, if using. Sprinkle 1 tablespoon of the sugar on top of each custard, spread over the surface, and tap out any excess. Place the ramekins on a broiling tray and broil until the top is melted and caramelized, about 30 seconds. Watch carefully; the sugar can burn easily. If using a torch (see Tip), pass the flame about 2 inches over the surface of the custards until the sugar is completely caramelized. Serve while the sugar is still warm.
notes: If using a torch, replace the superfine sugar with raw sugar, which caramelizes more successfully under a torch flame.
tags: dessert, creme brulee, cranberry, vanilla, custard, French

name: Vanilla Creme Brulee, 2 Ways
summary: Recipe courtesy Emeril Lagasse, 2001
ingredient_blocks:
4 cup heavy cream
1 cup granulated sugar (plus 8 teaspoons)
1  vanilla bean (split and seeds scraped)
8  large egg yolks
35  wafer cookies
instruction_blocks:
1. In medium, non-reactive saucepan, combine the cream, 1/2 cup of the sugar, and the vanilla bean and seeds over medium heat. Bring to a gentle boil, whisking to dissolve the sugar.
2. In a small mixing bowl, whisk the egg yolks and 1/2 cup of sugar. Whisk about 2 cups of the hot cream mixture into the egg yolk mixture until smooth.
3. Slowly pour this mixture into the hot cream mixture, whisk for 2 minutes, and remove from the heat. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container. Preheat the oven to 300 degrees F.
4. Plating 1: Fill 8 (6-ounce) custard cups with equal portions of the mixture. Place the cups in a deep roasting pan or baking dish large enough to accommodate the cups without touching. Fill the roasting pan with enough water to come halfway up the sides of the cups.
5. Bake in the lower third of the oven until just set, about 45 minutes. Remove from the oven and remove the cups from the water. Let cool. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
6. Sprinkle each cup with 1 teaspoon sugar. One at a time, using a kitchen blowtorch, approach the sugar with the torch at a low angle until the inner blue flame is 1/4-inch above the surface and move the flame in a continuous motion over the surface until the sugar has caramelized.
7. Or, preheat the broiler, sprinkle the sugar over the custards, and slide the dishes under the broiler. Broil until the sugar caramelizes, 1 to 2 minutes, watching carefully so the custard does not curdle or break. Remove and serve.
8. Before serving, sprinkle 1 teaspoon of the remaining sugar on the top of each custard.
9. Plating 2: Fill a shallow baking dish with the mixture. Place in a medium roasting pan and fill with enough water to come halfway up the sides of the dish.
10. Bake in the lower third of the oven until just set, about 45 minutes. Remove from the oven and remove the cups from the water. Let cool. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
11. To serve, arrange 25 to 35 wafer cookies on a baking sheet (the amount will depend upon the amount spread onto each cookie). With a flat knife, scoop and smoothly spread the custard mixture onto each cookie.
12. Sprinkle the top of each lightly with sugar, and using a kitchen blowtorch, approach the sugar with the torch at a low angle until the inner blue flame is 1/4-inch above the surface and move the flame in a continuous motion over the surface until the sugar has caramelized.
notes: Recipe offers two plating options: individual custard cups or a shallow baking dish spread onto wafer cookies. Refrigerate custard for at least 4 hours or overnight before caramelizing.
tags: dessert, French, custard, creme brulee, Emeril Lagasse

name: Cavatelli
ingredient_blocks:
1.5 lb Ricotta Cheese
1/3 cup Farina (0.33)
3  Eggs
6 cup Flour
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix all ingredients in mixer using the dough hook until dough no longer sticks to finger. May have to finish kneading by hand.
2. Let stand about 10 minutes.
3. On the countertop using the glass rack, roll the dough into 1/2" strands. DO NOT USE FLOUR TO ROLL THEM OUT.
4. After finished rolling out strand, then roll them in flour and let stand until ALL the dough is rolled out.
5. Then put through cavatelli machine. OR using the Binford 2000 Cavatelli Extruder you can crank out three pounds of cavatelli in 4.35 seconds.
notes: Utensils: Cavatelli machine or the Binford 2000 Cavatelli Extruder. Recipe source: Josie Lanzetti. Recipe dated Saturday, March 29, 1997.
tags: Italian, Vegetarian, pasta, homemade pasta

name: Granny's Green Apple Cake
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup unsifted all purpose flour
2 cup sugar
1 tsp baking soda
1 tsp salt
1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
1 cup vegetable oil
1/2 cup milk
3  eggs
1 tsp pure vanilla extract
3  granny smith green apples (peeled, cored and diced)
1 cup chopped walnuts
1/2 cup raisins
1 cup confectioners sugar (for lemon glaze)
2 tbsp lemon juice (for lemon glaze)
instruction_blocks:
1. In large bowl of electric mixer blend flour, sugar, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, oil, milk, eggs and vanilla. Beat on low speed for three minutes.
2. Fold in diced apples, nuts and raisins.
3. Pour batter into greased and floured 9 inch or 10 inch tube pan.
4. Bake in preheated 350 degree oven for one hour and 15 minutes or until cake tester inserted comes out clean.
5. Cool cake in pan on wire rack for 10 minutes. Remove from pan and cool completely.
6. For lemon glaze: Blend confectioners sugar and lemon juice until smooth.
7. Drizzle lemon glaze over top of cake.
notes: Recipe from Eleanor Cinelli
tags: cake, apple, dessert, tube pan, glazed

name: Lemon-Wine Chicken Cutlets
ingredient_blocks:
6  chicken breasts (with bones and skin removed)
flavored bread crumbs (Italian style)
2  eggs (beaten with a little milk)
salt
pepper
2  large cloves garlic
2  chicken bouillon cubes
2  lemons (OR 6 tablespoons of lemon juice)
3/4 cup dry white wine
3-4 tbsp olive oil
3-4 tbsp butter
3-4 tbsp grated Romano cheese
instruction_blocks:
1. Arrange the chicken breasts into medium thin, veal cutlet size pieces.
2. Beat eggs (with a little milk added). Dip chicken into egg batter, then bread crumbs, seasoned with salt and pepper until nicely coated.
3. Heat olive oil and butter gently. Slightly brown garlic and remove to paper towel.
4. Dissolve bouillon cubes in 3/4 cup boiling water, add juice of two lemons and pour over chicken casserole dish.
5. Bake at 325 degrees, covered with tin foil for 20 minutes.
6. Then add wine and bake uncovered for 15 minutes.
7. Sprinkle with Romano cheese before serving.
notes: From: A Taste of Palm Springs, Dick Van Patten
tags: Italian, chicken, lemon, wine, casserole

name: Lemon-Wine Chicken Cutlets
ingredient_blocks:
6  chicken breasts (with bones and skin removed)
flavored breadcrumbs (Italian style)
2  eggs (beaten with a little milk)
salt
pepper
2  large cloves garlic
2  chicken bouillon cubes
2  lemons (or 6 tbs of lemon juice)
3/4 cup dry white wine
3-4 tbsp olive oil
3-4 tbsp butter
3-4 tbsp grated Romano cheese
instruction_blocks:
1. Arrange the chicken breasts into medium thin, veal cutlet size pieces.
2. Beat eggs (with a little milk added).
3. Dip chicken into egg batter, then breadcrumbs, seasoned with salt and pepper, until nicely coated.
4. Heat olive oil and butter gently. Slightly brown garlic and remove from pan.
5. Gently brown cutlets on both sides and remove to paper towel.
6. Dissolve bouillon cubes in 3/4 cup boiling water, add juice of two lemons and pour over chicken in a casserole dish.
7. Bake at 325 degrees, covered with tin foil, for 20 minutes.
8. Then add wine and bake uncovered for 15 minutes.
9. Sprinkle with Romano cheese before serving.
notes: Serve with a vegetable, rice pilaf and salad. Recipe attributed to Dick Van Patten.
tags: Italian, chicken, lemon, wine, baked

name: Chicken Soup
ingredient_blocks:
3 lb chicken
4-5 quart water
5  carrots (finely chopped)
5  celery stalks (finely chopped)
1 tsp parsley
2  medium onions (chopped)
3-4  bouquets garnis (if desired)
2 tsp garlic (chopped)
8 oz tomato sauce or plum tomatoes (canned)
instruction_blocks:
1. Boil water. Put all ingredients in a one time.
2. Bring to a boil then simmer until chicken is cooked (approximately 2 hours).
3. Remove chicken from bone and shred. Place shredded chicken back in pot.
4. In another pot bring water to boil and cook acne pepi pasta. When done drain and add to soup.
notes: VARIATIONS: Option 1 - Beat 4-5 eggs with parmesan cheese to taste. Make soup come to a boil and add egg mixture stirring constantly until eggs are cooked (1-2 minutes). It will look like egg drop soup. Option 2 - Cook acne pepi pasta directly in soup. This will make a hearty soup. Note: 'acne pepi' pasta appears to be 'acini di pepe' pasta.
tags: soup, chicken, Italian, comfort food

name: Nancy's Carrot Cake
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup sugar
1 1/2 cup oil
3  eggs
2 tsp vanilla
2 tsp cinnamon
2 1/4 cup flour
2 tsp baking soda
1 tsp salt
1 bag coconut (About 2 cups)
1 can crushed pineapple (More than 8 oz)
1 cup walnut (Chopped)
2 cup carrots (Chopped)
8 oz cream cheese (For icing)
1 stick butter (For icing)
1/4 cup milk (For icing)
2 tsp vanilla (For icing)
1 dash salt (For icing)
3-4 cup powdered sugar (For icing)
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oven to 350. Grease 13" X 9" pan.
2. Combine sugar, oil, eggs and vanilla.
3. Stir in flour, cinnamon, baking soda and salt.
4. Fold in carrots, coconut, pineapple and walnuts.
5. Bake for 50 Minutes.
6. For icing: Mix all ingredients together thoroughly.
7. Frost cake.
tags: carrot cake, dessert, cream cheese frosting, family recipe

name: Devil's Food Cake
summary: A chocolate layer cake with Orange Cream Cheese Frosting
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup sifted SWANS DOWN Cake Flour
1 tsp baking soda
1/2 cup butter, softened
1 1/4 cup light brown sugar, packed
2  eggs
6  squares unsweetened chocolate, melted, cooled
1 tsp vanilla
1 1/4 cup milk
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (180°C).
2. Sift flour and baking soda. In large bowl, cream butter, gradually add brown sugar until light and fluffy. Add eggs; beat well.
3. Beat in chocolate and vanilla. Add dry ingredients alternately with milk, beating well after each addition.
4. Spread batter in 2 prepared 9-inch layer pans.
5. Bake until tester inserted in center comes out clean, 25-30 minutes.
6. Cool in pans 10 minutes. Remove from pans; cool thoroughly.
7. Frost with Orange Cream Cheese Frosting.
notes: Orange Cream Cheese Frosting: 2 3-oz pkgs (90g each) cream cheese, softened; 1/4 cup (60ml) butter, softened; 5 cups (1.2l) confectioners sugar; 1 tsp (5ml) grated orange zest; 2-3 tbs (30-45ml) orange juice. Beat cream cheese and butter until light and creamy. Gradually add confectioners sugar. Add orange zest and enough orange juice to reach desired spreading consistency.
tags: chocolate, layer cake, dessert, cream cheese frosting

name: KAPUSTA
summary: Christmas eve soup
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup split peas
3  onions, chopped (medium onions)
2 bag or can sauerkraut
1/4 lb margarine
6 tbsp flour
3 quart water
pepper (fresh ground pepper)
3  beef bouillon cubes
3 tbsp soy sauce
3  smoked ham hock (or use 4 Tbl of bacon fat)
instruction_blocks:
1. Drain the sauerkraut well and add to 3 quarts of water.
2. Add the 3 beef bouillon cubes and the smoked ham hocks, and fresh ground pepper, and soy sauce and cook over medium heat.
3. In a separate pot add the split peas to 2 cups of water. Cook at low heat until mushy.
4. Add to sauerkraut.
5. Fry the onions in margarine until very lightly browned or golden brown.
6. Add flour and cook over medium heat until flour starts to brown (5 minutes). Stir constantly.
7. Add to sauerkraut.
8. Simmer soup for 40 minutes.
notes: Handwritten note at bottom appears to read: Cover Shot, Excellent Sweet/Dries, 4 Celery
tags: soup, Christmas, Polish, dinner, holiday

name: Minestrone
summary: Low salt Italian vegetable soup
ingredient_blocks:
1 can chicken broth (more if you like)
1 can beef broth
2  medium onions (thinly sliced)
3 cloves garlic (chopped)
3  carrots (sliced)
1 package frozen spinach (chopped)
1  large potato (scrubbed and cubed, leave skins on)
1 can peeled tomatoes (medium size, chopped - no salt)
1 can beans (white or red, kidney beans)
1 package frozen broccoli (chopped, or chopped green beans)
1 sprig fresh rosemary (or 1/2 teaspoon dry)
ground pepper (to taste)
grated parmesan cheese (for serving)
olive oil (for sauteing)
thin spaghetti (broken, or capellini - optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. In pot, saute onions and garlic in olive oil until limp.
2. Add cabbage, celery, spinach, cubed potatoes, broccoli and/or beans and cook slowly for about 20 minutes or until limp.
3. If necessary add more olive oil. Add beans, peeled tomatoes, broth, pepper and rosemary.
4. Cook for a couple of hours on low setting.
5. If desired, add dry pasta like very thin spaghetti broken or capellini and let the pasta absorb the liquid.
6. Reheat when ready to serve and serve with grated parmesan cheese.
notes: Original recipe included 1/2 head of cabbage (chopped or sliced) and 2 stalks of celery (slices) which appear to be crossed out. Handwritten additions suggest using College Inn low salt broth, kidney beans, and optionally adding 1 can chick peas. Recipe marked as low salt version.
tags: Italian, soup, low salt, vegetable soup, comfort food

name: Manicotti Filling
ingredient_blocks:
3 lb ricotta cheese
8 oz mozzarella (shredded)
3  eggs
1 pinch salt
parsley (to taste)
tomato sauce (enough to cover)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix together 3 pounds of ricotta cheese, 3 eggs, parsley, and a pinch of salt.
2. After making the manicotti shells, place enough of the filling down the center of each shell and roll.
3. Place the stuffed shell in a dish with the seam facing down.
4. Cover with tomato sauce and bake for 1/2 hour.
notes: Source: Josie Lanzetti. Recipe dated Sunday, July 21, 1996.
tags: Italian

name: Mushroom soup
summary: Christmas Eve
ingredient_blocks:
4 oz dried mushrooms
4 cup water
2  onions (medium, finely chopped)
4 tbsp vegetable oil
4 tbsp flour
1 stick butter
2 cubes vegetable bouillon
1 dash garlic salt
1 dash pepper
1 dash soy sauce
instruction_blocks:
1. Soak the mushrooms in lukewarm water, until they are soft.
2. Rinse mushrooms thoroughly, then dice and boil in 2 quarts of water.
3. Prepare thickener, lightly brown 3 large finely chopped onions in the oil and butter.
4. Add the flour to get a pasty consistency, then add to soup.
5. Add bouillon cubes, garlic salt, pepper and accent. Stir until soup thickens.
6. If additional thickener is needed, mix flour with warm water and add slowly to soup.
notes: Source: Mom. Food Category: Dinner. Instructions mention 3 large onions but ingredients list 2 medium onions - there may be a discrepancy. Instructions also mention 'accent' (likely MSG seasoning) which is not listed in ingredients.
tags: soup, mushroom, Christmas Eve, dinner, vegetarian

name: Home-Style Meat Loaf
summary: This meat loaf is on our list of favorite comfort foods. For a healthier dish, be sure to use the lean ground beef.
ingredient_blocks:
1 tbsp butter (melted)
2  egg yolks (beaten)
1/4 cup parsley (finely snipped)
1/4 cup fine dry bread crumbs
3 tbsp chili sauce or catsup
1/4 cup onion (finely chopped)
2 lb lean ground beef
1  tomato sauce (8-ounce can)
1 tbsp brown sugar
1 tsp Worcestershire sauce
parsley (optional)
radish roses (optional)
1 tsp salt
1/2 tsp pepper
instruction_blocks: 1. Brush the butter onto bottom and sides of a 9x5x3-inch loaf pan. Set pan aside.
notes: Source: Midwest Living. Recipe page is incomplete - only first instruction step is visible on this page (Page 1 of 3).
tags: comfort food, meat loaf, beef, American, Midwest

name: Beef with Parsley and Radish Roses
summary: Baked beef dish served with tomato sauce and garnished with parsley and radish roses
ingredient_blocks:
egg yolks
parsley
bread crumbs
chili sauce
onion
salt
pepper
beef
tomato sauce
brown sugar
Worcestershire sauce
parsley (for garnish)
radish roses (for garnish)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a large bowl, combine the yolks, parsley, bread crumbs, chili sauce, onion, salt and pepper. Add beef; mix. Pat into pan.
2. Bake in a 350 degree F. oven for 1 hour or until a meat thermometer inserted in the center registers 170 degrees F. Transfer to platter. Cut into slices.
3. In small saucepan, combine tomato sauce, brown sugar and Worcestershire. Cook and stir until heated. Spoon over meat.
4. Garnish with parsley and radish roses, if you like. Serves 8 to 10.
notes: This is page 2 of 3 from a recipe, so the beginning instructions and full ingredient list with amounts are missing from this scan. The recipe appears to be from BHG.com (Better Homes & Gardens), dated 11/14/2002.
tags: beef, main dish, baked

name: Minestrone
ingredient_blocks:
1 can chicken broth (use more if you like)
1 can beef broth (use more if you like)
2  medium onions (thinly sliced)
3  cloves garlic (chopped)
3  carrots (sliced)
1/2  head of cabbage (chopped or sliced)
2  stalks celery (sliced)
1 package frozen spinach
1  large potato (scrubbed and cubed, leave skin on)
1 can peeled tomatoes (medium size, chopped)
1 can beans (white or red)
1 package frozen chopped broccoli (OR chopped green beans)
1 sprig fresh rosemary (OR 1/2 teaspoon dried rosemary)
ground pepper (to taste)
grated Parmesan cheese (for garnish)
instruction_blocks:
1. In pot saute onions and garlic in olive oil until limp.
2. Add cabbage, celery, spinach, cubed potatoes, broccoli and/or green beans and cook slowly for about 20 minutes or until limp. If necessary add more olive oil.
3. Add beans, peeled tomatoes, broth, pepper and rosemary.
4. Cook for 2 hours on low setting.
5. If desired add dry pasta like very thin spaghetti broken, or cappalini, and let the pasta absorb the liquid.
6. Reheat when ready to serve.
7. Garnish with grated Parmesan cheese.
notes: Recipe from Luci Vaughn. Optional to add thin spaghetti or cappalini pasta.
tags: soup, Italian, vegetable soup, comfort food

name: Minestrone
ingredient_blocks:
1  13-3/4 ounce can College Inn chicken broth (use more if you like)
1  13-3/4 ounce can College Inn beef broth (use more if you like)
2  medium onions (thinly sliced)
3  cloves garlic (chopped)
2  carrots (sliced)
olive oil
1 package frozen chopped spinach
2  large red potatoes (scrubbed and cubed, leave skin on)
1  16 ounce can tomato sauce
1  19 ounce can kidney beans (white or red)
1  19 ounce can chick peas
1 package frozen chopped broccoli florets
1 sprig fresh rosemary (OR 1/2 teaspoon dried rosemary)
ground pepper (to taste)
grated Parmesan cheese (for garnish)
1 cup dry pasta
instruction_blocks:
1. In pot saute onions and garlic in olive oil until limp.
2. Add spinach, carrots, potatoes, and broccoli and cook slowly for about 20 minutes or until limp. If necessary add more olive oil.
3. Add beans, tomatoes, sauce, broth, pepper and rosemary. Cook for 1 hour on low setting.
4. Add dry pasta. Let pasta absorb the liquid.
5. Reheat when ready to serve. Garnish with grated Parmesan cheese.
notes: From: Luci Vaughn (Carol Makar's version). Handwritten additions visible: 'carrots' and 'sauce' noted in margins.
tags: soup, Italian, vegetarian, comfort food

name: Neapolitan Meat Sauce---Ragu Napoletano
summary: Recipe copyright 2000, Mario Batali. All rights reserved.
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 lb veal (cut into chunks)
1/2 lb beef chuck (cut into chunks)
1  onion (finely chopped)
4 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil
3/4 cup red wine
2  San Marzano tomatoes (28-ounce cans, peeled, passed through a food mill)
1/2 lb sweet Italian sausages
1 tsp salt
1 pinch hot chili flakes
instruction_blocks:
1. In a large pasta pot or Dutch oven, combine the veal, beef, onion and oil over high heat and cook until meat is seared on all sides and meat juices have evaporated, 10 to 12 minutes.
2. Add the wine and cook, stirring constantly, until wine evaporates and meat is darker brown, about 5 minutes.
3. Stir in the tomatoes, sausages, salt and chili flakes. Reduce heat to simmer and cook 2 1/2 to 3 hours, stirring occasionally and skimming fat as necessary.
4. Remove from heat and remove meat from ragu. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
notes: Yield: 4 cups, for 8 pasta servings. From Molto Mario Show Schedule.
tags: Italian, meat sauce, ragu, Neapolitan, pasta sauce, Mario Batali

name: Neapolitan Meatballs - Polpette alla Napoletana
summary: Traditional Italian meatballs made with day-old bread and pecorino cheese. Note that Italians would rarely serve meatballs with pasta.
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup day-old bread (cut into 1-inch cubes)
1 1/4 lb ground beef
3  eggs (beaten)
3 clove garlic (minced)
3/4 cup pecorino (grated)
1 bunch Italian parsley (finely chopped to yield 14 cup)
1/4 cup pine nuts (toasted for 2 minutes in a 400 degree F oven)
1/2 tsp salt
1/2 tsp pepper
1/4 cup extra-virgin olive oil
instruction_blocks:
1. In a shallow bowl, soak the bread cubes in enough water to cover. Remove the bread cubes and squeeze by hand to wring excess moisture.
2. In a large bowl, combine the bread, beef, eggs, garlic, pecorino, parsley, pine nuts, salt and pepper and mix by hand to incorporate bread into meat.
3. With wet hands, form the mixture into 12 to 15 meatballs, each of a size somewhere between a tennis ball and a golf ball.
4. In a large, heavy-bottomed skillet, heat the oil until almost smoking. Add the meatballs, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding the pan, and cook until deep golden brown on all sides, about 10 minutes per batch.
5. Serve warm or at room temperature.
notes: Recipe copyright 2000, Mario Batali. All rights reserved. Note that Italians would rarely serve meatballs with pasta.
tags: Italian, Neapolitan, meatballs, Mario Batali, traditional

name: Neapolitan Baked Lasagne - Lasagne Napoletane
summary: Recipe copyright 2000, Mario Batali. All rights reserved.
ingredient_blocks:
1  basic pasta dough recipe (recipe follows)
2 tbsp salt
1 1/2  ragu Napoletana recipe (recipe follows, cooled, meat reserved for another use and sausages thinly sliced)
3 cup ricotta
1/2  polpette recipe (see attachment, cooled)
1 1/2 cup Parmigiano-Reggiano (freshly grated)
1 lb fresh mozzarella (shredded)
instruction_blocks:
1. Make the pasta dough according to the recipe and roll out to the thinnest setting on a pasta rolling machine.
2. Cut the pasta into strips 5 inches wide and 10 inches long. Cover with a damp towel and set aside.
3. Bring about 6 quarts of water to a boil and add 2 tablespoons salt. Set up an ice bath next to the stove top and cook the lasagne 1 minute in the boiling water.
4. Drain, refresh in the ice bath and drain again. Lay out on clean, damp towels.
5. In a 10 by 20-inch lasagne pan, layer the lasagne, beginning with 3/4 cup ragu, then a layer of pasta, then a layer of ricotta, then a layer of polpettine and sausage, then a layer of Parmigiano and mozzarella. Use all ingredients. The top layer should be covered with cheese.
6. Bake for 1 hour and 15 minutes, until edges are bubbling.
7. Let rest 15 minutes before slicing.
notes: From Molto Mario show on Food Network. Related recipes include Basic Pasta Dough, Neapolitan Meat Sauce (Ragu Napoletano), and Neapolitan Meatballs (Polpette alla Napoletana).
tags: Italian, Neapolitan, lasagne, pasta, baked, comfort food, Mario Batali

name: Pork Tenderloin with Apricot Sauce
summary: A barbecued pork tenderloin with a sweet and savory sauce, delicious cold as well as hot
ingredient_blocks:
pork tenderloin
oil (for drizzling)
rosemary (lots)
black pepper (lots)
red cooking wine (for sauce)
Dijon mustard (for sauce)
apricot preserves/jam (for sauce)
King Grey Chutney (for sauce)
instruction_blocks:
1. Drizzle oil on pork tenderloin and add lots of rosemary and black pepper.
2. Barbecue outdoors approximately 1/2 hour or broil 15-18 minutes.
3. Slice into medallion slices and add sauce.
4. Let sit for 1/2 minute before serving.
5. For the sauce: Blend red cooking wine, Dijon mustard, apricot preserves/jam, and King Grey Chutney together well.
notes: The author notes they cooked this one the day before and it's delicious cold as well as hot. This recipe was shared via email from Jdags@aol.com on June 29, 1997. Amounts for sauce ingredients are not specified - blend to taste.
tags: pork, barbecue, grilled, summer, make ahead

name: Key Lime Pie
ingredient_blocks:
1 tbsp unflavored gelatin (one envelope)
1/2 cup sugar
1/4 tsp salt
4  egg yolks
1/2 cup lime juice
1/4 cup water
1 tsp grated lime peel
green food coloring (several drops)
4  egg whites
1/2 cup sugar
1 cup heavy cream (whipped)
1  nine inch baked pastry shell
instruction_blocks:
1. Thoroughly mix gelatin, 1/2 cup sugar and salt in a sauce pan.
2. Beat together egg yolks, lime juice and water. Stir into gelatin mixture.
3. Cook and stir over medium heat just until mixture comes to boiling.
4. Remove from heat, stir in grated lime peel.
5. Add food coloring (enough to tint to a pale green color).
6. Chill, stirring occasionally until mixture mounds slightly.
7. Beat sugar into egg whites beating to stiff peaks.
8. Fold gelatin mixture into egg whites, then fold in whipped cream.
9. Pile into cooled baked pastry shell.
10. Chill until firm.
notes: Recipe from Elaine Barzou
tags: pie, dessert, key lime, chiffon pie

name: Pecan Fingers or Balls
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup butter or margarine
1/4 cup sugar
2 tsp vanilla
1 cup chopped pecans
2 cup flour
1/2 tsp salt
instruction_blocks:
1. Roll dough in long finger shapes or balls.
2. Bake at 350 degrees for 10 minutes.
3. After removing from oven roll in confectioners sugar.
4. Roll again after they are cooled.
notes: Roll in confectioners sugar twice - once while warm and once after cooled
tags: cookies, pecans, holiday, dessert

name: Pasta with Carbonara Sauce
ingredient_blocks:
4  eggs
1/4 cup butter or margarine
1/4 cup cream
1 lb fettucini or spaghetti
7 oz Hormel sliced pepperoni
1 cup grated parmesan cheese
1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley
pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Let eggs, butter or margarine and cream stand at room temperature for 2-3 hours.
2. Beat together the eggs and cream just until blended.
3. Add pasta to a large amount of boiling, salted water. Cook 10-12 minutes or until tender but firm. Drain well.
4. Heat an ovenproof serving dish in a 250 degree oven.
5. Turn pasta into heated serving dish. Toss pasta with butter and pepperoni.
6. Pour egg mixture over and toss until pasta is well coated.
7. Add cheese and parsley. Toss to mix.
8. Serve immediately.
notes: Makes 10-12 side dishes or 4-5 main servings. Recipe by Anne DelSoldato.
tags: Italian, pasta, carbonara, comfort food, main dish

name: Prosciutto Pie
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup flour (for dough)
1 pinch salt (for dough)
3  eggs (for dough, save one yolk)
1 tbsp Crisco (for dough)
2 tbsp baking powder (for dough)
1 cup warm water (for dough)
2 lb ricotta cheese (for filling)
3/4 cup Parmesan cheese (for filling)
1/2 cup chopped parsley (for filling)
1 lb prosciutto (diced, for filling)
4  eggs (for filling)
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine flour, salt, baking powder, and Crisco together in bowl. Make a well in center of mixture and place beaten eggs in the well, add mix from the center of the bowl to the outside. Bring the mixture into the eggs.
2. Mix together completely, use water as needed (avoid using too much water or the dough will become hard to work with). The dough should be pasty yet workable.
3. Knead well (5-7min). Place between two (floured) dinner plates for 15 to 20 minutes (to allow it to rise).
4. For the filling: Combine 2lbs ricotta cheese, 3/4 cup Parmesan cheese, 1/2 cup fresh CHOPPED parsley, 1 lb prosciutto diced (TINY PIECES), 4 eggs, MIX IN A LARGE BOWL.
5. Roll out the dough in a big oval approx. 2' x 2-1/2'. Place 1 half of dough on a jelly roll pan large enough to accommodate it.
6. Place prosciutto mixture in the center of the half of rolled out dough on the pan, then fold over and meet edges. (Like a big omelet)
7. Wet the edges with your fingers to make the edges stick together, press with a fork to seal.
8. Bake at 350 degrees, for 1 to 1-1/2 hours. Brush with yolk before placing in oven (for golden brown color).
notes: You may wish to increase or decrease any of the stuffing ingredients to suit your own taste. I usually add a little bit of pepper to the mixture. ENJOY! Note: A jelly roll pan is specified, but any big baking pan will be fine.
tags: Italian, savory pie, prosciutto, ricotta, family recipe

name: California Pot Roast
ingredient_blocks:
4-5 lb pot roast
3 tbsp fat or salad oil
2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp pepper
1/2 cup water
8 oz tomato sauce
3  medium onions (thinly sliced)
2  cloves garlic (minced)
2 tbsp brown sugar
1/2 tsp dry mustard
1/4 cup lemon juice
1/4 cup vinegar
1/4 cup ketchup
1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
instruction_blocks:
1. Brown roast well on both sides in fat in a heavy pan.
2. Add salt, pepper, water, tomato sauce, onions and garlic.
3. Cover tightly and simmer over low heat about 1 1/2 hours.
4. Combine brown sugar, mustard, lemon juice, vinegar, ketchup and Worcestershire sauce and pour over meat.
5. Continue cooking until meat is fork tender -- about another 1 1/2 hours.
6. Thicken gravy with cornstarch.
7. Serve with noodles or mashed potatoes.
tags: pot roast, beef, comfort food, slow cooked, American

name: Perohe
summary: Christmas Eve main course
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup sour cream (for dough option 1)
4 cup flour (for dough option 1)
2 tsp salt (for dough option 2)
1  egg (for dough option 2)
1 1/2 cup water (approximately, for dough option 2)
sauerkraut (for potato filling)
potatoes (for potato filling)
onions (chopped, for potato filling)
butter (for potato filling)
oil (small amount, for potato filling)
mushrooms (for mushroom filling)
onion (chopped, for mushroom filling)
oil (for mushroom filling)
4 tbsp butter (melted, for mushroom filling)
salt (to taste, for mushroom filling)
1/2 lb cheddar cheese (shredded, for cheddar cheese filling)
instruction_blocks:
1. DOUGH OPTION 1: Blend the 2 cups sour cream and 4 cups flour together in a mixer to make the dough. Cover dough with saran wrap and let dough rest about 30 minutes.
2. DOUGH OPTION 2: Sift flour with 2 tsp salt. Beat egg and enough water to bring measure to 1½ cups. Add slowly to flour in bowl, adding only enough to make it come together. Knead on bread board about 10 minutes; dough should be soft and light. Put dough back in bowl and cover with cloth. Let rest for 20 minutes.
3. POTATO FILLING: Rinse the sauerkraut well two times in lukewarm water. Squeeze sauerkraut until dry then cut with knife or scissor. Boil in fresh water; drain and let cool. Boil potatoes in water; drain; mash. Place small amount of oil in frying pan, add chopped onions and butter. Sauté until onions are soft. Add the mixture to the mashed potatoes; add sauerkraut. Mix all together.
4. MUSHROOM FILLING: Soak the mushrooms in lukewarm water, until they are soft. Rinse mushrooms thoroughly, then dice. In a large skillet add some oil and chopped onion. Sauté about 2 minutes then add the chopped mushrooms and about 4 tablespoons of melted butter. Add salt to taste. Continue cooking until onions are golden brown.
5. CHEDDAR CHEESE FILLING: Shred about ½ pound of cheddar cheese for easy filling. Roll shredded cheese into 1 inch logs (for stuffing).
6. PREPARATION: Take a small hand full of dough and pound with hand until fairly thin. Then use pasta maker to roll out. Start on setting number 8 and reduce it until you reach setting number 6. Cut into 3" circles (about the size of a 14 oz sauerkraut can). Spoon (1 tbs) filling into shells and seal well.
7. Place on dishtowels until ready to cook. Drop into boiling water and stir with a wooden spoon. When water comes to a second boil and perohe reach the top (about 10 minutes). Drain in colander and garnish with remaining butter and onions.
notes: TIP: If cut dough is too dry to seal then turn over the cut dough and fill and seal the table the under side. Source: Mom and Aunt Millie
tags: Christmas, Eastern European, pierogi, dumplings, holiday, main course

name: Pierogi
summary: Traditional pierogi recipe with two dough options and multiple filling variations
ingredient_blocks:
4 cup flour (for Dough 1)
2 cup sour cream (for Dough 1)
water (for Dough 1, add only if too dry)
4 1/2 cup flour (for Dough 2)
1 1/2 cup sour cream (for Dough 2)
2  eggs (for Dough 2)
2 tbsp salt (for Dough 2)
1  sauerkraut (14 oz can, save can to cut dough, for Potato Filling)
2  potatoes (large, peeled and boiled, for Potato Filling)
1  onions (medium, chopped, for Potato Filling)
4 tbsp butter (for Potato Filling)
oil (small amount for sautéing, for Potato Filling)
1 oz dried mushrooms (for Mushroom Filling)
1  onion (medium, for Mushroom Filling)
4 tbsp butter (for Mushroom Filling)
oil (small amount for sautéing, for Mushroom Filling)
1/2 lb cheddar cheese (shredded, for Cheddar Cheese Filling)
instruction_blocks: 1. No instructions provided in this recipe document - only ingredients listed

name: Potato Perohe
summary: Christmas eve main course
ingredient_blocks:
4.5 cup flour
2 tsp salt (for sifting with flour, plus pinch)
1  eggs
1.5 cup sour cream
2 lb sauerkraut (or 2 - 1 lb cans)
4  potatoes (large)
2  onions, chopped (large)
1/2 lb butter
instruction_blocks:
1. DOUGH: Sift flour with 2 tsp salt. Beat egg and enough sour cream to bring measure to 1 1/2 cups. Add slowly to flour in bowl, adding only enough to make it come together.
2. Knead on bread board about 10 minutes; dough should be soft and light. Put dough back in bowl and cover with cloth. Let rest for 20 minutes.
3. FILLING: Rinse the sauerkraut well two times in lukewarm water. Boil in fresh water; drain and let cool. Squeeze sauerkraut until dry then cut with knife or scissor.
4. Boil potatoes in water; drain; mash.
5. Place small amount of oil in frying pan, add chopped onions and butter. Saute until onions are soft.
6. Add 1/2 the mixture to the mashed potatoes; add sauerkraut and remaining salt. Mix all together.
7. PREPARATION: Roll dough like a pie crust. Use pasta maker to roll out. Start on setting number 8 and reduce it until you reach setting number 4.
8. Cut into 3" circles (about the size of a 1 lb sauerkraut can). Spoon (1 tbsp) filling into shells and seal well.
9. Place on dishtowels until ready to cook. Drop into boiling water and stir with a wooden spoon.
10. When water comes to a second boil and perohe reach the top (about 10 minutes). Drain in colander and garnish with remaining butter and onions.
notes: Source: Mom. This is a traditional Christmas Eve main course recipe.
tags: Eastern European, Christmas, pierogi, dinner, family recipe, Polish

name: Mushroom Perohe
summary: Christmas Eve
ingredient_blocks:
1 oz dried mushrooms
1  onion (large, chopped)
1/2 stick margarine
instruction_blocks:
1. For dough, see potato perohe recipe.
2. Soak the mushrooms in lukewarm water until all sand is removed.
3. Rinse several times and dice.
4. Brown onions in margarine and then add mushrooms.
notes: Source: mOM. This recipe references a separate potato perohe recipe for the dough.

name: Pasta Fagiola
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb Navy Beans (sorted and washed)
2  Pork Chops (Large, on the bone)
2  Smoked Ham Hocks (Harrington Ham Hocks are excellent)
1 1/2 tsp Wine Vinegar
2 quart Water
2  Celery (stalks)
3  Garlic (cloves)
3  Onion (large, chopped)
1  Red Pepper (diced)
2  Jalapeno Pepper (diced)
3  Carrots (diced)
8 oz Tomato Sauce (can)
1 dash Salt
1 lb Ditalini's (box)
instruction_blocks:
1. Sort and soak beans in cold water over night.
2. Drain and add all ingredients in large pot.
3. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 3 hours, stirring frequently until cooked.
4. When completed, dice the pork chop and Ham Hock meat and put back in soup.
5. Cook ditalini's until done.
6. Mix pasta and soup and serve.
notes: Source: Mom 2
tags: soup, Italian, beans, pasta

name: Traditional Pumpkin Pie
summary: A classic holiday recipe that utilizes the convenience of canned pumpkin.
ingredient_blocks:
1  Pastry for Single-Crust Pie (see below)
1  15-ounce can pumpkin
2/3 cup sugar
1 tsp ground cinnamon
1/2 tsp ground ginger
1/2 tsp ground nutmeg
3  eggs (slightly beaten)
1  5-ounce can evaporated milk (2/3 cup)
1/2 cup milk
instruction_blocks: 1. Prepare and roll out Pastry for Single-Crust Pie. Line a 9-inch pie plate with the pastry. Trim to 1/2 inch beyond edge of pie plate. Fold under extra pastry; crimp edge as desired.
notes: Source: Better Homes and Gardens. This recipe was tested in the Better Homes & Gardens Test Kitchen and meets our high standards for reliability and taste appeal.
tags: holiday, pumpkin, pie, dessert, Thanksgiving

name: Pumpkin Pie
ingredient_blocks:
1-1/4 cup all-purpose flour (for pastry)
1/4 tsp salt (for pastry)
1/3 cup shortening (cut in, for pastry)
4-5 tbsp cold water (for pastry)
pumpkin (for filling)
sugar (for filling)
cinnamon (for filling)
ginger (for filling)
nutmeg (for filling)
eggs (for filling)
evaporated milk (for filling)
milk (for filling)
instruction_blocks:
1. For filling, in a mixing bowl combine pumpkin, sugar, cinnamon, ginger, and nutmeg. Add eggs. Beat lightly with a rotary beater or fork just until combined. Gradually stir in evaporated milk and milk; mix well.
2. Place the pastry-lined pie plate on the oven rack. Carefully pour filling into pastry shell.
3. To prevent overbrowning, cover edge of the pie with foil. Bake in a 375 degree F oven for 25 minutes. Remove foil. Bake about 25 minutes more or until a knife inserted near the center comes out clean. Cool on a wire rack. Refrigerate within 2 hours; cover for longer storage.
notes: Pastry for Single-Crust Pie: Stir together 1-1/4 cups all-purpose flour and 1/4 teaspoon salt. Using a pastry blender cut in 1/3 cup shortening until pieces are pea-size. Using 4 to 5 tablespoons cold water, sprinkle 1 tablespoon of the water over part of the mixture; gently toss with a fork. Push moistened dough to side of the bowl. Repeat moistening dough, using 1 tablespoon of the water at a time, until all the dough is moistened. Form dough into a ball. On a lightly floured surface, use your hands to slightly flatten dough. Roll dough from center to edge into a circle about 12 inches in diameter. Make-Ahead Tip: Prepare, bake, and cool pie as above. Cover and refrigerate for up to 8 hours. Test Kitchen Tip: You can use 1-1/2 teaspoons pumpkin pie spice for the spices in this pie.
tags: pie, pumpkin, dessert, holiday, Thanksgiving

name: Balsamic Roast Pork Tenderloins
ingredient_blocks:
4 1/2 lb pork tenderloins (2 packages with 2 tenderloins in each package)
4 tbsp balsamic vinegar
4 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil
8  cloves garlic (cracked)
steak seasoning blend or coarse salt and black pepper
4  sprigs fresh rosemary (leaves stripped and finely chopped)
4  sprigs fresh thyme (leaves stripped and finely chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat oven to 500 degrees F.
2. Trim silver skin or connective tissue off tenderloins with a very sharp thin knife.
3. Place tender loins on a nonstick cookie sheet with a rim. Coat tenderloins in a few tablespoons of balsamic vinegar, rubbing vinegar into meat. Drizzle tenderloins with extra-virgin olive oil, just enough to coat. Cut small slits into meat and disperse chunks of cracked garlic cloves into meat. Combine steak seasoning blend or coarse salt and pepper with rosemary and thyme and rub meat with blend. Roast in hot oven 20 minutes.
4. Let meat rest, transfer to a carving board, slice and serve.
notes: Recipe courtesy Rachael Ray. Episode #: TM1B27
tags: pork, roast, easy, herbs, balsamic

name: Party Potatoes
summary: A creamy baked potato casserole that's better when refrigerated 24 hours before baking. Freezes well.
ingredient_blocks:
8-10  Maine potatoes
1 cup dairy sour cream
1 pkg cream cheese (8 oz)
1 tsp garlic salt
1 tsp onion salt
butter
paprika
instruction_blocks:
1. Boil potatoes. Drain.
2. Beat sour cream and cream cheese until blended.
3. Add hot potatoes gradually, beating constantly until light and fluffy.
4. Season with garlic and onion salt.
5. Spoon potatoes into 2-quart casserole, dot with butter, sprinkle with paprika and brown.
6. Much better if they are refrigerated at least 24 hours before baking.
7. Then bake in 350° oven about 45 minutes.
notes: This dish freezes well. Allow 1½ to 2 hours to bake when frozen.
tags: potatoes, casserole, make-ahead, comfort food, party food

name: Grits — Southern Style
summary: A Southern-style grits casserole that can be made the night before and baked the next morning.
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup grits
4 cup water
1 stick butter or margarine (1/4 lb.)
2  eggs
1 cup milk
instruction_blocks:
1. Cook as directed on grits box.
2. Then add 1 stick (1/4 lb.) butter or margarine. Stir constantly while melting process is going on.
3. Beat two eggs in a cup and fill with milk. Add to grits mixture.
4. Stir well and allow mixture to cook until it just begins to thicken.
5. Pour into greased casserole. Bake for 45 minutes at 350°.
6. The grits may be made the night before and put into the oven the next morning. In that case, bake the grits for about an hour. This allows for the coldness acquired in the refrigerator.
notes: Can be prepared the night before and refrigerated. If baking from cold, increase baking time to about 1 hour.
tags: Southern, breakfast, casserole, make-ahead, grits

name: Apple-Cranberry Crunch
summary: A crunchy alternative to cranberry sauce shared by Rosalie, a good friend from college, who brought the original to a Thanksgiving. Can be served as dessert.
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup McIntosh apples (coarsely chopped, cored, unpeeled (about 1 pound))
2 cup fresh or frozen cranberries
3/4 cup granulated sugar
1 1/2 cup old-fashioned oats
3/4 cup chopped pecans (3 ounces)
1/2 cup packed light-brown sugar
1/3 cup all-purpose flour
1/2 cup butter or margarine (melted)
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oven to 350°F. Have ready a 2½-quart round shallow baking dish or 9-inch deep-dish pie plate.
2. Mix all filling ingredients in a medium-size bowl.
3. Turn into ungreased baking dish.
4. To make topping, mix oats, pecans, brown sugar and the flour. Stir in butter until blended.
5. Crumble over filling; press down lightly.
6. Bake 45 minutes or until topping is browned and filling is bubbling.
notes: Makes 15 to 20 servings as accompaniment; 8 to 10 as dessert. Per serving (15): 225 cal, 2g pro, 31g car, 11g fat, 19mg chol with butter, 0 mg chol with margarine, 78 mg sod. MICROWAVE METHOD: Put butter in a 2-quart microwave-safe bowl. Microwave on high 1 to 2 minutes until melted. Add oats and remaining topping ingredients; stir until blended. Cover with lid or vented plastic wrap. Microwave on high 3 to 4 minutes, stirring twice, until slightly browned and bubbly. Spread out on a piece of foil to cool. Meanwhile assemble filling as directed; turn into a 2½-quart microwave-safe baking dish.
tags: Thanksgiving, dessert, cranberry, apple, fruit crunch, side dish

name: Thanksgiving Gravy
summary: When I have to go Thanksgiving on my own, I've been known (shhh) to pick up some powdered onion-soup mix and follow the gravy directions on the envelope. Now that I have (at last) this family recipe, I guess I won't have to (whew!). The broth can be made a day ahead.
ingredient_blocks:
Turkey giblets and neck
3 1/2 cup water
1  medium-size carrot, chopped (1/3 cup)
1/4 cup chopped celery
1 tsp dried thyme leaves
1/4 tsp ground black pepper
1/4 cup all-purpose flour
salt
instruction_blocks:
1. To make broth, put giblets (except liver) and neck, water, carrot, celery, thyme, salt and pepper in a 3-quart saucepan and bring to a boil.
2. Reduce heat, cover and simmer 1 hour.
3. Add liver, cover and simmer 30 minutes longer or until giblets and neck are tender.
4. Strain and reserve broth.
5. To make gravy, leave 1/4 cup drippings and all browned bits in roasting pan. Sprinkle in flour.
6. Stir over medium-high heat, scraping up browned bits in bottom of pan until smooth.
7. Remove from heat. Slowly stir in 3 cups giblet broth until blended.
8. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer 5 minutes until thickened.
notes: Makes 3 cups. Nutritional data unavailable. From Woman's Day 11/22/88. Handwritten additions on page indicate: 1/3 c. brown sugar, 1/2 tsp. allspice - these may be for a different recipe on the page.
tags: Thanksgiving, gravy, sauce, holiday, turkey

name: Impossibly Easy Pumpkin-Pecan Pie
summary: A truly delicious pie using Bisquick
ingredient_blocks:
1 cup canned pumpkin (not pumpkin pie mix)
1 cup evaporated milk
1/2 cup Original Bisquick
1/2 cup sugar
1 tbsp margarine or butter (softened)
1 1/2 tsp pumpkin pie spice
1 tsp vanilla
2  eggs
1/2 cup chopped pecans
Spiced Topping (see below)
8  pecan halves (if desired)
1 1/2 cup frozen whipped topping (thawed, for Spiced Topping)
1/4 tsp pumpkin pie spice (for Spiced Topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oven to 350°. Grease pie plate, 9 x 1 1/4 inches.
2. Stir all ingredients except pecans and Spiced Topping until blended. Stir in chopped pecans. Pour into pie plate.
3. Bake 35 to 40 minutes or until knife inserted in center comes out clean. Cool completely, about 1 hour. Cut pie into slices. Garnish each serving with Spiced Topping and pecan. Store covered in refrigerator.
4. For Spiced Topping: Stir ingredients until smooth.
notes: High Altitude (3500 to 6500 feet): Heat oven to 375°. 1 Serving: Calories 225 (Calories from fat 110); Fat 12g (Saturated 4g); Cholesterol 60mg; Sodium 180mg; Carbohydrate 26g (Dietary Fiber 2g); Protein 5g. % Daily Value: Vitamin A 74%; Vitamin C 0%; Calcium 12%; Iron 6%. Diet Exchanges: 1 Starch, 2 Vegetable, 2 Fat.
tags: pie, pumpkin, pecan, Bisquick, dessert, fall, Thanksgiving

name: Impossibly Easy Cherry-Almond Pie
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup Original Bisquick
1/4 cup sugar
3/4 cup milk
2 tbsp margarine or butter (softened)
1/4 tsp almond extract
2  eggs
1 can cherry pie filling (21 ounces)
Brown Sugar Streusel (see below)
slivered almonds (toasted, if desired)
1/2 cup Original Bisquick (for streusel)
1/2 cup packed brown sugar (for streusel)
1/2 tsp ground cinnamon (for streusel)
2 tbsp firm margarine or butter (for streusel)
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oven to 400°. Grease pie plate, 9 x 1 1/4 inches.
2. Stir all ingredients except pie filling, Brown Sugar Streusel and almonds until blended. Pour into pie plate. Spoon pie filling over top.
3. Bake 30 minutes. Sprinkle Brown Sugar Streusel over pie filling. Bake about 10 minutes or until streusel is golden brown; cool. Sprinkle with almonds.
4. Brown Sugar Streusel: Mix Bisquick, brown sugar and cinnamon. Cut in margarine, using fork or pastry blender, until mixture is crumbly.
notes: Betty's Tip: Stick with the stick! The streusel topping will blend better and be more crumbly if you use margarine or butter from a stick instead of a soft spread. High Altitude (3500 to 6500 feet): Increase second bake time to 15 to 20 minutes. Nutritional info per serving: Calories 295 (Calories from fat 90); Fat 10g (Saturated 5g); Cholesterol 70mg; Sodium 290mg; Carbohydrate 48g (Dietary Fiber 1g); Protein 4g. 9% Daily Value: Vitamin A 8%; Vitamin C 0%; Calcium 8%; Iron 6%. Diet Exchanges: 1 Starch, 2 Fruit, 2 Fat.
tags: dessert, pie, cherry, almond, Bisquick, easy

name: Impossibly Easy Ham and Swiss Pie
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 cup fully cooked ham (cut-up)
1 cup shredded Swiss cheese (4 ounces)
4  medium green onions (sliced (1/4 cup))
1/2 cup Original Bisquick
1 cup milk
1/4 tsp salt
1/8 tsp pepper
2  eggs
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oven to 400°. Grease pie plate, 9 x 1 1/4 inches. Sprinkle ham, cheese and onions in pie plate.
2. Stir remaining ingredients until blended. Pour into pie plate.
3. Bake 35 to 40 minutes or until knife inserted in center comes out clean. Let stand 5 minutes before serving.
notes: High Altitude (3500 to 6500 feet): Bake about 45 minutes. Betty's Tip: Impossibly Easy Pies are as easy to reheat as they are to make. To reheat in the microwave, arrange slices evenly spaced and with points toward the center on a large microwavable plate. Cover with waxed paper (except those with a cheese topping) and microwave on Medium 2 to 3 minutes per slice. Rotate 1/2 turn after 3 minutes. Let stand 2 minutes before serving.
tags: pie, ham, Swiss cheese, Bisquick, easy, comfort food

name: Santa Fe Potato Cakes
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup cooked instant mashed potato flakes or leftover unbuttered mashed potatoes
1 can diced green chilies (4 ounces, drained)
2/3 cup cornmeal (divided)
3  green onions (sliced)
1/3 cup shredded cheddar cheese (about 1 1/2 ounces)
2  eggs (beaten)
2 tbsp fresh cilantro (chopped)
1 tsp chili powder
1/2 tsp Lawry's Seasoned Salt
1/2 tsp Lawry's Seasoned Pepper
2 tbsp olive oil (divided)
salsa (for serving)
dairy sour cream (for serving)
instruction_blocks:
1. In large bowl, combine potatoes, chilies, 1/2 cup cornmeal, onions, cheese, eggs, cilantro, chili powder, Seasoned Salt and Seasoned Pepper; shape into eight patties.
2. Sprinkle both sides with remaining cornmeal; set aside.
3. In large nonstick skillet, heat 1 tablespoon oil over medium heat.
4. Add four patties; cook 5 to 7 minutes or until golden brown, turning once.
5. Remove from skillet; keep warm.
6. Repeat with remaining oil and patties.
7. Garnish as desired.
notes: Serving Suggestion: Serve with salsa and sour cream.
tags: Southwestern, potato, side dish, vegetarian

name: Perohe
summary: Christmas Eve main course
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup sour cream (for dough)
4 cup flour (for dough)
2 tsp salt (for dough)
1  egg (for dough)
1 1/2 cup water (for dough)
sauerkraut (for potato filling)
potatoes (for potato filling)
onions (chopped, for potato filling)
butter (for potato filling)
oil (small amount, for potato filling)
salt (for potato filling)
mushrooms (dried, for mushroom filling)
1  large onion (chopped, for mushroom filling)
oil (for mushroom filling)
1/4 lb butter (melted, for mushroom filling)
salt (to taste, for mushroom filling)
1/2 lb cheddar cheese (shredded, for cheese filling)
butter (for garnish)
onions (for garnish)
instruction_blocks:
1. DOUGH METHOD 1: Blend the 2 cups sour cream and 4 cups flour together in a mixer to make the dough. Cover dough with saran wrap and let dough rest about 30 minutes.
2. DOUGH METHOD 2: Sift flour with 2 tsp salt. Beat egg and enough water to bring measure to 1 1/2 cups. Add slowly to flour in bowl, adding only enough to make it come together. Knead on bread board about 10 minutes; dough should be soft and light. Put dough back in bowl and cover with cloth. Let rest for 20 minutes.
3. POTATO FILLING: Rinse the sauerkraut well two times in lukewarm water. Boil in fresh water; drain and let cool. Squeeze sauerkraut until dry then cut with knife or scissor. Boil potatoes in water; drain; mash. Place small amount of oil in frying pan, add chopped onions and butter. Sauté until onions are soft. Add the mixture to the mashed potatoes; add sauerkraut and remaining salt. Mix all together.
4. MUSHROOM FILLING: Soak the mushrooms in lukewarm water, until they are soft. Rinse mushrooms thoroughly, then dice. In a large skillet add some oil and 1 large chopped onion. Sauté about 2 minutes then add the chopped mushrooms and about 1/4 pound of melted butter. Add salt to taste. Continue cooking until onions are golden brown.
5. CHEDDAR CHEESE FILLING: Shred about 1/2 pound of cheddar cheese for easy filling.
6. PREPARATION: Roll dough like a pie crust. Use pasta maker to roll out. Start on setting number 8 and reduce it until you reach setting number 4. Cut into 3" circles (about the size of a 1 lb sauerkraut can). Spoon (1 tbsp) filling into shells and seal well. Place on dishtowels until ready to cook.
7. Drop into boiling water and stir with a wooden spoon. When water comes to a second boil and perohe reach the top (about 10 minutes). Drain in colander and garnish with remaining butter and onions.
notes: Source: Mom and Aunt Millie. Recipe includes three filling options: potato with sauerkraut, mushroom, or cheddar cheese.

13. Place on dishtowels until ready to cook.
14. Drop into boiling water and stir with a wooden spoon. When water comes to a second boil and perohe reach the top (about 10 minutes).
15. Drain in colander and garnish with remaining butter and onions.
notes: Recipe dated Friday, December 18, 1998. Some ingredient quantities not specified in original recipe.

name: Key Lime Pie
ingredient_blocks:
1 tbsp unflavored gelatin (1 envelope)
1/2 cup sugar
1/4 tsp salt
4  egg yolks
1/2 cup lime juice
1/4 cup water
1 tsp grated lime peel
green food coloring (few drops)
4  egg whites
1/2 cup sugar
1 cup heavy cream (whipped)
1  nine inch baked pastry shell
instruction_blocks:
1. Thoroughly mix gelatin, 1/2 cup sugar and salt in sauce pan.
2. Beat together egg yolks, lime juice and water. Stir into gelatin mixture.
3. Cook and stir over medium heat just till mixture comes to boiling.
4. Remove from heat, stir in grated peel. Add food coloring (to tint to pale green).
5. Chill, stirring occasionally until mixture mounds slightly.
6. Beat egg whites until soft peaks form. Gradually add 1/2 cup sugar into egg whites, beating to stiff peaks.
7. Fold gelatin mixture into egg whites.
8. Fold in whipped cream.
9. Pile into cooled baked pastry shell. Chill till firm.
notes: Handwritten notes indicate: Beat egg whites to stiff peaks. Fold gelatin mixture into egg whites. Recipe attributed to Elaine.
tags: pie, key lime, dessert, citrus

name: Pasta with Carbonara Sauce
ingredient_blocks:
4  eggs
1/4 cup butter or margarine
1/4 cup cream
1 lb fettucini or spaghetti
7 oz sliced pepperoni
1 cup grated Parmesan cheese
1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley
pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Let eggs, butter or margarine and cream stand at room temperature for 2 to 3 hours.
2. Beat together the eggs and cream just until blended.
3. Add pasta to a large amount of boiling, salted water. Cook pasta 10 to 12 minutes or until tender but firm. Drain well.
4. Heat an oven proof serving dish in a 250 degree oven.
5. Turn pasta into heated serving dish. Toss pasta with butter and pepperoni.
6. Pour egg mixture over and toss until pasta is well coated.
7. Add Parmesan cheese and parsley. Toss to mix. Serve immediately.
notes: Serves 10-12 side dishes OR 4-5 main dishes. Recipe from Ann DelSoldato.
tags: Italian, pasta, carbonara, comfort food

name: Proscuitto Pie
ingredient_blocks:

instruction_blocks:

2. Make a well, place eggs in center and add water as needed.
3. Knead well. Place dough between two dishes for 15-20 minutes.
4. While dough is resting, prepare the filling.
5. Mix all filling ingredients together.
6. Roll the dough out to twice the size of a cookie sheet.
7. Fill half the bread, seal, baste with the one egg yolk which has been beaten.
8. Bake at 375 degrees for 1-1 1/2 hours.
notes: Handwritten note says 'Make a well' which corresponds to the instruction about placing eggs in center. The word 'Make hole' was crossed out and replaced with this technique.

name: Basic Pasta Dough
ingredient_blocks:
3 1/2 cup flour
4  extra-large eggs
1/2 tsp extra-virgin olive oil
instruction_blocks:
1. Mound 3 1/2 cups of the flour in the center of a large wooden cutting board. Make a well in the middle of the flour and add the eggs and the olive oil.
2. Using a fork, beat together the eggs and oil and begin to incorporate the flour, starting with the inner rim of the well.
3. As you expand the well, keep pushing the flour up from the base of the mound to retain the well shape. The dough will come together when half of the flour is incorporated.
4. Start kneading the dough with both hands, using the palms of your hands. Once you have a cohesive mass, remove the dough from the board and scrape up and discard any leftover bits. Lightly reflour the board and continue kneading for 6 more minutes.
5. The dough should be elastic and a little sticky. Wrap the dough in plastic and allow to rest for 30 minutes at room temperature.
6. Roll or shape as desired.
notes: Recipe copyright 2000, Mario Batali. All rights reserved. From Molto Mario Show Schedule.
tags: Italian, pasta, basic, dough, homemade

instruction_blocks: 1. No specific instructions provided on this page. Recipe refers to standard pizzelle iron cooking method.
notes: Six eggs is a standard size batch, making around 60 cookies and takes about 45 minutes to 1 hour to complete. Half batch alternative: 3 eggs, 1 3/4 cup flour, 3/4 cup sugar, 1 tbsp vanilla essence, 1/2 cup butter (one stick; 1/4 pound). Additional classic recipes available: Classic Pizzelle Recipe 2, 3, 4, Minnie's Pizzelle, and Reduced Fat Pizzelle.

name: Classic Pizzelle Recipe 2
ingredient_blocks:
6  eggs
4 cup flour
2 cup sugar
1 tsp vanilla essence
1 cup butter (two sticks; 1/2 pound)
2 tsp baking powder
instruction_blocks:
1. Melt butter, add sugar and beat well.
2. Add egg yolks and vanilla and mix well.
3. In a separate bowl mix the flour and baking powder and then gradually add to the butter and egg mixture.
4. In a separate bowl beat the egg whites until stiff then fold carefully into the butter/egg/flour mixture.
5. Spoon onto pizzelle iron and bake.
notes: Half batch version: 3 eggs, 2 cups flour, 1 cup sugar, 1/2 tsp vanilla essence, 1/2 cup butter (one stick; 1/4 pound), 1 tsp baking powder. Page also contains partial recipes for Classic Pizzelle Recipe 1 and Classic Pizzelle Recipe 3.

instruction_blocks:

3  eggs (smaller batch)

3 1/2 tbsp sugar (smaller batch)
1 1/2 tsp anise extract (smaller batch)
4 tbsp margarine (smaller batch)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cream together margarine and sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Beat in eggs, one at a time. Beat mixture vigorously.
3. Add flour gradually, continuing to beat vigorously, until there are no lumps.
4. Beat in the anise.
5. The mixture should ribbon when dropped from a spoon.
6. Bake until golden.
notes: Reduced Fat Pizzelle variations submitted by Micki Centrone: 1. Use 'I Can't Believe it's Not Butter' brand in place of butter or oil. 2. Replace half of the eggs called for with egg whites (for 3 eggs use 1 egg and 2 egg whites). Recipe includes two batch sizes - a larger batch and a smaller batch.

instruction_blocks:

4. Sprinkle cheese over top if desired.
notes: The butter amount appears to be 1/4 cup plus 2 tablespoons

name: Rice Pudding
ingredient_blocks:
10 cup milk
1 cup plain white rice (NOT Uncle Ben's)
3  eggs
1/2 cup raisins
1 cup sugar
1 tbsp vanilla
cinnamon or nutmeg (for topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. Use a fairly big pot and stir with a wooden spoon.
2. Bring milk and 1/2 cup of sugar to a boil. Add rice. Stir as you pour.
3. Cook on medium heat and keep at a rolling boil stirring occasionally, for 35 minutes (no less).
4. Add raisins. Stir. Cook for an additional ten minutes. Remove from heat.
5. Stir egg and rest of sugar in a large bowl. Do not beat. Keep adding rice liquid into eggs (about 8 tablespoons), until eggs are warmed.
6. Pour egg mixture into rice pot and stir slowly. Add vanilla stirring slowly.
7. Pour into at least a 9 x 9 pan.
8. Shake cinnamon and sugar together OR nutmeg and sugar together, and sprinkle over pudding. Let cool.
9. To refrigerate, cover when cooled or water condenses and drips on pudding, OR cool in refrigerator and then cover.
notes: From: Rose D'Agostino. Sugar is divided - 1/2 cup goes into the milk mixture and the rest goes with the eggs.
tags: dessert, pudding, comfort food, family recipe

4. Blend salt with sugar and tapioca and sprinkle over ingredients.
5. Add tomato juice.
6. Cover pan with sheet of heavy duty foil and crimp edges to seal tightly.
7. Bake in slow oven (250 degrees) for four hours.
8. Serve with rice, noodles or mashed potatoes.

1/2 cup sweet milk (NOT EVAPORATED)
1 cup light brown sugar (topping)
1/2 cup plain flour (topping)
1 cup chopped nuts (topping)
1/2 stick butter (topping)
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Mix all casserole ingredients together and put in 10-inch Pyrex dish.
3. Mix all topping ingredients and spread on top of sweet potatoes.
4. Bake for 35 minutes and enjoy.

name: Sausage Bread
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb sausage (1/2 Hot, 1/2 Sweet)
1 lb bread dough (frozen)
0.5 lb mozzarella (1/2 LB shredded)
1.75  eggs (lightly beaten)
Parmesan cheese (add to taste)
1 tbsp oregano
parsley (fresh chopped)
0.25  eggs (lightly beaten, for sealing)
instruction_blocks:
1. Remove frozen bread dough from freezer and place in greased bowl.
2. Cover with waxed paper and let it rise.
3. In the meantime, remove casing from sausage, fry, and drain thoroughly on paper towel.
4. Mix sausage, mozzarella, eggs, parmesan, parsley, and oregano in large bowl.
5. When dough has risen roll out on jelly roll fashion.
6. Spread the sausage mixture on dough and roll like a jelly roll.
7. Seal edges with part of remainder of eggs and put the rest on top.
8. Bake in a 350 degree oven for 30 minutes.
9. Serve warm.
notes: Time to prepare listed as 5 hours, likely includes dough rising time. Eggs are listed twice in ingredients (1.75 and 0.25) which together make 2 eggs total.
tags: appetizers, Italian, sausage, bread

name: Ultimate Quick-and-Easy Pasta Sauce
summary: This easy-to-make sauce is so tasty and versatile, it's used as the basis of all the recipes that follow. You can substitute crushed or whole tomatoes for the diced. Crushed will give you a smooth, thick sauce; whole adds a nice chunkiness. Serve this sauce with your favorite pasta.
ingredient_blocks:
1 tsp olive oil
1 cup onion (chopped)
4  garlic cloves (minced)
1/2 cup dry red wine (or 2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar)
1 tbsp sugar
1 tbsp fresh basil (chopped, or 2 teaspoons dried basil)
2 tbsp tomato paste
1/2 tsp dried Italian seasoning
1/4 tsp black pepper
2  diced tomatoes (14.5-ounce cans, undrained)
2 tbsp fresh parsley (chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat oil in a saucepan or large skillet over medium-high heat.
2. Add onion and garlic; sauté 5 minutes.
3. Stir in wine and next 6 ingredients (wine through tomatoes), and bring to a boil.
4. Reduce heat to medium, and cook, uncovered, about 15 minutes.
5. Stir in parsley.
notes: Yield: 3 cups (serving size: 1 cup). This recipe has two variations: Puttanesca Sauce (adds olives, capers, anchovy paste, and crushed red pepper) and Salsa del Sol (adds jalapeño, cumin, cinnamon, tequila, and cilantro for Mexican-inspired flavors). Nutrition per serving: Calories 126 (16% from fat); Fat 2.3g (sat 0.4g, mono 1.3g, poly 0.5g); Protein 4.1g; Carb 25.2g; Fiber 3.7g; Chol 0mg; Iron 2.8mg; Sodium 461mg; Calc 110mg

name: Stuffed Cabbage
ingredient_blocks:
dill seed
2  large heads cabbage
1  large can crushed tomatoes
1  large can sauerkraut (rinsed and drained)
1  can tomato soup
1  large onion (grated)
3 lb ground beef
1 lb ground pork
3  slices bacon (diced, partially cooked)
2  eggs (beaten)
3/4 cup uncooked rice
2 tsp salt
1/2 tsp Lowrey salt
1/4 tsp garlic
1/2 tsp pepper
1/4-1/2 tsp dill weed
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix sauerkraut, crushed tomatoes and tomato soup together.
2. Rinse heads of cabbage, cut around core, microwave about 20 minutes or until tender enough to tear off and roll. If no microwave, boil in water until tender enough to roll.
3. Stuff leaves with meat mixture. Any left over cabbage, chop up and add to kraut mixture.
4. Have large roaster or deep casserole. Place a layer of kraut mixture in bottom of pan.
5. Add one layer of cabbage rolls, sprinkle with dill seed.
6. Add kraut mixture, cabbage rolls, dill seed, etc.
7. Pour about two cups of water over entire casserole.
8. Bake at 350 degrees for 3-4 hours.
9. Serve with sour cream.
notes: Handwritten note in margin says 'meat mixture' with an arrow pointing to the meat ingredients (ground beef, pork, bacon, eggs, rice, and seasonings). Cook time listed as 3-4 hours, entered as 210 minutes (3.5 hours average).

name: Lobster Stuffed Tenderloin of Beef
ingredient_blocks:
3-4 lb whole beef tenderloin
2  frozen lobster tails (4oz each)

instruction_blocks: 1. Instructions not provided on this recipe card - only ingredients are listed.

name: Lobster Stuffed Beef Tenderloin
summary: Beef tenderloin butterflied and stuffed with lobster tails
ingredient_blocks:
1  beef tenderloin
frozen lobster tails
1 tbsp melted butter
lemon juice
bacon slices
green onion
butter (for cooking onions)
wine
garlic salt
fresh thyme
instruction_blocks:
1. Cut tenderloin lengthwise to within 1/2 inch of bottom to butterfly.
2. Place frozen lobster tails in boiling salted water to cover. Return to boiling, reduce heat and simmer 5-6 minutes.
3. Carefully remove lobster from shells. Cut in half lengthwise. Place lobster end to end inside beef.
4. Combine the 1 tablespoon melted butter and lemon juice. Drizzle on lobster.
5. Close meat around lobster; tie roast together securely with string.
6. Place on rack in shallow roasting pan.
7. Roast in 425° oven for 45-50 minutes for rare.
8. Lay bacon slices atop; roast 5 minutes more.
9. Meanwhile, cook green onion in remaining butter til tender. Add wine, garlic salt and fresh thyme.
10. To serve, slice roast and spoon on wine sauce.
notes: Some handwriting was difficult to read, particularly around the sauce ingredients. The recipe appears to be for a special occasion dish combining surf and turf.
