name: Carolina Barbecue
summary: Favorite recipe from National Pork Producers Council
ingredient_blocks:
1  Boston butt roast (5-pound)
2 tsp vegetable oil
1 1/2 cup water
1  tomato sauce (8 ounces can)
1/4 cup packed brown sugar
1/4 cup cider vinegar
1/4 cup Worcestershire sauce
salt and pepper (to taste)
1 tsp celery seeds
1 tsp chili powder
dash hot pepper sauce
instruction_blocks:
1. Randomly pierce roast with sharp knife.
2. In Dutch oven, brown roast on all sides in hot oil.
3. In mixing bowl, combine remaining ingredients; mix well.
4. Pour sauce over roast and bring to a boil.
5. Reduce heat; cover and simmer 2 hours or until pork is fork-tender.
6. Baste roast with sauce during cooking time.
7. Slice or chop to serve.

name: Grilled Caribbean Steaks
summary: Rubbing a dry marinade on meat results in a more intense flavor than a liquid marinade. For best flavor in this recipe, rub the steaks with the herb and spice mixture two or three days before cooking.
ingredient_blocks:
6 tbsp brown sugar
2 1/2 tbsp paprika
2 tbsp granulated sugar
1 tbsp kosher salt
1 tbsp chili powder
1 1/4 tsp granulated garlic or garlic powder
1 1/4 tsp dried oregano leaves
1 1/4 tsp dried basil leaves
3/4 tsp dried thyme leaves
3/4 tsp celery seed
1/4 tsp cayenne pepper
2  lean beef T-bone steaks (12 to 16 ounces each, 1 inch thick)
instruction_blocks:
1. To prepare spice mix, combine all ingredients except steak in small bowl; mix well.
2. Measure out 1/4 cup spice mix, reserving remaining for other uses.
3. Rub steaks with 1/4 cup spice mix, using 1 tablespoon per side.
4. Refrigerate steaks, covered, overnight or up to 3 days.
5. Grill steaks on covered grill over medium Kingsford briquets 12 to 14 minutes for medium-rare or to desired doneness, turning once.
notes: Recipe for spice mix makes 1 1/4 cups. Store leftover spice mix in covered container in cool, dry place. Use with beef, pork or chicken. Nutrients per Serving (1/6 of recipe): Calories: 205, protein: 25 g, fat: 9 g, carbohydrate: 3 g, sodium: 297 mg, cholesterol: 72 mg
tags: Caribbean, grilling, beef, steak, entertaining, spice rub

name: Stracotto di Manzo (Italian Pot Roast)
ingredient_blocks:
1 oz dried porcini mushrooms
2 cup beef stock
salt and pepper
1 lb beef eye-of-round (4-pound)
2 tbsp olive oil
2 tbsp pancetta (chopped)
1  onion (finely chopped)
1  carrot (finely chopped)
1  celery stalk (finely chopped)
3  cloves garlic (minced)
2  bay leaves
1 tbsp fresh rosemary (chopped)
1/4 cup parsley leaves (tightly packed, chopped)
3 tbsp tomato paste
5 cup red wine
1  whole tomatoes (28-ounce can, drained and crushed)
instruction_blocks:
1. Soak the porcini mushrooms in 1 cup of hot stock until softened, about 30 minutes. Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or a sieve lined with a double layer of cheesecloth. Trim any hard bits from the mushrooms. Set aside the mushrooms and liquid separately.
2. Season the beef with salt and pepper. In a large casserole, heat 2 tablespoons of the olive oil over medium high heat until hot but not smoking. Brown the beef on all sides, about 15 minutes total. Transfer the beef to a plate.
3. Pour off browning fat. Add pancetta, onions, carrots, and celery, stirring, until they are golden. Add garlic and cook 1 minute more. Season lightly with salt and pepper. Stir in bay leaves, rosemary, parsley, and tomato paste. Add the wine and simmer until it is reduced by half, about 20 minutes.
4. Add the beef, tomatoes, porcini mushrooms, mushroom liquid, and enough beef stock to come 2/3 up the side of the beef. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook at a gentle simmer for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, until the meat is tender. Turn the roast every 30 minutes or so.
5. Transfer the meat to a cutting board and cover with aluminum foil to keep warm. Skim off any fat from the surface of the liquid. Strain the liquid, pressing on the solids with a spoon to extract as much liquid as possible. Discard the solids and return the strained sauce to the pot. Bring to a boil and let cook uncovered for a few minutes to further reduce and thicken the liquid. Season with salt and pepper, to taste. Slice the roast and serve with the sauce and soft polenta.
notes: Recipe Courtesy Sara Mouton. Episode#: CL9395. Copyright © 2003 Television Food Network, G.P., All Rights Reserved. Handwritten notes on recipe appear to say 'Food TV Recipes' and 'Every day Italian'.
tags: Italian, comfort food, pot roast, beef, braised, main course

name: Stracoto with Porcini Mushrooms
summary: Recipe courtesy Giada De Laurentiis
ingredient_blocks:
1  boneless beef chuck roast (4-pound)
salt (to taste)
freshly ground black pepper (to taste)
3 tbsp olive oil
2  onions (sliced)
6  garlic cloves (coarsely chopped)
1 cup dry red wine
1 3/4 cup canned beef broth
1/2 oz dried porcini mushrooms
1  large sprig fresh rosemary (plus extra for garnish)
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F.
2. Pat the beef dry with paper towels. Sprinkle the beef generously with salt and pepper.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a heavy 6-quart roasting pan over medium-high heat. Add the beef and cook until brown on all sides, about 15 minutes total cooking time.
4. Transfer the beef to a bowl. Add remaining tablespoon oil to the pan, add the onions and saute until tender, scraping up the brown bits on the bottom of the pot, about 5 minutes.
5. Add the garlic and saute 1 minute. Add the wine and boil 1 minute. Stir in the broth and mushrooms.
6. Return the beef to the pan. Bring the liquids to a boil. Cover and transfer to the oven. Braise until the beef is fork-tender, turning the beef over halfway through cooking, about 3 hours.
7. Transfer the beef to a cutting board. Tent the beef with foil and let stand 15 minutes.
8. Meanwhile, spoon any excess fat off the top of the pan juices. Transfer the pan juices and vegetables to a blender and puree until smooth. Combine the sauce and rosemary sprig in heavy medium saucepan. Bring to a boil. Season the sauce, to taste, with salt and pepper.
9. Cut the beef across the grain into 1/2-inch-thick slices. Arrange the sliced beef on a platter and garnish with rosemary. Spoon the sauce over and serve, passing the remaining sauce in a sauce boat.
notes: Episode#: EI1D11. Inactive prep time: 15 minutes (resting time for beef).
tags: Italian, beef, braised, comfort food, porcini mushrooms, Giada De Laurentiis

name: Turkey Soup
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp butter
1/2  onion (medium, diced, for sautéing)
2 1/2  onion (medium, diced, for soup)
2  carrots (cleaned and diced, for sautéing)
3  carrots (cleaned and diced, for soup)
2  celery (cleaned and diced, for sautéing)
4  celery (cleaned and diced, for soup)
1 clove garlic (pressed)
5 quart water
2  bouillon cubes
2  tomatoes (diced)
1  turkey carcass
2 cup turkey breast meat (shredded)
sausage stuffing (optional topping)
parmesan cheese (grated, optional topping)
orzo (cooked separately)
instruction_blocks:
1. In large cast iron pot spray the bottom with oil and then add butter, 1/2 onion, carrots, celery and garlic and sauté at VERY LOW HEAT until vegetables are clear, about 15 minutes.
2. Then add the water, carcass, and remaining vegetables EXCEPT the TOMATOES.
3. Bring soup up to slow rolling boil then add bouillon cubes.
4. Cook for about 1 1/2 hours then add the tomatoes.
5. Cook for another hour.
6. Then strain the soup into another pot. From the strained vegetable remove those pieces you do not want and add them back to the broth.
7. Using the hand mixture puree the broth completely.
8. Then add the shredded turkey.
9. When preparing for dinner heat up the soup, cook Orzo's independent of the soup and then add to the soup.
10. Options: Put into cups, top with sausage stuffing and a little grated parmesan. Put into broiler for a couple of minutes and serve.
notes: The soup can be frozen for later use. The recipe separates vegetables into two groups - some for sautéing and some added later with the water.
tags: soup, turkey, leftover turkey, comfort food, freezer-friendly

name: Delmonico's Yellowfin Tuna Nicoise
summary: Chef Neal likes to use picholine and nicoise olives when he makes this main course salad, but use whatever you like best. Tuna loins are preferable for this recipe, but use tuna steaks if you can't find the loins.
ingredient_blocks:
3/4 cup Creamy Parmesan Dressing (recipe follows)
1 lb red potatoes (scrubbed and cut lengthwise into 1/4-inch thick slices)
6 oz haricots verts (or small, thin green beans, ends trimmed)
1/2 cup olive oil (plus 1 tablespoon)
4 tsp salt
2 tsp ground black pepper
2 tsp garlic (minced)
1 lb Roma plum tomatoes (cut into 1-inch cubes)
1/2 cup black brine-cured olives (halved and seeded)
1/2 cup green brine-cured olives (halved and seeded)
1/2 cup mayonnaise (preferably homemade)
1 tsp lemon juice
1 tbsp red onion (minced)
1/2  hard boiled egg (peeled and coarsely sieved)
1 tsp capers (drained and chopped)
2 tsp fresh parsley (chopped)
1/2 tsp fresh tarragon (chopped)
2  hearts of romaine lettuce (rinsed and patted dry)
2 1/2 lb yellowfin tuna (trimmed of sinew, cut into 6 equal rectangular portions, about 1 by 1 by 4-inches each)
instruction_blocks:
1. Make the Creamy Parmesan Dressing and set aside.
2. Bring 2 medium pots of salted water to a boil. Add the potatoes to 1 pot and blanch until just tender, about 3 1/2 minutes. Drain in a colander.
3. Add the green beans to the other pot and blanch until tender, 4 minutes. Drain in a colander.
notes: Recipe courtesy Emeril Lagasse, 2002. From The Essence of Emeril show. Originally aired on 07/21/2002 at 9:00 AM ET. This is page 1 of 3, instructions may be incomplete.
tags: seafood, tuna, salad, nicoise, French, main course

name: Delmonico's Yellowfin Tuna Nicoise
summary: Seared yellowfin tuna served on a bed of dressed lettuce with warm potato and vegetable medley
ingredient_blocks:
1/4 cup oil (for potatoes, plus remaining tablespoon)
drained potatoes
1 tsp salt (divided)
1/2 tsp pepper
drained green beans
1 tbsp oil
garlic
tomatoes
olives
mayonnaise
lemon juice
minced onion
hard-boiled egg
capers
parsley
tarragon
lettuce
tuna
1/4 cup oil (for searing tuna)
1  large egg (for dressing)
1/4 tsp kosher salt (for dressing)
1/4 tsp freshly ground black pepper (for dressing)
1/2 tsp chopped garlic (for dressing)
2  anchovy fillets (for dressing)
2 tbsp grated Parmesan (for dressing)
1 tbsp fresh lemon juice (for dressing)
1 tsp Dijon mustard (for dressing)
1/4 cup extra virgin olive oil (for dressing)
1 tsp Worcestershire Sauce (for dressing)
2 drops hot pepper sauce (for dressing)
instruction_blocks:
1. Heat 1/4 cup of the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the drained potatoes, 1 teaspoon of the salt, and 1/2 teaspoon of the pepper, and cook, shaking the pan occasionally, until golden brown on both sides, 8 minutes.
2. Add the remaining tablespoon of oil, garlic, and drained green beans. Stir to combine and cook for 1 minute.
3. Add the tomatoes and olives and cook until the tomatoes are warmed through, stirring occasionally, 2 minutes. Remove from the heat.
4. Add the mayonnaise, lemon juice, minced onion, hard-boiled egg, capers, parsley, and tarragon and stir to combine. Taste and adjust seasoning, if necessary.
5. Combine the lettuce with the Creamy Parmesan Dressing and toss to coat. Arrange 3 or 4 leaves along the side of 6 large plates. Spoon the vegetables along the other side of the plate. Let sit while cooking the tuna.
6. Season each tuna portion with 1/2 teaspoon of the remaining salt, and 1/4 teaspoon of the remaining pepper.
7. Heat the remaining 1/4 cup of oil in 1 large skillet or 2 medium skillets, over medium-high heat. Add the tuna and sear, about 15 seconds per side. Remove from the pan and cut each crosswise into 4 equal pieces. Arrange the tuna slices down the middle of each plate, and serve.
8. For Creamy Parmesan Dressing: Bring a small saucepan of water to a boil. Add the egg and cook for 30 seconds until coddled.
9. In a small bowl, combine the salt, pepper, garlic, and anchovies. Mash the anchovies with the back of a fork and mix into the other ingredients. Add the egg and whisk well to combine.
10. Add the cheese, lemon juice, and mustard, and whisk well. Add the olive oil in a steady stream, whisking constantly. Add the Worcestershire and pepper sauces, and whisk well. Taste and adjust seasoning, if necessary.
notes: This is page 2 of 3 from a Food Network recipe. Some ingredient quantities from previous page may be missing. Recipe includes a separate Creamy Parmesan Dressing component.
tags: seafood, tuna, French, nicoise, salad, seared, Delmonico's, restaurant recipe

name: Tiramisu (Low Fat but Delicious)
summary: A lighter version of the classic Italian dessert
ingredient_blocks:
2/3 cup sifted powdered sugar
1  reduced-fat cream cheese (8oz tub)
1-1/2 cup frozen reduced-calorie whipped topping (thawed and divided)
1/2 cup sugar
1/4 cup water
3  egg whites
1/2 cup hot water
1 tbsp sugar
1 tbsp instant espresso coffee granules
2 tbsp Kahlúa or other coffee-flavored liqueur
20  ladyfingers
1/2 tsp unsweetened cocoa
1/2 tsp shaved chocolate (optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine powdered sugar and cream cheese in a bowl, and beat at high speed of a mixer until well blended. Gently fold 1 cup whipped topping into the cheese mixture.
2. Combine 1/2 cup sugar and next 2 ingredients (water and egg whites) in the top of a double boiler; place over simmering water. Beat at high speed of a mixer until stiff peaks form. Gently stir one-fourth of egg white mixture into cheese mixture. Gently fold in remaining egg white mixture, set aside.
3. Combine hot water and next 3 ingredients (1 tbsp sugar, espresso granules, and Kahlúa); stir well. Split the ladyfingers in half lengthwise. Arrange 20 ladyfinger halves, cut sides up, in the bottom of an 8-inch baking dish. Drizzle half of the espresso mixture over ladyfinger halves. Spread half of cheese mixture over ladyfinger halves; repeat procedure with the remaining ladyfinger halves, espresso mixture, and cheese mixture.
4. Spread remaining 1/2 cup whipped topping evenly over cheese mixture; sprinkle with cocoa. (Can also sprinkle shaved chocolate over the top as well).
5. Place one toothpick in each corner and in center of Tiramisu to prevent plastic wrap from sticking to whipped topping; cover with plastic wrap. Chill 2 hours.
notes: Try freezing Tiramisu for two hours before serving so it will cut cleanly. Serving size: 1 [4x2-inch piece]. Nutrition per serving: CALORIES 226 (28% from fat); PROTEIN 4.7g; FAT 7g (sat 4.1g, mono 2g, poly 0.8g); CARB 30g; FIBER 0g; CHOL 41g; IRON 0.1mg; SODIUM 199mg; CALC 49.
tags: Italian, dessert, low fat, tiramisu, coffee

name: Gingerbread Tiramisu Torte
summary: A gingerbread twist on classic tiramisu using gingersnap cookies
ingredient_blocks:
1/3 cup hot water
8 tbsp sugar (divided: 1/4 cup + 6 tbsp)
1 tbsp unsweetened cocoa powder (plus 1 tsp)
5 tsp vanilla extract (divided: 2 tsp + 2 tsp + remaining)
2 tsp instant espresso powder
1 qt heavy cream (divided: 2 cups + remaining)
8 oz mascarpone cheese (room temperature)
60  round gingersnaps (2 inch)
1 oz semisweet chocolate (grated)
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine hot water with 2 tbs sugar, 1 tbs cocoa, 2 tsp vanilla and espresso powder; set aside.
2. In bowl with mixer at high speed, beat 2 cups cream with 6 tbs sugar and 2 tsp vanilla until soft peaks form.
3. Fold in cheese until combined.
4. Line 9 x 5 x 2 3/4 pan with plastic wrap to overhang on all sides by 2 inches.
5. Arrange 12 (or less) cookies over bottom of pan.
6. Brush with 1/4 of cocoa mixture.
7. Top with 1 cup cream mixture; sprinkle with 1/4 of the chocolate.
8. Repeat layering 3 more times using cookies, remaining cocoa mixture, cream mixture and chocolate.
9. Arrange remaining cookies over top.
10. Cover with plastic. Refrigerate until set; at least 2 hours.
11. Invert onto platter. Remove plastic.
12. Beat together remaining cream, sugar and vanilla until stiff.
13. Spread remaining cream mixture over top and sides of cake.
14. Sprinkle with remaining cocoa.
notes: 569 calories and 43 grams of fat per serving. It's worth it!!!
tags: dessert, tiramisu, gingerbread, no-bake, Italian

name: Zucchini Au Gratin
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp olive or corn oil
3-4  tender zucchini (washed, peeled and cut into thin rounds)
1/2 lb mozzarella (cut in small cubes)
1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese
salt (to taste)
1 sprig fresh parsley (chopped)

instruction_blocks:

2. Pour oil into 8" baking dish.
3. Add 1/3 of the zucchini, mozzarella, parmesan, salt to taste, and parsley.
4. Layer remaining 2/3s.
5. Cover with bread crumbs and dot with butter.
6. Bake uncovered for 45-50 minutes.

name: Pumpkin Bread
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup sugar
1 cup vegetable oil
4  eggs
3 cup all-purpose flour (sifted)
1 tsp cinnamon
1 1/2 tsp salt
1 tsp nutmeg
2 tsp baking soda
1 tbsp baking powder
2/3 cup water
2 cup pumpkin (cooked, mashed)
1 cup chopped nuts or seeds
instruction_blocks:
1. In a mixing bowl, mix the sugar and oil. Beat in the eggs one at a time.
2. Onto a piece of waxed paper, sift together the flour, spices, soda and baking powder.
3. Add the dry ingredients to the first mixture alternately with the water, mixing well between additions.
4. Add the pumpkin, stir well, then stir in the nuts.
5. Pour into two greased 5-by-8-inch loaf pans.
6. Bake in a preheated 350-degree oven for 50 minutes or until breads test done.
notes: Makes two loaves. Use 5x8-inch loaf pans.

name: Broccoli Salad
ingredient_blocks:
4 cup broccoli (raw, chopped)
1/4 cup bacon bits
1/2 cup raisins
1/2 cup sunflower seeds
1  small sweet onion (cut in rings)
6-8  mushrooms (sliced)
1/2 cup mayo (for dressing)
1/2 cup sugar (for dressing)
1/4 cup vinegar (for dressing)
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine broccoli, bacon bits, raisins, sunflower seeds, sweet onion, and mushrooms.
2. Make dressing by mixing mayo, sugar, and vinegar.
3. Mix in the dressing before serving.
tags: salad, broccoli, side dish, no-cook

name: Mom's Chicken Soup
ingredient_blocks:
3 lb chicken breasts
4 qt water
5  carrot (chopped)
6  celery stalks
1 tsp parsley
2  onion (chopped)
1  bouquet garni
1 oz can tomato sauce (possibly 8 oz can)
2 tsp chopped garlic
instruction_blocks:
1. Boil water
2. Add all ingredients
3. Cook
notes: Handwriting is difficult to read in places. The tomato sauce amount appears to be '1 8oz can' but is unclear. There's a note that says 'take off' which may refer to removing chicken or skimming. Bouquet garni interpretation is uncertain.
tags: soup, chicken, comfort food, family recipe

name: Grilled Chicken Pasta Salad
ingredient_blocks:
2/3 cup olive oil
1/3 cup vinegar
1/2 cup torn basil leaves or coarsely chopped parsley
4  skinless, boneless chicken breast halves
4 cup cooked bow tie or medium tube-shaped macaroni
1 1/2 cup Pepperidge Farm Home Style Caesar Croutons
1/2 cup drained Sonoma Marinated Dried Tomatoes (cut into strips)
1/2 cup Planters Gold Measure Walnuts (coarsely chopped)
Grated Parmesan cheese (for garnish)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix oil, vinegar and basil. Reserve 2/3 cup dressing.
2. Grill or broil chicken 15 minutes or until done, turning and brushing often with remaining dressing. Slice chicken into thin strips.
3. Toss macaroni, croutons, tomatoes, chicken and walnuts with reserved dressing. Garnish with cheese. Serve immediately.
notes: This is a Planters Gold Measure Walnuts promotional recipe
tags: pasta salad, chicken, grilled, summer, main dish

name: Beef Stew
ingredient_blocks:
2 lb beef stew meat (cut in 1½" cubes)
1  medium onion
1 stalk celery
6  carrots
2 tsp salt
1 tbsp sugar
2 tbsp tapioca
1/2 cup tomato juice
instruction_blocks:
1. Place meat (cut in 1½" cubes) in pan 11 x 7 x 1½.
2. Peel and slice onion. Diagonally slice stalk of celery, pare carrots and cut into chunks.
3. Add vegetables to pan.
4. Blend salt with sugar and tapioca and sprinkle over ingredients.
5. Add tomato juice.
6. Cover pan with sheet of heavy duty foil and crimp edges to seal tightly.
7. Bake in slow oven (250 deg.) for 4 hours.
tags: beef, stew, comfort food, slow-cooked, one-pan

name: Apricot Nectar Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1  Duncan Hines Lemon Supreme Cake Mix (pkg)
4  eggs
1/3 cup salad oil
1 cup apricot nectar
1/2 cup sugar
1  lemon (juice of, for glaze)
confectioners sugar (enough to make pouring consistency, for glaze)
instruction_blocks:
1. Blend for 1 minute. Mix 4 minutes.
2. Bake in well floured greased bundt pan per directions - 38-45 min at 350°.
3. For glaze: Mix juice of 1 lemon with enough confectioners sugar to thin to pouring consistency.
4. Make holes with toothpick.
5. Pour over cake while cake is warm.
notes: Some handwriting was difficult to read. The instruction to make holes with toothpick allows the glaze to soak into the warm cake.
tags: cake, lemon, apricot, bundt cake, glazed

name: Totdoes
ingredient_blocks:
5 cup flour (sifted)
2  sticks margarine (Blue Bonnet, melted & cooled)
1/2 doz eggs (beaten)
5 tsp baking powder
1 tsp vanilla
1 cup sugar
instruction_blocks:
1. Measure flour then sift with baking powder added.
2. Mix eggs and sugar together. Add vanilla, then margarine.
3. Gradually add flour mixture.
4. Dough will be soft. Let sit about 10 minutes.
5. Put some flour on a sheet of waxed paper. Then flour your hands.
6. Scoop out by the teaspoonful (size of walnut) and then roll in the palm of your hand.
7. Place on greased cookie sheet 1" apart.
8. Bake at 400° (preheated) for 8-10 minutes on a rack in middle of oven.
notes: Title may be 'Totdoes' or similar - handwriting unclear. These appear to be small rolled cookies.
tags: cookies, vintage recipe, baked goods

name: Almond Crusted Chicken
ingredient_blocks:
1/4 cup dry bread crumbs
coarse salt (to taste)
freshly ground pepper (to taste)
2  large eggs
2 tsp water
2  whole boneless skinless chicken breasts (1 1/2 to 2 pounds, split)
1 1/2 cup sliced almonds (broken into pieces)
2 tbsp unsalted butter
2 tbsp canola oil
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat oven to 400°. In a medium bowl, season bread crumbs with salt and pepper. Place eggs in a small bowl with 2 teaspoons water, and beat lightly. Dip chicken in egg, wiping away excess with your fingers, and dip in bread-crumb mixture. Dredge until lightly coated. Dip in egg again, and coat thoroughly with almonds.
2. Heat butter and oil in a 12-inch ovenproof skillet over medium heat. Sauté chicken until nicely browned, about 3 minutes, and turn over. Cook 1 minute more; then transfer pan to oven, and bake until chicken is cooked through, about 10 minutes.
notes: Recipe from Martha Stewart Living, April 2001
tags: chicken, almond, main dish, baked

name: Romaine Salad with Prosciutto Crisps
summary: The small inner leaves of romaine lettuce are tender and crisp, perfect for this salad. Use a vegetable peeler to shave the Pecorino Romano.
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil (plus more for pan)
4  slices prosciutto (about 2 ounces)
1 tbsp balsamic vinegar
1 tsp fresh thyme leaves (roughly chopped)
coarse salt (to taste)
freshly ground pepper (to taste)
6 oz hearts of romaine (leaves torn in half)
1 oz Pecorino Romano shavings
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat oven to 400°. Lightly brush a baking sheet with olive oil, and arrange prosciutto in a single layer. Place in oven, and bake until crisp, 5 to 10 minutes. Remove from oven, and let cool on a wire rack.
2. Combine olive oil, balsamic vinegar, and thyme, season with salt and pepper, and whisk to combine. Add romaine and shaved Pecorino Romano, and toss to combine. Serve topped with prosciutto crisps.
notes: Recipe from Martha Stewart Living April 2001
tags: salad, Italian, prosciutto, appetizer, light

name: Susan's Cheesecake
summary: A rich cheesecake with graham cracker crust baked in a spring pan
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/2 pint sour cream
7  eggs
4 oz cream cheese (8-oz packages)
4 tsp vanilla
2 cup sugar
graham crackers (crushed, for crust)
butter (for greasing pan)
instruction_blocks:
1. Put all ingredients in a large bowl and beat for 15-20 minutes.
2. Butter bottom and sides of spring pan and line with a layer of crushed graham crackers to form crust.
3. Pour in filling mixture and bake in 375° oven until top turns brown (about 55 minutes).
notes: Document also contains a second cheesecake recipe: Roll out 17 zwiebacks with rolling pin and mix with 2 tablespoons butter and 2 tablespoons sugar. Line 8" pyrex dish with mixture. Filling: 1 cup sugar, 1/2 lb cream cheese, 1/2 lb cottage cheese, 2 tablespoons flour, 1 cup milk, 1 teaspoon vanilla, pinch of salt, 4 egg yolks, 4 egg whites. Cream cheeses and sugar together well. Add egg yolks, vanilla, flour and salt. Cream well together, add milk, fold in stiffened egg whites. Bake in greased pan or pyrex dish one hour at 350 degrees.
tags: cheesecake, dessert, baked, family recipe

name: Sour Cream Cake
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 lb soft salted butter
2 cup sugar
4  eggs (beaten lightly)
1 pint sour cream
2 tsp vanilla
4 cup sifted flour
2 tsp baking powder
2 tsp baking soda
1/2 tsp salt
1 cup chopped walnuts
1/2 cup sugar (for topping, mixed with cinnamon)
1 tsp cinnamon (for topping, mixed with sugar)
instruction_blocks:
1. In bowl cream butter and sugar. Add eggs, sour cream and vanilla, beating until well blended.
2. Sift flour, baking powder, salt, and baking soda together. Add gradually to cream mixture, beating after each addition.
3. Have the walnuts, cinnamon, and sugar mixed together.
4. Grease a tube pan. Turn half of the batter into the pan.
5. Sprinkle over the top half of the nut and sugar mixture.
6. Add remaining batter. Cover the top with the rest of nut and sugar mixture.
7. Bake in moderate oven (375 degrees) for 1 hour and 45 minutes.
notes: The cinnamon-sugar and walnut mixture is combined in one cup for the topping layers.
tags: cake, sour cream, coffee cake, tube pan, walnuts, cinnamon

name: Italian Cookies
ingredient_blocks:
3 cup flour
1/2 cup sugar
3 tsp baking powder
1/4 tsp salt
3 tbsp margarine
1 tbsp orange or lemon juice
3  eggs (unbeaten)
1 tsp anise
instruction_blocks:
1. Sift flour into large bowl.
2. Mix well. Into the well sift sugar, baking powder, and salt.
3. Mix. Cut in shortening.
4. Add lemon juice and anise flavor. Mix well.
5. Add eggs, knead well.
6. Shape cookies.
7. Bake about 10 minutes or until lightly brown at 350 degrees.
notes: Recipe page also contains a second recipe for Sesame Seed Cookies
tags: Italian, cookies, anise

name: 12 Bean Soup
summary: A hearty soup made with 12 different beans that can be made a day ahead and frozen. Serve with bread and green salad for a hearty meal.
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb 12 bean mix (assortment of aduki, black, black eye, garbanzo, great northern, green split, green lentils, jacobs cattle, pinto, red kidney, red lentils, soldier, yellow eye, yellow split)
2 qt water
tomatoes (canned or fresh)
onion (chopped)
lemon juice
salt (to taste)
pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Soak beans overnight.
2. Rinse beans and add to 2 quarts water.
3. Bring to boil, then simmer 2½-3 hours.
4. Add tomatoes (canned or fresh), chopped onion, lemon juice, salt and pepper to taste.
5. Simmer 30 minutes.
notes: Variations: Add rice, barley, or pasta. Put grated cheese in bottom of soup bowl and cover with hot soup. Add ham hock, smoked sausage, or chicken. Use beans to make chili. Add chopped celery, green pepper, garlic, and parsley. Soup can be made a day ahead and frozen. Serve with bread and green salad for a hearty meal. Recipe from Nilsdotter, Box 229, Orono Maine 04473. ©1986
tags: soup, beans, comfort food, make ahead, freezer friendly, vegetarian

name: Anise Biscuits
ingredient_blocks:
3 1/2 cup flour (1 pound or 3 1/2 cups)
1/2 tsp cream of tartar
1 tsp bicarbonate of soda
3 tbsp anise seed (optional)
1/2 lb butter or Crisco (1 stick)
1 cup sugar
2 tbsp anise extract
2  eggs
1/2 cup milk (use a little less)
instruction_blocks:
1. Mix together flour, cream of tartar, bicarbonate of soda, and anise seed (optional) and set aside.
2. Place butter in large bowl and beat until smooth with sugar.
3. Add eggs and extract. Add dry ingredients alternately with milk.
4. Divide into 3 or 4 equal parts. Make them 1/2 inch low and long and narrow.
5. Bake middle shelf 400 degrees for about 20 minutes.
6. Slice after cooling for a minute or so, place slices side down on cookie sheet and brown for about 5 to 10 minutes.
notes: You can brown them without turning them over. Handwritten annotations suggest using 3 1/2 cups flour and a little less than 1/2 cup milk.
tags: cookies, biscotti, anise, Italian

name: Ode to Potato Salad
summary: This is a humorous poem about a potato salad disaster, not an actual recipe. Written by Carol Ann for Memorial Day 1982, describing a failed potato salad brought to Pat's house.
notes: This document is a comedic poem rather than a recipe. It describes Carol Ann bringing a homemade potato salad to Pat's house that apparently tasted terrible. The poem mentions 'special ingredients' and that 'only Greg and Ann Marie know what she has faced.' It ends by offering 'a great big laugh' to whoever can guess what went wrong, 'and from Carol, a sigh.' The poem is dated Memorial Day 1982.
tags: poem, potato salad, humor, family memory, Memorial Day

name: Grated Potato Pancake
summary: It is important to wring all excess moisture from potatoes to ensure a crisp pancake. If you are uncomfortable flipping the pancake, you may invert it onto a plate, and slide it back into the pan after adding the remaining oil.
ingredient_blocks:
2  Idaho potatoes (about 1 1/4 pounds)
2 tsp canola oil
coarse salt (to taste)
freshly ground pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Peel potatoes, and grate on the large holes of a box grater. Place grated potatoes in a clean kitchen towel, and squeeze tightly, removing all excess moisture.
2. Heat a 10-inch nonstick skillet over medium-high heat, and add 1 teaspoon canola oil. When very hot, add grated potatoes, and season with salt and pepper. Use a spatula to press potatoes into a flat, round shape. Reduce heat to medium, and cook, shaking pan periodically, until pancake is nicely browned, 5 to 7 minutes. Flip pancake, and add remaining teaspoon of oil, drizzling around the edges of pancake. Gently swirl pan to distribute oil. Season again with salt and pepper. Continue cooking until golden brown and crisp on both sides, 5 to 7 minutes more.
3. Transfer to a cutting board; cut into wedges. Serve immediately.
notes: Recipe from Martha Stewart Living May 2001
tags: potato, pancake, side dish, vegetarian, crispy

name: Balsamic Marinated Skirt Steak
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup balsamic vinegar
1/4 cup olive oil
3  rosemary sprigs
3  garlic cloves (thinly sliced)
1 1/2 lb skirt steak
coarse salt (to taste)
freshly ground pepper (to taste)
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine balsamic vinegar, olive oil, rosemary, and garlic in a glass or plastic dish and stir to combine. Add skirt steak, and coat well with marinade. Cover, and refrigerate at least 30 minutes, and up to 4 hours.
2. Preheat broiler. Remove steak from marinade and transfer to a rimmed baking sheet. Season generously with salt and pepper on both sides. Place under broiler, and cook about 3 minutes. Turn over, and cook 3 minutes more for medium rare.
3. Transfer to a cutting board to rest for 5 to 10 minutes. Slice thinly across the grain, and serve.
notes: Recipe from Martha Stewart Living May 2001. Some text in the scanned recipe was partially obscured but reconstructed based on context.
tags: beef, grilled, marinated, quick

name: Eggnog
ingredient_blocks:
24  egg yolks
1 1/2 cup sugar
2 qt milk
2 fifth rum
2 qt heavy cream
instruction_blocks:
1. Beat yolks with beater until light.
2. Add sugar. Continue beating until thick and pale.
3. Stir in milk and rum.
4. Chill 3 hours.
5. Just before serving, fold in heavy cream stiffly whipped.
6. Chill 1 hour.
7. Sprinkle with nutmeg.
notes: Recipe card is stained and handwriting is difficult to read in places. Page 1 appears to be an alternate version mentioning 12 eggs, beating separately yolks and whites, 1 cup sugar for yolks, 1 cup for whites, 1 qt milk, and letting stand 1 hour. Page 2 contains the clearer version transcribed here.
tags: holiday, beverage, Christmas, dessert drink

name: Strawberry Bread
ingredient_blocks:
20 oz strawberries (thawed)
4  eggs
1 1/4 cup salad oil
3 cup flour
2 cup sugar
3 tsp cinnamon
1 tsp baking soda
1 tsp salt
1 cup nuts (chopped)
instruction_blocks:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Grease and flour two 9x5 inch loaf pans; set aside.
3. In medium bowl stir thawed strawberries, eggs and oil.
4. In large bowl combine flour, sugar, cinnamon, baking soda, salt and nuts.
5. Add strawberry mixture to dry ingredients and stir just until blended.
6. Pour into pans.
7. Bake one hour or until toothpick inserted in center comes out clean.
notes: Makes two loaves, about 195 calories per 1/2 inch slice. Serves 02 noted on recipe card.
tags: bread, strawberry, quick bread, dessert bread

name: Jasper White's Boston Baked Beans
summary: The Pacific Northwest saw the same thing happen to its traditional baked beans and limas.
ingredient_blocks:
1 lb dried navy or Great Northern beans
8 oz salt pork (thin strips and 1/2-inch dice)
1  medium onion (medium dice)
4  garlic cloves (finely chopped)
2  medium tomatoes (peeled, seeded, diced)
1 tsp salt
2 tsp freshly ground pepper
1/4 cup Coleman's mustard
1/3 cup maple syrup
1/3 cup molasses
2  bay leaves
2 tbsp cider vinegar
instruction_blocks:
1. Place beans in a pot, add water to cover and let soak overnight.
2. Drain the beans. Sprinkle the diced salt pork and onion over the bottom of an earthenware bean pot or crock and add the beans.
3. In a saucepan, bring 1 quart of water to a boil, add the garlic, tomatoes, salt, pepper, mustard, maple syrup, molasses, bay leaves and vinegar.
4. Reduce heat, simmer 1 minute, then pour over beans.
5. Preheat oven to 250 degrees. Arrange strips of salt pork over the beans, cover the pot and bake 4 hours, adding more boiling water halfway through the cooking if necessary, to keep beans covered.
6. Uncover and bake 1 hour more.
notes: From Maine Sunday Telegram, October 9, 1994. Allene White lives in Blue Hill Falls.
tags: baked beans, Boston, traditional, New England

name: Broiled Salmon with Lime and Cilantro
summary: A quick broiled salmon dish with fresh cilantro, garlic, and lime
ingredient_blocks:
1/2 cup cilantro leaves (finely chopped)
1  large clove garlic (finely chopped)
2 tbsp lime juice
1 tbsp olive oil
1/2 tsp salt
4  salmon steaks (3/4 inch thick, about 6 ounces each)
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine cilantro, garlic, lime juice, oil and salt in bowl. Reserve 2 tablespoons. Pour remainder over fish on plate. Let stand, covered, 10 minutes.
2. Spray broiler rack with nonstick cooking spray. Place salmon on rack. Brush with 1 tablespoon marinade.
3. Broil 6 inches from heat for 3 to 4 minutes. Turn steaks over. Brush with remaining tablespoon marinade. Broil 3 more minutes or until cooked through.
notes: Makes 4 servings at $2.83 each. Nutrient Value Per Serving: 273 calories, 34 g protein, 11 g fat, 0 g carbohydrate, 385 mg sodium, 94 mg cholesterol. Exchanges: 4 lean meat, 1 fat.
tags: seafood, salmon, broiled, quick, healthy, cilantro, lime

name: Caesar Salad
summary: You can make this famous salad your own thing and get yourself a reputation as a great salad showman. With the makings set up ahead, toss it dramatically and judiciously at the table, just like the captain does at one of your favorite fine restaurants.
ingredient_blocks:
6 tbsp olive oil
1  clove garlic (crushed)
1 cup French bread cubes
2  large heads romaine
1/4 cup salad oil
1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese
3/4 tsp salt
freshly ground pepper
1  raw egg
1  large lemon (or 3 tablespoons lemon juice)
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine olive oil and garlic; let stand overnight.
2. Toast bread cubes till golden (or fry bread cubes in 2 tablespoons garlic oil till crisp if you prefer).
3. Wash salad greens; dry on towel. Break into bite-size pieces and chill (you should have about 2 quarts).
4. To toss at table have everything else prepared. Put greens in large salad bowl with plenty of headroom for tossing.
5. Sprinkle with salad oil and 1/4 cup of the garlic oil. Toss by lifting greens with fork and spoon, then turning gently until greens are glistening.
6. Sprinkle with cheese; salt and pepper. Break egg on top; squeeze lemon juice over all. Toss until egg is blended completely.
7. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Toss croutons in rest of garlic oil; add to salad. Toss lightly and serve at once.
notes: Fry bread cubes in 2 tablespoons garlic oil till crisp if you prefer.
tags: salad, Italian, classic, tableside

name: Hot Pepper Stuffing
ingredient_blocks:
8 cup bread crumbs (seasoned)
2 lb Italian sausage (14 oz - possibly referring to weight)
1/8 cup oregano
1/8 cup parsley
1/4 cup red pepper
1/4 cup parmesan
1/4 cup vinegar
1/4 cup oil
2 tsp garlic
1 tsp black pepper
instruction_blocks: 1. No instructions provided - this appears to be an ingredient list only for stuffing hot peppers
notes: Handwriting is difficult to read in places. The '2# 14oz' likely refers to Italian sausage weight. Some measurements and ingredients are uncertain due to handwriting quality. This appears to be a stuffing mixture for hot peppers.
tags: Italian, stuffing, hot peppers, appetizer

name: Fleischmann's 60 Minute Reuben Loaf
summary: A meal-in-a-loaf. A long, large loaf of delicious homemade bread stuffed with a nutritious Reuben filling. And you can do it in just 60 minutes from start to finish.
ingredient_blocks:
3-1/4 cup all-purpose flour
1 tbsp sugar
1 tsp salt
1 package Fleischmann's RapidRise Yeast
1 cup hot water (125°-130°F)
1 tbsp Blue Bonnet Margarine (softened)
1/4 cup thousand island dressing
6 oz corned beef (thinly sliced, or 1 (7-ounce) can corned beef)
1/4 lb Swiss cheese (sliced)
1  8-ounce can sauerkraut (well-drained)
1  egg white (beaten)
caraway seed (optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. Set aside 1 cup flour. In large bowl, mix remaining flour, sugar, salt and yeast. Stir in hot water and margarine. Mix in only enough reserved flour to make soft dough. On floured surface, knead 4 min.
2. On greased baking sheet, roll dough to 14 x 10 inches. Spread dressing down center third of dough length. Top with layers of beef, cheese and sauerkraut. Cut 1-inch wide strips along sides of filling out to dough edges. Alternating sides, fold strips at an angle across filling. Cover dough; place sheet over large shallow pan half-filled with boiling water for 15 min.
3. Brush with egg white; sprinkle with caraway seed. Bake at 400°F for 25 min. or until done. Cool slightly; serve warm. Refrigerate leftovers; reheat to serve.
notes: Makes 1 loaf. To use Fleischmann's Regular Active Dry Yeast: Dissolve yeast in 1 cup warm water (105°-115°F). Mix in margarine, sugar, salt and enough flour to make dough. After kneading, cover; let rise in greased bowl over hot water 20 min. Punch down; shape, let rise 20 min. Bake as above. For more easy recipes, send $1.00 to: Bake-It-Easy Cookbook, P.O. Box 337, Department A-6, Teaneck, NJ 07666. Allow 4-6 weeks for delivery. Call Hotline (1-800-932-7800) if you need any help.
tags: bread, Reuben, stuffed bread, yeast bread, quick bread, meal-in-a-loaf, sandwich

name: Cranberry Bread
summary: From the Family Guide to Cape Cod
ingredient_blocks:
1/3 cup orange juice
1  beaten egg
2 cup flour
1/2 tsp salt
1 1/2 tsp baking powder
1 tsp baking soda
1 cup sugar
3 tbsp shortening
1/2 cup chopped cranberries
instruction_blocks:
1. Add enough hot water to orange juice to make 3/4 cup liquid.
2. Add 1 beaten egg.
3. Add dry ingredients and shortening to above. Beat then add cranberries.
4. Put in greased bread pan and bake at 350 for one hour.
notes: This handwritten recipe appears to be from a Cape Cod family cookbook. Some words were difficult to read due to handwriting style.
tags: bread, cranberry, quick bread, Cape Cod, baking

name: Bean Soup
ingredient_blocks:
6  bacon slices (diced)
1  onion (large, diced)
2  carrots (diced)
1  potato (large, diced)
4  celery stalks (diced)
1 lb white beans (Navy or Northern)
2 tsp garlic (Italian)
1 tsp Mrs. Dash salt
2 tbsp garlic olive oil (if necessary)
1  bay leaf
1  ham hock
1 dash garlic powder
7 cup water
instruction_blocks:
1. Rinse and soak beans overnight.
2. Spray soup pot with PAM. In soup pot, sauté bacon about 2 minutes on medium-high heat.
3. Add onion, sauté 2 minutes more.
4. Add remaining vegetables (and oil if necessary). Sauté 3 minutes.
5. Add beans, ham hock, and seasonings. Mix.
6. Add water. Bring to boil covered, rolling simmer covered for 3-4 hours.
notes: Some handwriting was difficult to read. 'Mrs. Dash salt' may be 'Mrs. Dash' seasoning salt. Cook time is 3-4 hours at a rolling simmer.
tags: soup, beans, comfort food, ham

name: Cheese Ball
ingredient_blocks:
12 oz softened cream cheese
6 oz softened blue cheese
6 oz processed cheddar cheese spread
2 tbsp grated onion
1 tsp Worcestershire sauce
1/8 tsp monosodium glutamate
1 cup ground pecans
1/2 cup finely chopped parsley
instruction_blocks:
1. In a medium size bowl, combine cheeses, onion, Worcestershire sauce and monosodium glutamate.
2. Beat until well blended.
3. Stir in 1/2 cup pecans and 1/4 cup of parsley.
4. Shape into a ball and wrap in freezer paper.
5. Refrigerate overnight.
6. About 1 hour before serving roll ball in remaining pecans and parsley.
7. Serve with crackers.
tags: appetizer, cheese, party food

name: Bubble Cinnamon Cake
summary: A favorite recipe from Patty DeGennaro
ingredient_blocks:
4  cans refrigerator buttermilk biscuits
3/4 cup sugar
1 tsp cinnamon (I double it)
1 stick butter or margarine (1/2 cup, mixed together with 1/3 cup sugar)
1/3 cup sugar (for butter mixture)
instruction_blocks:
1. Cut each biscuit into 4 quarters.
2. Roll each piece in the sugar/cinnamon mixture.
3. Throw each piece into a bundt pan that has been sprayed with Pam.
4. Bring the one stick of butter or margarine to a boil with the 1/3 cup sugar in it.
5. Pour this over the pieces of biscuits.
6. Bake at 350° for 45 min.
7. Remove from pan immediately.
notes: The cinnamon amount can be doubled according to the original recipe writer. Important to remove from pan immediately after baking.
tags: breakfast, cinnamon, monkey bread, pull-apart bread, easy

name: Chart House Bleu Cheese Dressing
summary: Did you know that the Chart House makes its own dressings from scratch?
ingredient_blocks:
3/4 cup sour cream
1/2 tsp dry mustard
1/2 tsp black pepper
1/2 tsp salt (scant)
1/3 tsp garlic powder (scant)
1 tsp Worcestershire sauce
1-1/3 cup mayonnaise
4 oz Danish Bleu Cheese (crumbled imported)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine first 6 ingredients and blend 2 minutes at low speed.
2. Add mayonnaise and blend 1/2 minute at low speed, then increase speed to medium and blend an additional 2 minutes.
3. Slowly add bleu cheese and blend at low speed no longer than 4 minutes.
4. Refrigerate for 24 hours before serving.
notes: Yield: 2-1/2 cups
tags: dressing, bleu cheese, Chart House, salad dressing

name: Foolproof Beef and Broccoli
ingredient_blocks:
3/4 lb boneless beef sirloin steak
1 tbsp vegetable oil
1  clove garlic (minced)
1  medium onion (cut into wedges)
1 can Campbell's Cream of Broccoli Soup (10 3/4 oz.)
1/2 cup water
1 tbsp soy sauce
2 cups broccoli flowerets
hot cooked noodles
instruction_blocks:
1. Slice beef across the grain into very thin strips.
2. In skillet, over medium-high heat, in hot oil, cook beef and garlic until beef is browned. Add onion. Cook 5 min., stirring often.
3. Stir in soup, water and soy sauce. Heat to boiling. Add broccoli. Reduce heat to low. Cover; simmer 5 min. or until vegetables are tender. Serve over noodles.
notes: TIP: To make slicing easier, freeze beef 1 hour
tags: beef, broccoli, stir-fry, quick, weeknight dinner

name: Anytime Broccoli Cheese Sauce
summary: A quick cheese sauce to serve over vegetables such as broccoli, cauliflower and carrots
ingredient_blocks:
1 can Campbell's NEW Broccoli Cheese Soup (10¾ oz.)
1/3 cup milk
instruction_blocks:
1. Combine soup and milk.
2. Conventional: Over medium heat, heat until hot and bubbling, stirring often.
3. Microwave: Microwave, uncovered, on HIGH 5 min. or until hot and bubbling, stirring halfway through heating.
4. Serve over vegetables such as broccoli, cauliflower and carrots (about 4 cups).
notes: Prep Time: 3 min. Conventional or Microwave Cook Time: 5 min. Variation - Broccoli Sauce: Add ¼ cup grated Parmesan cheese.
tags: cheese sauce, quick, microwave, vegetables, Campbell's

name: Food Processor Pesto
summary: From the kitchen of Luci - The New Italian Cooking
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup fresh basil leaves (loosely packed)
3 tbsp parsley (leaves from 5 sprigs)
1 tsp coarse salt
3  medium garlic cloves (peeled and cut in half)
2 tbsp pine nuts (lightly toasted)
2 tbsp walnuts (chopped, lightly toasted)
1 tbsp romano cheese (grated)
1 tbsp parmesan cheese (grated)
0.5 cup olive oil (Appolinari brand mentioned)
instruction_blocks:
1. Place everything but oil in processor without knife/blade. Process on/off briefly until all ingredients form a very fine mixture.
2. Don't add oil! Continuing to process should be consistency of a thick sauce.
3. Serve at room temp on individual portions of pasta to which either tbs of unsalted butter or boiling pasta water has been added.
4. To conserve, put in jar with tight lid, make sure a thin film of oil has formed on top. If not, add a few drops. Keep refrigerated until time to use, bring to room temperature.
notes: Handwriting is difficult to read in some places. The recipe emphasizes not adding oil during initial processing. Storage instructions included for preserving the pesto.
tags: Italian, pesto, sauce, pasta, basil, food processor

name: Baked Macaroni and Cheese
ingredient_blocks:
2 tbsp butter or margarine
2 tbsp flour
1 tsp salt
1 tsp dry mustard
2 1/2 cup milk
2 cup shredded cheddar cheese (8 ounces)
8 oz elbow macaroni (2 cups)
1/4 cup buttered bread crumbs
paprika
instruction_blocks:
1. In saucepan, melt butter. Remove from heat; blend in flour, salt, and mustard.
2. Add milk; heat, stirring constantly, until sauce thickens a little and is smooth.
3. Add 1 1/2 cups cheese; heat until melted, stirring occasionally.
4. Meanwhile, cook macaroni as directed; drain.
5. Combine with sauce in a 2-quart casserole; top with remaining cheese, bread crumbs, and paprika.
6. Bake at 375 degrees about 20 to 25 minutes or until nicely browned and bubbly.
notes: Mueller Egg Noodle Recipe. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
tags: comfort food, casserole, baked, pasta, cheese

name: Kebab Curry
summary: Curry-marinated beef kebabs grilled over charcoal and served with rice and marinade
ingredient_blocks:
3 tbsp butter (for sautéing)
3  onions (sliced)
2  garlic cloves (minced)
1 tbsp curry powder
1/2 tsp salt
3 tbsp hot water
3  large tomatoes (quartered)
1/2 cup hot beef stock
2 1/2 lb steak (cut into 1 1/2" cubes)
onion (sliced, for skewering)
fresh ginger (sliced, for skewering)
melted butter (for basting)
cooked rice (for serving)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a saucepan, melt 3 tablespoons of butter and in it sauté 3 onions sliced and 2 garlic cloves minced, until lightly browned.
2. Stir in 1 tablespoon of curry powder, 1/2 teaspoon of salt and 3 tablespoons of hot water and simmer for 15 minutes.
3. Add 3 large tomatoes quartered and 1/2 cup of hot beef stock. Simmer for 45 minutes.
4. Cool marinade before using.
5. Cut 2 1/2 pounds of steak into 1 1/2" cubes. Pour sauce over meat and let meat marinate in sauce for 2 hours.
6. Remove meat from sauce and skewer it alternating with slices of onion and sliced fresh ginger.
7. Brown meat on all sides over hot charcoal fire basting frequently with melted butter.
8. Serve with cooked rice and marinade. Reheat marinade to boiling point.
notes: Recipe from Gourmet Cookbook, Volume II
tags: Indian, curry, kebab, grilled, beef, charcoal, marinade

name: Blackberry Frozen Yogurt
summary: This frosty delight has less than 1 gram of fat and only 106 calories per serving
ingredient_blocks:
2 cup frozen or fresh unsweetened blackberries (or one 16½-oz. can blackberries, drained)
1/3 to 1/2 cup sugar
1 tsp unflavored gelatin
1/2 cup skim milk
1  8-oz. carton plain nonfat yogurt
1 tbsp orange peel (finely shredded)
1/4 cup orange juice
instruction_blocks:
1. Thaw berries, if frozen. Meanwhile, in medium saucepan combine sugar and gelatin; stir in milk and ¼ cup water. Heat just till gelatin dissolves. Set aside to cool.
2. In a food processor bowl process berries till smooth. Press through sieve; discard seeds. Stir puree, yogurt, orange peel, and juice into gelatin mixture. Turn into a 2-quart electric ice-cream freezer. Freeze according to manufacturer's directions.
3. Or, pour into a 9x5x3-inch pan. Cover; freeze about 6 hours. Break into chunks. Transfer to a chilled bowl. Beat with an electric mixer till smooth but not melted. Return to cold loaf pan. Cover; freeze about 8 hours. Makes six ½-cup servings.
notes: $200 Winner recipe by Georgia Baciu, Friday Harbor, Wash. Nutrition information per serving: 106 cal. (3% from fat), 0.3 g fat, 1 mg chol., 4 g pro., 24 g carbo., 41 mg sodium. U.S. RDA: 15% vit. C, 15% calcium.
tags: light desserts, frozen yogurt, low-fat, blackberry, prize winning

name: Shrimp Curry
summary: A lightened-up curry recipe nicknamed 'Snow on the Mountain' for the avalanche of fresh coconut sprinkled atop. This make-over version saved 39 grams of fat.
notes: This is a magazine clipping from Better Homes and Gardens, March 1992, page 123. The full recipe is referenced on page 135. Tips included: Flavor with buttermilk - Use low-calorie, low-fat buttermilk in the sauce. Choose condiments wisely - Both coconut and nuts, traditional toppings for curry dishes, pack a lot of calories. Top each serving with lighter condiments such as bananas, raisins, and pineapple. Or, use just a sprinkling of coconut or nuts, if you like. Thicken with cornstarch - Eliminate the fat needed for a flour roux by mixing cornstarch with water instead. We shaved off almost 1 tablespoon of fat per serving this way. Recipe submitted by Beryl Manley Wauson of Centennial, Wyoming.
tags: curry, shrimp, seafood, low-fat, make-over, healthy

name: Fall Apple Cake
summary: A healthier make-over recipe that saved 214 calories. My family liked the make-over better. The cake tastes marvelous!
ingredient_blocks:
1 1/4 cup sugar
1/2 cup cooking oil
2  egg whites
1  egg
2 tsp vanilla
1 cup apple juice
2 cup all-purpose flour
3/4 cup whole wheat flour
2 tsp baking powder
2 tsp ground cinnamon
1 tsp ground nutmeg
1/2 tsp baking soda
3 cup cooking apples (peeled, cored, and sliced (such as Golden Delicious or McIntosh))
1/2 cup walnuts (finely chopped, toasted)
powdered sugar (optional)
vanilla yogurt (optional)
instruction_blocks:
1. In a large mixer bowl beat sugar and oil on medium speed of an electric mixer till combined; add eggs and vanilla. Beat on medium speed for 1 minute or till creamy. Stir in juice.
2. In a medium mixing bowl stir together the all-purpose flour, whole wheat flour, baking powder, cinnamon, nutmeg, and baking soda. Stir flour mixture into egg mixture. Gently stir in the apples and the walnuts.
3. Pour the batter into a greased and floured 10-inch tube pan.
4. Bake in a 350° oven for 60 to 65 minutes or till wooden pick inserted comes out clean. Cool in pan for 10 minutes. Remove from pan; cool on a wire rack for 30 minutes. Serve warm.
5. If desired, sprinkle the cake with powdered sugar; top each slice with a dollop of vanilla yogurt and additional nutmeg.
notes: Tips for healthier baking: Substitute egg whites for whole eggs to cut cholesterol. The rule of thumb for replacing egg whites with whole eggs: Use 2 whites for every egg yolk. Replace only about half of the yolks in a baked product because the yolks act as a tenderizer. Try juice for some of the oil. You won't be able to replace all of the oil, but we replaced more than half of it with juice. Toss in only a nibble of nuts. Finely chop nuts so they'll go farther. Recipe from Marie Ann Donovan, Chicago, Illinois. Nutrition information per serving: 323 cal., 13 g fat, 17 mg chol., 5 g pro., 49 g carbo., 99 mg sodium, 2 g fiber. U.S. RDA: 49% calcium, 11% iron, 19% thiamine, and 13% riboflavin.
tags: apple, cake, healthy, low-fat, fall, dessert, baking
